title: Deep Learning-Enhanced Cloud Accounting Model for Real-Time Fraud and Financial Risk Prediction
authors: Saiprakash Kodela, Sai Bhuvana Kurada, Venkata Babu Mogili, Jagadeesh Duggirala
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Digital financial transactions have enhanced the chances of fraud and financial mismanagement, thus the need to have a strong real-time predictive system. Fraud detection and financial risk prediction are a major problem due to the multidimensional and intricate patterns of transactions. This paper introduces a deep learning boosted cloud accounting model, which can overcome these challenges that uses ResNet+UNet to extract hierarchical features, the Enhanced Doctor and Patient Optimization (EDPO) algorithm to select the top features, as well as the Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to predict fraud and financial risk in real-time. The model is tested on Kaggle synthetic financial dataset which includes six million transactions that represent various and realistic frauds. The findings indicate that the proposed work reaches an accuracy of 99.71%, which is greater than the traditional machine-learning algorithms, including the Random Forest (98.62%) and XGBoost (99.15%). The Brier Score is 0.0045 and log loss is 0.178, reaching a high value of 0.984, which means increased reliability and calibration. Top-K analysis shows that the model can detect 976 out of 1,000 fraudulent transactions and thus it shows that a deep feature extraction, best selection, and reinforcement learning can enable the creation of an accurate and scalable cloud-based system to predict fraud and risk in real-time.

title: An Intelligent Crop Recommendation System Using Ensemble and Probabilistic Learning Approaches
authors: Adesh Kumar, Abhishek Mathur, Manish Kumar Mishra, Amandeep Kaur, Kamalpreet Kaur, Dipika Mal
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: India where agriculture is the primary livelihood for a large population, recommending the most suitable crop based on environmental and soil parameters can drastically improve yield and resource utilization. This project uses a data set of various soil and weather parameters to develop a machine learning model that recommends the ideal crop for a given set of conditions. The system leverages classification algorithms and evaluates their performance to select the most accurate predictor. Precision agriculture is emerging as a transformative approach to improve productivity and sustainability in modern agriculture. This project presents an intelligent crop recommendation system powered by supervised machine learning techniques. Using a curated dataset comprising 2200 records with agroclimatic parameters — namely nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), potassium (K), temperature, humidity, pH, and rainfall — the system is designed to predict the most suitable crop for given environmental conditions. A comparative analysis of ten classification algorithms including Naive Bayes, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) was conducted. The Naive Bayes model demonstrated superior predictive performance, achieving a peak accuracy of 99.5%. The proposed framework offers a scalable decision-support mechanism for farmers, aligning with the objectives of precision agriculture.

title: Signal Processing in Communication Systems Using Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Shreesha Kalkoor Mudradi, Meenakshi Maindola, Smitha K, Nazeer Shaik, Harshal Patil, Natrayan L
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Signal control is considered to play a pivotal role in increasing the efficiency and dependability of the current communication networks. This analysis looks at how AI methods can be combined to address improve signal handling work processes, with a focus on three stages, namely, features selection, highlight determination and characterization. In signal information preprocessing Basic standardisation strategy is employed ensuring that the review is standardised and is working on the foundations of the results obtained. Throughout this paper, PCA is used to identify highlights and it is demonstrated that PCA can successfully reduce dimensions and maintain the fundamental data format whilst increasing the computational efficiency. The characterization is done using Angle Helping Machines (GBMs) because it is capable of processing the Information Appropriation and it is very accurate. Experimental results testify that it processes huge improvements on signal order quality in addition to speed contrast to traditional methods. Capable of addressing the problems of correspondence structures, the proposed framework suggests technology capable of accommodating the real implementation to an accommodating and effective degree. The analysis presented in this work paves the way even further to other developments in adroit sign management.

title: IoT-Enabled Remote Restoration and Enhancement of Mammogram Images adapted to Poisson Noise using Deep Residual CNN
authors: Sanjeev Singh Rawat, R.B. Yadav, Manoj Kumar, Tara
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Poisson noise poses significant challenges in medical imaging, particularly in mammography, where accurate representation is crucial for reliable diagnosis. To denoise mammography images affected by Poisson noise, this paper introduces a novel method utilizing a deep residual Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). By employing a residual learning framework, the proposed approach enables the network to effectively learn residual mappings. This enhanced denoising capability is vital for the rapidly evolving field of IoT-enabled medical imaging, where devices may capture and transmit images from diverse locations, often leveraging cloud infrastructure for storage and computational power, necessitating robust preprocessing to maintain data integrity and ensure diagnostic accuracy in real-time or near real-time. Comprehensive testing and comparison with standard Poisson denoising methods demonstrate that the deep residual CNN outperforms others. The evaluation considers various noise levels and reflects scenarios typical of real-world medical imaging, including data sourced from multiple IoT-connected sensors and imaging units. The deep residual CNN significantly enhances denoising performance, as shown by the results, indicating its ability to extract intricate structures and features from mammograms. This study identifies the deep residual CNN as a powerful and superior option, offering promising enhancements for mammogram images that are Poisson-corrupted, with the potential to improve diagnostic accuracy in breast cancer detection and characterization, ultimately bolstering the reliability of IoT-driven healthcare diagnostic workflows.

title: Metabolic-Based Prediction of Eye Disease Using a Random Forest Classifier and Clinical Feature Analysis
authors: Priya Pachouri, K. Keerthi Rishitha, K. Aneetta Vinu, K.Sanjana, K. Hari Keerthana
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Here we describe a novel approach to machine learning–based eye disease and diabetic retinopathy prediction from structured clinical data. The dataset that was used for this work derived from the synthetic-EPicastis database with 20,000 patient records containing real-life demographics, metabolic, cardiovascular and ocular health variables at most preprocessed through a single pipeline involving data sanitisation, feature scaling and one-hot encoding. We implemented Random Forest classifier, which is baseline and is robust for non-linear and high dimensional data, trained using 80:20 stratified split ensuring class balance. Results from model evaluation indicated clinically meaningful prediction patterns with 3 predictors with the highest influence including the presence of metabolic biomarkers, status with diabetic retinopathy and age (Table 1). The model performed reasonably well for overall accuracy and exceedingly well for false positive rates among all age groups, but high false negative rates suggested the inability to correctly identify true disease cases, even if the disease was present, particularly in younger populations. Calibration analysis showed that the model was very well calibrated for predictions with high confidence but it overstated risk on predictions with mid-probability ranges. The accuracy continued to improve with age peaked at individuals 80+ where disease markers are more apparent. In conclusion, these results emphasize that metabolic and demographic data can facilitate eye disease prediction while demonstrating the need for increased sensitivity and better probability calibration for the early-screening application of these models.

title: A Bibliometric Analysis of Victim Identification through AI-Based Image Forensics
authors: Smita Khairnar, Shilpa Gite, Keya Jadhav, Mohini Jadhav, Om Jadhav, Atharv Jadhav
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have significantly influenced digital image-based image forensic science by using super-advanced techniques to identify victims from visual evidence that is degraded, tampered, or manipulated. The fast spread of deepfakes, spoofing attacks, and synthetic media has raised serious doubts about the reliability of digital evidence; thus, traditional matcher-based techniques, manual visual inspection, and handcrafted feature descriptors are becoming less and less effective. As a result, research on AI-powered image restoration, recognition, and verification methods has increased dramatically, with the aim of recovering trustworthy visual cues from the degraded data. This research carries out an exhaustive bibliometric study of AI-based image forensics for victim identifi-cation, with Scopus as the main data source and Web of Science and Google Scholar for cross-verification. Structured Boolean queries led to the retrieval of 186 peer-reviewed documents published between 2015 and 2025, which were then analyzed with VOSviewer (version 1.6.19). The analysis is an examination of publication growth trends, citation patterns, co-authorship networks, keyword co-occurrences, top authors, institutions, source titles, and country contributions. The quantitative results show an almost nine-fold increase in annual publications, and the peak year is 2023 (54 documents), which represent the rapid adoption of deep learning methods in forensic investigation studies. The result of keyword co-occurrence analysis indicates a strong convergence of computer vision with digital forensics studies, therefore the most thematic clusters are centered on face recognition, deep learning-based image restoration, and anti-spoofing. Lastly, geographical analysis reveals China as the most prominent contributor, followed by substantial contributions from the United Kingdom and Saudi Arabia, while institutional collaboration networks demonstrate an increasing global and interdisciplinary approach.In conclusion, the research shows that AI-based image forensics has transformed into a quickly growing research domain, characterized by increasing methodological maturity, collaborative research arrangements, and forensic relevance with digital image forensics, computer vision, and law enforcement technologies [1].

title: A Static Sign Language Interpretation for Individuals with Hearing Impairment based on Deep CNN Framework
authors: Sanjoy Debnath, Amit Baran Dey, Ravi Singh Kurmvanshi, Ritwik Haldar, Sunandita Debnath, Wasim Arif
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: A highly efficient gesture recognition system is necessary for hearing-impaired persons to express their thoughts. An effective model that translate sign language to words is valuable for such communication. In this paper, we proposed a deep convolutional neural net-work (CNN) based approach to recognize the hand gesture accurately. American Sign Language (ASL) that consist of around 87,000 images of 26 classes (A-Z) is used for training purpose of the model. A comparative analysis is performed with the VGG-19 and AlexNet. The proposed model shows a significant performance characteristics with an accuracy of 98.70% as compare to the state-of-the-art models. The achieved results demonstrates the possible applicability of the proposed model in recognition of real-time hand gestures. The achieved results also highlights the potential incorporation of the proposed model in assistive and interactive communication systems.

title: AI-Driven Impact Analysis of Clinical Parameters Influencing Menopause
authors: Mane Krushna Balaji, Kiruthika V, Meghavika Baidya, Shery Angel Rajakumar, Kulkarni Atharva Gopinath
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Menopause, a critical transition in a woman's life, is associated with diverse symptoms. Most existing studies focus on the analysis of menopause severity based on a single or specific factor only. Interrelationship study between the influencing factors and severity of menopause remains under explored. This study explores the impact of various clinical parameters on menopause severity using a data-driven approach to improve clinical understanding, interpretability and management. By analyzing key clinical parametersincluding symptoms, hormonal levels, diagnostic, demographic parameters-this research identifies the most contributing features that influence the severity of menopause. The workflow involves data preprocessing, feature selection and validation. Initially the data is cleaned, and data imbalance is addressed using Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique. To facilitate the identification of key factors, feature selection techniques are integrated with machine learning techniques like Support Vector Machines (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest, and XGBoost. Model evaluation and hyperparameter tuning help to make reliable identification of the impactful features. The identified clinical features includeSystolic Blood Pressure, Estrogen, Free Triiodothyronine, Triglycerides, Free Thyroxine, Progesterone, Age, Follicle Stimulating Hormone, Prolactin and Body Mass Index whose significance was validated through statistical analyses and clinical evidence. This approach serves as a clinical decision support system aiding tailored treatment and management of menopause severity.

title: Menstrual Health Monitoring and Prediction System Using Machine Learning
authors: Janvi Gulati, Dhanashree Chila, Sneha Bhamare, Swati Nadkarni, Minakshi Retharekar, Sneha Chila
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Women's reproductive health depends on tracking their menstrual health, but the majority of tracking apps are calendar-based and lack predictive intelligence. In this paper, we introduce DateIncoming, a machine learning-based intelligent menstrual health tracking system that offers health risk analysis, symptom forecasts, and personalized predictions. Three domain-specific models are combined in the system: a weighted statistical approach with 86% accuracy for predicting cycles, a multi-output symptom prediction model with 82% accuracy for pain, flow, mood, and energy symptoms, and a condition risk indication model with 73% accuracy for various menstrual health conditions. The system is built with a secure backend, an interactive user interface, and Google Calendar integration. The app offers personalized recommendations, menstrual anomaly alerts, and next-day symptom forecasts.

title: LoRa based Fluid Monitoring System for Boiler Applications
authors: Raja M, Dhivya P, Harikrishna N, Kavin Kumar G, Soundar Balaji M A, Thiruchudar S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The monitoring of boiler systems plays a vital role in ensuring efficiency, safety, and reliability in industrial applications. Conventional monitoring techniques often rely on wired networks or internet connectivity, which may not be feasible. To overcome these challenges, this project proposes a LoRa Based Fluid Monitoring System for Boiler Applications. The system is designed to continuously measure and monitor critical water parameters such as flow rate, turbidity, and pH value using sensors. The flow sensor helps in tracking the amount of water passing through the system, while the turbidity sensor measures the clarity and purity of the water and the pH sensor ensures that the water remains within safe chemical limits for boiler operation. These measurements are crucial in preventing scale formation, corrosion, and operational hazards in boilers. A microcontroller is used as the core processing unit to acquire data from the sensors and the collected data is then transmitted wirelessly using LoRa technology, which provides a low-power, wide area communication solution. LoRa enables reliable transmission over several kilometers making it well-suited for industrial setups with large physical areas. At the receiving end, a central gateway is implemented to collect and store the transmitted data for further analysis. This gateway can be connected to data storage modules or monitoring dashboards, enabling operators to visualize real-time system performance. The proposed system eliminates dependency on internet or wired networks. Overall, this project offers cost effective, energy-efficient, and reliable solution for fluid monitoring in boilers and by providing accurate and timely information. The proposed system offers a practical and scalable solution for real-time boiler monitoring. By reducing manual inspection and providing early warning of abnormal conditions.

title: Proactive Safety Management Decision-Making Model for Power Transmission and Transformation Projects Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Guokai Weng, Huazhong Chen, Cheng Lin, Damiao Yang, Yi Huang, Jingwei Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Addressing bottlenecks in traditional safety management models for power transmission and transformation projects, such as delayed risk perception and passive decision-making, this paper proposes a proactive safety decision-making model based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL). This approach constructs a state space that integrates multi-source IoT data and operational information, designs a hierarchical safety intervention action space, and quantifies safety performance through a reward function based on domain knowledge. The Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm is then used to train an intelligent agent to autonomously learn optimal risk management strategies within a digital twin environment. Experimental results show that the PPO model's average warning time is 7.31 seconds faster than that of the expert system; the proactive detection rate of hidden dangers in unknown and complex working conditions reaches 90%; and the average investment cost of the PPO model's safety solutions in terms of safety resource input-output efficiency is 92,300 yuan. These experimental results verify the superiority of the PPO-based proactive decision-making model in risk perception, decision reliability, and optimal resource allocation, demonstrating its ability to effectively enhance the intelligent level of safety management in power transmission and transformation projects.

title: EduMind: AI Powered Interactive Study Companion
authors: G. Charles Babu, Bingi Jayanth, Padala Pranavsai, Tholakoppula Sai Kumar, Guduru Adeep Reddy, Inaganti Sandeep
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: EduMind: Providing a study aid via AI to revolutionize the involvement of students in learning. The study aid tool integrates vector search retrieval algorithms, online quiz generation mechanisms, and personal language models on a framework that maintains user privacy. The tool has a FastAPI-run backing application programming interface and a React Native-developed mobile app user interface. The study aid tool has real-time QA from learning materials, automatic topic summarization of study materials, as well as online quiz generation from study documents submitted by users/typed content. The study aid application promises secured learning information without resorting to cloud computing services using inference mechanisms from Ollama and LLMs such as Mistral 7B. The study will cover the architecture, approach/methodology, implementation process of EduMind, as well as its critical review with a focus on how the technology can help improve study outcomes while upholding user privacy.

title: Automated Quality Inspection in Robotic Assembly Lines Using Logistic Regression and Deep Feature
authors: P. Murugan, M. Manickam, B. Karthik, S Pradeksha, M Tharani.
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Robotic assembly lines require automated quality inspection to ensure precision and productivity in industrial production. It introduces a smart inspection model that combines deep feature mining and logistic regression classification to improve the level of defect detection. The model makes use of convolutional layers to learn features and logistic regression in the binary classification of defective and non-defective components. The testing accuracy of 97.9%, precision of 98.1%, recall of 97.6%, and F1-score of 97.8% obtained experimental validation with an inference time of 14.9 ms and a processing speed of 33 FPS, which allows the operation to be real-time. The system also saved the energy use by 23%, and the reliability of decision of the system improved by 4% as compared to the current approaches. The proposed solution has a better performance in terms of inspection and computational power, which has created a strong base to be used in the future in industrial applications in the fully automated robotic assembly setting.

title: Intelligent Field Monitoring For Real-Time Animal Movement Detection using A Yolo-based Deep Learning Approach
authors: D. Dhanasekaran, P. Kumar, Poovendan R, Sabari S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: One of the biggest problems in agricultural production is the intrusion of animals in crops which can result in loss of huge quantity and economic harm. Traditional methods such as manual surveillance, physical fencing and simple sensor-triggered alarms are labor intensive, expensive and likely to raise false alarms. To overcome these weaknesses, the current paper introduces a smart field monitoring system that will be used to detect movements of animals in real-time using a YOLOv11-based deep learning model. The suggested system is used to capture images and video streams in live fields, use preprocessing tools based on OpenCV, including noise reduction, resizing, and normalization, and locate animals with high precision in relation to changing lighting and environmental factors. In order to have real-time performance, feature optimization and dimensionality reduction techniques are used to reduce computational overhead and still have good detection accuracy. When an intrusion is detected, the system starts a multi-level response, which involves automatic logging of events to the system, real time email notification with details of the intrusion, and the activation of deterrence devices like buzzers and LED floodlights, particularly at night. A specific web and mobile interface makes farmers keep track of events remotely and regulate warning systems. As illustrated by experimental assessment, the system is characterized by high detection rate, low latency, and lower false positive rates and provides a cost-effective, scalable, and efficient system towards smart agriculture, crop protection, farm security, and sustainable food production.

title: TGAT-MARL: Deep Learning based Proactive Link Failure Prediction and Routing in MANETs
authors: D Kavitha, K Raghava Rao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) are highly vulnerable to frequent link failures due to node mobility, dynamic topology, and unstable wireless channels, which significantly degrade routing reliability and network performance. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a novel Proactive Link Failure Prediction and Reliable Routing framework based on Deep Learning-enabled Temporal Graph Attention and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (TGATMARL). The proposed approach leverages temporal graph attention networks to learn spatio-temporal link dynamics from realistic MANET mobility traces and proactively predicts imminent link failures, while a multi-agent reinforcement learning policy jointly optimizes routing decisions to ensure reliability and adaptability. The framework is implemented and evaluated using the SimPy simulator under a realistic MANET workload dataset derived from standard mobility and traffic patterns. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed TGAT-MARL approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, including Multi-Agent Deep Learning (MADL), GAT-based PPO, and conventional DRL. Specifically, it achieves up to 14-22% improvement in packet delivery ratio, 18-27% reduction in end-to-end delay, 20-30% reduction in routing overhead, and 16-25% improvement in throughput. These gains confirm that proactive link failure awareness combined with cooperative multi-agent learning enables more robust, efficient, and scalable routing in highly dynamic MANET environments.

title: Design and Performance Assessment of a PEM Fuel Cell–Supercapacitor Hybrid Powertrain for Green Electric Vehicles
authors: Vinaya B Koradoor, Rajath Shankar P. S, S Srinivasan, Jayachitra G, Janardhan L K
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Rising demand for eco-friendly transport fuels the rise of green hydrogen electric vehicles (GHEVs). This study introduces a scalable prototype GHEV system combining PEM fuel cells (using Nafion 117 membranes with 25 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> active area each in a two-cell series configuration), an efficient DC-DC boost converter, and supercapacitor-based hybrid energy storage. It covers the full hydrogen lifecycle from generation through vehicle integration, focusing on practical load balancing, voltage control, and system scalability factors such as weight and operating conditions. Tests demonstrated maximum PEM fuel cell power density of 0.32 W/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> at 80°C and 3 atm pressure (ranging 0.18-0.32 W/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> for 60-80°C and 1-3 atm), yielding 55% efficiency, while the complete prototype delivered 40-55% efficiency during variable load cycles. Arduino Nano-based control ensures reliable power distribution between sources. Findings validate potential for commercial stack scaling and advance India’s sustainable hydrogen transport initiatives.

title: Pose-Guided Multi-Scale Vision Transformer for Robust and Accurate Person Re-Identification in Visual Surveillance
authors: Shimja M, Anupriya Ramachandran, Krishna Priya, Saiqa Khan N, Abhilash K S, Sreekanth S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Person Re-Identification (Re-ID) is the task of matching images of the same individual captured across multiple non-overlapping surveillance cameras. It plays a crucial role in intelligent video surveillance, public safety, and criminal investigation by enabling automated tracking of individuals across different locations. However, challenges such as pose variation, occlusion, illumination changes, and inconsistent image resolution make accurate re-identification difficult. Traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and attention-based models have achieved moderate success but often fall short in capturing long-range dependencies and semantic understanding. To address these limitations, this research proposes a Pose-Guided Multi-Scale ViT-ReID model with Part Tokens and an Attribute Head, which integrates a Vision Transformer (ViT) backbone for global spatial reasoning, pose-aware token weighting to emphasize visible body parts, and multi-scale patch embedding for resolution robustness. An attribute prediction head is further employed to incorporate semantic-level identity cues. The model is trained and evaluated on the DukeMTMC-reID dataset, comprising 36,411 images across 1,812 identities from 8 cameras. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves 99.00% Rank-1 accuracy, 99.50% Rank-5 accuracy, 99.80% Rank-10 accuracy, and a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 85.12%, outperforming existing approaches. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of combining spatial, pose, and semantic cues in enhancing person re-identification performance under real-world surveillance conditions.

title: Spatial Metrics based evaluation of urban growth: A case of Jaipur
authors: Deeksha Pendharker, Asfa Siddiqui, Manoj Panwar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The spatial urbanization patterns show deviation from the developments proposed in master plans and highlights the timely interventions through informed decision making. Spatial metrics have been very helpful in examining the urban growth dynamics and its growth directions. Authors used well established Shannon Entropy and Area Weighted Mean Expansion Index (AWMEI) to analyze compactness and dispersal of urban growth; and Landscape Expansion Index (LEI) analyses urban growth type as infill, edge expansion or outlying growth. The analysis is done on opensource QGIS 3.24 by taking case of Jaipur urban area using data from Global Human Settlement Layer program of European Commission Joint Research Center from the year 1990 to year 2020. The results of Shannon Entropy and AWMEI are highly correlated and inform that urbanization rate is high in 2010-2020 decade in comparison to previous decades. The results of LEI apprise that the city maintains urban core and grows along transportation corridors while increased outgrowth is observed near satellite towns. These findings are significant for urban planners and policymakers as a base to formulate and establish long term strategies, and timely interventions in masterplan for managing Jaipur’s urbanization through informed decisions.

title: Design and Implementation of 32-bit Pipelined RISC Processor with VLSI optimized ALU and AI Acceleration : Efficient Pipeline Architecture with High Performance ALU Optimization
authors: Nithin V Y, Pramod Raj T, Shraddha Kalmath, Srushti R, Hariprasad T L
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Designing and implementing high-performance processors requires a trade-off between speed, area, and energy efficiency, increasingly relevant in modern computing environments where artificial intelligence (AI) workloads comprise a growing share of the total workloads. This paper presents the architecture and implementation of a 32-bit pipelined RISC processor with a VLSI-optimized Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), coupled with the processor’s specific AI acceleration. The processor uses a five-stage instruction pipeline—fetch, decode, execute, memory access, and write-back—to boost instruction throughput while reducing structural and data hazards. The ALU has been optimized at the transistor and gate levels to use low power while minimizing critical path delay for energy-efficient silicon real estate. Furthermore, an AI acceleration unit has been added to perform computationally heavy tasks, like machine learning inference, based on combining specialized multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations with parallel datapath structure. The processor was implemented in Verilog HDL, verified in ModelSim simulation, and shows statistically significant improvement in execution performance over a baseline RISC implementation, while minimizing hardware complexity. We further synthesised the design onto FPGA and explored area, timing, and power trade-offs to confirm viability for System-on-Chip (SoC) for embedded platforms. This work enhances capabilities in the development of energy-efficiend SoCs.

title: ResolveX: An AI-Driven Multilingual Grievance Management Framework using Transformer-based NLP
authors: Karthik K, Sakthi T S, Sivabalaji T, Sivasankar M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The recent advances in technology have enabled many companies to have better ways to deal with customer complaints than in the past. Today, most complaint management systems use out-of-date methods or only partially automated systems that lead to delayed responses, misaligned cases, and misdirected complaints. To solve this issue, we have developed an innovative complaint management system based on innovative language processing capabilities that enable customers to submit written complaints in multiple languages and receive a response with the correct department using an intelligent language processing algorithm that uses deep learning models that are based on the way humans read written text. In addition, we use another technological approach to measure the significance of each individual word in order to assess the urgency of a specific complaint. Additionally, the system provides customers with real-time notifications regarding the status of their complaints, maintains secure access to the customer's complaint data, provides graphical representations of the complaint's trends, and promotes effective resolution of complaints while instilling confidence in customers.

title: Cervical Cancer Target Segmentation Using Contrastive Learning Fusion of CBCT-MRI Features
authors: Songlin Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This paper focuses on the clinical problem of inaccurate target delineation in cervical cancer radiotherapy caused by low contrast of soft tissue in CBCT (Cone-Beam Computed Tomography) images. We propose an automatic segmentation method in a systematic way of contrastive learning fusion network (Contrastive Learning Fusion Network, CLF-Net). First, this paper builds a dual-path encoder to get geometric structural features from CBCT and soft tissue semantic features from MRI. Then, we design a cross-modal contrastive learning mechanism to explicitly impose the constraint of feature representation consistency between modalities. After that, we propose a feature interaction fusion module to dynamically integrate multi-level features using attention mechanism. Finally, we use a decoder to achieve end-to-end accurate segmentation. The experimental results show that our CLF-Net could get a Dice similarity coefficient of 86.2% on internal multimodal dataset. Meanwhile, the ablation experiments also validate the effectiveness of contrastive learning and attention fusion modules. We believe this study provides a robust and accurate method for cross-modal medical image segmentation and has a wide application prospect in clinical practice.

title: LLM-Powered Conversational Therapy System with Journaling and Cognitive Reframing Capabilities
authors: Shaik Aabid Farhan, E Udhayadhanush, R Poonkuzhali
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Global mental health services continue to struggle with workforce limitations, barriers in access, and treatment delays. The current study introduces a domain-adapted Large Language Model platform that fuses conversational support, structured journaling with embedded cognitive pattern detection, and dynamic affirmations into a monitoring framework. Fine-tuning the LLaMA architecture with Unsloth on a synthetic dataset of roughly 88,000 conversation samples derived from publicly available mental health dialogue corpora provides state-of-the-art performance in context-sensitive emotional understanding for multiple dialogue sessions. A novelty contribution is found in the shared computational memory layer enabling cross-modal information exchange across its conversational and journaling components. Comparative in silico assessment against rule-based, retrieval-based, and hybrid transformer baselines returns 62.3%, 66.8%, and 71.4% for response coherence score, respectively, against 87.2%, with 84.6% emotional recognition accuracy. Longitudinal trajectory analysis using this data identifies repeated stressors and emerging risk indicators, visualized in an interpretable format by the monitoring dashboard. This system does not constitute diagnostic clinical work or treatment, representing only a research tool. Supported cold-start adaptation enables appropriate contextual responses from the very first interactions based on a very short onboarding assessment. This integrated approach delivers some of the building blocks to underpin scalable digital mental health support research.

title: AI-based Video Manipulation Detection using Deep Learning
authors: K Mythili, Vimal Shankar A R, Sukhesh Kumar V, Sachin Vipu M D
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has enabled the creation of highly realistic manipulated videos, commonly known as deepfakes. While these technologies offer creative potential, they also pose serious threats in the form of misinformation, identity fraud, and digital media manipulation. This paper presents an AI-based video manipulation detection system using deep learning techniques to identify forged and tampered videos. The proposed framework analyzes spatial inconsistencies in video frames using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The system is trained and evaluated on the Face-Forensics++ and DeepFake Detection Challenge (DFDC) datasets comprising 3,000 videos. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 93.8%, precision of 94.1%, recall of 93.5%, and F1-score of 93.8%, with an inference speed of 35 FPS. These results indicate that spatial feature learning alone can provide reliable performance without relying on complex temporal modeling, making the system suitable for real-time applications such as social media monitoring, digital forensics, and cybersecurity.

title: Coverage Optimization Strategy for UAV Ad Hoc Network Based on Multi-Objective Improved Cooperative Search Algorithm
authors: Qiaqia Yin, Li Yang, Liu He
abstract: To address the issues of insufficient coverage quality and high secondary deployment energy consumption in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) ad hoc network during the coverage process in indoor obstacle environments, this paper proposes a coverage optimization strategy based on a multi-objective improved cooperative search algorithm (MOICSA). The proposed strategy constructs a multi-objective optimization model that integrates the coverage quality index and secondary deployment energy consumption. Building upon this, three key improvements are introduced to the classical cooperative search algorithm (CSA): (1) the incorporation of fast non-dominated sorting to mitigate local optima trapping, (2) the integration of a crowding distance mechanism to enhance solution set diversity, and (3) the embedding of a dynamic Lévy flight strategy to balance global exploration and local exploitation capabilities. Simulation experiments demonstrate that, under equivalent computational cost, the multi-objective deep Q-network algorithm (MODQN) exhibits the lowest secondary deployment energy consumption, while its coverage quality is far lower than that of other comparative algorithms, eventually leading to its suboptimal comprehensive performance in multi-objective optimization. In contrast, the proposed MOICSA outperforms multi-objective particle swarm optimization (MOPSO), non-dominated sorting genetic algorithm II (NSGA-II), multi-objective virtual force-guided whale optimization algorithm (MOVFWOA), and multi-objective cooperative search algorithm (MOCSA), achieving improvements of 10.719%, 6.667%, 4.348%, and 7.368% in coverage quality index, respectively, while simultaneously reducing secondary deployment energy consumption by 17.697%, 15.784%, 28.206%, and 12.667%. These results confirm that the proposed strategy provides a reliable solution for deploying UAV swarms in obstacle environments.

title: Integrated Multimodal Sensing, Communication, and Computation With Adaptive Splitting and Dynamic Routing in Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Jin Yang, Danpu Liu, Zhilong Zhang, Xinxin He
abstract: In real-time remote sensing scenarios, satellites typically acquire environmental data and relay it to ground data centers for inference, which incurs substantial latency and backhaul consumption. On-satellite inference has been explored to alleviate this overhead. However, the limited computation and energy budgets of a single satellite lead to processing latency on large-scale workloads. Accordingly, a collective inference of satellites and ground servers is utilized to realize compute-while-forwarding inference in an integrated multimodal sensing, communication, and computation (ISCC) framework. A central challenge is that sensing modality selection, deep neural network (DNN) splitting, and dynamic routing are tightly coupled in this scenario. Optimizing these dimensions separately is suboptimal under resource constraints. To manage the coupled multi-domain resources, a joint optimization problem is formulated to maximize the overall task inference accuracy while minimizing end-to-end latency and energy consumption. This problem jointly optimizes sensing modality selection, DNN layer splitting, dynamic routing, and resource allocation. To address this non-convex problem, a hybrid proximal policy optimization (HPPO)-based reinforcement learning algorithm is developed with a dual-actor structure handling discrete and continuous actions. The features related to network topology, transmission capacity, computational loads, and task requirements are extracted for a phase-aware sequential decision design aligning sensing, routing, and computation adaptive decisions with constraint-integrated reward shaping that stabilizes learning and preserves feasibility under time-varying network connectivity. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme can achieve 23.79% <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula> 51.04% improvements in system utility compared to baseline methods, demonstrating its effectiveness for resource management in satellite-terrestrial ISCC systems.

title: SSFNet: Spatial-Spectral Frequency-Aware Network for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Guanliang Wan, Danqing Liu, Yunxin Liu, Tengyue Yang, Yanhui Guo
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Spatial–spectral modeling methods have achieved remarkable progress in hyperspectral image (HSI) classification. However, most existing approaches primarily emphasize spatial–spectral feature fusion or multi-scale representation learning, while the intrinsic frequency-discriminative characteristics of spatial and spectral signals remain underexplored for coordinated local–global modeling. To address these issues, we propose a novel Spatial–Spectral Frequency-Aware Network (SSFNet) for hyperspectral image classification. SSFNet adopts a dual-branch architecture, consisting of a Frequency-Aware Spatial Modeling (FASM) branch and a Frequency-Based Spectral Modeling (FBSM) branch. The spatial branch (FASM) enhances boundary sensitivity and global contextual consistency through explicit high–low frequency modulation, enabling improved spatial representation learning. The FBSM branch performs spectral frequency decomposition, where the low-frequency component captures smooth, large-scale spectral trends, and the high-frequency component models fine-grained variations and discriminative fluctuations. A gated fusion mechanism is further designed to adaptively integrate complementary frequency features across different scales. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate that SSFNet consistently outperforms eight state-of-the-art methods in terms of classification accuracy and generalization capability. These results verify the effectiveness of explicit frequency-aware spatial–spectral modeling for hyperspectral image classification.

title: NOMA-Enabled Covert and Fair Data Collection for Multi-UAV Wireless Network Under Imperfect CSI
authors: Menglong Cheng, Juan Li, Chaoxiong Ye, Byungjin Cho, Zheng Chang
abstract: This paper studies NOMA-enabled covert and fair short-packet data collection in multi-UAV networks under imperfect channel state information (CSI), assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) to serve Internet of Things (IoT) nodes (INs). Motivated by sensitive IoT missions, we consider a scenario in which suppressing detectability can be more restrictive than maximizing throughput, and where finite-blocklength transmission and link heterogeneity necessitate a joint treatment of covertness, reliability, and fairness. Accordingly, this paper first characterizes the feasible boundary of covert communication under imperfect CSI and constructs an optimization-compatible covert constraint formulation. Then, a joint optimization framework is proposed to maximize the minimum amount of data collected from all INs, by jointly optimizing INs’ transmit power, RIS phase shifts, and the 3D trajectories of UAVs. To address the strong coupling and high non-convexity in the short-packet reliable rate expression with respect to the power variables under imperfect CSI, a two-layer nested alternating optimization algorithm for 3D covert and fair data collection (TLNA-3DCF) is developed. In the inner layer, robust NOMA power allocation is performed under imperfect CSI; in the outer layer, the RIS phase shifts and UAV 3D trajectories are alternately optimized based on the inner-layer results, with feasibility enforcement and convex approximations used to ensure convergence and manageable complexity. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme, while strictly satisfying covert communication constraints, significantly improves the service level of edge INs, thereby enhancing fairness in data collection.

title: Joint Information-Theoretic Threshold Optimization and Error Bounds for MAP Decoding in Parallel Stochastic Quantizer Architectures
authors: Hassan Tavakoli, Thinh Nguyen, Bella Bose
abstract: This paper studies a Parallel Stochastic Quantizer architecture, in which multiple low-resolution comparators process an input signal in parallel, each corrupted by additive noise. We model the system as a discrete memoryless channel and analyze the mutual information (MI) between the input and the aggregated quantized output. To optimize information transfer, we propose an iterative algorithm that alternates between maximizing MI via momentum-accelerated gradient ascent and minimizing an upper bound on the MAP error probability using gradient descent. Theoretical analysis establishes that the MAP error probability decays exponentially with the number of parallel comparators, and the MI approaches the input entropy in the large-branch regime. Simulation results demonstrate the convergence of the algorithm, the dynamic adaptation of thresholds and noise parameters, and the tightness of the Bhattacharyya bound in predicting the system’s error probability. While minimizing the MAP error probability targets task-specific performance, maximizing mutual information provides a general-purpose objective that preserves task-relevant structure, ensuring robust performance across a wide range of downstream inference tasks. This joint optimization enables the system to achieve both low error rates and broad utility without being tailored to a single application. Our approach provides an efficient framework for designing low-power, high-speed quantization systems suitable for edge computing and sensing applications.

title: Resource Allocation Strategy for Multiple Maneuvering Target Tracking in Networked Radar System
authors: Jieyu Huang, Junwei Xie, Haowei Zhang, Weike Feng, Kun Wang
abstract: Networked collocated multiple-input multiple-output (C-MIMO) radar system has attracted more and more attention in recent years. Networked radar system can offer a higher tracking accuracy for multiple maneuvering targets tracking (MTT). This article proposes a joint resource allocation (JRA) strategy in networked C-MIMO radar system. Specifically, the power, bandwidth and beam resources for each radar are optimized by the JRA strategy to improve the MTT accuracy. The predicted conditional Cramér–Rao lower bound is utilized as the tracking evaluation criterion. A fast three-stage solution algorithm is proposed to solve the nonconvex and mixed-integer problem. The simulation results show that the strategy is effective and efficient.

title: Lithium-Ion Battery SOH Estimation via Mixture Correntropy Loss-Based PCA and Kolmogorov-Arnold-Enhanced Liquid Neural Networks
authors: Wentao Ma, Zhuo Li, Peng Guo, Yang Li, Badong Chen
abstract: Accurate state of health (SOH) estimation of lithium-ion batteries is essential for reliable battery management but is particularly challenging under mixed non-Gaussian measurement noise and data scarcity. This article proposes a robust SOH estimation framework that combines noise-resistant feature extraction with data-efficient, interpretable continuous-time dynamic modeling. A mixture correntropy loss-based principal component analysis (MCL-PCA) method is first developed, which employs a hybrid Gaussian–Laplacian correntropy criterion to adaptively suppress mixed non-Gaussian noise and extract stable low-dimensional health features (HFs). These features serve as inputs to a newly designed Kolmogorov-Arnold-enhanced liquid neural network (KLNN), which augments continuous-time liquid dynamics with structured nonlinear mappings to improve stability and nonlinear generalization under limited small-sample conditions. These two components are integrated into a unified framework that yields coherent and physically consistent SOH degradation trajectories. Experimental results on laboratory and public datasets demonstrate that MCL-PCA significantly improves feature robustness, while KLNN achieves superior SOH estimation accuracy in noisy and small-sample scenarios, resulting in notably lower prediction errors than conventional PCA-based methods and consistent advantages over baseline models.

title: Enhancing Physical Layer Security in Modern Transmission Systems via Semantic Similarity–Aware Signal Processing
authors: Haythem Hayouni
abstract: Semantic similarity is explored as a novel dimension for enhancing physical layer security in wireless transmission systems. A new semantic-aware security model is proposed, in which transmitted signals are adaptively shaped according to their semantic alignment with the legitimate receiver. By embedding semantic similarity into the signal generation process, the proposed approach inherently limits information leakage to unintended receivers without relying on artificial noise, secret keys, or additional power. Analytical insights and numerical results demonstrate improved secrecy performance compared with conventional physical layer security schemes.

title: Four Quadrants Operations of Single-Stage CSI With Discharge Path in Motor Drives Applications
authors: Giovanni Migliazza, Emilio Carfagna, Fabio Immovilli, Emilio Lorenzani
abstract: Classical single-stage current source inverters (CSIs), i.e., without a prestage converter, powered by a DC voltage source can only operate when the output line-to-line voltages are greater than the input DC voltage, thanks to their inherent boost capability. The recently proposed single-stage CSI with discharge path is able to control the drive from zero speed to the maximum speed only in the first quadrant without the insertion of any additional prestage converter to control the input DC current. Thanks to a deeper analysis on the topology and on the control strategy, this work shows a new control scheme and, consequently, a space vector modulation able to cover the four quadrant operations of the machine without any use of bidirectional or additional switches or other modifications of the topology. The single-stage CSI with discharge path state-space equations are presented with the average model and verified through simulations. Stability analysis is considered through Nyquist criterion. Simulations and experimental results confirm the validity of the proposed solution for single-stage CSI with discharge path topology, paving the way for a broader adoption of this power converter in motor drive applications.

title: Multisource Multimodal Calibration for Precise Disconnector Recognition in Power Grids
authors: Yucen Fan, Jingsong Hu
abstract: Accurate extrinsic calibration between light detection and ranging (LiDAR) and cameras is essential for multisensor fusion in industrial perception. Most existing approaches rely on rigid sensor rigs and synchronized capture, which limits their applicability to real-world industrial scenarios where sensors are often deployed independently or operate asynchronously. In contrast, this article presents a structure-aware, targetless calibration framework that enables reliable LiDAR–camera alignment across heterogeneous and unsynchronized data sources. The method establishes robust 3D–2D correspondence through a dual-line strategy that anchors the calibration on structural priors—using device-specific main lines and surrounding auxiliary lines—to ensure stability under large viewpoint offsets. Unlike conventional autonomous-driving setups, the proposed framework addresses the geometric misalignment challenges inherent to cross-platform sensing, such as uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV)–ground robot cooperation or multirobot inspection fleets, where rigid coupling is infeasible. Experiments on real-world power-system inspection data further validate the method’s accuracy and robustness, achieving subdegree alignment precision and enabling fine-grained disconnector state verification as a representative industrial use case. The results demonstrate that the proposed asynchronous calibration paradigm offers a general and practical solution for multisource fusion in complex industrial environments.

title: Microwave Probe Based on an Inverted Microstrip Line for Broadband Electron Paramagnetic Resonance Spectroscopy
authors: Selina Eckel, Matthias Oliver Beck, Ahmet Çağrı Ulusoy
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work presents a broadband inverted microstrip line (IMSL) as a microwave probe for electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) spectroscopy, a technique used to detect unpaired electron spins in biological and chemical samples. The IMSL geometry enables sample placement in the most homogeneous region of the microwave magnetic field, overcoming limitations of standard coplanar waveguide (CPW) and microstrip line (MSL) probes. Simulated and measured S-parameters confirm broadband operation up to 67 GHz. Improved field homogeneity relative to MSLs is demonstrated by a reduced coefficient of variation of the simulated magnetic field and by EPR measurements showing signal amplitudes independent of sample orientation. The IMSL geometry offers a compact microwave probe with straightforward fabrication for broadband EPR sensing in biological and healthcare applications.

title: Symptom-Aware Adaptation of CLIP for Few-Shot Medical Image Classification_supp2-3685174.pdf
authors: Wenxu Wang
abstract: Vision-language pre-training model yields powerful general-purpose representations, yet its direct application to medical images is hindered by two key gaps: (i) low sensitivity to fine-grained pathological cues and (ii) semantic misalignment with structured clinical terminology. We propose SITSA, a framework that adapts a frozen CLIP backbone for data-scarce medical tasks without full fine-tuning. SITSA introduces three synergistic components: (1) MedVisExtractor, a depthwise-separable convolutional adapter that recalibrates patch tokens to emphasize subtle lesions; (2) SymSemLoRA, which injects low-rank updates into the text encoder conditioned on large-language-model-generated prompts that encode texture, location, and shape attributes; and (3) Prototype-Guided Alignment, a cross-modal module that binds image patches and prompt tokens via shared learnable prototypes. Extensive experiments on COVID-QU-Ex, OCTMNIST, and DERMAMNIST demonstrate that SITSA surpasses thirteen state-of-the-art baselines in both few-shot and base-to-novel generalization, achieving the highest harmonic mean accuracy while keeping the backbone frozen. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/enfantsRichesDeprimes/sitsa</uri>.

title: Adversarial Visual Domain Adaptation with Bioinspired Optimization: A Translational Framework for Rice Leaf Pathology
authors: Sejal Hanmante, Anjali S. More, Shilpa Bade-Gite, Uzma Patil, Shivani Patil
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: This study introduces an improved CycleGAN-based framework for simulating rice leaf disease symptoms, specifically Brown Spot disease on a given healthy rice leaf. The methodology proposed integrates Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) to dynamically adapt training hyperparameters during training, enabling improved balance between structural maintenance and disease expression. Experiments conducted on 350 training images at a resolution of 512 × 512 over 400 epochs show that the proposed method achieves a higher structural similarity (SSIM = 0.93) and lower perceptual distance (LPIPS = 0.12) compared to conventional CycleGAN training. In addition, the model produces more consistent disease manifestation, with an average lesion coverage of 16.1%, while maintaining stable visual quality. Although distribution-level realism measured by FID remains challenging for agricultural imagery, relative improvements are observed over the baseline. The results demonstrate that bio-inspired hyperparameter optimization can effectively enhance unpaired disease image synthesis for agricultural applications.

title: AI-Optimized Quantum Key Distribution for Satellite-to-Ground Cybersecurity
authors: Kunwar Toyesh Singh Vaisla, Kunwar Singh Vaisla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Because quantum computing can break the hard mathematical problems that protect RSA and ECC, classical cryptography is entering a period of risk. QKD offers a different kind of protection, using quantum physics itself to guarantee secrecy. Bringing QKD to satellites helps cover long distances, but that link must withstand disturbances from the atmosphere, precision-related optical challenges, and sophisticated adversaries. In this paper, we explore how artificial intelligence can support satellite QKD. Our framework predicts channel changes using machine learning, tunes system parameters through reinforcement learning, and watches for suspicious activity using anomaly detection. When tested in simulations, the approach improved secure key generation by about 35%, cut QBER from 8% to 5%, and held up strongly against quantum-level attacks. These results demonstrate that integrating AI techniques with satellite-based QKD provides a resilient and scalable solution for secure global communications.

title: Design of an Intelligent Decision Support System for Hotel Resource Allocation Based on Machine Learning
authors: Guo Zheng
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: In response to the high volatility and dynamic nonlinearity challenges faced by hotel resource allocation in the post-pandemic era, this paper designs and validates a complete-process, multi-objective, and interpretable intelligent decision support system (IDSS). Firstly, based on rigorous exploratory data analysis (EDA) and distribution tests, a demand prediction module based on the Stacked Ensemble (GA-ANN, SVM, RF) fusion model is proposed, combined with SHAP technology to achieve explainability of the early warning. Secondly, abandoning the traditional static revenue rules based on normal assumptions, a dynamic pricing engine based on bidirectional long short-term memory network (Bi-LSTM) and attention mechanism is constructed to fit the deep price elasticity time series. Finally, a mixed integer programming (MIP) shift scheduling model including employee welfare constraints is established and solved using the improved spider bee optimization algorithm (SWOIM). Based on a dataset of 83,590 real customer instances from a hotel in Portugal for verification, the results show that the order cancellation prediction accuracy of this system reaches 96.1%; the Bi-LSTM dynamic pricing strategy increases the average available room revenue (RevPAR) by 16.6%; at the same time, the intelligent shift scheduling model reduces labor costs by 13.2% while significantly improving employee satisfaction scores, verifying the system’s ability to reduce costs and increase efficiency in highly uncertain environments.

title: A Hybrid Image Processing and Deep Learning Framework for Automated Detection of Diabetic Maculopathy Markers in OCT Images
authors: Savita Sheelavant, Preethi N Patil
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) remains a primary contributor to vision loss worldwide, with early indicators like exudates and microaneurysms playing a crucial role in diagnosis. Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) stands out as a non-invasive, high-resolution imaging technique for retinal assessment. In this work, an automated framework designed to detect and annotate exudates and microaneurysms within OCT images are proposed. The approach combines contrast enhancement, morphological processing, multi-scale matched filtering, and with deep learning-based supervised classification to achieve precise localization of these pathologies. By combining anatomical knowledge with deep learning feature representations, the framework is better able to distinguish true lesions from imaging artifacts, which helps reduce false positives and makes the results more reliable for clinical use. When evaluated on a locally collected dataset post ethical committee clearance from Karnataka Institute of Endocrinology & Research (KIER), Bengaluru and publicly available OCT dataset, the proposed method demonstrated a sensitivity of 93.4%, specificity of 91.2%, and an average error rate of 6.5% relative to expert ground truth. This framework surpasses existing methods in detection accuracy and robustness, representing a significant advancement for early DR screening.

title: Conversation-Aware Sentiment Modeling: Integrating Graph Structures and Sequential Context
authors: Deepak Kumar, Sumit Kumar Rana, Ankita Dhiman, Mansi, Punit Kumar, Vansh Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The rapid increase in the number of users and their content over the social media platforms has created a need for development of advanced sentiment analysis frameworks capable of detecting and analyzing nuanced emotional patterns within the threads of conversations. Existing sentiment analysis tools or models fail to consider the context and time-related changes that drive sentiment shifts in the user content threads. This paper proposes a novel sentiment analysis framework to capture contextual, hierarchical and sequential dependencies in comment threads by synergistically integrating transformer-based language models with graph neural networks (GNNs). The proposed architecture uses a cross-attention fusion mechanism, dynamic graph construction and a bias-aware loss function that corresponds with Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 16 to reduce online toxicity. Results of the benchmark datasets of the proposed model exceeds the strong baselines including RoBERTa-only, GCN-only, and BiLSTM models in sentiment classification accuracy, toxicity detection (AUC), and thread-level sentiment consistency (TSCI). The holdings underscore the promise of merging sequential and relational models for thread-aware sentiment analysis and establish the ground for future research work in multimodal and conversational AI.

title: Cloud-Enabled Grievance Redressal using Real-Time Dashboards and Heatmaps for Urban Management
authors: Prabavathi S, Naveen Karthick S, Nithin Raj K, Vijay M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Cities receive a large number of public complaints often, and many of them often remain unresolved due to the poor coordination and also due to delays in communication. SmartResolve is built on cloud technology and is a web-based system to simplify the lodging process, monitoring, and resolving civic issues. We have bridged the gap between the community and leadership. Citizens can easily file detailed reports. Authorities have a central hub to manage workflows and make sure every request receives a response. Problem prone areas can be easily identified with the help of Heatmap based visualization and respond more effectively. Smart Resolve improves transparency by moving the entire workflow to a cloud environment so that the manual effort will be reduced and also ensures quicker service delivery.

title: Robust Lung Cancer Detection Model using Autoencoder-Learned Features and Machine Learing Algorithms
authors: Gowripushpa Geddam, D.Sameera, Karri Ganga Parvathi, A. Leela Prasanna, A.Lakshmanarao, P.Chandra Sekhar Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Lung cancer diagnosis using computed tomography (CT) images has gained significant attention due to the potential of automated image analysis in supporting early detection. Conventional machine learning approaches applied directly to raw image features often suffer from limited representation capability, which restricts their classification performance. To address this limitation, this work presents a robust lung cancer detection framework that combines autoencoder-based feature learning with multiple machine learning algorithms. Initially, baseline machine learning models are evaluated using raw pixel intensity features extracted from CT images. A denoising convolutional autoencoder is then employed to learn compact and noise-resilient latent representations, which are subsequently used for classification. Logistic Regression, Multilayer Perceptron, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost are systematically evaluated using both raw and autoencoder-learned features to analyze the impact of representation learning. Experimental results on a publicly available lung CT image dataset demonstrate consistent performance improvements across all models when autoencoder-based features are used. Among the evaluated classifiers, XGBoost achieves the best overall performance, highlighting the effectiveness of combining representation learning with ensemble-based machine learning techniques. The findings confirm that autoencoder-driven feature learning significantly enhances the robustness and accuracy of CT-based lung cancer detection.
