To overcome these limitations, we propose the Multi-Scale Residual Denoising Network (MSRD-Net), a hierarchical phase reconstruction framework that integrates the nonlinear recovery capability of deep learning with the robustness of frequency-domain filtering. MSRD-Net decomposes interferometric phase into low-frequency and corresponding residual components, preserving dominant structures while progressively restoring fine-scale details. A self-boosting training strategy using real-world noise extracted from Sentinel-1 and TanDEM-X data further improves the network's robustness and generalization. Evaluations across four representative scenarios—earthquakes, landslides, rainforests, and urban areas—show that MSRD-Net achieves a balanced improvement in noise suppression, structural fidelity, and efficiency. Furthermore, its scalable design allows adaptive adjustment of denoising strength, while its iterative nature ensures reliable performance under extreme noise. These characteristics establish MSRD-Net as a practical and versatile solution for both large-scale operational monitoring and high-precision geoscience studies with InSAR.

title: Developing and Validating RGB UAV-Based NDVI Estimation Maps in a Heterogeneous Landscape Using Multiple Machine and Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: I. Aydin, U. G. Sefercik
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Technological advances in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) systems have enhanced their spectral capabilities and enabled the acquisition of multispectral data for real-time NDVI mapping. However, the high hardware cost of multispectral sensors and the additional requirements of radiometric calibration limit their widespread use. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes innovative artificial intelligence (AI)-based approaches to estimate NDVI from low-cost RGB UAV imagery. The primary objective of this research is to generate NDVI maps using only RGB UAV data and to evaluate the performance of different machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) algorithms. Unlike previous studies in the literature that typically relied on a single image or a single model for NDVI estimation, eight models were employed in this study, including Linear Regression, Extreme Gradient Boosting, Light Gradient Boosting, CatBoost, Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), Pix2Pix, and two stacking ensemble models, to generate georeferenced NDVI maps from RGB UAV imagery. In addition to focusing on individual classes, the models were tested over a heterogeneous area containing various land use and land cover (LULC) classes, and their performance was evaluated across all classes using accuracy metrics such as the R² and RMSE, based on reference NDVI maps derived from multispectral UAV imagery. In conclusion, ML-based RGB-derived NDVI mapping, with CatBoost achieving the highest accuracy (RMSE = 0.09, R² = 0.83), offers a reliable and cost-effective alternative for vegetation monitoring; notably, stacking models showed no significant advantage over single models (p > 0.05, Kruskal–Wallis and Dunn's post hoc).

title: A Physics Parameter-Guided Metalearning Framework for Temperature Prediction of Double-Sided Double-Glass TopCon Photovoltaic Modules
authors: Huajian Ding, Zhongyuan Su, Jun Wang
abstract: Aiming at the need for accurate prediction operational temperature of N-type TOPCon bifacial double-glass photovoltaic modules, this article proposes an integrated forecasting method combining physical principles with metalearning. A metalearner model based on a Stacking ensemble framework is constructed, which integrates predictions from a thermodynamic model and a dual-branch long short-term memory (LSTM) network through a dynamic weighting mechanism. Cross-validation strategies are employed to train deep neural networks, enabling multiscale feature integration and weight self-adjustment. Experimental results achieve a root mean square error (RMSE) of only 0.95°C, representing a 66% reduction compared to the physical model (RMSE = 2.81°C) and a 14% reduction compared to the LSTM model (RMSE = 1.11°C), substantially improving the model’s adaptability in nonlinear and dynamic scenarios.

title: IGBT Open-Circuit Fault Detection and Localization Method for Cascaded H-Bridge Stage of Power Electronic Transformer
authors: Tao Zheng, Chong Wang, Yufei Zhao
abstract: The rectifier stage of power electronic transformers (PETs) commonly employs either a cascaded H-bridge (CHB) or a modular multilevel converter (MMC) topology. Due to its high modularity, scalability, and ease of redundancy design, the CHB topology is widely adopted in PETs. However, the use of numerous power electronic devices in PETs increases the probability of faults. In this paper, a novel fault detection and localization method is proposed for the rapid identification of IGBT open-circuit faults in the CHB stages of PETs. A mathematical model of the CHB grid-side voltage is established, which contains abundant harmonic information. The harmonic characteristics of the grid-side voltage under different IGBT open-circuit faults are analyzed, revealing that the amplitude and phase of even-order carrier frequency harmonics depend on the location of the faulty H-bridges. Based on this characteristic, the fault region is identified. By adjusting the modulation scheme of specific H-bridges and analyzing the resulting variations in the grid-side voltage, multiple IGBT open-circuit faults can be accurately localized. The proposed method utilizes the characteristic harmonics of the H-bridge port theoretical voltage as a reference, thereby reducing fault-identification time and remaining unaffected by load conditions. Its effectiveness is validated through PSCAD/EMTDC simulations and hardware-in-the-loop experiments.

title: AdpFL: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Framework through Adaptive Model Pruning on Non-IID Data
authors: Dewei Ning, Yong-Feng Ge, Elisa Bertino, Zhonglong Zheng, Yunliang Jiang, Hua Wang
abstract: Federated learning (FL) has shown great potential, especially with the rise of complex foundation models and growing privacy needs. FL has experienced challenges, including high communication costs, privacy concerns of user data, and the complexities of non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data. This paper aims to address these challenges through our proposed FL framework called Adaptive Pruning FL (AdpFL). AdpFL conducts an exploration phase prior to the formal FL process, during which each local dataset's deviation and corresponding threshold are computed locally. These results are then utilised to perform model pruning during the formal FL training. By doing so, the method achieves full adaptivity while simultaneously addressing challenges related to communications costs and non-IID data. Meanwhile, AdpFL enhances pruning by replacing part of the parameters with zeros, thereby preventing membership inference attacks (MIA) and ensuring privacy in both local and global models. The extensive experiments on various datasets and FL tasks demonstrate that our method not only efficiently accelerates global convergence but also achieves superior accuracy and robustness compared to traditional methods. AdpFL reduces computational overhead and decreases the effective update size (i.e., the proportion of non-zero parameters) by approximately half while improving global accuracy by up to 20%, and it also lowers the accuracy of white-box MIA attacks across various models. These improvements make FL more efficient, scalable, and practical in real-world situations.

title: Collaborative Neural Networks significantly Improve Global Aerosol Retrievals From Multi-Angle Polarimeters_supp1-3685881.pdf
authors: Guangliang Fu
abstract: We present the RemoTAP-Hybrid algorithm for global aerosol products retrieved from Multi-Angle Polarimeters (MAP), and apply it to one-year aerosol retrievals from the SPEXone MAP instrument. For the first time, Multiple Collaborated Neural Networks (MCNN) are used to perform global aerosol MAP retrievals over land and ocean. The combined use of MCNN with a full physics inversion in RemoTAP-Hybrid, significantly improves accuracy and computational speed of SPEXone aerosol retrievals. We validate the retrieved Aerosol Optical Depth (AOD), Angstrom Exponent (AE), Single Scattering Albedo (SSA), Absorption AOD (AAOD), Fine mode AOD (FAOD), Coarse mode AOD (CAOD), and fine mode effective radius (reff_fine) with AERONET data. The validation suggests that aerosol retrievals can be further improved when extending the aerosol description to include a fine non-spherical mode. For AOD, the Root-Mean-Square Error (RMSE) is 0.051 (0.037) over land (ocean), while 78.3% (80.8%) of the retrievals are within the requirement formulated by the Global Climate Observing System (GCOS). The RMSE is 0.186 (0.207) for AE, 0.038 (0.044) for SSA, 0.023 (0.014) for AAOD, 0.047 (0.031) for FAOD, 0.043 (0.027) for CAOD, and 0.021 (0.026) for reff_fine over land (ocean). Compared to the current RemoTAP algorithm, RemoTAP-Hybrid shows improved agreement with AERONET for AOD, AE, and FAOD over land and ocean while the number of valid retrievals is increased by 4% (3%) over land (ocean) and the computational time is reduced by 43% (32%). Finally, we present the first global seasonal maps of aerosol optical and microphysical properties and chemical composition from SPEXone.

title: Single-Channel sEMG-Based Wrist Gesture Recognition for Gamified Rehabilitation Interfaces
authors: Chanjhana Elango, Kaniska M, Vijay Santhosh P, Amrutha Veluppal
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Surface electromyography (sEMG) is commonly used to recognize wrist gestures in human-computer interaction and assistive applications. In this work, a single-channel sEMG-based gesture classification approach is presented using Support Vector Machine(SVM), Random Forest, and Neural Network models. The study focuses on designing lightweight models that are suitable for deployment in edge-oriented environments. A signal processing pipeline was developed to extract 16 timeand frequency-domain features from Flexor Carpi Radialis muscle signals for classifying four hand gestures: rest, flexion, radial deviation, and pronation. The study combines laboratoryrecorded signals with an online dataset and applies standard preprocessing techniques. Among the evaluated models, Random Forest achieved the highest classification accuracy of 79.79 %. To demonstrate application-level usability, the classified gestures were integrated into a rehabilitation-oriented interactive game interface, where pre-recorded sEMG signals were streamed sequentially to simulate real-time interaction and validate gesture-to-action mapping.

title: An Automated ML-Based Framework for Predicting Mutation-Induced Drug Resistance via ESMFold and Molecular Docking
authors: Bharath P, Dakshesh T, P. Sardar Maran
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Mutations in the genome that lead to drug resistance are major problem in treating cancer. The study proposes an automated computer pipeline that does in silico docking to figure out how mutations affect binding to drugs. The workflow integrates modules like Biopython, PubChemPy, and the ESMFold API. They use it to model the EGFR protein, specifically the wild type version NP 005219.2, and then the L858R mutant variant. With AutoDock Vina for the molecular docking, it showed a reduction in how stable the binding is for Gefitinib when in mutant form when compared to the wild type. But the predictions from it still need future experiments to validate them, to confirm they work in actual clinical situations.

title: IDEA-FastTEN: A Real-Time ADS-B Flight Trajectory Prediction Method With Independent Distance Extrapolation
authors: Lindong Wang, Hongya Tuo, Minzhe Li, Zhongliang Jing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Real-Time Flight trajectory prediction (RT-FTP) is an important task for future air traffic management (ATC). Real ADS-B trajectories are often irregularly sampled, i.e. the data arrive at irregular intervals, there exists much missing data after converting it to regularly sampled. Most existing methods mitigate this problem by offline data preprocessing (e.g. outlier detection, trajectory imputation). However, these methods add additional processing steps and are not always applicable for real-time application. In this work, we introduce the RT-FTP task and transform it into an short-term time-series prediction task with missing data. We have collected real ADS-B data and constructed a new Real-Time Flight Trajectory dataset ( RTFT ). We replaced the normalization methods of some existing advanced time series forecasting methods with Mask Stationary Standardization (MSS) and established the RTFT benchmark. Furthermore, we designed a novel RT-FTP architecture named Independent Distance Extrapolation Architecture (IDEA), IDEA consists of a decomposition module, Range Attention module and the Temporal Extrapolation Network (TEN). TEN is an improvement over Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) and it can extract independent features at different distances, thereby providing a certain degree of interpretability. We further simplified the structure of TEN to introduce a more lightweight model called FastTEN. IDEA-FastTEN have achieved SOTA in both performance and performance-speed balance on the RTFT benchmark. We hope that our work can help advance ADS-B trajectory prediction towards real-time application.

title: Entropy Injection in Fused Market Networks: HSBM–MDL Quantification of News-Driven Structural Reconfiguration
authors: Jinze Yang, Kaira Sekiguchi, Yukio Ohsawa
abstract: Understanding how semantic information flow reorganizes market topology remains an open challenge in financial network analysis. Prior work typically emphasizes price correlations or sentiment polarity, but rarely quantifies the structural response of the market network to information inflow within a unified coding framework. We propose a fusion-based approach to measure topology-aware information impact. Specifically, we construct a Price Network GP from return co-movements and a Semantic Network GN from LLM-based text embeddings, and integrate them into a Fused Network GF . Using the Hierarchical Stochastic Block Model (HSBM) under the minimum description length principle, we estimate structural entropies for GF and GP (HF and HP), and define the marginal structural cost of information injection as ΔH = HF − HP. Empirically, using S&P 100 constituents over 2023–2025, we find that ΔH is consistently positive, indicating that semantic information inflow acts as an entropy injector relative to the price backbone. High-risk regimes (periods of elevated volatility) exhibit markedly larger ΔH together with a reorganization of the inferred hierarchy, characterized by deeper partitions and increased fine-grained communities, consistent with micro-fragmentation of market structure. At the macro level, normalized modularity ZQ declines on high-volatility days, suggesting weaker sectoral separability and intensified cross-community coupling. Finally, permutation-based counterfactual tests that disrupt semantic alignment show that the observed regime separation inΔH is not attributable to arbitrary mixing, supporting the interpretation that information shocks induce organized structural reconfiguration rather than random disorder. Overall, our framework provides a principled, topology-aware link between semantic information flow and market-wide risk states.

title: IEEE Robotics and Automation Society Publication Information
authors: N/A

title: Contrastive Learning for Terrain Identification of Earthworm-Like Robots with Limited Labeled Proprioceptive Data
authors: Hongsen Pang, Hanzhuo Zhang, Yunfei Zheng, Hongbin Fang
abstract: Accurate terrain perception is essential for autonomous decision-making in earthworm-like robots, which navigate complex environments using retrograde peristaltic gaits. During locomotion, these robots generate proprioceptive feedback through ground interaction, making visionless terrain recognition possible. However, conventional learning-based methods typically rely on large labeled datasets, which are costly and impractical for field-deployed earthworm-like robots. In this study, we develop an inertial sensor system to capture the robot’s proprioceptive signals and propose a contrastive learning framework for efficient terrain identification with minimal supervision. The model employs data augmentation strategies—both strong and weak—to generate diverse views of the same data, facilitating robust feature extraction. A Temporal Convolutional Network and autoregressive Transformer are used to capture multi-scale temporal dependencies from raw IMU signals. To further enhance performance under limited labeled data, we introduce a semi-supervised fine-tuning stage, which generates pseudo-labels for unlabeled data and applies supervised contrastive loss to refine the learned representations. Extensive experiments across varied terrains and gaits show that our method consistently outperforms existing supervised and self-supervised baselines, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy with minimal labeled data. This approach addresses a key challenge in proprioceptive terrain recognition and offers a scalable, efficient solution for real-time deployment in resource-constrained earthworm-like robotic platforms.

title: A Bioimpedance Sensor for Real-Time Urea Monitoring in Hemodialysis Using Characteristic Frequency Shifts
authors: Adel Reyhanian, Rasool Baghbani, Masoomeh Ashoorirad, Silvia Del Din
abstract: Real-time assessment of hemodialysis adequacy requires continuous urea monitoring, but current methods depend on intermittent blood sampling. This paper presents a bioimpedance sensor that enables non-invasive urea quantification using characteristic frequency (fc) shifts derived from Randles circuit modeling. A four-electrode Wenner configuration sensor (10 kHz-2 MHz) was integrated into a flow system simulating dialysis conditions. Blood samples with urea concentrations from 0 to 1.5 mg/mL were measured, and complex nonlinear least-squares fitting extracted Randles circuit parameters (Rs, Rp, Cp). Results demonstrate a strong linear correlation between fc and urea concentration (R² > 0.98) with modeling errors below 1% (NRSSE). Critically, fc shifts are detectable below 100 kHz, enabling implementation with low-cost hardware. The fc- urea calibration curve provides a direct pathway to convert sensor measurements to clinical adequacy metrics without blood sampling. This work establishes characteristic frequency as a sensitive electrical biomarker for real-time dialysis monitoring, addressing critical complications like hypotension while enabling personalized treatment through continuous feedback.

title: IRKAN: Wavelet-based Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks for Infrared Small Target Detection
authors: Peng Ding, Xiaolin Wei, Weihua He, Wuming Wu
abstract: Infrared small target detection is fundamentally challenged by the poor generalization of deep learning models across diverse sensors and imaging resolutions. To address this limitation, a novel framework named IRKAN is proposed, which reimagines the network’s core feature extraction mechanism. The proposed approach moves beyond conventional architectures by adopting the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network paradigm, replacing fixed, node-based activations with learnable functions on the network edges. This design endows the model with the intrinsic capability to learn optimal, data-driven transformations for complex infrared signals. To tailor this adaptive framework for the specific physics of infrared small target detection, wavelet basis functions are employed to parameterize these activations. This innovation infuses a powerful signal-processing prior into the network, leveraging the time-frequency localization properties of wavelets to function as a learnable matched filter, adept at isolating the faint, high-frequency signatures of targets from background clutter. By strategically embedding these specialized wavelet-parameterized Kolmogorov-Arnold Network modules into a U-Net backbone, IRKAN achieves strong performance on the public WideIRSTD-Full dataset. Evaluated over ten independent runs, it attains a mean normalized Intersection over Union of 68.89% and a low False Alarm Rate of 8.33 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup>, while maintaining computational efficiency (3.96M parameters). The synergy between the architectural flexibility of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks and the signal-aware inductive bias of wavelets provides a robust solution to the challenges of modern infrared small target detection. Zero-shot evaluations on the unseen NUDT-SIRSTD (512 × 512) and IRSTD-1K (256 × 256) datasets indicate that IRKAN possesses strong cross-resolution generalization capabilities, particularly under resolution shifts, without requiring fine-tuning.

title: Joint Illumination, Power and Band Allocation for Multi-beam LEO Satellites with Beam-Hopping Using Mixed Integer Linear Programming
authors: Samuel Martínez Zamacola, Nils Pachler, Ramón Martínez Rodríguez-Osorio, Bruce G. Cameron
abstract: The paper presents a novel optimization approach for resource allocation in non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite constellations employing beam-hopping (BH) payloads for large-scale broadband services. Current literature has proposed several optimization strategies to deal with the intricacies of BH applied to large satellite constellations. However, most works limit their study to only sub-portions of the complete problem, often fixing certain variables, such as frequency or power, or disregarding specific objectives, such as user fairness or excess of allocated resources. To address this gap, this work introduces a novel mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) formulation that integrates all available degrees of freedom, namely, power, frequency, and beam illumination, while optimizing for performance, quality of service and fairness through three objectives: unserved capacity (UC), extra-served capacity (EC), and time-to-serve (TTS). To reduce the computational complexity, an alternative time-split MILP is proposed by partitioning the problem into smaller time-based subproblems. The time-split MILP maintains the performance of the full MILP formulation in larger user scenarios and balances execution time with superior computational performance. Simulations demonstrate that the time-split MILP method surpasses other approaches, including deterministic (DB) and meta-heuristic (GA) solutions. It achieves up to a 6% reduction in UC, maintains EC levels below those of DB and GA in low-user scenarios, and nearly halves the TTS, highlighting its efficiency in resource allocation and service optimization.

title: A High Gain and Fully Metallic Ku-Band Antenna Design based on High-Order Mode Method
authors: Zhaoqi Bian, Yuanxi Cao, Fei Yang, Hengfeng Wang, Sen Yan
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a fully metallic high-gain antenna operating in Ku-band. A high-order mode (HOM) method is employed to calculate the dispersion curves of periodic structure accurately. This approach enables convenient acquisition of the radiation efficiency of slot antennas, and provide a guidance for slot array design. A single-ridge waveguide is adopted as the transmission waveguide of the slot array, which effectively expands the operating bandwidth and narrows the array element spacing, thus greatly improving the aperture efficiency. Additionally, to accommodate the small spacing caused by the ridge waveguide, a compact H-plane ridge waveguide T-junction with a matching slab is designed for the feeding network. This T-junction requires no complex ridge deformation or additional impedance matching lines, thus effectively saving the space of feeding network. The measured peak gain of the antenna prototype reaches 33.45 dBi, with an operating bandwidth of 7.3%. Its radiation efficiency and aperture efficiency reach 85.2% and 81.4%, respectively.

title: Stain Consistency Learning: Handling Stain Variation for Automatic Digital Pathology Segmentation
authors: Michael Yeung, Todd Watts, Sean YW Tan, Peiyuan Jing, Pedro F. Ferreira, Andrew D. Scott, Sonia Nielles-Vallespin, Guang Yang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Engineering in Medicine and Biology
abstract: Stain variation poses a major challenge for automated digital pathology. Numerous techniques address this issue, yet show limited success, especially outside H&E stains and classification tasks. We propose Stain Consistency Learning (SCL), combining stain-specific augmentation and a novel consistency loss to learn stain-invariant features. We conduct the first large-scale evaluation of ten methods on Masson's trichrome and H&E datasets for segmentation. Our results demonstrate that traditional stain normalization offers little benefit, while stain augmentation and adversarial learning significantly improve performance. SCL consistently outperforms all other methods. Code is available at:<uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/mlyg/stain_consistency_learning</uri>.

title: High Gain Observer Based Simultaneous Identification of Stator Resistance and Rotor Speed for Sensorless Induction Motor Drives
authors: Lisi Tian, Lijuan Wang, Tiantuo He, Lei Xu, Yu Wang, Yujie Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: To address the issues of system instability and control performance degradation in sensorless induction motor drives caused by stator resistance mismatch under low-speed conditions, this paper proposes and investigates a dual-parameter simultaneous identification method based on an Adaptive High-Gain Observer (AHGO). By constructing an augmented system, the proposed method achieves real-time, synchronous online identification of both rotor speed and stator resistance. Consequently, it effectively mitigates the risk of system instability induced by the excessive parameter sensitivity inherent in conventional single-parameter identification strategies. Experimental results fully validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method under various complex low-speed operating conditions.

title: A Baseline Torque Controller Synchronized with Adaptive Oscillators Improves Transparency of a Six DoF Lower-Limb Exoskeleton_supp1-3686286.docx
authors: Rafhael Milanezi de Andrade

title: An LSTM-Based Path Loss Prediction Model for Short-Term Highly Dynamic Bistatic Ground-Air-Ground Channels
authors: Tianhao Li, Xi Liao, Panpan Shi
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: To achieve accurate prediction of the short-term highly dynamic bistatic ground-air-ground channel between ground stations and aerospace targets under high-speed mobility, this letter proposes a long short-term memory (LSTM)-based path loss prediction model combined with a spatio-temporal attention mechanism (STAM). The LSTM captures dynamic features, whereas STAM adaptively emphasizes salient time steps and influential features to enhance the representation of complex spatio-temporal dependencies. Equipment parameters, aerospace target characteristics, and climate features are further incorporated as inputs to characterize the impact of both the propagation environment and system characteristics on path loss prediction. Experiments on a dataset that combines simulated and measured path loss data demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms existing models in short-term highly dynamic bistatic ground-air-ground channels.

title: Sub-30 nm Silicon Nanopatterning: Toward Reduced Reflectance and Enhanced Sensitivity in CMOS Image Sensors
authors: ByoungGyu Kim, Yongkeun Lee
abstract: This work investigated how subwavelength silicon patterning and dielectric passivation influence the optical and electrical performance of advanced CMOS image sensors (CIS). Hole arrays with sidewall angles of 73°, 79°, and 86° were fabricated using industrial plasma-etch processes, with and without aluminum-oxide/hafnium-oxide passivation and antireflective (AR) coatings. Reflectance measurements from 360–1000 nm showed that etch geometry and dielectric filling substantially reduced Fresnel reflection, with the 79° profile achieving the lowest broadband reflectance and film-filled structures providing additional suppression in the blue–green region. Device-level evaluations on 0.64 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m-pixel CIS sensors demonstrated RGB sensitivity improvements of 0.7–3.3% under low illumination (23 lux, 30 ms), with no increase in crosstalk. These trends correlated closely with wafer-level reflectance, confirming reflectance as a reliable early predictor of device performance. Although certain etch conditions increased dark current due to plasma-induced damage, the aluminum-oxide/hafnium-oxide passivation stack effectively mitigated interface traps, enabling stable electrical behavior for optimized profiles. Overall, the results demonstrated that co-optimizing etch geometry, dielectric materials, and process conditions enhances both optical efficiency and electrical robustness, providing a manufacturable path toward higher-sensitivity CIS devices in submicron-pixel technology.[2025-0151]

title: AI-Driven Fault Tolerance Mechanisms for Real-Time Cloud Service Recovery in Hybrid Environments
authors: Nidhi Mishra, Aakansha Soy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The hybrid cloud environments are also brought about by scalability and flexibility of combining both the public and the private infrastructures, but they also come with the difficulties of sustaining consistent services because of the changing workload, heterogeneous platforms, and intermittent failures. The conventional methods of fault tolerance, like redundancy and replication, are mostly reactive and do not work effectively in dynamic environments. This paper suggests an AI-assisted fault tolerance system that would combine machine-learning-based fault prediction and automatic recovery of real-time cloud services restoration. Historical and live performance metrics such as CPU usage, memory usage, network bandwidth usage, and disk I/O are constantly analyzed by the system, anticipating when failures may occur beforehand. Proactive recovery measures like load balancing, resource migration, and dynamic scaling are automatically implemented to predict the likelihood of high confidence and to reduce the service disruption. Experimental analysis of a hybrid cloud environment reveals that the suggested model could achieve a high fault prediction rate of about 95, a mean time to recovery of about 50, the uptime of the system was about 98, and the computational and network overhead in the model were minimized as compared to the traditional and rule-based methods. These results prove that predictive fault tolerance using AI can be a major contributor to the reliability, efficiency, and resilience of the hybrid cloud service management.

title: AI-Augmented FinTech Platforms for Real-Time Credit Risk and Supply Chain Financing in Smart Industries
authors: Swetha Sistla, Mohan Sankaran, Nagaraju Jooluri, Yahya Fikri, Shruthaalaxmi, Sonal Devesh
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: A combination of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and FinTech platforms has transformed the financial services sector, specifically, real-time credit risk evaluation and supply chain financing of smart industries. The conventional models of credit assessment, based on the use of fixed financial information and manual processing, are ineffective in capturing the dynamic nature of the modern-day industrial process. This paper conducts empirical research on AI-enhanced FinTech applications and utilizes machine learning (ML), natural language processing (NLP), and multi-modal industrial data, such as financial data, IoT sensor data, and supply chain data, to enable better predictive models and decision processes. The study compares several models, such as ensemble learning and deep neural networks, to predict credit risk and maximize the financing. Findings show that this is highly improved with AUC scores more than 0.88 and reduction in decision latency up to 70 percent showing quicker more information-oriented and context-sensitive risk management. It offers practical implications in the design of AI-based financial solutions, which will allow making smarter credit decisions and allocating working capital in intelligent industries more effectively, and it also notes that AI can transform industrial FinTech ecosystems.

title: Intelligent Workplace Enhancement using Wearable Stress Detection and Adaptive Interventions
authors: K. Sundararaju, Mohanbalaji V, Nandha Kumar R, Sachin S, Ramakrishnan J
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Work stress has a great impact on the health, productivity and efficiency of the organization in general. The present paper suggests a smart wearable-based system that will track stress in real-time and offer adaptive interventions that can be used to improve workplace well-being. The wearable bands used in the system have physiological sensors such as heart rate sensors and galvanic skin response sensors used to continuously monitor the level of stress. Collected data will be safely sent to a centralized web portal that will enable the supervisors to analyze stress patterns, monitor employee well-being, and assign tasks more successfully depending on the circumstances of the individual stressors. The system has adaptive interventions by adjusting to real-time stress levels like playing music to calm or activate energy, changing lighting conditions and showing timely relaxation cues. These interventions are expected to be used to minimize stress, and increase concentration and productivity at work. Simulations and user feedback show that the system is an effective stress detection system and mechanical to provide adaptive reactions, which results in a higher employee satisfaction and productivity. The proposed methodology will provide a multifaceted solution to current-day workplace stress management, ensuring cognitive well-being and giving organizations the opportunity to act proactively and make data-driven decisions.

title: Efficient Prediction of Real vs Fake Face using Transfer Learning and Deep Learning
authors: Dasari Yugandhar, L.Saravanan, Abrayev Bakhrom, Davlatova Sayyora Toshpulatovna, Anorgul Ashirova, Shapali Bansal
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence introduced for human activity recognition in real time. This technology is used by many people. Fake faces are used by cyber hackers for money debit and other suspicious activity. Government need to monitor more on those who creates fake face for suspicious activity. Recently fake faces create several issues in society and for example social media, theft in society. Accurate Fake face detection is necessary for eliminating issues created by fake faces in society. Deep learning is a model to predict real faces and fake faces. Transfer learning is highly accurate in prediction over deep learning. ResNet50, MobileNetv2 from transfer learning and Convolution Neural Network(CNN ) from deep learning are used in proposed research. ResNet50 transfer learning model shows 96% accuracy over other transfer learning model MobileNetv2 to predict real and fake faces. Proposed transfer learning models are highly efficient to detect real and fake faces over deep learning models such as CNN.

title: Cryogenic-Refined MOSFET Modeling for Oscillator, Frequency Divider, and Amplifier Designs Below 4 K
authors: Nikhil Jain, Wei-Yu Lin, Yu-Hsuan Huang, Min-Jui Lin, Bozhi Yin, Vladimir Stojanović, Jiun-Yun Li, Jun-Chau Chien
abstract: Capturing device characteristic changes at cryogenic temperatures is crucial for cryo-CMOS circuit designs. In this work, we present an isothermal cryogenic-refined modeling approach for CMOS transistors that is simple, low overhead, and easy to implement while offering the required accuracy for predicting circuit performance at the designated temperatures. Guided by die-level measurement data and circuit design principles, the model introduces corrections to only five critical parameters: threshold voltage, carrier mobility, elevated low-frequency flicker noise, dominant high-frequency shot noise, and subthreshold swing (SS). These refinements are implemented around the foundry-provided SPICE model, which is typically validated only down to about 200 K. With these adjustments, the proposed cryogenic-refined model achieves less than 5% error in both large-signal metrics (I–V characteristics) and small-signal parameters (e.g., transconductance) when compared with device measurements at deep-cryogenic temperatures. The methodology is validated in two advanced technologies: TSMC 40-nm CMOS and GlobalFoundries (GF) 45-nm RF-SOI. We further demonstrate its applicability in three representative RF circuits: a 30-GHz LC oscillator, a high-speed current-mode-logic (CML) frequency divider (FD), and a subthreshold Gb/s amplifier, all showing close agreement between simulated predictions and measurements performed at 4 and 2.5 K. We believe that the proposed approach is implementation-friendly and can significantly accelerate the development of cryo-CMOS integrated circuits.

title: Resource-Efficient Adaptation of Large Multimodal Models in Medical Visual Question Answering
authors: Zahra Rezaei, Sara Safi Samghabadi, Mohammad Amin Amini, Yaser Mike Banad
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: This study evaluates the effectiveness of parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques for large multimodal models in Medical Visual Question Answering (Med-VQA), focusing on optimizing performance while reducing computational demands in clinical applications. Using the VQARAD dataset, which comprises 2,244 question-answer pairs across 314 radiological images, we implemented Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) and Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA) techniques to fine-tune four state-of-the-art models: Idefics3-8B-Llama3, idefics2-8b, llava_hf_llava_1_5_7b_hf, and blip-vqa-large. Systematic hyperparameter optimization was conducted, including rank dimension, adaptation scaling, and 4bit quantization, to assess performance through accuracy metrics and computational efficiency. The Idefics3-8B-Llama3 model achieved 90% accuracy with minimal computational overhead (2,239 seconds training time), outperforming idefics28b, which reached 88% accuracy with a longer training duration of 5,145 seconds. Both models significantly outperformed llava_hf_llava_1_5_7b_hf (74% accuracy) and blip-vqa-large (55% accuracy). The integration of LoRA and QLoRA demonstrated substantial memory efficiency improvements while maintaining high accuracy, particularly in the Idefics models. These findings establish new performance benchmarks for Med-VQA tasks and provide a practical framework for deploying advanced AI models in resourceconstrained clinical settings, highlighting their potential to enhance medical image interpretation through efficient model optimization.

title: Semi-Supervised Open-Set Federated Learning for Heterogeneous Respiratory Sound Classification
authors: Won-Yang Cho, HyeSun Chang, Sangjun Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Deep learning-based classification study for the noninvasive diagnosis of respiratory diseases using auscultation sounds is actively progressing. Practical clinical applications face limitations arising from the high cost of medical data labeling, data heterogeneity across institutions, the existence of previously unseen classes, and stringent privacy constraints. This study proposes a Semi-Supervised Open-Set Heterogeneous Federated Learning (SOHFL) framework to address these multifaceted issues simultaneously. SOHFL effectively addresses label scarcity and open-set characteristics within a multi-institutional, heterogeneous environment. This is achieved by integrating prototypebased federated learning with GMM-based test-time adaptation, without sharing raw data. Experiments utilizing four distinct respiratory sound datasets confirm the superior performance of the proposed method, achieving an average score of $\mathbf{6 2. 2 - 6 2. 9 \%}$ compared to existing federated approaches. The framework demonstrates increased effectiveness in settings with a high proportion of previously unseen samples. This study presents a practical approach that addresses the complex challenges inherent in real-world clinical settings while preserving patient privacy.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Based Mobile Application for Pigeon Pea Disease Classification
authors: Prabhat Kumar Chaudhary, Santosh Kumar Chaudhary, Ujjwal Kumar Karn, Deepika Neupane, Rajiv Kumar Yadav, Deepesh Prakash Guragain, Bijaya Shrestha
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: About 65 percent of Nepal’s population depends on agriculture, with pigeon pea (‘Rahar’) being a key legume crop for traditional food products. Frequent disease outbreaks, coupled with limited access to agronomists and challenging terrain, force small-scale farmers to rely on manual visual inspection, often resulting in delayed or inaccurate disease identification and excessive pesticide use. This study presents an end-to-end mobile application for real-time pigeon pea disease classification. A self-collected dataset of 7,930 leaf images from multiple farms was used to train a VGG16-based deep learning model, achieving 98 percent accuracy and an F1-score of 94 percent. For deployment on resource-constrained devices, the model was optimized using TensorFlow Lite, with an average inference time of 18 ms per image. The results demonstrate the system’s feasibility and effectiveness for early field-level disease detection. Future work will expand the dataset, improve the interpretability of the model, and integrate additional features such as video-based diagnosis, agronomist guidance, and market insight.

title: Next-Generation Smart Home System: A Research on AR-Enhanced Interfaces, Multimodal Interaction, and AI-Driven Personalization
authors: Angel, Saksham Malhotra, Priyanka Yadav, Sumit Kumar, Surbhi Vijh, Roop Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Smart home technology is changing quickly, and this has helped us understand the problems with current designs, such as fragmented system controls, lack of context, and static control which can’t adapt to different user settings and preferences. This study analyses at recent developments in three areas that overlap: augmented reality user interfaces, multimodal communication strategies, and AI-based personalisation. We look at 20 of these papers from the year 2022 to 2025 to find the most important ones and the problems that affect these fields. AR-based interfaces are 30–40% quicker than smartphone interfaces, and gesture recognition techniques that use the MediaPipe system are 88–95% accurate in the best conditions. Even with these changes, present systems are still limited by fixed learning models, sensitivity to the environment, and limited integration across domains. To solve these problems, we suggest a single framework that combines augmented reality overlays with spatial context, multiple modes of voice and gesture interaction for natural management, and a generative computing intelligent Multi-Armed Bandit (GenMAB) system for adaptive automation. GenMAB combines positive reinforcement training for making the best decisions with generative modelling for context-aware exploration. This lets you keep personalising without having to retrain the whole model. Our results show that user satisfaction and availability have improved significantly, energy optimisation has increased by 10–30%, and habit-tracking adherence has increased by more than 80%. This research not only provides a roadmap for the development of future-proof autonomous houses that are readily available, adaptable, and genuinely intelligent, but it also offers a thorough comprehension of current research trajectories.

title: Closing the Proof-of-Stake Security Gap: A Signature-Based Defense Against Malicious Validators in Long-Range Attacks
authors: Zhanwen Chen, Yannan Li, Willy Susilo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Long-range attacks pose a significant threat to the integrity of Proof-of-Stake (PoS) blockchains by enabling adversaries to reconstruct an alternative chain history embedded with fraudulent transactions. These attacks can deceive honest participants into accepting a maliciously crafted branch as the canonical chain. While Key Evolving Signature (KES) schemes are widely adopted to mitigate such threats, they typically rely on the assumption that validators behave honestly. In this work, we challenge this assumption by demonstrating how a malicious validator can exploit inherent limitations in existing KES-based mechanisms to mount a successful long-range attack. To address this critical vulnerability, we introduce a novel cryptographic construction that combines one-time signatures with commitment schemes. Our approach imposes constraints on the signing capabilities of validators, thereby significantly reducing the feasibility of long-range attacks. We provide rigorous formal security proofs to substantiate the robustness of our scheme and conduct a comprehensive performance evaluation. The results show that our solution is both computationally and storage efficient, making it a practical and scalable defense mechanism for real-world PoS blockchain deployments.

title: Optimized Gene Selection Using Nomadic People and Salp Swarm Algorithms for Cancer Detection
authors: Sadyaa Fahad Jabar, M. A. Balafar, Ali Jameel Hashim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Accurately detecting cancer through gene expression analysis is crucial for early diagnosis and effective treatment. However, gene expression data's high dimensionality and redundancy pose significant challenges, such as overfitting and computational inefficiency. To address these issues, we propose a hybrid feature selection framework that integrates a filter-wrapper approach with swarm intelligence for optimized gene selection. The proposed method utilizes the Nomadic People Optimizer (NPO) in conjunction with Mutual Information (MI) to identify a relevant subset of genes from high-dimensional datasets. An optimized Support Vector Machine (SVM) is employed to further enhance classification accuracy, with its hyperparameters fine-tuned using an enhanced Salp Swarm Algorithm (SSA) incorporating a crossover operator. This hybrid approach not only reduces the search space but also mitigates overfitting by leveraging the exploration and exploitation capabilities of the NPO and SSA. Experimental results on five cancer gene expression datasets-lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD), breast cancer (GSE2034), glioblastoma multiforme (GBM), ovarian cancer (GSE2109), and colorectal adenocarcinoma (COAD)-demonstrate that the proposed NPO-SSVM achieves classification accuracies ranging from 91.25% to 97.02% with AUC-ROC values between 0.85 and 0.97. The framework achieves an average feature reduction of 51% while outperforming state-of-the-art methods (GA, PSO, GWO, SSA) by 3-12% in classification accuracy and 15% in computational efficiency. These findings confirm that NPO-SSVM provides a robust and efficient solution for gene selection in cancer detection, offering significant advancements for personalized medicine and early diagnosis.

title: Implementation of Artificial Intelligence Models for the Prediction of Plant Diseases
authors: Abhishek Pujari, Vinutha Asundi, Rashmi S
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Plant disease leads to major yield loss and compromises its quality, making early and accurate detection essential for sustainable farming. Conventional diagnostic methods such as ELISA and PCR are promising but not easily accessible to all farmers, because of its cost and time consuming. This study reviews how Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools—especially machine learning, deep learning, and image processing—can be used to predict and detect plant diseases earlier and more accurate. Tool like PlantVillage dataset analyses the captured leaf disease images, followed through multiple steps such as capturing, cleaning, segmenting, and extracting features. Theses gathered images were classified using techniques such as Support Vector Machines, K-means clustering, and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). Among these, newer CNN architectures like DenseNet-121, ResNet-50, and EfficientNetV2B2 showed, the highest accuracy, reaching above 98–99%. Complex image data are analysed effectively by deep learning, but yet challenges remain in handling unbalanced or limited datasets and variations in real field conditions. The findings of this study indicates that AI-based systems can support farmers by enabling quick and reliable disease diagnosis, achieving Zero Hunger, reducing pesticide use, and improving crop productivity. Further work should aim at developing larger, well-annotated datasets and simple mobile applications for practical use in the field.

title: An Optimization-Driven Framework for Refactoring Sequence Selection to Improve Software Maintainability
authors: Ritika Maini, Navdeep Kaur, Amandeep Kaur
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Most people concur that code smells indicate more serious issues with software system architecture. If these issues are not resolved, they accumulate throughout the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC), increasing the complexity of the code and the expense of maintenance. Code smells can be eliminated through refactoring while maintaining the external functionality of the code. Its advantages, however, rely heavily on how effectively you select and arrange the refactoring procedures. In order to improve software maintainability, this study suggests an optimisation-based approach for proactively creating refactoring sequences. The suggested system predicts refactoring sequences using machine learning, meta-heuristic, and hybrid optimisation techniques after ranking smell-prone classes using a severity-driven index. This differs from conventional approaches, which only restructure when a chance arises. Extensive experimental evaluation of several open-source object-oriented systems shows notable improvements in maintainability and a notable reduction in refactoring work. Sequence-aware refactoring outperforms ad hoc and single-technique refactoring techniques, according to the results.

title: Ethical Hacking in the Age of Autonomous AI: A Governance-Centric Approach to Cyber Defense
authors: Swetha Sistla
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Autonomous and generative AI systems have turned cybersecurity into an adaptive environment in which attackers and defenders alike are becoming more algorithmic. Classical ethical hacking, which used to be based on human intuition and manual exploit trials, sees today’s artificially intelligent opponents able to learn and adapt in real time. In this paper, we introduce a Governance-Centric Ethical Hacking (GCEH) framework that bakes algorithmic accountability, explainability, and blockchain-enabled auditability into each phase of AI-driven penetration testing. Through governance encoded as code, the model allows red-team efforts to respect ethical and regulatory limits without sacrificing operational flexibility. Experiments conducted in simulated enterprise testbeds show that governance-integrated AI agents enhanced the vulnerability coverage by 40%, achieved a GCI value of 0.94, and elevated ETS from 0.62 to 0.76. Moreover, the number of non-compliance violations reduced by 70%, and the ability to reproduce across runs was 95% reproducible. These findings validate that integrating dynamic policy logic inside AI operations converts cybersecurity from a reactive defense to a proactive, explainable, and ethical discipline. The GCEH model then reframes the ethical hacking activity as reproducible, auditable, and governance-aligned responsible autonomy.

title: A Integrating Approach for Oral Cancer Detection using Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Salman Khan, Ranjeet Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Oral cancer is a prevalent and challenging disease characterized by a high mortality prevalence. Numerous diagnostic methods exist for oral cancer; yet, their efficacy in precisely detecting cancer cells is limited. This oral cancer presents a major and considerable concern to public health because to its substantial influence on death rates. Consequently, novel methodologies are necessary to enhance the identification of oral cancer. Deep learning algorithms provide an alternate technique that, via their advanced algorithms and image processing capabilities, plays a crucial role in cancer detection. The proposed study seeks to detect oral cancer at its onset, hence improving treatment outcomes and survival rates via deep learning methodologies. The goal of the research is to improve the accuracy of diagnosing oral cancer using deep learning architectures. The emphasis is on enhancing the efficacy of oral cancer detection by using innovative approach the Adaptive Class-Aware Feature Pruning (ACAFP) for feature selection algorithm and integrating algorithm with the deep learning classifier improve overall effectiveness. The study attains 97% accuracy in early oral cancer diagnosis via the sophisticated deep learning model VGG16, indicating enhanced patient outcomes and treatment efficacy.

title: Hybrid Soft Computing Models for Real-Time Intrusion Detection in Industrial IoT Networks
authors: Shivendra Kumar Singh, Bassem Hassan Khader, Mouhamd Hashim, Ali Ihsan Alanssari, Riyam Adnan Hasan, Alaa Abdulahusin
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has revolutionized manufacturing and critical infrastructure by enabling smart, connected, and automated operations. However, this increased connectivity also introduced significant cybersecurity challenges, and real-time, autonomous intrusion detection became a critical requirement. Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are unsuitable for heterogeneity, high velocity, and resource constraints commonly found in IIoT. This paper introduces the Hybrid Soft Computing Intrusion Detection System (HSC-IDS), which combines intelligent techniques to meet these requirements efficiently. The model includes a Genetic Algorithm (GA) to identify the best feature subsets, a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-BiLSTM) model for accurate anomaly detection, and a Fuzzy Inference System (FIS) to handle uncertainty in decisions. GA enhances performance and decreases computational overhead through the selection of the most suitable features, while CNN-BiLSTM architecture tracks both spatial and temporal patterns across IIoT traffic. The fuzzy logic layer increases interpretability and resilience, as it allows the system to effectively cope with ambiguous and borderline instances. The suggested model is tested on various benchmark datasets, UNSW-NB15, ToN-IoT, and Edge-IIoTset. Experimental findings prove that HSC-IDS performs better than conventional and single-technique models in detection accuracy, false positive ratio, and processing delay. Furthermore, the light and modular architecture of HSC-IDS qualifies it for deployment in real-time IIoT settings, especially at the network edge or as part of fog computing infrastructure.

title: A Multimodal Artificial Intelligence Framework for predicting Periodontal Bone Loss and Inflammation using Imaging, Biomarkers and Behavioral Data
authors: Oorja Shanker, Santosh Martande, Swathi PV, Sharath Shetty, Anita Kulloli, Nomita Yein, Neha Nerkar
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Microbial challenge, host response, systemic state, and behavioural factors like tobacco use all have an impact on periodontitis, a complex inflammatory disease. Conventional periodontal assessment techniques mainly analyse current tissue degradation and offer little information about how the disease may develop in the future. Artificial intelligence developments present chances to improve predicted, individualized periodontal care by integrating heterogeneous clinical data. A multimodal clinical decision support system based on artificial intelligence by combining radiographic imaging, intraoral photos, biochemical biomarkers, and patient-specific risk factors like tobacco use, oral hygiene habits, and metabolic disorders is proposed to predict periodontal bone loss and inflammation. This further contributes to individualized risk profiling. Longitudinal monitoring and early detection of patients at higher risk of disease progression are made possible by the integration of these many data sources. Such an approach maintains clinical judgment while supporting risk classification, individualized preventative interventions, and optimized maintenance routines.

title: A Study on Adaptive Deep Learning Models for Network Intrusion Detection in Heterogeneous Environments
authors: Tarak Hussain, Nara Sreekanth, K Kiran, Sandeep Kumar Rathore, Anandakumar Haldorai, C. Rajesh Babu
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: In modern heterogeneous network architectures composed of cloud, Internet of Things (IoT) and enterprise systems, the traditional IDS by virtue of the dynamic traffic, feature heterogeneity and unknown attacks cannot meet the high accuracy and adaptability. This study addresses the challenge by designing an Adaptive Deep Learning Model (AdaptiveDeepNIDS) which has heterogeneity-aware encoding, federated pretraining, and an uncertainty-based meta-controller that ensures the robustness of concept drift and distribution shift. The model causes the latent resolution of shared representations in several different feature spaces, adaptive parameters balancing to a new environment on a fly, the combination of supervised and unsupervised detection mechanisms. Experiments done on the CIC-IDS2017 dataset shows better performance with 99.12% accuracy, 99.0% precision, 99.3% recall and false positive rate of 0.40%, which outperforms 5 state of the art methods including federated and Transformer based baselines. The results of this paper confirm the usefulness of adaptive deep learning in a heterogeneous environment and offer a scalable, interpretable and privacy-preserving framework for the next generation of network intrusion detection.

title: Multi-Class Fault Type Classification Using Sequence Components and Wavelet Features with Comparative Study of Machine Learning Models and Neural Networks
authors: Santosh Kumar Chaudhary, Subash Khanal, Prabhat Kumar Chaudhary, Basanta Pancha, Jitesh Kumar Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: The growing complexity of modern power networks requires intelligent fault classification techniques capable of handling multiple fault conditions with high accuracy. Classifying faults in transmission lines with higher accuracy is very important to provide reliability to the power system along with a shorter fault clearance time. The presented work provides a reliable fault classification system in transmission lines using a hybrid approach in feature extraction, taking into consideration both the symmetrical components and time-frequency analysis using wavelet transform. The hybrid technique will be able to analyze imbalance in both transients. Some machine learning algorithms along with an artificial neural network are presented for their implementation in a unified platform. A comparative study will be performed based on standard parameters for fault classification accuracy. The simulation results show improved accuracy with lower misclassifications among related faults using ensemble machine learning algorithms.

title: Detecting Malicious Clients in Federated Learning
authors: Saikat Sinha Ray, Karthikeyan Periyasami
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Purpose: We look into a major problem in Federated Learning(FL): some devices, instead of helping, try to harm the system. These devices send misleading updates during training, which can weaken the overall model and thinking a real threat is safe. This is especially dangerous in areas like fraud detection or cybersecurity. Our aim is to find a way to spot and ignore these harmful updates so that the final model stays accurate and reliable.Survey: We explored different methods that researchers have suggested to spot bad actors in FL. We read about a few ideas like combining models in a smart way, spotting strange behavior using stats, and giving trust scores to each client. I wasn’t just looking at what they do, but also how well they work—especially when it comes to handling many clients and keeping data private.Key Findings of Research Gap: We noticed that most of the accurate detection methods are either too slow or can’t be used easily when there are many clients. On the flip side, faster methods tend to miss subtle or complex attacks.

title: Impact of AI-Assisted Online Compilers in Programming Education
authors: Amrit Poudel, Pratikshya Shrestha
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: In computer programming education, the choice of code compilers significantly influences student learning outcomes. This study compares legacy offline compilers, specifically Dev C++, with modern AI-assisted online compilers via the Process Feedback (PF) platform (processfeedback.org). A Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT) was conducted with first-semester undergraduates at Gandaki University, comprising a control group using Dev C++ and an experimental group using PF for lab sessions. The experimental group achieved substantially higher practical coding assessment score ((μ = 7.79) than the control group (μ = 6.29,p < 0.001), with a large effect size (Cohen’s d = 1.00), and demonstrated significantly greater theoretical improvement (Mann–WhitneyU =382.0,p =0.026). A strong positive correlation ((r =0.68,p < 0.001) was observed between coding proficiency and theoretical gains, suggesting that improved practical skills reinforce conceptual understanding. These findings support integrating AI-assisted compilation environments to enhance learning outcomes in C programming courses.

title: News from Japan: TEPCO Power Grid Builds Japan's First Extra-High-Voltage Digital Substation
authors: Yoshimichi Ohki
abstract: In Japan, overall electricity demand had remained almost constant, or even dropped slightly, for a long time until 2023, thanks to the progress of energy conservation measures. However, this situation is about to change drastically. This is because the progress in green transformation (GX) and digital transformation (DX) is driving an increase in large-scale electricity demand for data centers (DC), semiconductor factories, and the decarbonization of factories, including conversion to electric furnaces for steel production.

title: A Constant Current and Constant Voltage Hybrid Wireless Battery Charging With Dual Frequency Data Transfer System
authors: Xiang Xu, Ping Jin, Yujing Guo, Junshun Wang, Gang Lei, Jianguo Zhu
abstract: To achieve constant current (CC) and constant voltage (CV) outputs in the power transmission terminal of the simultaneous wireless power and data transfer (SWPDT) system, and to address the research gap in existing data transmission ends regarding single-side dual-frequency parallel data streams, this article proposes a novel dual-channel SWPDT topology. In the power transmission channel, an LCC-CLC compensation structure with a switching circuit is introduced, which reduces the interference of power transmission on data transmission and enables the load to operate in a wide-range resonance under both CC and CV modes. In the data transmission channel, based on a dual-frequency resonance decoupling compensation (DRDC) network, the decoupling of parallel data branches are realized. Moreover, an LC parallel branch is utilized to suppress the interference between the power and the data transmission channels. Theoretical analyses are conducted on CC and CV output characteristics of the power channel topology and the dual-frequency transmission characteristics of the parallel data channel topology. Finally, an experimental prototype is built to verify the correctness and effectiveness of the proposed SWPDT topology.

title: Disturbance Rejection Control Strategy for Suspension System of 12/8 Single Winding Bearingless Switched Reluctance Motor
authors: Kai Xie, Ye Yuan, Shihong Ding, Wei Xu, Fan Yang
abstract: In 12/8 single winding bearingless switched reluctance motor (SWBSRM), the performance of the suspension control system is reduced due to the disturbance caused by change of torque current since the torque system and suspension system share the same winding and mechanical structure. Hence, it is necessary to study a control strategy with strong disturbance rejection capability. This article proposes a disturbance rejection control strategy for the suspension system of 12/8 SWBSRM, which is based on the direct suspension force control strategy (DSFCS) and an adaptive sliding mode states observer (ASMSO). The DSFCS directly control the suspension forces, therefore the suspension system of 12/8 SWBSRM can be constructed to a second-order state equation. On the basis of the state equation, the ASMSO can be designed and used to observe the coupling disturbances and other unmodeled disturbances. In addition, the observed disturbances are compensated to the given suspension force through a feedforward channel. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy for suspension system is validated by simulations and experiments.

title: LEO Satellites Cooperative Channel Estimation for Space-MIMO OFDM Uplink
authors: Zohre Mashayekh Bakhsh, Zhenyu Liu, Ke-Xin Li, Yi Ma, Rahim Tafazolli
abstract: This letter explores cooperative channel estimation for space-MIMO OFDM uplinks with multiple low-Earth-orbit (LEO) satellites. By leveraging spatial orthogonality across satellites, we mitigate pilot contamination in massive MIMO multi-user scenarios. To reduce inter-satellite link (ISL) feedback overhead, we propose a sensing-matrix-based scheme, where satellites forward only selected antenna signals to the master node (MN), preserving dominant signal energy. Simulation results show that cooperative satellites improve spatial decorrelation by 55% and reduce normalized mean square error (NMSE) by 83% compared to non-cooperative random pilot assignment. The proposed compression scheme effectively reduces ISL payload while maintaining channel estimation performance.

title: Polarimetric Analysis and Dielectric Constant Retrieval of Lunar Regolith Using Mini-RF Compact-Pol SAR Data
authors: Liting Liang, Yunhua Zhang, Dong Li, Xiao Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Characterizing the dielectric properties of lunar regolith is crucial for revealing surface composition, assessing in-situ resource potential, and deciphering geological evolution of the Moon. The availability of near-global, high-resolution synthetic aperture radar (SAR) observations from Mini-RF has stimulated growing interest in retrieving the real part of the dielectric constant (ε′r) from its S-band compact-polarimetric (CP) data. Existing methods, however, suffer from model-data inconsistencies, limited physical interpretability, and excessive prior assumptions, making accurate and reliable ε′r estimation remain a significant challenge. This paper analyzes the CP scattering characteristics of the lunar regolith and develops a new decomposition technique to extract its dominant scattering contribution. Then, a parameterized, physically interpretable full-polarimetric scattering-matrix representation is established, according to which the scattering mechanism of the regolith is elucidated through experimental analysis, enabling accurate ε′r estimation. Using datasets from the Apollo 16, Apollo 17, and Chang’E-4 missions, the method is comprehensively validated, demonstrating superior estimation accuracy and improved physical consistency compared with existing techniques. These results highlight the potential of the proposed method for advancing future lunar exploration applications.

title: Digital and Hybrid Precoding and RF Chain Selection Designs for Energy-Efficient Multi-User MIMO-OFDM ISAC Systems
authors: Po-Chun Kang, Ming-Chun Lee, Tzu-Chien Chiu, Ting-Yao Kuo, Ta-Sung Lee
abstract: Using multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) with orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) has attracted considerable attention in recent years. While most existing works focus on improving MIMO-OFDM ISAC performance, the impact of transmit power and radio-frequency (RF) circuit power consumption on energy efficiency (EE) remains relatively underexplored. To address this gap, this paper investigates joint precoding and RF chain selection for multi-user MIMO-OFDM ISAC systems, and develops energy-efficient designs for both fully digital and hybrid precoding architectures through the joint optimization of precoding and RF-chain activation. Specifically, we first formulate a novel EE maximization problem subject to sensing performance constraints. Then, efficient optimization algorithms are proposed for both architectures, together with analyses of their computational complexity and convergence behavior. Building on the proposed approaches, spectral efficiency-power consumption tradeoff designs are also provided. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared with existing schemes, the proposed approaches achieve significant improvements in the EE-sensing tradeoff for ISAC systems.

title: HSRNet: Lightweight Remote Sensing Small Object Detection for Multi Scale and Dense Scenes
authors: Boxiang Xie, Yao Zhang, Lingfeng Lin, Wei Zhang, Jie Zhang, Honggang Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing images often exhibit characteristics such as significant scale variation, dense distribution, and complex background textures, which lead to the obscuring of small target features and result in missed and false detections in occlusion scenarios. To address this, we propose a lightweight remote sensing dense small target detection network, HSRNet. This method introduces a Sparse Adaptive Attention Enhancement Module (SASR Transformer) in the deep features to strengthen effective context and suppress background interference. An Asymmetric Feature Aggregation Module (AFAM) is designed at the neck stage to improve multi-scale feature aggregation quality. Additionally, the detection head incorporates hypergraph relation modeling to construct higher-order semantic topology and better distinguish adjacent dense targets through gated distribution. Experimental results on the DOTA1.0, DIOR, and NWPU VHR-10 datasets demonstrate that HSRNet achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$AP_{50}$</tex-math></inline-formula> scores of 0.712, 0.864, and 0.936, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$AP_{50:95}$</tex-math></inline-formula> scores of 0.435, 0.662, and 0.611. Notably, in terms of small object detection (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$AP_{S}$</tex-math></inline-formula>), compared with other state-of-the-art models, our method achieves significant improvements of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.5\%--14.7\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.3\%--14.2\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6.6\%--27.0\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> on these datasets, respectively. In terms of efficiency, the model has approximately 3.5M parameters, a computational cost of about 9.0G FLOPs, and achieves real-time inference at 58.4 FPS on the Jetson Orin Nano, demonstrating its deployment potential in resource-constrained scenarios.

title: Electroless-Plated Gold Nanolayer for Low Temperature Copper Hybrid Bonding
authors: Byeongchan Go, Hoogwan Lee, Keiyu Komamura, Takanori Kishida, Sarah Eunkyung Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: This study investigates the deposition and characterization of electroless-plated gold (Au) nanolayers for low-temperature copper (Cu) hybrid bonding applications. A 0.7 μm-thick thermally grown SiO₂ layer on 8-inch Si wafers was coated with a 50 nm Ti adhesion layer and 1 μm Cu layer, followed by electroless Au plating to form ~7 nm nanolayers. Prior to plating, samples underwent wet pretreatment, and post-deposition analyses included X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), grazing-incidence X-ray diffraction (GIXRD), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), and atomic force microscopy (AFM). The electroless-plated Au exhibited predominantly metallic characteristics with minimal Cu oxidation and conformally covered the underlying Cu, achieving an average surface roughness (Rq) of 3.21 nm. Die bonding at 200 °C and 15 MPa demonstrated successful Cu–Au interface formation, and shear strength of 14.9 MPa showed mechanical integrity. Furthermore, selective deposition of Au on patterned Cu pads surrounded by SiO₂ was achieved, and its potential as a cost-effective solution for metal nanolayer formation in Cu hybrid bonding technologies.

title: Diabetic Retinopathy Classification for Consumer Electronics Using DenseNet and Grad-CAM++
authors: Hafsa Ahmad, Nauman Ali Khan, Nabeela Bibi, Mudassar Ali Khan, Ikram Ud Din, Kamran Ahmad Awan, Ahmad Almogren
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a progressive eye disorder that may lead to permanent blindness when it is not treated. Nevertheless, the issue of early recognition is a problem since the retinal assessment takes a lot of time, and ophthalmologists are in short supply in most places. The paper fulfills this urgent requirement by suggesting a light-weight deep learning architecture to classify DR, namely, in the consumer electronics domain. The model combines DenseNet models and Grad-CAM++ to enhance both interpretation and extraction of features. It involves three dense net models that are used to label retinal images, D121- [M1], D169- [M2], and D121- [M3] to classify APTOS-2019 retinal image samples, 44570 sample images of 5 levels of DR. The framework produces detailed heatmaps which indicate significant parts of the retina which affect classification through incorporation of Grad-CAM++. The design is made to comply with the requirements of consumer devices with a focus on efficiency and flexibility. The steps involved in preprocessing are image resizing to 224x224 pixels and data augmentation to enhance strength. D121-[M3] was the best performing of the models; it received 95.63 percent training accuracy, 0.9921 precision, 0.9930 recall, and a 0.9925 F1-score.

title: Hybrid Poisoning Attacks Leveraging Fake Identities in Cross-Device Federated Learning-Edge Consumer IoT Networks
authors: Xiong Xiao, Zhuo Tang, Yingjie Song, Jiawei Tan, Xin Wang, Lin Chen, Zhihong Liang, Kenli Li
abstract: In federated learning (FL)-enabled edge Consumer IoT (CIoT) networks, multiple participants collaboratively train a joint model by sharing local updates with the server while preserving raw data locality. Although this paradigm offers inherent privacy advantages, the lack of verifiable trust in cross-device communication renders FL systems highly susceptible to poisoning attacks. In such attacks, adversaries covertly inject poisoned samples into the training pipeline to manipulate the joint model’s behavior in a targeted manner. A more severe yet often impractical threat is collusion among multiple compromised honest participants, which incurs high cost and limited scalability in massive-scale FL deployments. Consequently, an adversary with the ability to exert significant poisoning influence at low cost remains a critical yet underexplored vulnerability. To address this gap, this work devises a novel hybrid poisoning attacks strategy leveraging fake identities, termed FIHA. Specifically, the attacker poisons local samples and implements covert hybrid attacks that integrate label flipping attacks and backdoor poisoning attacks to complete more misclassifications of target samples, while ensuring minimal disruption to non-target tasks. Additionally, due to the vulnerability that participants in the FL network are easily disconnected, attackers can simulate multiple fake identities to participate in the global update with a more significant aggregation effect. Notably, FIHA achieves broad poisoning coverage with significantly reduced adversarial resources, eliminating the need for costly collusion with honest participants. Extensive tests substantiate that FIHA surpasses state-of-the-art across various performance evaluation metrics, even with a robust defense strategy employed in the system.

title: Cardiovascular Risk Prediction Using ECG via A Time-Frequency Embedding Pre-trained Model
authors: Junhao Lin, Guanhua Ren, Changhong Jing, Hongyan Wu, Yanyan Shen, Bingcheng Zhao, Kexuan Liu, Baiying Lei, Yi Pan, Kim-Fung Tsang, Shuqiang Wang
abstract: Perioperative cardiovascular events are a major threat to patient safety and constitute one of the leading causes of mortality worldwide. Traditional preoperative risk assessment tools often fail to detect subclinical abnormalities, resulting in missed diagnoses and underestimated risk.
