Electrocardiography (ECG), a widely used non-invasive modality, offers valuable information for preoperative cardiovascular risk assessment. This study proposes a Time–Frequency Complementary Fusion Network (TFCFN) for ECG-based automated assessment of preoperative cardiovascular risk. The model employs a dual-branch architecture: the time branch utilizes a large pre-trained time series model to capture dynamic ECG features, while the frequency branch combines discrete Fourier transform (DFT) and dilated convolution to extract rhythm and spectral energy features, which are then adaptively integrated by a gated fusion mechanism for automated multi-label prediction of cardiovascular risk. Results on four public datasets and one real-world clinical dataset demonstrate that TFCFN achieves excellent generalization and robustness. In addition, an adversarial debiasing mechanism is introduced to mitigate potential fairness issues, effectively reducing gender bias with negligible impact on overall performance. These findings highlight the potential of TFCFN as a reliable tool for perioperative cardiovascular risk stratification in clinical practice.

title: Suppressing Gradient Conflict for Generalizable Deepfake Detection
authors: Ming-Hui Liu, Harry Cheng, Xin Luo, Xin-Shun Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Robust deepfake detection models must be capable of generalizing to ever-evolving manipulation techniques beyond training data. A promising strategy is to augment the training data with online synthesized fake images containing broadly generalizable artifacts. However, in the context of deepfake detection, it is surprising that jointly training on both original and online synthesized forgeries may result in degraded performance. This contradicts the common belief that incorporating more source-domain data should enhance detection accuracy. Through empirical analysis, we trace this degradation to gradient conflicts during backpropagation which force a trade-off between source domain accuracy and target domain generalization. To overcome this issue, we propose a Conflict-Suppressed Deepfake Detection (CS-DFD) framework that explicitly mitigates the gradient conflict via two synergistic modules. First, an Update Vector Search (UVS) module searches for an alternative update vector near the initial gradient vector to reconcile the disparities of the original and online synthesized forgeries. By further transforming the search process into an extremum optimization problem, UVS yields the uniquely update vector, which maximizes the simultaneous loss reductions for each data type. Second, a Conflict Gradient Reduction (CGR) module enforces a low-conflict feature embedding space through a novel Conflict Descent Loss. This loss penalizes misaligned gradient directions and guides the learning of representations with aligned, non-conflicting gradients. The synergy of UVS and CGR alleviates gradient interference in both parameter optimization and representation learning. Experiments on multiple deepfake benchmarks demonstrate that CS-DFD achieves state-of-the-art performance in both in-domain detection accuracy and cross-domain generalization.

title: LMF-Net: Lightweight Multi-dimensional Feature Fusion Network for Floating Debris Detection on Water Surfaces
authors: Pengfei Shi, Hao Wu, Yang Gu, Yuanxue Xin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Detecting floating debris on water surfaces is essential for environmental protection but remains highly challenging due to low contrast, irregular shapes, and significant noise interference in complex aquatic environments. Existing end-to-end detectors based on the DEIM framework, while efficient, struggle to preserve fine-grained spatial details and effectively suppress noise under such conditions, leading to degraded feature discriminability and frequent missed detections. To address these limitations, we propose LMF-Net, a lightweight multi-dimensional feature fusion network that enhances representation learning through three synergistic innovations. First, the Spatial-Edge Fusion module employs a dual-stage multi-path architecture to collaboratively extract local texture details and global contour features, preserving critical edge information that standard convolutions tend to smooth out. Second, the Frequency-Decomposed Hybrid Attention module decouples features into high- and low-frequency components and adaptively fuses them, effectively suppressing water ripple noise while enhancing feature discriminability. Third, the Adaptive Weighted Downsampling module replaces static pooling with content-aware feature reorganization, mitigating information loss during multi-scale pyramid construction. Experimental results on our self-built FloatDataset demonstrate that, compared to the baseline, LMF-Net reduces parameters by 1.2% and FLOPs by 0.7% while achieving significant improvements of 3.5% in AP50 and 6.5% in AP50:95. On the public VisDrone2019 dataset, it outperforms state-of-the-art real-time detectors such as YOLOv8 and RT-DETR by 3.4% and 2.7% in AP50:95, respectively, demonstrating exceptional cross-domain generalization capability and superior performance.

title: Domain Dropout-driven Prototype Enhancement Network for Cross-Domain Few-Shot Hyperspectral Change Detection
authors: Dehao Wang, Lina Xu, Yanni Dong, Ke Wu, Tao Chen, Yuxiang Zhang, Bo Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep learning has shown remarkable effectiveness in hyperspectral image change detection. Cross-domain few-shot learning has emerged as an effective solution to label scarcity in the target domain by exploiting labeled source domain data. However, existing methods often suffer from the interference of domain-sensitive channels and the instability of class prototypes. To address these issues, this article proposes a domain dropout-driven prototype enhancement network for cross-domain few-shot hyperspectral change detection. The method is built upon the metric-based meta-learning to learn transferable knowledge before adapting to new hyperspectral image change detection tasks. Specifically, a dual-branch domain dropout-driven feature extractor is constructed to adaptively dropout domain-sensitive channels, thereby extracting domain-invariant features to mitigate domain shift. Subsequently, a history-aware prototype enhancement module is designed to accumulate historical and current prototype information, thus improving the representativeness and stability of class prototypes. Finally, the trained model is transferred to the target domain to construct a change detection inference module under few-shot conditions. Experiments on several hyperspectral datasets illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: D2VAuth: Efficient Drone-to-Vehicle Authentication Using Blockchain and Hyperelliptic Curve Cryptography
authors: Zeeshan Ali Haider, Muhammad Fayaz, Inam Ullah, Muneera Altayeb, Fahad Alblehai, Amr Tolba
abstract: Integrating consumer-grade drones into the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) can extend coverage and enable low-latency services, but it also introduces authentication challenges due to high mobility, intermittent connectivity, resource constraints, and the reliance on centralized trusted authorities, which increases exposure to physical compromise and sophisticated attacks. Existing drone-assisted IoV authentication schemes are often computationally expensive, insufficiently scalable, or fragile under disconnections. To address these gaps, we propose drone-to-vehicle authentication (D2VAuth), a lightweight drone-to-vehicle authentication framework that combines Hyperelliptic Curve Cryptography (HECC) for energy-efficient key establishment with a permissioned blockchain using Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance (PBFT) for decentralized trust management, auditability, and revocation. D2VAuth supports offline and delayed authentication and resists common attacks, including Sybil, replay, cloning, Man-in-the-Middle (MITM), and Denial of Service (DoS). OMNeT++/Castalia simulations across six mobility and density scenarios demonstrate stable overhead, with per-node energy consumption of 32.82-32.86 mJ, single-session creation time of 5.1-135 ms, two-session creation time of 39.2-162 ms, and low packet loss of 1.8-4.1 under higher-density deployments. These results indicate that D2VAuth provides scalable, energy-efficient, and resilient authentication suitable for practical drone-assisted IoV applications such as logistics, surveillance, and secure vehicular networks.

title: Fluid Antennas Meet Intelligent Surfaces: Security Analysis of NOMA Systems Under Hardware Impairments
authors: Tuo Wu, Jianchao Zheng, Xiazhi Lai, Maged Elkashlan, Hyundong Shin, Naofal Al-Dhahir
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: The revolutionary convergence of fluid antenna systems (FAS) and reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) creates unprecedented opportunities for secure wireless communications, yet the practical implications of hardware impairments on this promising combination remain largely unexplored. This paper investigates the security performance of non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) systems when fluid antennas (FAs) meet intelligent surfaces under realistic hardware constraints. We develop a comprehensive analytical framework that captures the complex interplay between adaptive spatial diversity, intelligent signal reflection, and hardware-induced distortions in short-packet communications. Through novel piecewise linear approximations and block-correlation models, we derive tractable expressions for average secure block error rate (BLER) that reveal fundamental performance limits imposed by hardware impairments. Our analysis demonstrates that while the synergy between FAs and intelligent surfaces offers remarkable degrees of freedom for security enhancement, practical hardware imperfections create performance ceilings that persist regardless of spatial diversity gains. The theoretical framework exposes critical design trade-offs between system complexity and achievable security performance, showing that hardware quality becomes a decisive factor in realizing the full potential of FAS-RIS architectures. Extensive simulations validate our analytical insights and provide practical design guidelines for implementing secure NOMA systems that effectively balance the benefits of fluid-intelligent cooperation against the constraints of realistic hardware limitations.

title: PVDSF: A Photovoltaic Generation Forecasting Network With Dynamic-Static Correlation Fusion on Endogenous and Exogenous Variables
authors: Tianze Deng, Zengni Zhang, Kai Zhang, Yuhan Dong
abstract: Predicting photovoltaic (PV) generation is essential for ensuring grid reliability, optimizing energy allocation, and promoting a green transition in the global energy structure. Previous PV power prediction models only focus on the temporal relationships within exogenous sequences (such as temperature, cloud cover, and humidity) or endogenous sequences (i.e., PV generation), neglecting the impact of exogenous factors on the vulnerable PV generation process. However, the complexity of the physical environment makes it challenging to accurately model the interaction between the two types of variables. To address this issue, we propose a PV generation forecasting network with dynamic-static correlation fusion between endogenous and exogenous variables to improve prediction accuracy. Specifically, we, respectively, encode the exogenous and endogenous variables into static and dynamic components, and accordingly design static and dynamic networks to learn the complex environment, fully capturing the impact of different exogenous components on PV generation. Subsequently, we integrate the dynamic-static enhanced representations through a dual network, achieving lightweight representation prediction. Our model significantly improves PV generation prediction accuracy, achieving state-of-the-art (SOTA) results across four real-world datasets, with average reductions of 12.87% in mean squared error (MSE) and 13.89% in mean absolute error (MAE) compared with competitive baselines. This advancement significantly enhances PV generation forecasting and fosters global sustainability.

title: Multiexpert Inference Model Based on Belief Rule Base Under Uncertainty for Complex System Performance Evaluation
authors: Qiangqiang He, Zhichao Feng, Zhijie Zhou, Changhua Hu, Zheng Lian
abstract: Performance evaluation of complex system (CS) is critical for ensuring their operational efficiency. Under limited monitoring data, incorporating expert knowledge becomes essential to achieve reliable evaluation results. However, the multiple uncertainties from the interfering monitoring data, subjectivity of expert judgments, and difficulty of quantifying critical performance states pose challenges to developing models integrating data with knowledge. To address these challenges, this article proposes a multiexpert inference model based on belief rule base (BRB-ME) for CS performance evaluation under uncertainty. In the proposed framework, multiple belief rule bases are embedded within a mixture-of-experts architecture to fuse multisource monitoring data, mitigating data uncertainty and avoiding rule explosion. Expert cognitive strength and prior knowledge are encoded into the beta distribution's shape parameters to obtain the reference values, enabling the probabilistic modeling of expert knowledge uncertainty. Moreover, single-proposition outputs are extended to the power set to explicitly represent both partial and complete ignorance, enhancing uncertainty characterization. An alternating iteration optimization strategy is further designed to optimize parameters across all BRB-ME components. Finally, by the case study on rocket structure performance inference, the BRB-ME model achieves an average root mean square error of 0.0065, validating the effectiveness of the BRB-ME evaluation results.

title: Enhancement and Regulation of Mono-Mode Bandwidth in a Slab Folded SIW
authors: Ken Ning, Hao Liu, Yan-Rong Zhu, Lin-Fei Gao, Xiyao Liu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: In this article, a slab folded substrate integrated waveguide (SFSIW) is proposed for mono-mode bandwidth (MMB) enhancement. The SFSIW can be regarded as a folded substrate-integrated waveguide (FSIW) loaded with an air-via array perforated dielectric slab, where the distinct field distributions of the first two modes lead to a wider MMB than that of the FSIW. The propagation characteristics of the SFSIW are investigated and quasi-static formulas for estimating the MMB are derived. Two prototypes for exciting the first two modes of the SFSIW are designed and fabricated. The measured MMB of the SFSIW is around 11GHz, representing an 18% improvement over that of the FSIW. Owing to its wide MMB, compact structure, and low cost, the proposed SFSIW is well suited for integrated microwave components operating in the X-/Ku-bands for satellite communication, radar systems, and other broadband microwave front-end applications.

title: Quantum key recovery attack on post-quantum cryptosystems
authors: Yaru Wang, Jianmei Liu
abstract: Post-quantum cryptography (PQC), designed to resist quantum attacks, has received significant attention. Băaetu et al. (EUROCRYPT 2019) found that most lattice-based PKEs follow the same design called meta-cryptosystem, and analyzed the security of meta-cryptosystems under key reuse by mounting a quantum key recovery under chosen-ciphertext attacks (KR-CCA). However, Băaetu et al.’s quantum KR-CCA requires the meta-cryptosystem without ciphertext compression, which is used by CRYSTALS-Kyber to reduce the size of ciphertexts. Developing a quantum KR-CCA against lattice-based PKEs with ciphertext compression, e.g., CRYSTALS-Kyber, is left as a problem by Băaetu et al. (EUROCRYPT 2019). This paper presents an efficient quantum KR-CCA against RLWE/MLWE-based PKEs with ciphertext compression. In particular, we first translate the key recovery problem into a quantum LWE problem, and consider the case of the errors follow centered binomial distribution for quantum LWE problem. Next, we define the meta-cryptosystems with the compression/decompression function under key reuse. Equipped with the aforementioned new algorithm for the quantum LWE problem with centered binomial distribution, we give a quantum KR-CCA for the redefined meta-cryptosystems. Finally, we demonstrate the application of our quantum KR-CCA to NewHope and Kyber and analyze their time complexity.

title: Harnessing Vision-Language Pretrained Models with Temporal-Aware Adaptation for Referring Video Object Segmentation
authors: Zikun Zhou, Wentao Xiong, Li Zhou, Xin Li, Zhenyu He, Yaowei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Referring Video Object Segmentation (RVOS) is a task that involves segmenting target objects in a video based on the given referring expressions. It is critical for video editing and analysis. The crux of RVOS is to model dense text-video relations to associate abstract linguistic concepts with dynamic visual contents at pixel-level. Most RVOS methods typically use vision and language models pretrained independently as backbones, mapping images and texts to uncoupled feature spaces. As a result, they must learn Vision-Language (VL) relation modeling from scratch. Vision-Language Pretrained (VLP) models have achieved remarkable success. Inspired by this, we propose to explore relation modeling for RVOS based on their aligned VL feature space. Nevertheless, transferring VLP models to RVOS is deceptively challenging, due to the gap between static image/region-level pretraining and dynamic pixel-level prediction. To bridge this gap, we introduce a framework named VLP-RVOS, which harnesses VLP models for RVOS through temporal-aware adaptation. We first propose temporal-aware prompt-tuning to adapt pretrained representations for pixel-level prediction and empower the vision encoder to model temporal contexts. We further customize a cube-frame attention mechanism for robust spatial-temporal reasoning. Besides, we propose to perform multi-stage VL relation modeling while and after feature extraction for comprehensive understanding. Extensive experiments demonstrate that VLP-RVOS performs favorably against state-of-the-art algorithms and generalizes well. Our codes are available at https://github.com/xwt909090/VLP-RVOS.

title: A Measurement-Calibrated Vegetation Propagation Model via Leaf-Wood Mesh Reconstruction and Ray Tracing Inversion
authors: Zeyu Cui, Guozhen Xia, Xinyu Liao, Chengkai Liu, Shiyang Jiang, Yuqiang Zhang, Jia Shi, Chen Zhou, Zan Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Accurate radio propagation modeling through vegetation is challenged by highly irregular three-dimensional tree geometry and uncertain electromagnetic parameters, especially when scattering is non-negligible. This letter presents a measurement-calibrated vegetation propagation framework that integrates LiDAR-based tree mesh reconstruction with ray tracing (RT)-based parameter inversion. A handheld LiDAR scan is processed to separate woody components and leaf components, followed by leaf-cluster meshing and woody mesh construction to obtain a high-fidelity tree model. Deterministic RT serves as the forward simulator, while particle swarm optimization (PSO) calibrates a set of effective vegetation parameters, including relative permittivity, conductivity, thickness, scattering coefficient, and scattering-lobe width, by minimizing the mismatch between simulations and a small set of field measurements. Experiments at 2.4 GHz on a real tree show that calibration substantially improves RT accuracy across multiple receiver locations, reducing the received-power RMSE from 4.066 dB to 1.342 dB and the MAE from 3.157 dB to 1.097 dB, and consistently outperforming ITU-R, COST235, and Weissberger baselines.

title: LLM-Driven Stationarity-Aware Expert Demonstrations for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning in Mobile Systems_supp1-3682994.pdf
authors: Zongyuan Zhang
abstract: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) has been increasingly adopted in many real-world applications. While MARL enables decentralized deployment on resource-constrained edge devices, it suffers from severe non-stationarity due to the synchronous updates of agent policies. This non-stationarity results in unstable training and poor policy convergence, especially as the number of agents increases. In this paper, we propose Refinement-Enhanced LLM Expert Demonstrations (RELED), a scalable MARL framework that integrates large language model (LLM)-driven expert demonstrations with autonomous agent exploration. RELED incorporates a Stationarity-Aware Expert Demonstration module, which leverages theoretical non-stationarity bounds to enhance the quality of LLM-generated expert trajectories, thus providing high-reward and training-stable samples for each agent. Moreover, a Hybrid Expert-Agent Policy Optimization module adaptively balances each agent's learning from both expert-generated and agent-generated trajectories, accelerating policy convergence and improving generalization. Extensive experiments with real city networks based on OpenStreetMap demonstrate that RELED achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art MARL methods.

title: A Multidimensional Assessment of Embodied Interaction in Large-Scale Full-Body Interactive Environments_supp1-3682920.pdf
authors: Sena Beste Ercan
abstract: As Mixed Reality (MR) systems evolve toward richer, full-body interactive experiences, understanding how spatial configurations shape user behavior becomes essential for designing usable, engaging, and cognitively coherent environments. This paper presents the design and evaluation of three MR mini-games developed to investigate key embodied interaction properties: (1) territoriality and social distance, (2) proprioception and kinaesthesia, and (3) navigation and wayfinding. Implemented across four differently scaled MR footprints, the games were tested with participants from three age groups, children, adolescents, and young adults, to assess how interaction space size influences bodily coordination, spatial negotiation, and social behavior. Using a multimodal analysis approach, we analyze behavioral patterns that emerge at different scales, revealing how users' interaction strategies change based on space. Our findings contribute to the human-centered design of MR systems by offering a behavioral lens on how scale influences user experience, embodiment, and interaction quality. The study informs future applications in education, social interaction, and rehabilitation, where adaptive MR design is critical for inclusive and effective engagement.

title: Research on Collaborative Optimization of Near-Field ISAC Systems Based on Multi-Waveguide Pinching Antenna Arrays
authors: Yuanhao Chang, Fenggan Zhang, Fengcaho Zhu, Jianwei Zhao, Ziwei Xiang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: In this paper, a near-field integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) using a multi-waveguide pinching antenna system (PASS) is proposed, where discrete radiating particles are flexibly clamped along multiple dielectric waveguides to realize reconfigurable near-field beam focusing. To accurately characterize the underlying propagation mechanism, a spherical-wave channel model is established by jointly accounting for free-space distance-dependent attenuation, waveguide propagation loss, and phase accumulation. Based on this model, a constrained joint design problem is formulated to simultaneously optimize pinching antenna (PA) positions, communication-and-sensing power allocation, and quantized phase-only beamforming under total power, minimum power, minimum inter-element spacing, and finite-resolution hardware constraints. The resulting problem is a high-dimensional nonconvex mixed-integer optimization problem. To address it, a collaborative alternating particle swarm optimization-genetic algorithm (PSO-GA) framework is developed, where PSO is employed to optimize continuous variables, including PA positions and power allocation, while GA is used to search over discrete beamforming phases. In addition, feasibility is maintained through projection and power normalization, and a physics-informed initialization strategy based on MRT and weighted ISAC beamformers is introduced to accelerate convergence. Furthermore, exact channel gradients, a closed-form Fisher information matrix (FIM), and corresponding Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) expressions are derived to analytically characterize geometry-dependent sensing performance. Simulation results for an eight-waveguide, 64-element PASS operating at 28 GHz with 500 MHz bandwidth demonstrate that the proposed design achieves about 6.1 Gbps communication rate while maintaining about 0.01 m localization accuracy. The results also show that the proposed method attains a favorable communication-sensing tradeoff, converges faster than standalone heuristics, and remains robust under discrete configurability, waveguide attenuation, and imperfect channel state information (CSI).

title: Characterizing Block Acknowledgment Window Reordering in Multi-Link Wi-Fi
authors: Rafael Oliveira, Duarte Raposo, Miguel Luís
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Wi-Fi has become a vital wireless technology across many sectors that require networks capable of handling reliable high-speed transmissions. The IEEE 802.11be standard, also known as Wi-Fi 7, further improves network performance by introducing Multi-Link Operation (MLO) with support for wireless link aggregation in the 2.4, 5 and 6 GHz. However, this feature increases the likelihood of out-of-order delivery, as Block Acknowledgment (BA) windows can experience stalls that, under burst loss, are associated with delay spikes at the application level. Because the MLO feature is relatively recent, this paper provides a detailed analysis of out-of-order delivery costs when switching from Single-Link Operation (SLO) to Simultaneous Transmit and Receive (STR) MLO with Wi-Fi 7. The tests are conducted in NS-3 with Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) and User Datagram Protocol (UDP) traffic in burst loss scenarios and with different network features, such as a larger BA window and Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiple Access (OFDMA). The results show that STR MLO increases out-of-order delivery, leading to longer waiting times for frames inside the BA buffer at the receiver, with UDP traffic being more susceptible to these delays compared to TCP. However, MLO also offers greater flexibility in channel access, allowing for faster window repairs than SLO by steering retransmissions onto cleaner links. Nevertheless, the observations suggest that current BA coordination may be insufficient for asynchronous multi-link transmissions under burst loss, motivating further study of window repair policies for lossy multi-link Wi-Fi networks.

title: Multi-Robot Target Monitoring and Encirclement via Triggered Distributed Feedback Optimization_supp1-3686280.mp4
authors: Lorenzo Pichierri
abstract: We design a distributed feedback optimization strategy, embedded into a modular ROS 2 control architecture, which allows a team of heterogeneous robots to cooperatively monitor and encircle a target while patrolling points of interest. By relying on the aggregative feedback optimization framework, we handle multi-robot dynamics while minimizing a global performance index depending on both microscopic (e.g., the location of single robots) and macroscopic variables (e.g., the spatial distribution of the team). The proposed distributed policy allows the robots to cooperatively address the global problem by employing only local measurements and neighboring data exchanges. These exchanges are performed through an asynchronous communication protocol ruled by locally-verifiable triggering conditions. We formally prove that our strategy steers the robots to a set of configurations representing stationary points of the considered optimization problem. The effectiveness and scalability of the overall strategy are tested via Monte Carlo campaigns of realistic Webots ROS 2 virtual experiments. Finally, the applicability of our solution is shown with real experiments on ground and aerial robots.

title: Dual Age Estimation Framework Using PINN and Modified AlexNet with Grad-CAM Visualization: A Deep Learning Approach for Chronological and Biological(Apparent) Age Prediction
authors: Divilin Sweety D S, Abinaya K M, Ananthi S
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Age estimation on the face has gained more relevance in healthcare, biometrics as well as human-computer interaction. The dual-AlexNet architecture learns parallel deep learning approximations that differentiate chronological age (calendar age) and biological indicators of physiological aging on parallel deep learning frameworks trained with fine-grained age annotations. Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) constraints are incorporated into the training loss to enforce biological plausibility of age predictions. Although the majority of available systems look at the chronological age; an increasing understanding is that biological age as a measure of physiological aging and not the time elapsed is of greater benefit in clinical decision-making. Our proposed dual-stream deep learning architecture concurrently predicts chronological and biological age based on faces. Two parallel variants of the AlexNet architecture are employed with the modification to age classification using discrete values (one as discrete) and predicting the biological age using weighted probability averaging (the other). In order to increase clinical trust and interpretability, we combine Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) visualization that identifies age-relevant parts of the face. The framework supports various age label formats by doing intelligent parsing and offers a REST API that can be production ready to enable easy integration with healthcare systems.

title: Design Optimization of a Dual-Cuff Supraglottic Airway for Enhanced Seal Performance
authors: Sudha S, Sushmitha Sree S, Shruthi Lashmi N, Sridharan P
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Supraglottic airway devices (SADs) are essential for maintaining the prolonged efficacy of a consistent airway, which is influenced by structural robustness and flow resistance, by preventing gastric reflux. Conventional (SADs) offer modest advancements in sealing dynamics, but they are limited by their inability to regulate downstream pressure and ensure proper sealing to prevent regurgitation. A previously designed external channel dual-cuff SAD enhanced sealing, but its disproportionate geometry induced displacement in stress-strain concentration and disruption of airflow. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A$</tex> remodelled dual-cuff airway tube with an integrated gastric drainage channel (Design2) was evaluated through a computational analysis to compare the performance of the our previous design external-channel dual-cuff (Design1) and conventional SADs. The integrated channel model depicted significantly enhanced operational implications in functional outcomes, with almost an 85% reduction in peak pressure and 73% reduction in unstable laminar flow. Substantially, a 70% reduction in peak stress with improved uniform distribution of loads was observed compared to the external gastric channel dual-cuff approach. If implemented practically, these outcomes can help produce a significantly more resilient and clinically adaptive airway pathway. The outcomes depict that the revised geometry yields substantial improvements in mechanical endurance.

title: Embedded AI System Continuous Surveillance for Neurofibromatosis Using Wearable Bioimpedance and Nerve Signal Sensors
authors: Vadivelan M, Padmaja J, Bhuvaneswari R, Sajini Navab N
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Neurofibromatosis is a progressive neurological disorder that requires continuous observation to detect subtle changes in nerve and tissue characteristics at an early stage. The risks of neurofibromatosis range from cosmetic and functional impairments to potential malignant transformation of tumors, emphasizing the importance of timely diagnosis and intervention. Early identification of neurofibromatosis-related changes is crucial to prevent long-term complications and improve patient quality of life. This work proposes a wearable and non-invasive monitoring approach that focuses on tracking physiological variations over time and interpreting these patterns to assess potential risk levels. The proposed approach emphasizes intelligent signal interpretation and real-time assessment to support early abnormality identification without revealing underlying implementation details. Clinically meaningful outputs are provided to assist healthcare professionals in long-term monitoring and timely intervention, while prioritizing portability, patient comfort, and practical usability in everyday clinical environments.

title: Segmentation of Plasmodium Falciparum Infected Cell in Blood Smear Images Using Optimized U-Net
authors: R Saranya, A. Bakiya
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Malaria is a major global health concern, contributing to significant illness and mortality across many regions of the world. Every year a millions of cases reported, early detection and accurate diagnosis are critical for effective disease control and timely treatment. Malaria diagnosis has been regarded as the gold standard of the manual microscopic analysis of the blood smears. The manual microscopic examination of blood smears has been considered as the gold standard for malaria diagnosis. The identification of infected cell using microscope is highly challenging during mass screening in rural areas. Hence, the identification of infected cells in blood smear images requires the efficient computer assisted system. In this study, the camera was used to capture the images of the thin blood smear of Plasmodium Falciparum in microscope after staining the slides. The quality of collected blood smear images was enhanced by Histogram Equalization (HE) techniques. Further, the Honey Badger Optimization based U Net (HBA-UNet) was developed to segment the infected cells in thin blood smear images of Plasmodium Falciparum species. The performance of the developed segmentation model is compared with existing deep learning models such as Mobilenet, Efficientnet, and UNet. The results demonstrated that the dice coefficient of HBA based U Net model attains higher (96.07%) when compared to the deep learning models. Similarly, recall, precision, accuracy and IOU of HBA based UNet model achieves 90.93%, 91.26%, 98.77% and 91.24 % respectively, which are higher when compared to the other deep learning models. This finding reveals that the HBA-UNet model is efficient and accurate segmentation of Plasmodium Falciparum infected cell in the thin blood smear images.

title: IoT–Enabled Smart Pest Detection and Control System for Sustainable Agriculture
authors: Sridharan S, Dharani M, Esther Abraham, Deepthi Jenni Priya, Chemmozhi B.
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Agriculture continues to face serious problems with pest attacks that not only reduce the quality of crops but also re- duce the yield. Traditional pesticide-based control systems rely on chemical interventions, which negatively impact the soil fertility, human health and the ecological balance in the long term. The latest developments in Internet of Things (IoT) technologies have enabled low-cost environmental sensing and remote agricultural monitoring, thus providing new opportunities for automated pest detection. This paper presents an IoT-based Smart Pest Detection and Control System incorporating motion sensing, temperature- humidity sensor, wireless connectivity, cloud data recording and real-time ultrasonic repelling of pests. The suggested system is an autonomous system that constantly monitors the field conditions, detects the movement patterns of pests, and triggers a non-lethal ultrasonic repellent when needed. The system has been shown through experimental implementation in a controlled field setting to reduce considerably the amount of manual monitoring effort, enhance the accuracy of pest detection, and provide effective real- time response. The project aims to offer a low-cost, scalable, and ecologically sound substitute to the use of chemical pesticides by small and medium-sized farmers.

title: A BERT-Enhanced Framework for Semantic-Aware Phishing Email Detection and Real-Time Analysis
authors: Piya Mondal, Sudakshina Mandal, Debrupa Pal, Rituparna Mondal, Sabari Kumar
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The problem of phishing emails is still one of the most serious cyber threats. It calls for smarter and more reliable detection mechanisms. This paper proposes a deep learning-based approach using Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers for improving the understanding of contextual information within email text. The paper first constructs and preprocesses a phishing e-mail dataset, showing in detail the process of data cleaning and how to address class imbalance. In an attempt to establish a baseline, several traditional machine learning models, namely Logistic Regression, KNN, SVM, Random Forest and XGBoost were evaluated using TF-IDF features. Among these models, Logistic Regression and SVM achieved full precision, while Random Forest attained the maximum accuracy of 99.56%. Despite this strong performance, it is observed that TF-IDF cannot capture deeper semantic patterns which motivated shifting towards a transformer-based model. The BERT model demonstrated superior language understanding and achieved 99.47% accuracy and a precision score of 0.989. It was useful in practical real-time phishing e-mail detection to prepare the trained BERT model integrated into a RESTful API implemented in Flask and connected with a user-friendly web-based interface for live analysis of e-mails. Results show that though traditional models are effective, BERT’s contextual capabilities make it a more suitable choice for practical phishing email detection.

title: Decoding Indian Market Direction using Indicator-based Deep Gated and Bidirectional Networks
authors: S. Yuvaraj, Terrance Frederick Fernandez, S S Arumugam, Jayanthi G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Accurate identification of stock market trends is essential for informed investment decision-making. This study presents a Deep Learning (DL)–based framework for multi-class stock trend prediction using Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) networks. The proposed approach leverages widely used technical indicators, including Exponential Moving Averages (EMA-9, EMA-21, EMA-50), Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD), signal line, and Relative Strength Index (RSI), to characterize market momentum and trend behavior. Stock trends are categorized into three classes—Bullish, Bearish, and Neutral—based on indicator-driven rule-based labeling. Both models are trained and evaluated on five years of historical stock data for selected Indian equities. Experimental results demonstrate that Bi-LSTM and GRU models achieve high predictive performance, with testing accuracies exceeding 93% across all stocks. While Bi-LSTM effectively captures bidirectional temporal dependencies, the GRU model exhibits superior generalization and more balanced precision–recall performance, particularly under class-imbalanced conditions. The findings indicate that GRU offers an efficient and robust alternative to Bi-LSTM for technical indicator–based stock trend prediction, making it suitable for practical financial time-series analysis.

title: A Lightweight Graph Contrastive Model for Cross Language Semantic Clone Detection
authors: M Jaithoon Bibi, M.Malathi, R.Subha, M.Suganthi, Vinitha Jeevanandham, R Gayathri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The main challenge in cross language semantic code clone detection in software engineering exists owing to the syntax differences in various computer programming languages, resulting in difficulties in applying traditional code clone detection methods. Even though transformer models have shown promising results in code clone detection, they have a high computational overhead, thereby imposing a constraint on scalability for deployment in real world software development settings. In light of these requirements, a new approach named Lightweight Graph Contrastive Model (GCM) has been proposed for efficient semantic code clones across multiple computer programming languages. The proposed strategy models code using language independent program graphs constructed using Abstract Syntax Trees and control flow relationships to support robust semantic abstraction. A highly optimized Graph Neural Network with a small parameter set is employed to transform semantically similar code snippets from multiple computer programming languages into a common vector space using a contrastively learned objective to emphasize robust semantic abstraction with lower computational complexity. The proposed approach has demonstrated promising results on BigCloneBench, POJ 104, and the cross language CodeNet benchmarking dataset, with an overall F1 score of 96.1%, 92.3%, and 88.7%, respectively, with a significant reduction in computational latency of nearly 2x, while reducing overall parameters by 65% in the cross language semantic code clones to outperform the existing state of the art baseline GraphCodeBERT models.

title: Implementation of Higher Order Sectorization Techniques for Efficient Resource Allocation in mmWave 6G Networks During Peak Demand
authors: Preeti Badhani, Nookala Venu, Prateek Srivastava, Deepak Upadhyay, Seema Sharma, Chetan Trivedi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: A study in this paper demonstrated a method using higher-order sectorization along with reinforcement learning (RL) for managing resource distribution in mmWave 6G networks under high-traffic conditions. By dividing a single cell into multiple subcells or "sectors" each with their own antenna array, higher order systems will improve frequency reuse and reduce co-channel interference. Additionally, RL will provide an intelligent way to determine how to distribute available bandwidth between sectors. Using simulations on synthetic traffic models to evaluate several metrics including throughput, fairness, delay and outage, it was found that the higher-order with RL systems performed significantly better than traditional three-sector systems at large user densities. Specifically, the average delay was reduced by approximately 25% and the outage probability was significantly decreased as well. In addition, the degree of fairness among users was also increased with the higher-order with RL system. Therefore, this study concludes that such resource management techniques are expected to be beneficial for supporting heavy traffics in dense environments in future 6G wireless networks.

title: Conversational Career Guidance System using LangChain and Agentic AI Framework
authors: Shivansh Pandey, Sandhya Avasthi, Aditya Tandan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The modern job market is changing incredibly fast due to new technology, and it's getting much harder for students and professionals to figure out what skills they need. The old way of giving career advice—like static lists of recommendations—just doesn't work well anymore because it can't keep up with complex career decisions. We designed and built an Intelligent Career Guidance System to solve this. It uses an agentic AI framework, essentially creating a smart, autonomous agent powered by the popular LangChain toolkit and the OpenAI API. This agent can chat naturally with a user to understand their specific skills, interests, and education, and then do a lot of sophisticated work behind the scenes. Our system can tap into live data and use a multi-step thinking process to come up with completely personalized career paths and pinpoint where a user's skills are lacking. We built the system using an agent-based setup, giving the AI specific "tools" (task-specific tools) so that it can provide high-quality, highly relevant advice. The result is an application that feels like a truly interactive and iterative counseling session. We found that the system is very effective: it gives users proactive, relevant guidance that significantly boosts their confidence in making smart career choices.

title:
Temperature-Invariant TMR in Fe
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Si/2L-WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /Fe
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Si Magnetic Tunnel Junctions from First-Principles Simulations
authors: Saisha Gandagatri, Shagufta Parveen Asif Akhtar, Sanskar Kumar Karn, Gadipalli Varun Sai Kumar, Aminul Islam, Md. Waseem Akram
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Magnetic Tunnel Junctions (MTJs) are among the most essential elements of modern spintronic devices and enable spin-polarization-controlled electron transport. In this work, we present a Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si/2L-WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si MTJ studied at the quantum level using QuantumATK. The major emphasis is put on TMR properties under different thermal conditions. The MTJ consists of a 2-layer WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> barrier placed between two ferromagnetic electrodes of Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si. We have calculated the spin-polarized transmission spectrum and conductance for parallel (P) and anti-parallel (AP) magnetic configurations at 50 K, 100 K, 200 K, and 300 K. The device demonstrates consistently high TMR values (up to 102.81%) that do not exhibit any noticeable variation throughout the investigated range of temperatures. This temperature-independent behavior proves the stability of the Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si/2L-WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si material stack. Conductance analysis demonstrates strong spin filtering due to strong spin polarization in Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si and pronounced spin-orbit coupling in WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>. Finally, some points have been discussed regarding realistic integration perspectives: fabrication compatibility, scalability, and thermal endurance. Based on these results, the proposed MTJ is suitable for stable and high-performance spintronic applications.

title: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Automated Glaucoma Detection using Optic Disc-Cup Segmentation and Deep Learning
authors: T. Sangeetha, Sangamithra U, Sharon Reshma Arulprakash, Subanu R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Glaucoma is one of the leading causes of irreversible blindness and early, accurate, clinically interpretable detection is necessary for effective screening and treatment. While the deep learning-based methods to automated glaucoma detection from retinal fundus images have achieved promising performance, their black-box operation raises concerns on clinical trust and practical utility. In this work we propose a novel confidence-driven neuro-symbolic concept that combines deep learning and interpretable clinical reasoning with high diagnostic performance. The proposed system adopts a ResUNet model utilizing an encoder of pre-trained ResNet-34 on ImageNet to accurately segment the optic disc and optic cup, and further derive the clinically relevant cup-to-disc ratio (CDR), which is then applied to grade glaucoma severity according to well-established ophthalmological rules. Meanwhile, a convolutional neural network model VGG-19 predicts images from glaucoma and provides confidence-aware predictions. A neuro-symbolic decision engine aggregates the structure CDR-based evidence with probabilistic deep learning predictions, favoring robust anatomical signposts and disambiguating uncertain cases by confidence reasoning. Experiments on the RIM-ONE DL dataset demonstrate that our method outperforms both segmentation-based and classification-based methods, achieving an accuracy of 86.0%, sensitivity of 63.4%, specificity of 97.8% and AUC of 94.9%. The novelty of our work lies in an interpretable fusion between quantitative anatomical biomarker and deep-learned visual pattern, leading to a robust, explainable, and therapeutic-orientated procedure for automated glaucoma screening.

title: Real-Time Patient Condition Prediction in Ambulances Using Explainable AI
authors: Prem Nanda Kishore, Mattaparthi Pardhasaradhi, Mallivalasa Jaideep, Kusumalatha Reddy, Padarthi Layateja, Ankur Saxena
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Emergency medical services must make high-stakes decisions under severe time pressure, often before a patient arrives at the hospital. Real-time prediction of patient condition in the ambulance can support earlier stabilization, resource allocation, and medication preparation. However, many high-performing machine learning models are opaque, which limits trust and adoption in safety-critical settings. This paper presents an end-to-end system for real-time patient condition prediction in ambulances using explainable AI (XAI). The pipeline ingests ambulance-collected data (demographics, vital signs, symptoms, medications, and attachments) via a Streamlit-based interface, processes it through a FastAPI backend, and serves risk predictions and explanations to a hospital dashboard. Gradient boosting (GBM) is used as the primary model, with L2-regularized logistic regression as an interpretable baseline. Models are trained and evaluated on publicly available critical-care datasets, primarily MIMIC-IV and eICU-CRD, after mapping ICU records to a prehospital schema. Explanations are generated using SHAP and LIME to provide both global and local interpretability. Experimental findings indicate that the GBM model provides superior discrimination and calibration compared with the logistic baseline, while remaining explainable through XAI techniques. We discuss system latency, domain shift from ICU to prehospital settings, and future directions for multimodal integration and federated learning.

title: Deployable CNN-Based System for Real-Time Aloe Vera Leaf Disease Monitoring
authors: Sakshi Koli, Anil Kumar Lamba, Puneet Banga, Nagendar Yamsani, Rohini Rohini
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The aloe vera plant leaf is renowned universal for its therapeutic possessions and finds applications in various industries, including pharmaceuticals and cosmetics, due to its numerous benefits. It is recognized for its soothing and healing properties, as well as its hydrating and rejuvenating effects. However, the quality and quantity of aloe vera plant leaves go down due to the various diseases present in the aloe vera leaf. So to detect these diseases deep learning approach is discussed in this article. The study indicates that CNN, despite having the fewest parameters ranging up to 278K as compared achieves the most efficiency in detecting the aloe vera plant leaf disease. The discussed CNN architecture outperformed the pre-trained named vgg16, inception, and Alexnet by providing an accuracy of 92.42 %, precision of 90.4%, recall of 88.32 and F1-score of 88.46. The work found that CNN is ideal for lightweight applications with lower computational demands and higher response time.

title: PACA: 3D Physical Adversarial Camouflage for Aircraft in Remote Sensing under Multi-View and Varying Weather Conditions
authors: Jinpeng Luo, Gang Liu, Min Dang, Zhaolu Zheng, Xulong Zhao, Nan Luo
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Most existing physical adversarial attack methods for remote sensing object detectors are optimized from a single nadir viewpoint, leading to significant performance degradation under multi-view observations. Moreover, most methods directly overlay adversarial textures onto images during digital training without adequately modeling the color distribution of complex environmental backgrounds, causing severe color inconsistency between adversarial textures and their surroundings. This reduces visual realism, training stability, and environmental adapt ability. To address these limitations, this paper proposes Physical full-coverage Adversarial Camouflage for Aircraft (PACA), a framework designed to achieve robust deception across multiple viewpoints and complex environmental conditions. PACA integrates differentiable neural rendering with an environment-aware texture optimization mechanism: a neural rendering module (NeuroCam) enables precise texture mapping and multi-view projection on 3D aircraft surfaces, while an environment-aware module (FIRNet) extracts and fuses environmental color features to generate visually consistent and physically realizable full coverage adversarial textures. Extensive experiments on the high fidelity simulated remote sensing dataset (RS-AirSim) and real world physical prototype tests demonstrate that the proposed method effectively degrades detection performance under multi view observations. Compared with traditional remote sensing camouflage strategies, PACA reduces the detection AP from 0.30–0.60 to below 0.02 in most white-box settings, and maintains strong attack effectiveness in real-world physical deployments, while remaining robust to viewpoint variations and complex weather conditions. Source code, dataset, and 3D simulation environment will be publicly available at our GitHub repository.

title: A Wearable Footstep Energy Harvester With Novel Dual-Clutch Mechanical Motion Rectification for Self-Powered Sensing_supp2-3675109.mp4
authors: Fang Deng
abstract: Wearable and autonomous sensing systems require efficient energy conversion under low-frequency, bidirectional mechanical excitations generated by daily human motion. This work presents a novel dual-clutch ratchet energy harvester (DREH) that converts bidirectional heel motions into unidirectional high-speed rotation, enabling direct DC power generation without conventional AC–DC rectification losses. The dual-clutch architecture ensures robust motion rectification under weak excitation and enables recovery of elastic potential energy during the liftoff phase, thereby improving effective energy utilization. A coupled dynamic–electromechanical model is developed to analyze the system behavior and predict electrical output. A compact prototype (86.4 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, 89 g) is fabricated and experimentally evaluated under pseudowalk and natural walking conditions. Experimental results show that elastic energy recovery increases output power by 59.5% at 0.3 Hz; at 6 km/h with a 75 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\Omega}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> load, the prototype delivers 82 mW peak and 28 mW average power with a driving force below 14 N. System-level validation demonstrates continuous operation of a wireless sensing node, confirming the DREH as an effective self-sustained power source for wearable and industrial sensing applications.

title: Scheduling Training-Inference Co-Location in Demand Response for Sustainable Edge AI
authors: Konglin Zhu, Siyuan Wei, Xuan'er Wu, Lei Jiao, Jin Dong, Lin Zhang
abstract: In the pursuit of data privacy and reduced latency, the adoption of edge intelligence has surged. Meanwhile, the enormous increase in AI has resulted in significant energy consumption. Edge intelligence plays a crucial role in Energy Demand Response (EDR). However, existing edge intelligence falls short of meeting the demands of co-locating training and inference tasks while satisfying EDR. Specifically, the intertwinement between balancing energy consumption, system delay and model accuracy, and uncertain future inputs adds to the challenge of designing an online sustainable system for co-located training and inference tasks. To address these challenges, we propose a novel two-timescale system for co-locating training and inference EDR. Our approach satisfies EDR by strategically planning training schedules on macro-timescales and migrating inference requests between heterogeneous edges on micro-timescales while minimizing long-term cost. We introduce a novel online polynomial time algorithm that first breaks down the problem into two subproblems, which are subsequently solved using an online-learning-based fractional algorithm and a randomized roun ding algorithm, respectively. Rigorous analysis demonstrates that our approach achieves both sublinear dynamic regret and sublinear dynamic fit. Extensive trace-driven evaluations validate the practical superiority of our approach over multiple existing methods, highlighting its effectiveness in real-world scenarios.

title: Adaptive Grouping-Based Offspring Generation for Sparse Large-Scale Multimodal Multi-objective Optimization_supp1-3685860.pdf
authors: Jinhua Zheng
abstract: Sparse large-scale multimodal multi-objective optimization problems (SLMMOPs) are characterized by high dimensionality, sparse effective variables, and multiple disconnected Pareto-optimal regions. These properties create vast and deceptive search spaces, where only a small subset of variables contributes meaningfully to optimal solutions. Existing algorithms often emphasize diversity preservation, but struggle to identify and exploit sparse structures effectively, thus leading to inefficient search and premature convergence. To address this challenge, we propose AGOEA, an Adaptive Grouping-based Offspring Generation Evolutionary Algorithm. AGOEA streamlines the search process through two core innovations. First, it introduces a dual-granularity decision variable grouping strategy. A coarse-grained grouping leverages long-term population statistics to identify globally important variables and differentiate between Pareto sets, while a fine-grained grouping utilizes local feedback to refine variable selection within subpopulations. These grouping insights subsequently guide customized reproduction operators that perform dimension-specific variation to balance exploration and exploitation. Second, AGOEA adopts a multi-population evolution framework, enabling subpopulations to explore distinct sparse structures in parallel. To coordinate these subpopulations and balance computational effort between discovering new Pareto sets and refining sparse variable structures, a dynamic monitoring and resource allocation mechanism is employed. This mechanism adaptively identifies stagnation and redistributes resources to under-explored regions, thereby enhancing global coverage and convergence. Extensive experiments on benchmark SMMOPs with 500 to 1000 decision variables show that AGOEA consistently achieves superior convergence and diversity compared to six state-of-the-art algorithms. (The source code is available at https://github.com/WP0102/AGOEA.).

title: Strategies to Improve Efficiency of Select-Gate Voltage Floating During GIDL-Assisted Erase in 3-D NAND Flash Memory
authors: Sungju Kim, Jaeyeop Jung, Hyungcheol Shin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: This study addresses the issue in 3-D NAND flash memory where, during GIDL (Gate-Induced Drain Leakage)-assisted erase, the floating (FT) select-gate cell (SG) approach induces uncertainty in SG voltage (VSG) rise due to the parasitic capacitive suppression (PCS), degrading SG reliability. First, Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) simulations were performed to elucidate the mechanism of VSG rise in the FT approach (VSG,FT), and a physics-based compact model capable of quantitatively predicting VSG,FT variations was developed. Strategies to mitigate the degradation of VSG,FT rise were proposed, ultimately achieving both SG reliability and GIDL erase efficiency while offering a scalable approach. The validity of the proposed strategies was confirmed through a Simulation Program with Integrated Circuit Emphasis (SPICE)-based Monte Carlo simulations using the developed compact model, evaluated from the perspective of threshold voltage distribution.

title: Evolutionary Neural Networks for Mental Health Monitoring in Digital Therapeutics Platforms
authors: Koushik Chakraborty, Hussana Johar R B, Saleh Naji Sreseh, Jamal K. Abbas, Liwaa Ali Hussein, Anurag Tewari
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Mental health disorders have become a growing global concern, necessitating the development of intelligent, scalable, and personalized monitoring systems. Digital therapeutics (DTx) platforms offer an innovative solution by delivering real-time, software-driven interventions through mobile and wearable devices. However, their effectiveness depends largely on accurate, adaptive analysis of complex, noisy behavioral data. This paper presents a novel approach utilizing Evolutionary Neural Networks (ENNs) for mental health monitoring within DTx platforms. ENNs combine the learning power of neural networks with the global optimization capabilities of evolutionary algorithms, enabling automatic adaptation to individual behavioral baselines and improving generalization across diverse populations. The proposed architecture processes multimodal data from sources such as speech, facial expressions, physiological signals, and mobile usage patterns. A simulated study and experimental validation using public mental health datasets demonstrate that ENNs outperform traditional deep learning models in classification accuracy, adaptability, and computational efficiency.

title: Alzheimer’s Disease Detection using Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Technique
authors: Ashok Koujalagi, Saragadam Anuradha, B.Venkata Varma, P Chandra Sekhar Reddy, A. Lakshmanarao, Naladi Ram Babu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Early identification of Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) is crucial for timely intervention, yet traditional diagnostic pathways are costly, time-intensive, and prone to variability. This paper presents a hybrid learning framework that combines deep representation learning with ensemble classifiers for AD detection from structured, non-imaging clinical and lifestyle data. Using a Kaggle dataset of 2,149 records, the pipeline includes rigorous preprocessing, stratified splitting, and class-imbalance mitigation via Adaptive Synthetic Sampling (ADASYN) applied only to the training set. Three machine-learning baselines—XGBoost, CatBoost, and KNN to establish reference performance. Deep models (TabTransformer and Wide & Deep) are then trained as supervised classifiers, while an Autoencoder is employed strictly as an unsupervised feature extractor. Hybrid systems are formed by feeding deep embeddings into boosting classifiers. Results show consistent gains from deep models over ML baselines, with the hybrid configurations performing best. The proposed TabTransformer–CatBoost model attains 0.98 accuracy, 0.97 F1-score, and 0.97 ROC-AUC, indicating improved generalization and sensitivity to AD-positive cases.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Battery Exchange System for Electric Two-Wheelers
authors: R. Indhumathi, P. V. Adhini, S. Aravind, S. Agilesh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Two-wheelers powered by electricity are gradually taking over as a choice of urban transport because of their low emissions and low prices. Nevertheless, the issue of a small number of charging stations, hours to charge, and reliance on grid power make their mass adoption difficult. In this paper, an IoT enabled smart battery change system of two-wheeler electric vehicles is introduced, which incorporates solar energy harvesting, automatic battery changeover and real-time monitoring to sustain uninterrupted functioning. The system uses voltage sensors to check on the main battery and an auxiliary battery which is solar powered. Upon the main battery being low in power, an ATmega328 microcontroller will trigger a relay to turn the power on to the solar battery and the vehicle will keep running just as usual. The vehicle is propelled by a DC motor and an LCD display gives the riders real time data about the battery condition, voltages and power source used. Moreover, an IoT cloud application will allow remote monitoring, collecting and analyzing data and will help preventative maintenance and efficient energy consumption. The proposed system will minimize the use of conventional charge infrastructure by integrating renewable energy as well as smart battery control and improving the efficiency of the entire energy network and optimizing the energy-saving use of transport, thereby supporting an environmentally friendly movement. The practical aspect of the approach is proved by its implementation, which is effective and feasible, and it has the potential to provide sustainable urban mobility.

title: A Review of Deep Learning–based Malaria Disease Detection Techniques: Challenges and Gaps
authors: Nallagoni Rajitha, Akhila Ravi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Malaria remains one of the most life-threatening infectious diseases, particularly in developing and resource-limited regions. Early and accurate diagnosis plays a critical role in reducing mortality and preventing disease spread. In recent years, Deep Learning (DL) and computer vision techniques have been widely adopted for automated malaria detection using microscopic blood smear images. This work presents a comprehensive review of existing malaria disease detection models, focusing on convolutional neural networks, hybrid DL architectures, and object detection frameworks. The review identifies common limitations such as reliance on single datasets, loss of fine morphological features due to image resizing, lack of species and parasite stage classification, high computational complexity, and limited real-world validation. Based on these observations, key research gaps are highlighted, and sustainable solution directions are discussed to guide future research toward accurate, efficient, and clinically deployable malaria detection systems.

title: Secure and Explainable Generative AI and Consumer Trust in India: A Thematic Analysis and Systematic Review
authors: Subhadeep Ghosh, Loveleen Gaur, R Meenakshi, Ramakrishnan Raman
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Generative AI application is becoming one of the most effective technologies that attracts a large number of consumers and organization. The lack of research on explainable AI (XAI) created an influence on the author to initiate this study. The primary objective of this study is to identify the impact of secure and trustworthy XAI on Indian consumer purchase decisions, trust, and behavior. Prisma 2020 and the systematic literature review method have been used by the researcher to evaluate 45 peer-reviewed journal articles. The overall findings indicate consumer preference are being increased towards the Explainable AI and trustworthiness in the Generative AI. This study discusses different psychological factors along with the regulatory framework to derive the implications of this technology and the policies. This SLR highlights the emergency of contact-aware emergency responses and responsible approaches to foster sustainable consumer trust.

title: Design and Implementation of a Raspberry Pi–Based Multilingual Image-to-Speech System
authors: Nayana R, Gowthami H R, Likhitha Shree K R, Athul Ghosh P
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The paper deals with a Raspberry Pi based multilingual image to speech converter system, which can help the visually impaired people to speak out the meaningful words from images in different languages. The gadget takes pictures of printed text in any language using a camera. OCR technology will be applied to the received image. The text which is extracted is run through a multilingual TTS engine which converts the written text into natural human voice in the respective language. The implementation uses open-source software libraries are Tesseract OCR with multilingual language packs and e-Speak or Festival for speech synthesis, all operated locally on a low-cost portable Raspberry Pi device. Image pre-processing techniques enhance text recognition accuracy, and the blur detection mechanism ensures reliable input quality. This system doesn’t require internet to function, making it usable in any setting for real-time application. The solution can recognize and produce multilingual text and speech. This solution aids in the inclusion of assistive technologies for the disabled. It enables users to gain access to printed information in various languages using human-like speech output.

title: IoT-based Secure Health Monitoring System in Machine Learning using Deep Neural Network
authors: R. Satheesh Kumar, M S. Sachika, A P. Priyavarshini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The proposed method for remote healthcare monitoring is based on the Internet of Things, which allows for a secure method of collecting, analyzing, managing, and storing physiological data through machine learning and deep neural network algorithms. The IoT will allow for real-time measurements of vital signs as opposed to relying on periodic clinical evaluations. Using wearable devices equipped with sensors, real time values will be captured and securely transmitted to a cloud-based server. A deep learning neural network model will be created and trained to determine the complex, non-linear correlations between multiple biomedical signals and identify whether a health condition is normal or abnormal. This deep learning model will provide an early prediction of a potential medical risk. The proposed method will allow for continued monitoring of patients, and the data will remain secure and confidential using cryptographic hashes and the blockchain-based logging of immutable and tamper resistant records. The combination of IoT sensing, deep learning-based inference, and decentralized security will provide a means for continuous health evaluation while still protecting the patient’s right to privacy. The proposed method will also produce alerts to caregivers and/or clinicians during critical events that require immediate intervention and will decrease the need for patients to return to the hospital for evaluation. By linking intelligent prediction with secure data governance, the proposed method provides a scalable and trustworthy solution to healthcare delivered remotely, in a clinically significant manner.

title: DTBA for Smart Grid Optimization: A Case Study on Loss Reduction and Voltage Improvement in Distribution Networks
authors: Yam Krishna Poudel, Asmita Rijal, Rashik Kumar Badgami, Nava Raj Karki
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: This research paper focus on novel Driving Training-Based Algorithm (DTBA) for optimal placement and sizing of distributed generation (DG) solar PV and shunt capacitors. DTBA is used in radial distribution networks to minimize power losses and improve voltage profiles. The increasing complexity of power distribution systems, coupled with rising electricity demand all over the world and renewable energy integration, necessitates advanced optimization techniques. Classical methods Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) have limitations in convergence speed and solution accuracy. This research applies DTBA, a human-inspired metaheuristic algorithm, to solve the nonlinear optimization problem of DG and capacitor placement. The proposed algorithm is tested on the IEEE 33-bus system and a real-world Thimi-Sallaghari 11 kV feeder in Nepal. Load flow analysis is performed using the backward/forward sweep (BFS) method in MATLAB, with validation in ETAP. Results shows that DTBA reduces active power losses by 85.98% in the combined DG-capacitor case, outperforming GA (72.91%), PSO (74.85%), and Reptile Search Algorithm (RSA) (75.27%). Voltage profiles improve by 5.89%, ensuring stable operation within standard limits (0.95–1.05 pu). The findings of this research show the DTBA’s superiority in convergence speed and solution quality, making it a promising tool for smart grid optimization in radial distribution system.

title: MetaFlex: A Flexible Architecture for Efficient Packet Scheduling and Memory Allocation
authors: Anthony Dalleggio, Peixuan Gao, Yongbo Gao, Hao Wang, Yang Xu, H. Jonathan Chao
abstract: Packet schedulers are essential for managing packet transmission order in high-speed networks, where scheduling metadata must be processed at line rate under bursty traffic conditions. In such systems, packet scheduling and traffic management operate on compact packet descriptors rather than on full packets. FIFO-based schedulers are attractive for their simplicity and throughput, but implementations that statically partition descriptor memory across queues must provision for worst-case occupancy, leading to inefficient memory utilization. This paper presents MetaFlex, a scalable architecture for efficient implementation of calendar-queue–based packet scheduling using shared descriptor memory. MetaFlex dynamically allocates descriptor storage to scheduling queues on demand, allowing memory to be effectively shared across a large number of rank bins while maintaining constant-time enqueue and dequeue operations. Rather than introducing a new scheduling algorithm, MetaFlex focuses on the architectural realization of shared-memory descriptor queues that scale to large queue counts with predictable timing and modest hardware cost. We evaluate MetaFlex using NS2 simulations of weighted fair scheduling and a hardware prototype implemented in VHDL on an AMD/Xilinx Alveo U250 FPGA board. Simulation results show that MetaFlex achieves comparable or lower packet loss than fixed-memory calendar queues under identical descriptor-memory budgets, while using substantially less descriptor storage under typical traffic conditions. The FPGA prototype operates at 322 MHz, sustains 100 Gb/s line rate for packets larger than 370 bytes, and uses less than 1% of logic resources and less than 5% of on-chip memory, demonstrating the practicality of MetaFlex for high-speed hardware datapaths.

title: Teleoperating Autonomous Vehicles over Commercial 5G Networks: Are We There Yet?
authors: Rostand A. K. Fezeu, Jason Carpenter, Rushikesh Zende, Sree Ganesh Lalitaditya Divakarla, Nitin Varyani, Faaiq Bilal, Steven Sleder, Nanditha Naik, Duncan Joly, Eman Ramadan, Ajay Kumar Gurumadaiah, Zhi-Li Zhang
abstract: Remote driving, or teleoperating Autonomous Vehicles (AVs), is a key application that emerging 5G networks aim to support. In this paper, we conduct a systematic feasibility study of AV teleoperation over commercial 5G networks from both cross-layer and end-to-end (E2E) perspectives. Given the critical importance of timely delivery of sensor data, such as camera and LiDAR data, for AV teleoperation, we focus in particular on the performance of uplink sensor data delivery. We analyze the impact of Physical Layer (PHY layer) 5G radio network factors, including channel conditions, radio resource allocation, and Handovers (HOs), on E2E latency performance. We also examine the impact of 5G networks on the performance of upper-layer protocols and E2E application Quality-of-Experience (QoE) of the adaptation mechanisms used for real-time sensor data delivery, such as Real-Time Streaming Protocol (RTSP) and Web Real Time Communication (WebRTC). Our study reveals the challenges posed by today’s 5G networks and the limitations of existing sensor data streaming mechanisms. The insights gained will help inform the co-design of future-generation wireless networks, edge cloud systems, and applications to overcome the low-latency barriers in AV teleoperation.

title: IEEE Standard for Geometry Point Cloud Compression
authors: N/A
abstract: A set of tools for efficient compression of geometry point clouds, along with the corresponding definitions of syntax, semantics, parsing, and decoding processes, including but not limited to prediction, transform, quantization, and entropy coding, is defined in this standard. This standard provides efficient coding tool sets for compression, decompression, processing, and packaging of point cloud data, which can significantly reduce the disk storage and communication bandwidth. The target applications and services include but are not limited to manufacturing, mapping, e-commerce, e-tourism, digital culture heritage.

title: Personalized E-Commerce Recommendations through Hybrid Social Media-Graph Neural Networks
authors: Amarthaluri Tirupathaiah, Mulamraju Sai Sumanth, T. Seshasai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The use of recommender systems to direct consumers towards certain products has grown in tandem with the popularity of online marketplaces. Despite their initial naiveté, these systems have grown into an invaluable asset for businesses, making it easier for customers to discover things they love.
