Although there are still major obstacles in the form of research gaps and privacy concerns, recommender systems do use data mined from user activity or hand-coded product knowledge to analyse databases. To tackle these difficulties, this paper presents a platform for E-Commerce Recommendation that is built on GNN. After tokenisation, lemmatisation, and snowball stemming were used for data preprocessing, dimensionality reduction, and feature extraction, PCA was applied. The next step was to build prediction models using GNN techniques and compare them to more traditional machine learning methods. The results showed that the proposed GNN model outperformed competing models in e-commerce recommendation tasks and related domains like cardiovascular illness prediction, reaching an accuracy of 96.35%. Although user privacy must be carefully considered in recommender system design, these results show that GNN-based architectures have the ability to improve recommendation accuracy, fill in research gaps, and make online shopping more personalised.

title: Enhancing Agricultural Pest Monitoring with AEC-YOLOv8n and EfficientNetV2-B0: A Hybrid Approach
authors: Rajasekaran Arunachalam, J Mohana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: In precision farming, identification of agricultural pests is essential and directly affects crop output, food security and sustainable agricultural practices. Pest monitoring procedures that have been traditionally used are often labor-intensive, inefficient and unsuitable in real-time applications, especially in low resource settings. The advanced pest detection system described in this paper was developed to eliminate these challenges through joint application of deep learning and adaptive optimization methods in an innovative manner. Having added the AFEM_SIE and Concat Weighting modules to enhance the spatial attention and feature fusion, the proposed model combines the AEC-YOLOv8n detector with EfficientNetV2-B0 as a feature extractor. The model, using the IP102 data and a selected sample of the three principal rice pests (stem borer, rice leaf caterpillar, and brown planthopper), has an accuracy of 99.01 and F1-score of 91.27, surpassing the existing benchmarking models. The system is optimized with an Improved Harris Hawks Optimization (IHHO) method to tune the hyperparameters of the models to enhance the generalization and faster convergence. The lightweight design of this framework and its low computational footprint allow it to be deployed on the edge devices with IoT capabilities, and hence permit real time detection of pests and response in intelligent agriculture environment. This research is a significant contribution to the efforts of precision agriculture in search of practical methods of pest management that can be used on large scale.

title: Title Page iii
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)

title: From Text to Ticker: A Comprehensive Survey and Methodological Guide to Named Entity Recognition in Finance
authors: Nilesh Vidhani, Gobi Ramasamy, Syam Mohan E
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The financial industry generates vast volumes of unstructured textual data from sources such as regulatory filings, news articles, social media, and earnings call transcripts. Extracting structured and actionable intelligence from this data remains a significant challenge. Named Entity Recognition (NER) is a fundamental task in natural language processing that supports this process by identifying and categorizing key information within text. However, the linguistic complexity, contextual ambiguity, and domain-specific terminology of financial text require specialized approaches that extend beyond general-purpose NER models. This paper presents a comprehensive survey and methodological guide to Financial Named Entity Recognition (Fin-NER). It begins by introducing the core concepts of NER while highlighting the unique challenges posed by financial text. The paper then reviews the evolution of Fin-NER approaches, ranging from rule-based systems and classical machine learning techniques to modern deep learning architectures. Furthermore, it analyzes the distinction between fine-tuned transformer-based models and general-purpose large language models in the current research. The study also examines commonly used datasets and evaluation metrics for benchmarking Fin-NER systems. Finally, it discusses key findings, existing methodological limitations, and future research directions, including hybrid modeling strategies, cross-lingual datasets, and the development of more reliable and explainable systems. Overall, this work serves both as a scholarly review of the Fin-NER field and as a practical guide for researchers and practitioners seeking to transform unstructured financial text into structured and informative representations.

title: An IoT and Machine Learning-based Digital Framework for Smart Agriculture: Crop and Action Recommendations
authors: Sathya J, V. Asha, A. Kalaivani, Sanuth Bibin B, Sharfaraj Kamalsab Gumnalli
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This paper presents a smart agriculture framework that integrates Internet of Things (IoT) sensing with machine learning techniques to support precision farming and sustainable agricultural practices. Environmental parameters such as temperature, humidity, rainfall, soil moisture, and soil pH are continuously captured using field-deployed sensors. Soil pH estimation is performed through image-based analysis using a Support Vector Machine (SVM) model. The collected data are processed using Decision Tree, Random Forest, and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithms to identify suitable crops and generate actionable recommendations, including irrigation scheduling, fertilizer management, and soil treatment. By functioning as a digital representation of the farm environment, the proposed framework enables real-time, data-driven decision-making that enhances crop productivity and efficient resource utilization.

title: Contrastive Dual-Prototypical Learning for Efficient Multi-Label Ophthalmic Diagnosis
authors: R.S.Naga Kumar Ganugula, Sri Phani Krishna karri
abstract: Early screening for ophthalmic diseases is a public health imperative for preventing irreversible vision loss. However, real-world fundus datasets often exhibit multi-label comorbidities and long-tailed distributions, inducing diagnostic bias in conventional deep learning models. This letter proposes the Contrastive Dual-Prototypical Learning (CDPL) framework to address these challenges. CDPL utilizes a two-stage curriculum: Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) pre-training to extract invariant features, followed by orthogonality-regularized dual-prototypical fine-tuning that enforces angular separation to decouple overlapping pathological signals. Evaluated on the ODIR dataset using a ResNet-18 backbone, CDPL achieves a 9.7% Macro-F1 improvement over standard benchmarks. Qualitative t-SNE and Grad-CAM analyses confirm the structural refinement of the metric space and superior sensitivity to rare tail-class pathologies. The framework is designed for extensibility to multi-label medical imaging domains characterised by class imbalance, with theoretical generalizability supported by the dataset-agnostic construction of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {L}_{\rm{ortho}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>.

title: Reliable and Energy-Efficient MAC Protocols in Industrial IoT Networks via Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Luciano Miuccio, Daniela Panno, Salvatore Riolo, Anita Schilirò
publish: IEEE Transactions on Machine Learning in Communications and Networking
abstract: The transition from wired to wireless communications in industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) networks introduces stringent challenges in terms of reliability and energy efficiency, aggravated by harsh propagation conditions and contention for a shared radio medium. These constraints require advanced medium access control (MAC) protocols capable of jointly managing channel access, packet retransmissions, and buffer operations while accounting for the battery limitations of IoT devices (IoTDs). This paper proposes a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework for the autonomous design of energy-efficient and reliable MAC protocols in uplink wireless IIoT networks supporting time–frequency multiplexing. Moving away from conventional decentralized partially observable Markov decision process (Dec-POMDP)-based MARL designs, the framework adopts a partially observable Markov game (POMG), thereby enabling per-device policy learning. A novel reward mechanism is introduced, in which the base station broadcasts a resource-level feedback, and each device constructs a local reward based solely on its own observations and past actions, ensuring feasibility in real deployments. Simulation results show that the proposed framework achieves maximum reliability, whereas state-of-the-art MARL benchmarks based on global rewards fail to meet the required target, highlighting the importance of POMG modeling and local reward structures for reliable wireless IIoT networks. Furthermore, a comparison with conventional grant-based protocols, which inherently achieve maximum reliability, demonstrates that the proposed solution significantly reduces the active-mode duration, thereby improving overall energy efficiency.

title: Dielectric Performance and Reprocessability of Boronic-Ester Vitrimeric Epoxy with Nanoscale Phase Separation_supp1-3685648.docx
authors: Liang Zou
abstract: Epoxy resins are widely employed as insulating materials in ultra-high-voltage (UHV) power equipment thanks to their excellent electrical properties, yet their permanently cross-linked networks are insoluble, infusible and difficult to recycle, and the electrical behavior of their dynamic-bonded counterparts remains poorly understood. Here, Bisphenol A diglycidyl ether (BADGE) was co-cured with a boronic-ester diamine and a flexible monoamine to generate a vitrimer network (EP-NBN) featuring 10 nm hard/soft periodic domains. The electrical behavior of EP-NBN was systematically evaluated at 20 ℃ to 60 ℃: Dielectric analysis and Havriliak–Negami fitting show that increasing temperature weakens interfacial polarization while activating local boronic-ester–related relaxation. The fitted apparent dc conductivity (σdc) decreases with temperature, accompanied by reduced low-frequency dielectric loss, due to diminished electrical contrast between hard and soft phases. Space-charge peaks at both electrodes decrease in magnitude and broaden with temperature. The current density-electric field (J-E) curves evolve from a two-regime lnJ-E behavior consistent with field-assisted interfacial hopping to an approximately single-slope behavior at 60 ℃. EP-NBN shows high AC breakdown strength (38.32 kV/mm) and retains 69.2 % of this value after reprocessing, outperforming the non-dynamic EP-ref. EP-NBN can also self-heal surface scratches and breakdown channels at elevated temperature. DFT calculations indicate a bipolar localization tendency in boronic-ester units, where electrons preferentially localize near boron sites and holes near O/N sites, providing a microscopic basis for interfacial trapping behavior associated with boronic-ester units, while the wide band gap (7.3 eV) supports the high intrinsic insulation level of the network. Overall, the microphase separation + boronic-ester dynamic bonds strategy enables high insulation performance together with reprocessability and self-healing.

title: CORE: Common Random Reconstruction for Distributed Optimization with Provable Lower Total Communication Costs_supp1-3677732.pdf
authors: Cong Fang
abstract: In this paper, we propose a new compression technique named Common randOm REconstruction (CORE), which can be used to compress the information transmitted between agents to reduce the total communication costs (TCCs) without other strict conditions. Previous communication compression techniques cannot ensure the improvement of TCCs due to the additional communication rounds caused by information distortion. CORE projects the vector-valued information to a low dimensional one through common Gaussian random variables and reconstructs the information with the same random noises after communication. We design varieties of CORE-based algorithms in different problem settings, including linear models, general convex optimization, and non-convex optimization, achieving provably lossless communication rounds and extremely lower TCCs. Specifically, for linear models, the CORE-based algorithm can encode the gradient vector into a lower constant dimension without increasing the number of communication rounds. For general convex and non-convex optimization problems, we characterize the TCCs by the effective dimension and prove the improvement beyond previous methods.

title: An INS/DVL/PS Integrated Underwater System Based on NLS-Transformer-LSTM Velocity Prediction Model
authors: Xinhui Zhang, Wenfeng Nie, Yangfan Liu, Yubo Li, Tianhe Xu
abstract: High-precision underwater navigation is a prerequisite for underwater vehicles to explore the marine environment and resources. Inertial navigation system (INS), Doppler velocity logger (DVL) and Pressure sensor (PS) onboard the underwater vehicles are favored for integrate due to their effectiveness and user-friendly configuration. Specifically, the velocity measurement from the DVL is important to reduce the accumulated error of the INS. Different from the traditional filter-based fusion solution, we firstly introduce a Factor graph optimization (FGO) framework to integrate the INS/DVL/PS navigation system. Since the DVL observations often experience outliers and outage due to the harsh sea condition and range limitation, it can lead to rapid position drift and error accumulation in the navigation system. Thus, we develop an outlier detection scheme based on the improved interquartile range method, which combines sliding window and dynamic threshold adjustment of the propeller revolutions per minute (RPM). Then, we propose a DVL velocity prediction method based on the nonlinear least squares (NLS)-Transformer-LSTM model. We refine the RPM by NLS and use it as one of the key features in the prediction model. Finally, the constructed system is compared with several classical Kalman filters through both simulated and measured experiments. The performance of the NLS-Transformer-LSTM model is comprehensively evaluated. The results indicate that the system can provide more accurate pseudo-DVL velocity estimates. This capability ensures more stable and reliable underwater navigation accuracy during DVL outages. Consequently, it offers strong support for autonomous underwater vehicle applications in complex marine environments.

title: Bio-Inspired Flower Robot for Emotional Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Qingyue Liu, Guojie Xu, Canrui Yang, Xunsheng Du, Igarashi Go, Hongqiang Wang
abstract: Mental health problems are common worldwide, but the available support systems and healthcare services are insufficient and inadequate. Social robots offer a promising solution to achieve social interaction and emotional connection. Nature-inspired architectural robotics can seamlessly blend into human daily life by reshaping future living environments. In this work, inspired by natural flowers, we designed a flower robot with a realistic morphology that simulates the opening-closing, rhythmic movements, and stem-bending movement. Building on the morphological design, we integrated a flex sensor into the petals to enable touch-induced tactile feedback, mimicking the touch-induced plant-like seismonastic movement. Furthermore, we incorporated voice interaction powered by a local large language model (LLM) to achieve adaptive intent understanding and personalized semantic engagement, thereby enhancing interaction flexibility compared to the repetitive tasks and rigid protocols of traditional social robots. Finally, the human-robot interaction (HRI) experiment demonstrated that the flower robot significantly reduced participants' negative emotions and provided positive social engagement. This work developed a bio-inspired flower robot, offering a promising pathway to social interaction and soothing presence.

title: Single-Motor-Driven Robotic Hand with Multiple Grippers using Gravity-Driven Torque-Path Switching Mechanism_supp1-3685935.mp4
authors: Toshihiro Nishimura

title: DRL-Assisted Null Space Aware (DNSA) Framework for MU-MISO ISAC Systems
authors: Amit Agarwal
abstract: This paper presents a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-assisted framework for joint communication and sensing in next-generation 6G systems. A novel DRL-assisted Null Space Aware (DNSA) approach is proposed for a multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) setup under practical power constraints. The proposed two-stage design employs Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to optimize beamforming for communication in the first stage. In the second stage, a radar sensing beam is constructed within the null space of the effective communication channel, ensuring interference-free sensing. A closed form solution for radar beamformer is derived. A dynamic power-splitting mechanism, controlled via a factor α, enables flexible trade-offs between sensing accuracy and communication throughput. Simulation results confirm that the DNSA framework achieves significant sensing gains (up to 18.6 dB) and high communication rates (up to 5.09 bps/Hz). A comparison with conventional techniques like Maximum Ratio Transmission (MRT) and Zero-Forcing (ZF) is also presented as baseline. Moreover, the learned beamforming policy adapts efficiently to spatial channel variations, and also could be easily modified for fairness based rate allocation offering intelligent resource allocation suited for 6G-enabled IoT environments. The simulation results further corroborate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Influences of bending degree on non-contact gap and pressure measurements by using eddyingized conductive polymer composite
authors: Luheng Wang, Zhen Gui
abstract: The advancement of flexible sensing technology has created a strong demand for reliable operation on curved surfaces, but characterizing and predicting sensor performance under bending remains a fundamental challenge. This study systematically investigates the influence of bending deformation on the impedance response of a flexible eddyingized sensor made from a carbon nanotube/silicone rubber composite. The research reveals that curvature significantly modulates the electromagnetic-structure interplay, altering both the magnetic coupling and the internal conductive network response across non-contact, loading, and relaxation stages. A larger radius of curvature enhances impedance magnitude variation but reduces impedance angle change, while also decreasing the critical impedance and DC resistance. By integrating these curvature-dependent features with an interpolation algorithm, a sensing methodology is established to decouple gap and pressure under arbitrary curvature conditions accurately. This work provides both mechanistic insight and a practical framework for curvature-adaptive flexible sensing, laying the foundation for high-performance soft robotics and bio-integrated devices operating on curved surfaces.

title: FI-StackNet: An Optimized Feature-Interaction Stacking Network for Advanced Fraud Detection
authors: Mostafa Gamal, Hanin Ardah, Mohammed Aly
abstract: Credit card fraud detection remains a challenging problem due to extreme class imbalance, anonymized feature representations, and the absence of reliable sequential dependencies in widely used benchmark datasets. Although deep learning architectures have been extensively explored, their structural assumptions are often misaligned with non-sequential, PCA-transformed tabular data. To address these limitations, this paper proposes FI-StackNet, an interaction-aware stacking framework specifically designed for highly imbalanced tabular environments. The proposed method integrates heterogeneous base learners, including Random Forest, XGBoost, CatBoost, and Logistic Regression, and constructs a decision-level representation from their probabilistic outputs. Unlike conventional stacking approaches that rely on implicit or linear aggregation, FI-StackNet explicitly models pairwise interactions between base learner predictions, enabling the capture of complementary agreement, disagreement, and confidence patterns. This interaction-enhanced representation is then processed using a lightweight neural meta-learner, achieving a balance between expressive modeling and computational efficiency. Extensive experimental evaluation using stratified 5-fold cross-validation demonstrates that FI-StackNet achieves a superior F1-score of 86.63% with low variability (±0.35), outperforming strong ensemble baselines such as CatBoost (85.27% ± 0.45) and advanced deep learning models including CNN-BiLSTM (83.96% ± 1.78). Additional benchmarking against multiple deep learning architectures further confirms that FI-StackNet provides a more balanced and stable performance across evaluation metrics, particularly in highly imbalanced settings. Scalability analysis shows strong data efficiency, achieving competitive performance with limited training data and converging to optimal performance as data increases. Moreover, cost-sensitive evaluation on real transaction data indicates a 21.7% reduction in estimated financial loss compared to CatBoost, primarily due to improved control of false negatives. The framework also maintains low inference latency (4.8 ms per transaction), supporting real-time deployment. These results demonstrate that interaction-aware decision-level stacking represents a principled and effective advancement over conventional ensemble and deep learning approaches, offering a robust, stable, and cost-efficient solution for fraud detection in non-sequential tabular data.

title: Fringe projection profilometry method based on dual-epipolar and phase constraint
authors: Limei Song, Zongyang Zhang, Haozhen Huang
abstract: This letter proposes a stereo matching method based on dual-epipolar constraint (DEC) and phase neighborhood constraint (PNC). Epipolar constraints are established according to the spatial relationship among the left camera, projector, and right camera, which narrows the search range between left and right images with low computational cost. Fine matching is achieved using phase neighborhood constraint to quickly obtain high-precision 3D data. This method enables an average improvement of over 40% in the overall computational speed of stereo matching. In the reconstruction experiments of the standard sphere and checkerboard, the obtained root mean square errors (RMSE) are 0.023 mm and 0.018 mm, respectively. The PNC method reduces the RMSE induced by the DEC method by 99.19% and 98.10%, respectively.

title: Development of a Low-Cost Flex-Sensor and Machine Learning Based Sign Language Interpreter
authors: Adeyinka Ajao Adewale, Wou Onn Choo, Ebubechukwu I. Onwudiegwu, John S. Wejin, Aitimbetov Maksetbay, Stephen Aderibigbe
abstract: Over the years, sign language has been a paramount approach for bridging the communication gap between people with normal hearing and those with hearing challenges, thereby achieving inclusive education and social integration. With the diversity of modern-day gestures and body positions during communication, developing automated Signed Language Interpreters (SLIs) and recognition systems is complex and challenging. Thus, scientists and researchers worldwide are developing simpler and more affordable SLI systems. This research addresses the issue of complex, unaffordable, and ineffective communication between hearing- and hearing-impaired communities by developing a wearable SLI using a flex-sensor-based glove and a machine learning algorithm. The primary aim is to assist individuals with hearing impairments by converting American Sign Language (ASL) finger spelling into text. The study focused on ASL due to its extensive research base, global reach, and historical ties with Nigerian sign language. The methodology involved designing and integrating flex sensors into a glove to capture hand gestures, which were then processed using a machine learning algorithm to interpret the ASL finger spelling. The hardware components included flex sensors, a NodeMCU-32S LUA development board (based on the ESP32 microcontroller), and an MPU6050 accelerometer. At the same time, the software implementation utilised Python and the Arduino Integrated Development Environment (IDE) for data acquisition and model development. The results demonstrated the system's effectiveness in accurately interpreting ASL finger spelling, with significant improvements in training and test accuracy over multiple epochs. The system achieved satisfactory performance, indicating its potential for real-world applications in assisting communication for people who are deaf or hard of hearing. The implications of an accuracy of 92% indicate that a low-cost wearable device can interpret sign language correctly, bridging communication gaps for deaf people, thereby enhancing SLI availability and enabling hearing-challenged individuals to interact more freely and confidently across various social settings.

title: Covert Knowledge Poisoning Attacks in Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation
authors: Yu Zhang, Miao Chen, Xinlei He, Tianshuo Cong, Ke Xu, Qi Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation (RACG) systems enhance code generation by dynamically integrating examples retrieved from open knowledge bases, but their reliance on external sources exposes them to knowledge poisoning. While prior attacks inject explicit vulnerable code, such payloads are easily detected, limiting their real-world impact and failing to probe the full attack surface. To overcome this limitation, we propose Arachne, a covert knowledge poisoning attack that, for the first time, achieves fine-grained control over vulnerability types while evading detection. Arachne relies on two mechanisms: (1) Benign-Appearing Fragment Construction, where vulnerable code is decomposed into benign-appearing fragments that pre serve compilability and contextual cues, rendering them effectively undetectable by vulnerability analyzers; and (2) Retrieval Driven Knowledge Completion, in which retrieved fragments activate the large language model's (LLM) contextual reasoning to autonomously generate complete vulnerable code. We evaluate Arachne on eight mainstream LLMs and four retrievers across four common vulnerability types. On GPT-4, Qwen2.5, Gemini 3-flash, Claude-4-sonnet and DeepSeekCoder, Arachne achieves attack success rates exceeding 70% in most settings for each vulnerability type across CWE-78, CWE-295, CWE-367, and CWE-614, peaking at 100% for CWE-367. Arachne demonstrates up to a 37% improvement in attack success rate over existing poisoning attacks. In two real-world applications, the attack achieves 87% success rate with 70% benign utility, indicating that the generated code often satisfies user requirements while introducing vulnerable code. Critically, our poisoned samples, which are designed without explicit malicious patterns, fully bypass rule-based analyzers and challenge state-of-the-art LLM based detectors, exemplifying their stealth among multiple failed defense paradigms. Our findings expose critical limitations in existing defense frameworks, highlighting the urgent need for novel defense mechanisms specifically designed for RACG systems.

title: RideAgent: An LLM-Enhanced Optimization Framework for Automated Taxi Fleet Operations_supp1-3685621.pdf
authors: Wei Qi
abstract: Efficient management of electric ride-hailing fleets, particularly pre-allocation and pricing during peak periods to balance spatio-temporal supply and demand, is crucial for urban traffic efficiency. However, practical challenges include unpredictable demand and translating diverse, qualitative managerial objectives from non-expert operators into tractable optimization models. This paper introduces RideAgent, a Large Language Model (LLM)-powered agent framework that automates and enhances electric ride-hailing fleet management. First, an LLM interprets natural language queries from fleet operators to formulate corresponding mathematical objective functions. These user-defined objectives are then optimized within a Mixed-Integer Programming (MIP) framework, subject to the constraint of maintaining high operational profit. The profit itself is a primary objective, estimated by an embedded Random Forest (RF) model leveraging exogenous features. To accelerate the solution of this MIP, a prompt-guided LLM analyzes a small sample of historical optimal decision data to guide a variable fixing strategy. Experiments on real-world data show that the LLM-generated objectives achieve an 86% text similarity to standard formulations in a zero-shot setting. Following this, the LLM-guided variable fixing strategy reduces computation time by 60.22% compared to solving the full MIP with only a 2.96% average optimality gap. Moreover, this variable fixing strategy outperforms five cutting plane methods by 42.3% time reduction with minimal compromise to solution quality. RideAgent offers a reliable and scalable automated framework for objective modeling and accelerated optimization. This framework empowers non-expert fleet operators to personalize operations and improve urban transportation system performance.

title: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology

title: Constitutional Challenges in AI-Driven Public Health Emergency Management in India
authors: Deval Vashishtha, Bhupnesh Kumar
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The speed at which AI is being applied for managing public health emergencies has been studied, particularly for surveillance, predictive modelling, and automatic triage. There were many examples of AI based emergency responses developed and implemented in India for the purpose of responding to the COVID-19 pandemic under emergency laws. These emergency responses allowed states to quickly respond to an emergency situation by providing them with greater capability, but also raised numerous constitutional issues related to privacy, equity, due process, and democracy. The purpose of this article is to provide a conceptual analysis of the role of AI in public health governance, using the framework of the constitution. This study will be done through the lens of Indian case law, international standards for human rights, and comparative regulations used in other countries to identify deficiencies in the legislative protection, institutional oversight, and public participation in the development and implementation of AI systems. This study provides a rights-based approach to governance grounded in the constitutional principles of legality, proportionality, and accountability to ensure that the application of AI during emergencies does not compromise the value of democracy.

title: ProLiBatt: An AI-Powered System for Predicting Lithium-Ion Battery Health in Electric Vehicles
authors: Vinay Kalluri, Nithik C Reddy, Diya Ponnarian, Sudhakiran Ponnuru
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The lithium-ion battery accurate estimation of State of Health (SoH) is critical for ensuring safety, reliability, and cost efficiency in electric vehicles and grid storage. In this work, we proposed a hybrid framework that integrates Random Forest– based feature selection with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) temporal predictor, designed to capture both nonlinear feature importance and long-term degradation trends. The method is evaluated on the NASA and Oxford datasets, covering multiple chemistries and usage profiles. Our model achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.7 % ± 0.2 and a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 3.1 % capacity under cross-cell validation, reducing error by up to 18 % compared with the best baseline (Gradient Boosting). Furthermore, we validate generalization by training on one chemistry (NMC) and testing on another (LFP), where the model retains an R2R^2R2 above 0.92. Runtime analysis shows inference latency of under 12 m/s on an ARM Cortex-A53 with less than 3 MB memory usage, demonstrating feasibility for embedded Battery Management Systems. These results highlight that hybrid machine learning approaches, when combined with careful feature engineering, can deliver accurate and deployable SoH estimation for next generation energy systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence Driven Congestion Aware Routing Framework for Next Generation Communication Networks
authors: T. Saravanan, Yousuf Hazaimeh, S.Agnes Shifani, Jayashree N, V. Manasa, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: With the exponential rise in connected devices and data-heavy applications, traditional routing protocols struggle to efficiently manage congestion in next-generation communication networks. In this paper I introduce a congestion-aware routing framework (AI-CARF) that was developed based on Artificial Intelligence (AI) to proactively control the impact of congestion through a multi-layered intelligence framework. The framework includes Graph Signal Processing to encode topology, Topological Data Analysis to identify anomalies using persistence, and Neuroevolution to develop context-based routing capabilities. Moreover, Fuzzy Reinforcement Learning model is used to interpret quality-of-service measures, whereas Multi-Agent Deep Q-Learning helps to make decentralized and real-time decisions. Knowledge distillation is done in a simulated digital twin environment, which enables lightweight deployment of the model in edge devices. The model was experimented with various synthetic and real-world traffic topologies and demonstrated a congestion-aware routing quality of 96.42 which is better than the conventional AODV and DSR protocols in terms of packet delivery ratio, delay, and jitter. Findings verify the strength of AI-CARF when a load is varied, a node failed and topology changed dynamically. The framework is a breakthrough towards autonomous, scalable and adaptive routing in ultra-dense communication networks like 6G, IoT mesh networks, VANETs and satellite communications, which will enable intelligent infrastructure optimization in network generations to come.

title: Hybrid K-Means–Logistic Regression Framework for Automatic Resume Short-Listing and Job Matching in HR Systems
authors: Afreen Bari, K Kalyani, Prakash Ranjan Parida, K. Anju Aravind, Anuradha, Naveen Nandal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Companies get thousands of applicants for a single position in today's competitive employment market, which makes manual short-listing laborious, irregular, and sometimes difficult for HR staff. We created a Hybrid K-Means–Logistic Regression Framework that functions as a recruiter's smart assistant in order to tackle this bigger problem. After reading each CV, the algorithm uses K-Means to group similar profiles together before applying Logistic Regression to precisely match each applicant with the best position. The model is better able to understand both broad trends and small shifts in expertise, experience, and domain relevance thanks to this layered approach. In comparison to current approaches, the system produced quicker decisions, more accuracy, and more reliable candidate-job suggestions when evaluated on actual resume collections. This hybrid model provides a practical, adaptable approach that adds intelligence, equity, and efficiency to contemporary HR recruiting systems by combining supervised prediction with unsupervised neural networks.

title: Scalable Financial Strategy Validation using Distributed Data Architectures
authors: Pramath Parashar
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The highly competitive nature of financial markets necessitates robust methods for validating automated trading strategies. This paper presents an approach to build an efficient and extensible backtesting platform leveraging distributed data processing tools. Addressing the substantial computational demands of historical market data, the implemented system utilizes a big data framework for scalable storage and rapid processing of time series. Beyond validating trading strategies, the architecture supports potential extensions for real-time operations and enhanced functionalities.

title: Crop Recommendation System using Confidence-Weighted Ensemble Optimization: A Comprehensive Multi-Objective Approach for Sustainable Precision Agriculture
authors: Pranav Ramachandra Hegde, V. Srinivasan, Bharath M V, Prajapati Darshan Jayeshbhai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This study presents a novel crop recommendation system for precision agriculture, addressing limitations of existing machine learning approaches. The proposed system employs a Confidence-Weighted Ensemble of five models: Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and Gaussian Naive Bayes. It integrates a Multi-Objective Optimization framework balancing yield, water efficiency, and profitability. Tested on 2,200 agricultural samples across 22 crops from diverse Indian agro-climatic zones, the model achieves 98.86% accuracy with high stability (0.83% std dev). The ensemble outperforms the best single model by 0.45% and engineered meta-features improve accuracy by 1.36%. With an inference time of 0.20 seconds and a 403 MB footprint, the system is suitable for resource-constrained settings. It facilitates informed decisions, potentially increasing yields by 15-25% and reducing water use by 20-30%.

title: Greener Web: Minimizing the Carbon Footprint of Web Applications
authors: Kavinmathi V, A. Shanthakumari
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Web applications have become an emerging issue in relation to the environment as digital services continue to grow at an alarming rate. Despite websites and online platforms are not seen as big obstacle, they draw a lot of consumption of energy by means of data centers, communication networks and the devices people use. In this paper, the carbon footprint of the current web development has been discussed and suggests a design that is sustainable in order to cut down emissions. By analyzing and improving massive web performance features, including page size, resource loading, server hosting, and caching plans, this paper gives a presentation of how energy consumption can be empirically compared and cleared out on a user-by-user basis. A case study comparison is carried out through audit tools to examine the amount of carbon emitted by a standard and an optimized version of a sample site. The results indicate the use of environmentally friendly design. Not only these practices reduce the environmental impact, but also delivers a more user-friendly experience by making web pages more mobile efficient. This paper offers useful advice to developers, designers, and organizations that intend on developing more sustainable digital systems that are compliant with world environmental objectives.

title: Privacy-Preserving Coordinated Scheduling of Distributed Resources Using ADMM and Tianji’s Horse Racing Optimization
authors: S. Sankara Prasad, Kammari Tharunsai, Sampangi Sai Kumar, Vele Jaya Krishna
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The accelerating electrification of transport and integration of distributed renewables are reshaping modern distribution networks, where uncoordinated scheduling of electric vehicle (EV) aggregators, energy storage systems (ESS), and distributed generators (DG) can compromise voltage stability, increase network losses, and distort operational economics. This paper introduces a distributed scheduling framework that integrates the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) with Tianji’s Horse Racing Optimization (THRO) to achieve scalable and privacy-preserving coordination among multiple flexible resources. In the proposed approach, ADMM decomposes the global problem into independent quadratic sub-problems solved locally by EV, ESS, and DG agents, while a THRO-based projection at the distribution system operator enforces true AC power-flow feasibility without relying on linearized or convexified approximations. Battery degradation costs are explicitly modeled to ensure economically realistic scheduling decisions. Extensive simulations on a benchmark 15-bus feeder demonstrate that the ADMM–THRO framework eliminates voltage violations, reduces network losses significantly compared to convex-projection baselines, and delivers robust convergence under nonlinear operating conditions. These results confirm that coupling metaheuristic projection with distributed optimization provides a technically faithful and economically sound solution for coordinated scheduling in next-generation distribution grids.

title: Soft Computing Techniques for Real-Time Financial Fraud Detection in Online Transactions
authors: Sanjay Padav, Turki Meften Saad, Taha Raad Al-Shaikhli, Iyad Waad, Sadiq Juma, Ahmad Fadhil
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of digital payment systems and online financial services, the risk of financial fraud has considerably increased. Traditional rule-based systems are often not sufficient for detecting detailed and evolving patterns of fraud in real-time. To deal with such problems, this paper will suggest the application of soft computing methods - fuzzy logic, artificial neural networks (ANNs), support vector machines (SVM), and genetic algorithms (GA) - to come up with intelligent and strong systems of fraud detection in electronic transactions. The suggested system is based on the idea that learnability, flexibility, and tolerance to imprecision of soft computing methods are used to process transaction records and detect outliers of fraudulent action. The hybrid system is created by incorporating the neural networks to perform the pattern recognition, the use of fuzzy logic to manage the uncertainty, and tuning the parameters by genetic algorithms. The given system is trained and tested on real-life sets of financial transactions with much consideration to the unbalanced distribution of classes and the aspects of performance needs in real-time. Results of the experiments show that the model developed works better than the traditional machine learning models in terms of detection accuracy, precision, recall, and response time. The hybrid model has been effective in identifying the known and new types of fraud, which indicates that the model can be used as an efficient solution in preventing financial fraud in real-time. This study highlights the significance of soft computing in developing intelligent, scalable, and adaptive fraud detection systems capable of evolving with changing threat landscapes in the fintech ecosystem.

title: Design and Development of a Smart Wrist Band for Remote Rehabilitation and Vital Sign Monitoring
authors: Bethanney Janney. J, Jeffin Jose, Janne Chetan, Angeline. J, Manjari. S. V, Raghupathi Prahala
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: This work proposes the design and development of a smart rehabilitation band that integrates biosensors with advanced features, wireless communication, and therapeutic assistance. The proposed system includes a blood pressure sensor with the ability to measure systolic, diastolic, and pulse values in real time, therefore allowing for continuous monitoring of a patient's vital signs. A Global System for Mobile Communication module is used for sending automated alerts in cases of physiological abnormalities to the healthcare provider for immediate medical response. An OLED display on the wearable facilitates the live visualisation of health parameters directly by the user, therefore fostering better user engagement and motivating patients toward their rehabilitation process. The therapeutic mode in the smart band leads patients through exercises of wrist movements that are vital for physical recovery. The architecture includes an Arduino Nano (Atmega328P) that will handle the operation of sensors and motors, and the ESP32 module that will provide wireless connectivity for seamless wireless transmission of data. This architecture uses a two-controller system to enable efficient sharing of tasks and ensure reliable communication with external monitoring platforms. The proposed smart band combines continuous physiological monitoring, real-time alerts, and interactive rehabilitation assistance to offer a comprehensive solution for post-surgical recovery. It is particularly useful in remote or underserved areas where frequent clinical visits are difficult, thus enhancing access and efficiency of rehabilitation services. Monitoring blood pressure is a safety and risk-reduction feature, particularly for stroke patients and older adults undergoing unsupervised rehabilitation at home.

title: Simulation Study of Data Skew in Federated Learning for Tuberculosis Detection
authors: Masoodhu Banu N. M, Jehosheba Margaret M, P Vanitha, Madhumithaa. S, Thiyam Deepa Beeta
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Deep learning (DL) plays a major role in the automation area, from robotics to the medical field. However, it suffers from two issues, one is the need for a huge amount of data, and the other is explainability. Though the DL models failed to give explanations, the recent explainable algorithms provide detailed explanations, and hence DL can be used for automation. In the medical field, it is impossible to get more data due to the restrictions imposed by regulatory standards. The objective of this paper is to reduce the dependency of the number of medical images required for the DL network training using Federated Learning in order to increase the accuracy. This work simulates collaborative work on the same DL model environment by generating multiple clients with data dissimilarity among them, and also investigates the impact of multiple data dissimilarities, such as the number of data available with each client and machine-dependent data dissimilarity. The results of sample size skew illustrate that the proposed FL framework achieves a global accuracy of up to 95.39% within 2 communication rounds, closely approaching the centralized model accuracy of 98.7%, while maintaining consistent performance across additional rounds. With an increasing number of clients with equal or unequal datasets, the accuracy is 97%, which is the same as independently using the dataset with the same model. As it increases the privacy of the dataset and the model developer owns their model, the FLDL technique will rule the future in the field of diagnostic tool automation.

title: Stridesync: A Multisensory Framework for Movement Monitoring and Response
authors: Smritti Bala R, Deborah, Pushpa B
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Accurate monitoring of biomechanical parameters with precision is essential in rehabilitation and mobility training to facilitate effective recovery, prevent reinjuries. Conventional walkers primarily provide mechanical support and lack the ability to yield quantify posture, grip stability, or gait coordination. Therefore, the development of the smart walker system, Stridesync, is designed to enhance rehabilitation through real-time sensing and feedback. The system integrates force sensors mounted on the walker handles, with an accelerometer module to monitor variations in motion and assess the stability of an individual's locomotion. A haptic unit provides feedback in the form of vibration to the user when walking assistance is active and deviation in posture or an imbalance occurs. Governed by a microcontroller that processes sensor data and transmits the output both visually and tactually via a digital display interface for therapist's evaluation, this prototype continuously monitors biomechanical changes, effectively corrects posture, and facilitates safe movement training. Therefore, the Smart Walker constitutes an economical, sensor-enabled rehabilitative assistive device.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Human Embryo Developmental Stages from Time-Lapse Imaging
authors: Carolina Jennifer. S, Saranya S, Chandana Mohanty, Remya Raj
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Although IVF success rates have remained inconsistent and largely reliant on subjective morphological embryo evaluation, time-lapse imaging technology emerged with prospects for objective morphokinetic analysis based on embryo developmental stage evaluation. Analyzing morphokinetics with time-lapse imaging technology in this study aims to automate embryo development stage assessment into <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> distinct morphokinetic phases using computer vision algorithms on time-lapse imaging videos. The proposed deep learning model is based on a ResNet-50 neural network serving as a vision embedding model pre-trained on an image recognition dataset called ImageNet, focusing on spatial image representation based on individual image frames rather than vision based solely on image elements. The model accuracy in classifying embryo developmental phases using a transform model focused on self-attention analysis is tested with a classweighted focal loss function to cope with class imbalance in a standard IVF embryo developmental stage assessment dataset with a total of 704 embryo developmental phases labeled in a publicly accessible online competition. The individual model performance accuracy in assessing embryo developmental phases can be measured by accuracy of 0.6359 and balanced accuracy of 0.5359. The individual model performance can be measured by macro-average metrics including precision of 0.5315, recall of 0.5359, and F1-score of 0.5273 with Cohen's Kappa statistic of 0.5955 with a level of being in moderate agreement. The model performance in assessing embryo developmental phases in early frequently observed phases such as tPNa with F1-score of 0.9004 and receiver operating characteristic curve accuracy of 0.9924, showing better performance in frequently observed developmental phases.

title: Impact of AI-Powered Recommendations on Consumer Satisfaction and Buying Behaviour in E-Commerce
authors: Karthick K K, Lakshmi K K, V. Ganesan, Arul Kumar Natarajan, M. Sivaraman
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has transformed digital commerce, with AI-powered recommendation systems significantly shaping consumer behaviour. These systems guide product discovery, influence purchasing decisions, and enhance overall consumer satisfaction, making them crucial for businesses aiming to improve engagement and remain competitive. This study aims to examine the relationship between brand and platform loyalty driven by AI recommendations, the influence of AI recommendations on impulse buying, and consumer satisfaction with AI-powered recommendation systems. By analysing these factors, the study provides insights into how AI personalization affects consumer responses in e-commerce. A quantitative research design was adopted, collecting data from 211 online consumers with prior experience using AI recommendations. A structured questionnaire with a five-point Likert scale captured information on loyalty, impulse buying, and satisfaction. Data were analysed using SPSS, and multiple linear regression tested the hypothesized relationships. The results indicate a statistically significant model, showing that both brand and platform loyalty and AI-driven impulse buying positively affect consumer satisfaction, with brand and platform loyalty being the strongest predictor. This highlights the importance of consistent, relevant, and reliable AI personalization in fostering trust and engagement. The study concludes that AI-powered recommendation systems not only stimulate immediate purchases but also strengthen long-term consumer satisfaction and loyalty. These findings offer practical guidance for e-commerce platforms and marketers to optimize AI-driven strategies while maintaining a consumer-focused approach.

title: Federated Learning in Healthcare: A Survey of Privacy-Preserving Medical AI Solutions
authors: Ritu, Sakshi Koli, Nagendar Yamsani, Ankur Dumka
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Learning (FL) is a promising approach for collaborative Medical AI while maintaining patient privacy. This study surveys the use of FL in radiology, genomics, and electronic health records. The (More) effective methods for model aggregation (such as FedAvg and FedProx), differential privacy, and secure multiparty computation were surveyed. Cases from multi-hospital collaborations were used to demonstrate how FL facilitates learning without centralizing data sharing. Challenges of data heterogeneity, communication cost, and model convergence are discussed in the review. Comparative experiments demonstrate that FL can achieve close-to-centralized performance and preserve data protection requirements, such as HIPAA and GDPR. We focus on tools such as NVIDIA Clara, TensorFlow Federated, and PySyft. Future work will include federated reinforcement learning, personalization of FL models, and combination with blockchain to enable secure and auditable AI pipelines in clinical settings.

title: Real Time Agriculture Monitoring and Communication System using Video Streams and IoT Networking
authors: A. Prasanth, E. Rajini, E. Meghana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The need for optimal resource consumption and real-time monitoring in the field has significantly led to the growth of smart agriculture technologies. The Internet of Things (IoT) provides an environmental parameter to monitor and support the concept of data-driven agricultural-related decisions. This paper describes the design of a real-time agriculture monitoring and communication system. It combines environmental parameter monitoring through IoT technology and data processing via cloud technology to emphasize the concept of precision agriculture. The proposed design uses the concept of sensor technology to monitor the critical parameters of the environment such as the moisture content, the temperature, and the humidity levels. The parameters are then sent to the cloud platform for real-time monitoring. In addition to the sensor-based monitoring, the system allows for real-time observation of the field conditions for enhancing the situational awareness that helps in making timely decisions.

title: Utilizing Multi Layer Perceptron (MLP) to Enhance Stability Analysis of Geotechnical Engineering
authors: Lixia Li, Peng Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: In the study of geotechnical mechanics, slope stability is always a key problem, which is directly related to the design and construction of the whole project. Conventional methods of stability calculation have some shortcomings, such as low precision, long time consuming, unable to deal with the complex relationship between complex models, etc. In this project, Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) is taken as the research object, and the corresponding geotechnical parameters and characteristics are introduced to model and predict the MLP model. This project will make use of the relation and nonlinear mapping function of multi-layer neural network to describe the complex relation and rule of rock mass structure better, and improve the calculation precision and efficiency. Through the training and testing of MLP, it is found that MLP has higher accuracy and predictability in stability study, and can evaluate the stability, deformation and bearing capacity of soil (rock) more accurately. Compared with the conventional numerical simulation results, the MLP numerical simulation results can reach 95.9%. The results show that it is feasible to use multi-layer sensors for slope stability analysis. MLP model is used to analyze the stability of slope, which makes it more scientific and accurate, and provides a new and efficient analysis method for safety problems in geotechnical engineering. The research of this project will play an active role in promoting the development of geotechnical mechanics in China.

title: Stress Detection Using Physiological Signals and Machine Learning
authors: V. Subha Ramya, Bethanney Janney. J
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Stress is a critical physiological and psychological condition that, if left undetected, could result in severe health complications, namely cardiovascular disorders, depression, cognitive impairment, reduced productivity. Advancements in wearable sensing technologies and artificial intelligence have enabled continuous monitoring of stressrelated physiological changes, making automated stress detection more practical and reliable. This paper offers comprehensive review of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches utilized for stress prediction using biomarkers, namely Electrocardiogram (ECG), Galvanic Skin Response (GSR), Respiration Rate, Heart Rate Variability (HRV), multimodal sensor data. A generalised methodological pipeline is proposed, which includes physiological signal acquisition, feature extraction, preprocessing in time and frequency domains, classification utilizing intelligent models such as Random Forests (RF), Support Vector Machines (SVM), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) networks. Literature studies demonstrate that performance of stress detection systems significantly relies on quality of physiological data, feature representation, and model architecture. The review also highlights recent advancements, including remote photoplethysmography-based stress analysis, transfer learning, and hybrid fusion models that improve robustness in realworld conditions. This work provides insights into state-of-theart (SOTA) techniques, existing limitations, upcoming research opportunities to facilitate development of efficient real-time stress monitoring systems for personalised healthcare and wellbeing enhancement.

title: Hybrid Multilevel Thresholding for Skull Base Tumor Segmentation and Classification Using an Improved YOLOv8s Model
authors: M Ranjith Kumar, Yadeesh T, Kishore A G
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The very early and precise identification of skull base tumors is key to better prognosis and treatment planning. In that complex anatomic area, tumors present diagnostic issues due to their variable presentation and proximity to critical neural and vascular structures. Current manual methods for segmentation and classification from MR and CT scans are time-intensive, subjective, and observer-dependent. Automated methods exist but still struggle with poor boundary definition, distinguishing tumor from adjacent healthy tissue, and boundary and performance discrepancies with different imaging and tumor types. These poor performance boundary discrepancies lead to diagnostic delays and poor patient outcomes. To overcome these obstacles, we introduce a fully automated, hybrid framework that integrates classical image analysis and deep learning. Using Multi-Otsu thresholding for accurate tumor region segmentation, we optimize precision before classification. The derived segments are then processed by a tuned medical-grade YOLOv8s augmented with attention layers and structural enhancements to better capture fine tumor details. On a large skull base tumor dataset, the model achieved strong results (mAP @ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50=0.8362$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 50-95=0.6499$</tex>, classification accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=99.41 \%$</tex>), proving efficient and clinically compatible. This work contributes an accurate, stand-alone, and computationally efficient tool to assist radiologists and neurosurgeons in rapid, reliable skull base tumor diagnosis, supporting timely and precise clinical management.

title: RegEase: Simplifying Insurance Compliance
authors: Samhitha Poreddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Regulatory compliance is not just necessary, but a transformative journey for organizations in highly regulated industries such as insurance, finance, and healthcare. The traditional approach to compliance reviews, often manual, can hinder progress, leading to inefficiencies and increased risk. In this paper, we introduce RegEase, an innovative AI-driven solution that revolutionizes the regulatory compliance landscape. Our framework empowers users by automatically extracting text from regulatory documents, intelligent summarization of compliance obligations, and semantic retrieval of regulations using customizable metadata. This tool frees compliance officers at insurance companies from laborintensive tasks, enabling them to focus their energy and creativity on shaping and implementing effective compliance policies.

title: Deep Learning Based Vegetable Price Forecasting for Nepalese Markets: An STL Attention LSTM Approach
authors: Pranaya Nakarmi, Nabin Lamichhane, Nabaraj Subedi, Janak Kumar Lal, Pravin Poudel, Bikrant Poudel
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Agricultural price volatility poses significant challenges to food security in developing economies. This paper proposes a hybrid deep learning architecture that combines Seasonal-Trend decomposition using local Estimated Scatterplot Smoothing (STL) with attention-enhanced Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for daily vegetable price forecasting. The STL component explicitly decomposes price series into interpretable trend, seasonal, and residual patterns, while the attention mechanism automatically identifies temporally important observations. The model integrates 11.5 years of historical price data from Kalimati market with meteorological variables and cultural event indicators. Evaluation on five major vegetables demonstrates strong predictive capability with mean coefficient of determination of 0.833 and mean absolute percentage error of 15.67%. Analysis of the attention weights further demonstrated that the vegetable price forecasts made by the LSTM and Attention model rely heavily upon data from vegetables traded on the market approximately 18-22 days prior to the forecast day, which confirms that the LSTM+Attention model is best suited to detect relatively short-term trends in the agricultural market.

title: AI-Powered Health Monitoring Using IoT Devices and Wearable Technologies for Continuous Patient Assessment
authors: Sanjay Kurkute, Jai Singh W., R Sujitha, Ajitesh Kumar, Keerthika J, Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Continuous remote patient monitoring is becoming critical with the increasing burden of chronic diseases, but existing wearable-based patient monitoring systems are limited in terms of accuracy and false alarms and face challenges with heterogeneous sensor data integration within the real world settings. To overcome these difficulties, an AI-based health monitoring system based on multimodal IoT and wearable signals is studied in this paper for continuous cardiac monitoring. The goal is to create a powerful and low-latency privacy-aware system that is able to reliably detect events in ambulatory environments. The methodology takes a combination of edge preprocessing to happen, uncertainty aware multimodal fusion, personalized model-adaptation and a hybrid clinician-AI labeling pipeline. Experiments using big data (600 subjects, 9 months follow up) demonstrate substantial performance improvements over 5 state of the art baselines for 0.92 sensitivity, 0.96 specificity, 0.89 PPV and 0.12 false alarms for patients per patient-day with clinically feasible latency. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the combination of signal quality awareness fusion and adaptive personalization, which contributes to the robustness and clinical relevance in the real world. Overall, the study demonstrates evidence that AI-driven multimodal monitoring can help to move forward the standard of care for patients through proactive care and help support early detection of clinically actionable events.
