To address this, this paper proposes a lightweight graph-based consensus algorithm (LCAG) for the Internet of Things (IoT). LCAG studies low-level consensus mechanisms. The algorithm adds an access control table to reduce communication latency. It also quantifies how nodes achieve consensus within distributed systems. Experiments show that LCAG requires less time during node consensus than both classical and practical Byzantine fault-tolerant algorithms.

title: An Adaptive interval-constrained Tucker Decomposi-tion for Efficient Multilevel Fast Multipole Algorithm
authors: Haohui Ge, Yang Liu, Sheng Sun, Hanyu Li, Haijing Zhou
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: To enhance far-field tensor storage efficiency and computational performance in the multilevel fast multipole algorithm (MLFMA), we propose (i) an adaptive interval-constrained Tucker decomposition that reformulates rank selection under an error interval ϵ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">lower</sub> [ϵ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">lower</sub>, ϵ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">lower</sub>] to avoid overly conservative truncation and eliminate the manual tolerance adjustments, and (ii) a slice precomputation strategy that converts runtime Tucker contractions into matrix–vector products without repeated reshaping overhead to accelerate Tucker-compressed tensor–vector multiplication. Across five PEC-sphere cases, the method achieves 8.0–11.5:1 compression under 0.80-1.00 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> tensor-level relative error and delivers 2.3–6.3× speedup in aggregation and 4.9–7.9× speedup in disaggregation.

title: Prescribed Performance based Alpha-Matrix Adaptive Model-free Control for Operational Quadrotor with Manipulator
authors: Haoping Wang, Jiang Guo, Yang Tian, Ding Ding, Nicolai Christov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The article addresses the development of α-matrix model-free adaptive control based on prescribed performance (α<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</sub>-PPAMFC) for manipulating the position and attitude of an operational quadrotor with manipulator. The system is composed of a quadrotor and a two-degree-of-freedom (2-DOF) manipulator, and it functions in the presence of friction and wind disruption. Unlike conventional time-delay estimation (TDE) based methods that rely on a fixed scalar gain α, the proposed approach addresses the fundamental limitation of fixed-gain TDE in time-varying multi-input multi-output (MIMO) systems by introducing an online adaptive α-matrix. Specifically, the controller integrates intelligent PD control with TDE (TDE-iPD) for lumped disturbance estimation, a forgetting factor recursive least squares (FFRLS) algorithm to adaptively update the full α-matrix in real time, and prescribed performance control (PPC) to enforce transient and steady-state error constraints. This synergistic combination enables the controller to dynamically sense and adapt to changes in the system's structural dynamics caused by manipulator motion, thereby overcoming the performance degradation and potential instability inherent in fixed-gain designs. Finally, Lyapunov function is used to analyze the stability of the entire control system. co-simulation results demonstrate that the proposed α<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</sub>-PPAMFC achieves a superior tracking performance, reducing the root mean square error (RMSE) of position tracking by over 30% compared to the TDE-iPD controller under internal uncertainties and external wind disturbances. Compared to the MFTSMC controller, it reduces RMSE for position tracking by over 10% and achieves better dynamic performance for attitude tracking.

title: When Steganography Meets Semantic Communication: An Authorizable Data Transmission Paradigm
authors: Boyu Han, Gaolei Li, Xiao Yang, Jun Wu, Jianhua Li, Yuchen Liu
abstract: Recent advances in Semantic Communication (SemCom) have integrated semantically aware encoding and channel adaptation to enhance wireless transmission efficiency. In SemCom, covert communication is extensively adopted to secure data transmission, but current methodologies remain constrained by signal-level obfuscation strategies that rely on bit-level manipulations. Consequently, they are loosely coupled with semantic feature learning and often face a rigid trade-off between covert robustness and undetectability in practice. To address these limitations, we fuse steganography with SemCom to propose an authorizable SemCom framework, embedding the true transmission data within cover inputs for covert delivery. The integration focuses on achieving highly covert secret transmission within the semantic communication architecture. In our framework, the private transmitter embeds true (i.e., secret) data into cover inputs using a secret hiding module, then applies semantic encoding for transmission. The receiving ends ensure covert data accessibility in multi-client scenarios: authorized users with the secret decoding module can fully semantically restore the true data, whereas unauthorized users solely reconstruct the apparent cover details and stay unaware of the hidden data. Moreover, to realize the above functionalities, the proposed SemCom system leverages a dual-objective joint training strategy, simultaneously learning shared knowledge of stealthy transmission and preserving the normal capability for regular data. Empirical results prove that our framework attains high-fidelity SemCom transmission for secret (PSNR $\sim 31$ dB, SSIM $\gt 91$%, and LPIPS $\sim 0.04$), and holds robustness under multiple adaptive and adversarial attack scenarios. Meanwhile, the embedded true data manifests no perceptible visual artifacts, and SemCom regular input efficacy is consistently preserved.

title: DL-based Robust Learning Fuzzy Tracking Control with Vibration Suppression for Flexible Morphing Hypersonic Vehicles under Dynamic Flight Environments
authors: Jin-Feng Zhang, Jun-Wei Wang, Huai-Ning Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper deals with the issue of output tracking control with fuselage's elastic vibration suppression for a flexible variable wingspan hypersonic vehicle (FVWHV) under dynamic f light environments. The rigid-flexible coupled flight dynamics of the FVWHV is described by a coupled model of ordinary differential equations and the beam equation. To figure out the above control issue, a DL-based robust learning fuzzy tracking control algorithm is developed in this work. Specifically, a DL based adaptive reduced-order coupled model is constructed based on a deep auto-encoder and sparse identification of nonlinear dynamics to approximate the rigid-flexible coupled flight dynamics. This reduced-order coupled model is periodically updated in a block-wise manner using the high-dimensional fuselage's elastic vibration measurement data. Then, a robust learning fuzzy tracking control algorithm is proposed based on the adaptive reduced-order coupled model. The corresponding control performance is also analyzed. Finally, extensive experiments are presented to illustrate the effectiveness and superiority of the theoretical algorithm.

title: Optically Navigated Surgical Robotic System for Automated In Situ Bioprinting in Confined Anatomical Spaces_supp3-3680970.mp4
authors: Jinwu Wang
abstract: Robot-assisted in situ bioprinting allows for the direct deposition of biomaterials at defect sites, offering a minimally invasive alternative to traditional scaffold implantation. Yet, its clinical translation remains hindered by limited accessibility in anatomically constrained regions and the lack of precise control. To address these challenges, this study introduces optical navigation from computer-assisted surgery into in situ bioprinting to achieve explicit image–patient registration and translate anatomical localization into accurate robot motion. An optically navigated active remote center of motion (RCM) strategy is further developed to support constrained motion in confined anatomical spaces. The system was validated through autonomous in situ printing of a two-layer scaffold on a femoral defect phantom using a six-degree-of-freedom robotic arm. Quantitative evaluation demonstrated a target registration error of 0.583 mm and a maximum RCM distance error of 0.052 mm, indicating accurate localization and constrained motion. The printed scaffold exhibited high geometric fidelity based on multiple structural metrics. These results highlight the precision and reliability of the proposed framework and its potential for autonomous in situ bioprinting in deep or subdermal environments.

title: Feature-Based Retinal Image Analysis for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection using Optical Coherence Tomography Angiography and Fundus Imaging Techniques
authors: Vanitha Devi K L, Shreenidhi N, Vaideeshwaran P, G. Kavitha
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is one of the major causes of preventable blindness and timely detection is essential. Manual screening is time-consuming and subjective. This work presents an AI-based system for retinal vessel and lesion features analysis and severity grading of Diabetic Retinopathy using Fundus images and detection of Retinal Neovascularisation (RNV) using Optical Coherence Tomography Angiography (OCTA). The IDRiD and DRIVE datasets were used for fundus analysis. Images were preprocessed with green channel extraction, Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalisation (CLAHE) and gamma correction. A two-stage segmentation pipeline was employed namely: U-Net for vessel extraction and a fine-tuned U-Net with ResNet-50 encoder for detecting lesions. The predicted masks when compared with the ground truths, achieved Dice scores of 0.81 for vessels and 0.74 for lesions. Vascular and lesion features were then extracted and classified using XGBoost, achieving an accuracy of 95.5%. For OCTA, TUH dataset and DRAC 2022 dataset were used. Vessel segmentation using Full-Resolution Network (FRNet) achieved a Dice score of 0.801. Vascular biomarkers were computed from the segmented vessel masks. A modified U-Net was used for the detection of RNV. The proposed multimodal framework has the capacity to facilitate large-scale screening by reducing the need for subjective grading, particularly in resource limited areas.

title: SEGA-Net: LLM-Guided Semantic-Enhanced GAN Augmentation Network for Low-Resolution Image Classification
authors: Mohammad Shahedur Rahman, Mohammad Tahmid Bari, Md Delwar Hossain, Roya Choupani, Erdogan Dogdu
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Low-resolution image classification (e.g., CIFAR and Tiny-ImageNet at $32 \times 32$) faces persistent challenges due to limited contextual diversity, overfitting, and vulnerability to adversarial perturbations. Conventional augmentations provide only superficial variability, while recent LLM-guided diffusion approaches, though semantically rich, suffer from computational inefficiency and resolution mismatch. To address these challenges, we present SEGA-Net, a novel semantic-enhanced augmentation framework that bridges this gap through two key innovations. First, SEGA-NET introduces a lightweight text-tolatent projector that maps LLM-derived prompts to the latent space of a frozen class-conditional GAN, enabling efficient and semantically controlled $32 \times 32$ image synthesis. Second, it implements a security-aware filtering pipeline combining CLIP-based semantic alignment, SSIM de-duplication, and blur detection to ensure that only high-fidelity, label-consistent samples are used for training. Operating in three stages, (i) LLM-guided prompt generation, (ii) projector-steered GAN synthesis, and (iii) quality-filtered augmentation, SEGA-NET achieves substantial improvements across five benchmarks (CIFAR-10/100, SVHN, Tiny-ImageNet, and Fashion-MNIST) and four architectures (ResNet, MobileNetV2, VGG-16, and ViT). Compared to conventional augmentations, SEGA-Net delivers $\boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{8} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 0 \%}$ gains in clean accuracy and adversarial robustness (FGSM, PGD, BIM, AutoAttack), while reducing generation cost by $6.7 \times$ relative to diffusion-based methods. These results demonstrate that semantic-guided synthesis with explicit quality control significantly enhances robustness and efficiency for resourceconstrained vision tasks.

title: Monitoring and Mitigating Data Drift in Batch-based Machine Learning Systems using Adaptive Retraining
authors: G.Bharath Kumar Reddy, G.Charan Kumar Reddy, P.J.Beslin Pajila
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Machine learning models deployed often exhibit a loss of performance when they are used in non-stationary settings, where data distributions change with time. This is referred to as data drift, which is a major issue with batch-based systems, where delayed feedback may cause early indicators of a poorer model to be obscured. In this paper, a batch-based adaptive learning system will be introduced, and it will incorporate statistical drift detection and performance-based monitoring to detect both covariate and concept drift. The selective sliding-window retraining approach is adopted to retrain models using recent batches of data but preventing future data leakage. The proposed framework is tested on an actual credit-risk dataset and compared to the strategies of retraining the baseline and the other drift-adaptation methods. The effectiveness of the proposed approach in terms of performance stability during a drift, the absence of excessive retraining overhead, and the achievable deployment latency is proved by experimental results. The paper also examines sensitivity to window-size, overhead of deployment, and limitations intrinsic to it, and the paper notes the applicability of this framework into application in the real-world setting of batch-processing.

title: Predictive Machine Learning Models for Optimizing the Financial Close Process
authors: Puneet Thakkar, Rahul Chawla
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Within the financial organizations, financial close remains a process that consumes a lot of resources and missioncritical processes. However, performance on these affected by ongoing issues such as longer close cycles, reconciliation bottlenecks, rates of error, and gaps of compliance readiness, even with large investments in ERP systems and process automation. In order to maximize financial closing results in a variety of ways, this study suggests an integrated machine learning-based predictive analytics architecture. Four KPIsClose Cycle Time, Reconciliation Effort and compliance quality)-were employed to judge the quality of the work of regression models, such as linear regression, kernel-based Support Vector Regression, tree-based ensemble models, and neural network architectures. The top-performing model, which result in an R-Squared value of 0.9739 in error prediction and 0.8646 for days to close, and demonstrate the superiority are Multilayer Perceptrons with radial basis function, and Support Vector Regressors. Automation level, team experience, and manual journal entries are key predictive factors based on permutation-based feature significance analysis.

title: Development of a Unified Resilience Metric for Microgrids and Its Improvement Through Building Energy Management Systems
authors: Mohammadreza Fathizadan, Mahesh S. Illindala
abstract: The increasing frequency and severity of natural disasters driven by climate changes have caused widespread power disruptions and prolonged outages globally. As a result, improving power system resilience has become a major research focus. This paper introduces a Unified Resilience Metric (URM) that provides a consistent and intuitive quantification of system resilience, independent of event type. Additionally, a microgrid Energy Management System (EMS) using Building Energy Management System (BEMS)-informed demand profiles is developed to enhance operational performance during major disruptions. Using a modified IEEE 13-bus system as a case study, the study evaluates the proposed framework based on established reliability indices and the introduced resilience metric. Simulation results demonstrate that integrating BEMS significantly improves microgrid performance by enabling dynamic load shedding, reducing total curtailed load, and enhancing system recovery. Specifically, the proposed framework reduces load shedding by up to 6.5 MWh and lowers operating costs by as much as $27,000 over a 24-hour period under contingency scenarios, while achieving a 3.74% improvement in the proposed resilience metric and notable reductions in standard reliability indices. These improvements not only strengthen technical resilience and reliability but also contribute to the overall social welfare of the community.

title: OpenStack Release Evolution: Enabling Practical Upgrade Choices for Cloud Operators Through Benchmarking
authors: Rekha Jain, Vansh Agarwal, Narendra Kumar Meena
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: OpenStack is a widely adopted open-source cloud computing platform, where rapid release cycles and evolving architecture introduce significant challenges for operators planning system upgrades. In this work, we systematically analyze the evolution of OpenStack from Austin (2010) to Epoxy (2025), focusing on the practical implications of upgrading across major releases. We conduct empirical benchmarking of instance lifecycle operations using Rally, and employ an M/M/1 queueing model to estimate provisioning delay and headroom under increasing load. Our results reveal that while recent releases such as Caracal exhibit modest performance overheads with instance boot times increasing from 10.71 s (Queens) to 12.57 s (17% increase) and modeled system response times rising from 23.05 s to 33.80 s, they introduce stronger security and automation compared to earlier versions. Notably, Rocky demonstrates the highest operational overhead (16.14 s boot time, 83.52 s modeled system time under λ = 0.05 req/s), while Caracal achieves a balance between performance and advanced functionality. The study provides actionable insights to guide practitioners in making informed upgrade decisions, balancing new feature adoption with operational risks and system complexity.

title: Large Language Models for Explainable and Self-Service Business Intelligence in Large Enterprises
authors: Ajith Suresh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Business intelligence (BI) in large enterprises refers to the systematic process of collecting, integrating, analyzing, and interpreting organizational data to support strategic and operational decision-making. Traditional enterprise BI systems rely heavily on predefined dashboards, static reports, and manually authored Structured Query Language (SQL) queries, which ensure governance and reliability but require technical expertise and limit flexibility for non-technical users. As data volumes and analytical demands increase, these approaches struggle to provide accessible, interactive, and explainable analytics at scale. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have introduced new possibilities for natural language-driven analytics; however, existing LLM-based BI solutions often lack semantic grounding, governance enforcement, and explanation fidelity, rendering them unsuitable for deployment in large enterprise environments. This study proposes a semantic-constrained LLM architecture leveraging Generative Pre-trained Transformer 4 (GPT-4) to enables explainable and self-service BI while preserving enterprise analytical correctness and control. The approach integrates GPT-4-based natural language understanding and explanation generation with an enterprise semantic layer and symbolic query planning, ensuring alignment with enterprise-defined metrics, schemas, and aggregation rules. The proposed system is evaluated using the AdventureWorks enterprise data warehouse. Experimental results demonstrate a query correctness of 94.6% and semantic validity of 97.8%, indicating reliable interpretation of natural language analytical requests. Explanation faithfulness reaches 95.2%, and explanation quality achieves an average score of 4.5 out of 5, supporting transparent and interpretable analytics. A self-service success rate of 92.1% further confirms effective support for non-technical users with interactive response times. This study paves the way for the adoption of LLM-enabled, explainable, and governance-aware BI systems in large enterprises, enabling scalable and trustworthy natural language analytics.

title: An Explainable Machine Learning Based Clinical Decision Support Framework for Early Heart Disease Risk Stratification
authors: Shubham Gupta, Suhaib Ahmed
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases are still the primary cause of mortality globally, and there is a necessity of credible and clear clinical decision support system to assist in risk identification at an early stage. Although machine learning methods have proved to be very strong predictors of heart diseases, their usage in clinical practice has been hindered by the lack of understanding and decision-oriented results. To overcome those issues, this paper describes an interpretable machine learning-driven clinical decision support system to risk-stratify patients with early heart disease by using structured clinical information. The framework incorporates Shapley Additive explanations (SHAP) with ensemble-based predictive modeling to give the right predictions and explanations of features (transparent). Various machine learning models (Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, and XGBoost) are tested based on standard performance measures. The experimental data indicate that the XGBoost model has very high performance with an accuracy of 93.2, precision of 93, recall of 92, F1-score of 92, and AUC of 0.96. Analysis of the confusion matrix shows that the false-negative rate is low, and this makes it applicable in clinical screening applications. Moreover, the risk stratification mechanism based on probability classifies patients into low risk, medium risk and high-risk groups, which allows making actionable clinical decisions beyond binary classification. The analysis done using global SHAP determines the clinical features, such as the type of chest pain, the highest heart rate reached, and exercise-induced angina, as important risk prediction factors. The findings indicate that the suggested framework is capable of balancing predictive performance, interpretability, and clinical usability to become one of the promising methods of reliable AI-assisted cardiovascular risk assessment.

title: Automated Vulnerability Scanning and Bug Detection for Secure Web Applications
authors: Atharva Kekare, Vedaant Chaudhari, Vidhya R.
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Automated vulnerability identification is essential for protecting digital systems since web applications are increasingly vulnerable to cybersecurity threats. This paper introduces a web security assessment method that uses automated URL-based scanning to find vulnerabilities that match the OWASP Top 10. In addition to identifying software flaws that could compromise dependability, the method combines rule-based detection with structured logging to identify important threats including SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), resource-intensive Dynamic Application Security Testing platforms, is especially well-suited for early-stage security assessments and instructional use because it is built for quick analysis with minimal computational overhead. Live bug detection words on HTTP traffic to encourage responsible testing, and non-intrusive scanning techniques are used to evaluate HTTPS destinations. To preserve report integrity, scan findings are kept as safe, timestamped documents. This study enhances web application resilience against changing cyberthreats and fortifies automated auditing procedures.

title: Reliability Assessment of Early Exit Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Georgios Konstantinidis, Maria K. Michael, Theocharis Theocharides
abstract: Machine learning inference deployed on edge devices is subject to limited resources, which pushes for better energy and resource efficiency, while ensuring high performance. At the algorithmic level, dynamic Deep Neural Networks (dDNNs) have been proposed in an effort to improve performance and energy efficiency which is particularly useful in time-critical systems such as autonomous navigation and search and rescue operations. These systems are often deployed in harsh environments, making the hardware vulnerable to soft errors. This work investigates the reliability of such dDNNs, specifically convolutional, focusing on early exit approaches, in an effort to better comprehend the impact of the early exits on the overall reliability of dDNNs. We consider transient faults and use hardware-aware software fault models, combined with a fine-grained fault taxonomy, to derive failures-in-time (FIT) rate and assess reliability. We observe a new category of faults specific to dDNNs and we refer to them as non-materialized faults. In our assessment, we use a new fault taxonomy that is more fine-grained than existing ones, also including the new category of non-materialized faults. We explore various early exit scenarios, having as baseline the Google Inception V1 network deployed on an NVDLA accelerator to generate FIT rates and analyze the faults per network architecture block and operation type. Furthermore, we investigate the impact of faults on the overall performance (accuracy) and execution time (average inference time) of the neural network models. Based on our analysis we observe that the overall FIT rate remains similar among different early exit implementations (8.40-8.89), while the distribution of the critical faults is influenced by the network’s design-time decisions on early exit block placement and architecture.

title: Memory-Bounded A/RED: Scalable Active Detection of Rare Relevant Events in Indefinite Length Streams
authors: Rohan Loveland, Nathaniel Clark, Jason Gentile, Christopher Ritter
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: We present a memory-bounded, real-time Anomalous/Relevant Event Detection (A/RED) system for indefinitely long data streams. Extending prior work, our algorithm maintains bounded memory using a circular buffer of queried points and accelerates nearest-neighbor search via ball trees, reducing complexity from $O\left(n^{2}\right)$ to $O(\log n)$ per point. Small clusters are periodically merged to control model size, while a tunable paranoia parameter $\kappa$ regulates query rate. Evaluated on synthetic (NICE), projected (MNIST-2D), and high-dimensional (EMNIST) streams, A/RED achieves near-perfect relevant recall and query precision on well-separated data, and significantly outperforms random querying on challenging real-world distributions discovering rare relevant classes with minimal user burden. This work enables scalable, human-in-the-loop anomaly detection in continuous, high-volume sensor environments.

title: Effects of Displacement Damage on the Structural and Electrical Properties of Ferroelectric Hafnium Zirconium Oxide Capacitors
authors: Samantha T. Jaszewski, Colin McKay, David R. Hughart, John M. Cain, Zachary T. Piontkowski, Toai Ton-That, Anthony McDonald, Megan K. Lenox, Jon F. Ihlefeld, M. David Henry
abstract: This study investigates the effects of displacement damage induced by heavy ion irradiation on the structural and electrical properties of ferroelectric hafnium zirconium oxide (HZO) capacitors and thin films, establishing displacement-damage limits and decoupling the electrical degradation from irradiation-induced phase transformation by comparing films with different starting phase constitution. Using 6 and 8 MeV Au<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2+</sup> ions, the radiation tolerance of HZO capacitors was assessed through polarization-voltage and capacitance-voltage measurements. The findings reveal that significant losses in remanent polarization do not occur until high displacements per atoms (DPA), around 10-2 DPA. While lower DPA values allow for partial recovery of polarization through voltage cycling, the highest levels result in irreversible degradation. Structural characterization via grazing-incidence X-ray diffraction and Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy indicates that the HZO films containing only the metastable phases maintain their phase stability, with no significant amorphization or phase transformations, even at the highest DPA levels. However, films containing the equilibrium monoclinic phase experience phase transformations, likely due to increased oxygen vacancies which destabilize this phase. Photoluminescence spectroscopy provides evidence of a significant change of the defect populations in the irradiated samples. The introduction of oxygen vacancies during irradiation is identified as a primary factor contributing to the degradation of ferroelectric properties due to their ability to pin ferroelectric domains. This work underscores the critical role of phase composition in determining the radiation tolerance and performance of HZO capacitors and demonstrates that these devices are promising candidates for radiation environments due to their high intrinsic radiation hardness.

title: LPM-Aug: Latent Pathology-Informed Multimodal Augmentation for Generalized Cognitive Decline Detection Via Speech
authors: Xiaobao Wang, Bin Wen, Xiao Wei, Yuqin Lin, Kai Li, Yaru Cao, Tianrui Wang, Mingyang Gu, Yu Jiang, Jinyu Li, Longbiao Wang, Jianwu Dang
abstract: Speech-based cognitive decline detection is a promising non-invasive screening approach, yet its practical application remains challenging, particularly in modeling the complex affective-behavioral shifts associated with cognitive impairment in low-resource settings. Existing methods frequently exhibit unintended reliance on non-pathological biases, including speaker identity, transcription errors, and model-induced artifacts, which leads to shortcuts that are specific to particular datasets and tasks rather than stable pathology-focused representations. In this work, we propose LPM-Aug, a latent pathology-informed multimodal augmentation framework that aims to weaken the influence of such unintended factors while preserving disease-related acoustic and linguistic cues. Rather than indiscriminately increasing data diversity, the proposed approach encourages pathological-focused representation learning across audio and text modalities and incorporates complementary pathological cues to enhance speech-based modeling of cognitive decline. Experiments on three public datasets covering different languages and cognitive stages demonstrate that LPM-Aug consistently improves robustness and generalization compared with unimodal and non-augmented baselines.

title: Cybersecurity in eHealth: A Scoping Review of Current Research and Trends
authors: Michael Winter, Robin Kraft, Pascal Leber, Manfred Reichert, Helmut Greger, Johannes Mühr, Rüdiger Pryss
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Healthcare ranks among the most vulnerable sectors to cybersecurity threats, experiencing widespread security incidents and substantial data breach costs. Despite growing global cybersecurity expenditures, health care systems remain distinctively vulnerable. This paper presents a scoping review of research on cybersecurity in eHealth. Following PRISMA-ScR guidelines, we searched and analyzed literature across four major databases: ACM Digital Library, IEEE Xplore, PubMed, and Web of Science. From an initial 3,146 identified papers, 567 met inclusion criteria and underwent detailed analysis. The analysis iden tifies blockchain as the most researched technical solution, with system and communication protection dominating cybersecurity categories. Geographically, research output has shifted significantly toward India and China. However, the study highlights critical gaps in real-world implemen tations, user-centric perspectives, and evaluation studies. This comprehensive review offers insights for researchers, healthcare professionals, and policy makers to develop targeted, evidence-based cybersecurity strategies that protect sensitive health data and ensure the integrity of digital health systems.

title: Optimized Triple Phase Shift Variable Frequency Modulation for Single Stage SR-DAB based DC-AC Converters with Wide ZVS Range Operation
authors: Fangyong Wang, Junyang Bao, Yan Mao, Bangyin Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The single-stage series-resonant dual-active-bridge (SR-DAB) DC-AC converter, serving as a critical interface connecting DC sources and the AC grid, is often constrained by a narrow zero-voltage switching (ZVS) range and severe circulating current losses, while also requiring the capability to handle varying grid voltages and load fluctuations. To mitigate these limitations, this paper proposes a real-time, analytical Triple-Phase-Shift Variable Frequency Modulation (TPSVFM) strategy. This optimized scheme employs a real-time computational framework that synergizes a simplified analytical model with high-fidelity correction, thereby balancing computational feasibility with control precision. Additionally, a complementary Filtering Compensation Feedforward (FCF) strategy is introduced to suppress output current distortion and optimize system efficiency across the entire operating range. The proposed methodology ensures high flexibility and efficiency across the entire active and reactive power plane, enabling seamless bidirectional power transfer without complex auxiliary controls. Validation using a 500-W experimental prototype confirms the efficacy of the proposed approach, demonstrating a peak efficiency of 98.3%.

title: Multi-Carrier Modulation: An Evolution from Time-Frequency Domain to Delay-Doppler Domain
authors: Hai Lin, Jinhong Yuan, Wei Yu, Jingxian Wu, Lajos Hanzo
abstract: The recently proposed orthogonal delay-Doppler division multiplexing (ODDM) modulation, which is a delay-Doppler (DD) domain multi-carrier (DDMC) modulation scheme based on the DD domain orthogonal pulse (DDOP), is studied. We first revisit the linear time-varying (LTV) channel model for the wireless channel, and review the conventional multi-carrier (MC) modulation schemes and their design guidelines for both linear time-invariant (LTI) and LTV channels. We then focus on the representation of the LTV channel in an equivalent sampled DD (ESDD) domain, and propose an impulse-function-based transmission strategy for the ESDD channel. Next, we take an in-depth look into the DDOP and show that it achieves orthogonality with respect to the fine time and frequency resolutions in the ESDD domain thus behaves like an impulse function. This allows us to unveil the unique input-output relation of the resultant ODDM modulation over the ESDD channel. We point out that the conventional MC modulation design guidelines based on the Weyl-Heisenberg (WH) frame theory can be relaxed without compromising its orthogonality or violating the WH frame theory. More specifically, for a practical communication system with bandwidth and duration constraints, MC modulation signals can be designed considering so-called local or sufficient (bi)orthogonality, which refers to the (bi)orthogonality among a WH subset for the MC signal within a specific bandwidth and duration. This is different from the conventional MC modulation waveform design guidelines (such as for orthogonal frequency division multiplexing and orthogonal time frequency space) based on the global (bi)orthogonality, which is the (bi)orthogonality among a WH full set corresponding to the MC signal occupying the entire TF domain. This novel design guideline could potentially open up opportunities for developing future waveforms required by new applications such as communication systems associated with high delay and/or Doppler shifts, as well as integrated sensing and communications.

title: Introducing Graph Attention Network to Improve Wang Zhengfei Semi-Empirical Model for Accurately Simulating Forest Fire Spread in Western Guizhou Province
authors: Yuying Gong, Jingyi Wang, Mingshi Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate simulation of forest fire spread is crucial for enhancing emergency response. Currently coupling the Wang Zhengfei model with Cellular Automata (CA) is regarded as an effective method for the simulation, but the rigid neighborhood rules inherent to CA often limit the ability to flexibly adapt to non-linear fire propagation. To overcome this defect, this paper utilized six major forest fires in western Guizhou province taking place on February 20, 2024, as a case study. We developed an improved model by training a Graph Attention Network (GAT) on historical Guizhou fire incidents to adaptively adjust the CA's neighborhood rules, utilizing the Wang Zhengfei spread rate as a key input feature. The fires were simulated and validated against the Sentinel-2 derived 20 meter resolution burned patches via calculating a spatial agreement (SA) measure. Additionally, the largest fire underwent dynamic assessment using the vegetation damage patterns (dNDVI) derived from high-frequency Gaofen-4 (GF-4) satellite observations. Results showed that SA measures from the GAT-improved model ranged from 0.713 to 0.990, much higher than those from the original Wang Zhengfei-CA model ranging from 0.095 to 0.773. And the dynamic simulation of the largest fire among the six was relatively consistent with the evolutionary patterns of vegetation damage characterized by GF-4 satellite observations derived NDVI changes. Overall, the GAT-improved model is a potentially effective framework for depicting the spatio-temporal evolution of forest fires in Guizhou province, providing a reliable tool for targeted forest fire prevention and suppression.

title: Intelligent Diagnosis Method for Open Circuit Faults in On-Board Chargers Based on Adaptive Attention Convolutional Residual Network
authors: Jingzheng Li, Xidong Zheng, Zhiwen Zhao, Tao Jin, Mohamed A. Mohamed
abstract: The rapid deployment of electric vehicles (EV) is accelerating the electrification of transportation. During peak demand periods, EV can feed power back to the grid through onboard chargers (OBC), enabling vehicle-to-grid (V2G) interactions and enhancing grid flexibility. Consequently, accurate diagnosis of open-circuit (OC) faults in OBC is essential to ensure the reliable operation of EV and the safe functioning of the power grid. To address the complex and nonstationary characteristics of OC fault signals in OBC, this paper proposes an intelligent diagnostic model based on an adaptive attention convolutional residual network (AACRN). The proposed model integrates depthwise separable convolution (DSC) for efficient local feature extraction and residual network (ResNet) structures for deep hierarchical representation learning. Furthermore, a convolutional block attention module (CBAM) is incorporated to adaptively emphasize fault-relevant features while suppressing redundant information, thereby improving feature discrimination and enabling accurate fault localization. In addition, the model's interpretability is enhanced through a unified framework that integrates t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) visualization, attention heatmaps, and shapley additive explanations (SHAP) analysis. This framework highlights fault-relevant regions and quantifies key feature contributions, thereby enhancing the transparency and reliability of fault diagnosis. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a diagnostic accuracy of 96.54% under noise-free conditions and maintains an accuracy of 93.21% under real electromagnetic interference (EMI) in physical tests, confirming its strong anti-interference robustness in practical engineering scenarios.

title: Context-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination Framework for Intelligent Electric Vehicle Charging Optimization
authors: Muddsair Sharif, Huseyin Seker, Yasir Javed
abstract: The accelerating transition toward electric mobility presents unprecedented challenges in coordinating distributed charging infrastructure across multi-stakeholder ecosystems. This paper introduces a novel Context-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Dynamic Resource Allocation (CAMAC-DRA) framework that addresses these challenges through intelligent integration of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). Our framework enables autonomous coordination of 250 electric vehicles (EVs) across 45 charging stations while dynamically adapting to real-time environmental conditions including weather patterns, traffic dynamics, grid fluctuations, and electricity pricing mechanisms. The proposed system processes 20 contextual features through sophisticated attention mechanisms, implementing hierarchical coordination protocols that balance competing objectives across five key stakeholder groups: EV users (25% weight), grid operators (20%), charging station operators (20%), fleet operators (20%), and environmental entities (15%). Through comprehensive validation using real-world datasets encompassing 441,077 charging transactions from diverse operational scenarios, the CAMAC-DRA framework demonstrates substantial improvements over state-of-the-art baseline algorithms including Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG), Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (A3C), Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), and conventional GNN approaches. Experimental results reveal exceptional performance metrics: 92% multi-agent coordination success rate, 15% energy efficiency improvement, 10% operational cost reduction, 20% grid strain mitigation, and 2.3× faster convergence compared to existing methods, while maintaining 88% training stability and 85% sample efficiency. Real-world validation confirms commercial viability with a Net Present Cost of -$122,962 and 69% cost reduction through strategic renewable energy integration. The framework’s unique contribution lies in synthesizing context-aware adaptation with multi-stakeholder optimization, achieving unprecedented balance between competing objectives while maintaining real-time responsiveness. These advances position the Smart2Charge application as a transformative solution for sustainable transportation electrification, addressing critical scalability and coordination challenges in next-generation smart grid ecosystems. The demonstrated capabilities establish new benchmarks for intelligent EV charging coordination, with significant implications for urban energy management, Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology deployment valued at $25.5 billion by 2029, and the broader transition toward carbon-neutral transportation infrastructure.

title: Prioritizing Feature Requests from App Reviews in Agile Mobile Software Development: A Mixed-Methods Empirical Study of Practitioners’ Perspectives
authors: Mansour Albidah, Noureddine Abbadeni, Khalid Almutib
abstract: Agile mobile software development increasingly relies on user feedback from app store reviews to inform requirements and feature request prioritization. While prior research has explored requirement elicitation and classification from user reviews, limited empirical evidence exists on how practitioners prioritize feature requests derived from such feedback in real-world agile mobile development contexts. This paper presents a mixed-methods empirical study combining a large-scale survey of 169 practitioners and semi-structured interviews with 12 practitioners to examine current practices, decision criteria, and challenges in prioritizing feature requests from app reviews. The study investigates (i) the factors influencing prioritization decisions, (ii) commonly used prioritization strategies, and (iii) how different types of feature requests are handled in practice. The results indicate that business value, implementation cost, and technical risk are the most influential prioritization factors, while contextual elements such as app store platform, organizational context, and regulatory constraints further shape decision-making. Ranking-based and classification-based components are frequently used in combination, and enhancement-related feature requests are typically addressed before other types. Although practitioners report interest in automation and machine learning support, concerns related to data quality, contextual interpretation, and trust limit their current adoption. These findings provide empirical insights into how app review feedback is translated into prioritization decisions and offer guidance for designing context-aware prioritization strategies in agile mobile software development.

title: HITE: A Hierarchical Intrinsically Interpretable Trust Evaluation Framework for Industrial IoT
authors: Meng Zhang, Yuliang Cheng, Changyu Xi, Hangyu Wang, Tong Cui
abstract: With the rapid evolution of the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), dynamic trust evaluation for substantial industrial devices has emerged as a critical defense line for IIoT security. Although Deep Learning (DL) models are widely adopted due to their superior performance, their inherent “black-box” nature causes severe feature entanglement, hindering the establishment of trustworthy decision-making grounds. To mitigate this opacity, Explainable AI (XAI) paradigms—primarily Post-hoc Explanation and Intrinsic Interpretability—have been introduced. However, existing approaches face a critical dilemma: post-hoc methods merely approximate model behaviors via input perturbations, often suffering from low fidelity and approximation errors in high-dimensional IIoT scenarios; traditional intrinsic models guarantee transparency but often fail to capture complex nonlinear patterns, leading to suboptimal detection accuracy. To reconcile this trade-off, we propose HITE, a Hierarchical Intrinsically Interpretable Trust Evaluation framework. Uniquely, this framework leverages the additive structure of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KAN) to achieve explicit additive decomposition of raw features. By synergizing with structured decision trees, HITE constructs a hybrid reasoning chain characterized by transparent representation and explicit decision logic. It provides end-to-end traceable explanations across four dimensions: feature mapping, representation attribution, decision path, and outcome scoring. Experiments conducted on two real-world industrial benchmarks, X-IIoTID and ToN-IoT, demonstrate that HITE achieves superior evaluation performance, attaining accuracies of 99.31% and 98.96%, respectively. Crucially, HITE outperforms mainstream explanation methods in terms of faithfulness and stability, realizing a critical paradigm shift from “Numerical Prediction” to “Trusted Reasoning”.

title: DistShield: Distribution Preserving Model Obfuscation for Real-Time TEE-Shielded Secure Inference on IoT devices
authors: Qinglin Song, Gaojian Xiong, Yu Sun
abstract: While on-device inference avoids network latency and private data uploading in IoT, the risk of model thefts has raised serious concern. As a solution, state-of-the-art approach obfuscates critical parameters and shields de-obfuscate keys in TEE, ensuring model confidentiality with minimal overhead. However, we reveal that existing methods lead to anomalous clustering in distribution, undermining the anonymity of protected parameters. Based on this vulnerability, we demonstrate a model stealing attack which could recover over 97% of the model performance without any queries or training, severely compromising the model security. Additionally, existing methods struggle to scale for large language models. To address these challenges, we propose DistShield, which leverages distribution preserving obfuscation to generate obfuscated parameters with no anomaly. Moreover, to minimize the inference latency and adapt to large language models, iterative weight pruning is tailored to precisely narrow down the range of critical parameters. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves robust model security by protecting only 0.013% of the parameters, leading to a 10× reduction in model stealing attack accuracy, with only 11% additional TEE computation latency introduced. DistShield provides promising obfuscation scheme against model thefts on edge, while maintaining real-time inference capabilities.

title: A Comparative Study of Lightweight Convolutional Models for Visual Surface Material Classification
authors: Jeremy Sean Tan, Aaron Raymond See, Aaron Benjmin Alcuitas
abstract: Surface material classification is essential for robotic perception, enabling grasp adaptation, slip prediction, and safe manipulation. This work presents a systematic analysis of accuracy-efficiency trade-offs among lightweight convolutional neural networks for visual-only surface material classification on the LMT-108 database. The evaluation spans both classical and compact models, including AlexNet, ResNet-18, ShuffleNetV2 (x0.5, x1.0, x1.5), MobileNetV2, MobileNetV3 (Small and Large), and EfficientNet (B0–B2), all trained under an identical pipeline and assessed in terms of accuracy, parameter count, floating-point operations (FLOPs), and inference latency. We apply a lightweight augmentation and regularization strategy to enhance generalization on texture-rich visual data. Across all architectures, EfficientNet-B2 achieved the highest mean Top-1 accuracy (97.8%). However, multi-objective analysis reveals that ShuffleNetV2 and MobileNet architectures provided the best accuracy–efficiency balance, attaining ≈97% accuracy while reducing parameters and latency by more than 70% relative to larger models. These results highlight the suitability of modern lightweight CNNs for computationally efficient surface material classification. A supplementary haptic fusion experiment further demonstrates that top lightweight visual backbones extend naturally to compact multimodal perception systems.

title: Post-Disaster Mobile Network Resilience Through Variance-Aware Framework with Population Weighting
authors: Merve Güllü, Sümeye Nur Karahan, Sedat Çimen, Ahmet Yazar, Mustafa Serdar Osmanca
abstract: Disaster events significantly affect communication network performance by causing spatially localized degradations that may not be captured by conventional monitoring approaches. Traditional network assessment relies primarily on average Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), which may obscure localized anomalies and fail to reveal region-specific disruptions. To address this limitation, this study proposes a multi-stage statistical and spatial analysis framework that integrates non-parametric statistical testing with density-based spatial clustering to detect localized performance degradation patterns under disaster conditions. The proposed framework combines statistical significance testing and Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) to identify abnormal regions and quantify performance variations across network infrastructure. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively detects spatially localized anomalies and provides improved insight into network behavior during abnormal operational conditions. The findings indicate that the proposed framework enables more reliable identification of performance degradation patterns and supports more effective network monitoring and decision-making processes in disaster scenarios.

title: Prompt-Free Efficient Adaptation of Segment Anything Model for Remote Sensing Landslide Detection
authors: Zuolei Li, Xingyu Gao, Zhenyu Chen, Yawen Duan, Fei Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Landslides pose serious risks to human safety and infrastructure, making accurate and timely detection essential for disaster assessment. Although deep learning has advanced landslide recognition from remote sensing imagery, existing methods typically rely on task-specific designs, which limit their generalization ability across diverse geographic environments. The Segment Anything Model (SAM) offers strong generalization and zero-shot segmentation ability, yet its dependence on manually provided prompts and limited suitability for remote sensing imagery constrain its effectiveness in landslide detection. To address these limitations, we propose PF-SAM, a prompt-free and parameter-efficient adaptation framework tailored for landslide segmentation. PF-SAM eliminates manual prompts by autonomously constructing the prompt embeddings that drive SAM’s segmentation process and adapting SAM to landslide remote sensing data. Specifically, we first propose the CNN-Token Adapter, which extracts multi-scale CNN features and transforms them into prompt tokens, providing SAM with localized geometric and textural cues essential for detecting landslides of diverse shapes and sizes. Then, we propose the Global Prompt Generation Mechanism (GPGM) that further enhances segmentation: the Dense Prompt Generation Module (DPGM) generates the coarse mask as the dense prompt to guide SAM toward the correct landslide regions, while the Sparse Prompt Generation Module (SPGM) selects high-confidence embeddings to generate sparse prompts that enhance the precise localization of landslide regions. Extensive experiments on multiple landslide datasets show that PF-SAM achieves state-of-the-art performance, substantially surpassing existing methods in detection accuracy and robustness.

title: Experimental Study on Condensation Heat Transfer Enhancement under Non-Uniform Electric Fields_supp1-3685987.pdf
authors: Jian Wu
abstract: Condensation heat-transfer enhancement remains a key focus in thermal science and engineering. Conventional methods face limitations in efficiency, energy demand, and applicability, while non-uniform electric fields have recently shown distinctive advantages. Yet, previous work has often lacked clear visualization and systematic analysis of coupled influencing factors. In this study, a custom platform combining high-resolution imaging and multi-channel data acquisition was developed to investigate condensation under electric fields. The setup enables real-time observation of droplet dynamics together with synchronous heat-flux and temperature measurements across varied electrode structures, polarities, spacings, and voltages. Results reveal that enhancement mechanisms involve earlier droplet onset, accelerated droplet coalescence, and reduced detachment size. Negative corona generally outperforms positive corona under the tested conditions, although the magnitude of the polarity effect depends on electrode configuration and operating parameters. Moreover, under nominal average field strength, larger electrode spacing is positively correlated with enhancement. The optimal configuration (dual-needle electrodes, 10 mm spacing, +10 kV) yielded a maximum 32.78% improvement. This study provides a visualization-based and heat-flux-resolved experimental framework for understanding condensation under non-uniform electric fields and offers guidance for the design of compact electric-field-assisted condensation systems.

title: Charge-Storage Interlayer Strategy for High-Performance Droplet Energy Harvesting via Liquid–Solid Triboelectrification
authors: Xiaobo Meng, Zhe Huang, Kailin Li, Zong Li, Bin Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Harvesting electrical energy from water droplets via triboelectrification is a promising strategy, but conventional droplet electricity generators (DEGs) usually exhibit limited output. Here, we propose an interlayer-modified DEG (IML-DEG) by introducing a polyethylene terephthalate (PET) film between the polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) triboelectric layer and the bottom electrode. Under optimized conditions, the open-circuit voltage of the IML-DEG is approximately 2.9 times that of the conventional DEG. The enhanced output is interpreted as being consistent with interfacial-polarization-assisted field regulation and trap-assisted suppression of charge dissipation, based on Maxwell–Wagner–Sillars interfacial polarization and the charge-trapping characteristics of PET. The effects of droplet impact height, impact frequency, PTFE thickness, PET thickness, electrode stiffness, and tilt angle are systematically investigated to clarify the roles of key geometric and dynamic parameters in device performance. Humidity-dependent, cycling-stability, load-dependent, capacitor-charging, and liquid-chemistry measurements further show that the PET interlayer design improves practical output behavior, maintains higher output than the conventional DEG over the investigated humidity range and under repeated droplet impacts, and is sensitive to liquid ionic content.

title: A Motion Control Algorithm of Wheel-Legged Robot via Adaptive Dynamic Programming and Data-Driven Value Iteration_supp1-3685946.mp4
authors: Aihong Ji
abstract: To enhance the motion adaptability of wheel legged robot in complex environments, a novel data-driven value iteration (VI) control algorithm based on adaptive dynamic programming (ADP) is proposed. A state-space system model of the wheel-legged robot is established, from which the algebraic Riccati equation containing the weighting matrices is derived based on the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation. The ADP framework is employed to collect state variable data under external disturbance, and an offline VI-based iteration learning process is conducted to obtain a set of optimal gain matrices. These gain matrices are subsequently integrated with multi-subtask motion control and torque allocation strategy to construct a unified control framework. Finally, a series of ex perimental evaluations, including dynamic balance, disturbance robustness, and stair-crossing, are conducted. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm exhibits strong adaptability and superior disturbance robustness across complex and unstructured terrains.

title: Deep Learning-Based Process Control of Microrobot Swarms Guided by Phase Diagrams_supp4-3686187.mp4
authors: Jiangfan Yu
abstract: Microrobot swarms with locomotion dexterity and shape reconfigurability show immense potential in biomedical applications. Automatic control strategies are critical for the navigation of swarms in unstructured environments. Existing control methods mainly focus on initial and final states of swarms, and swarm process control designed to avoid undesired states throughout the control process is yet investigated. In this work, we develop a deep learning-based process control strategy for swarms guided by phase diagrams. Two deep neural networks are respectively built to model the swarm shape and kinematics. Control approaches based on precise swarm models are designed to automatically tune multiple swarm parameters. A phase diagram-based controller is proposed to guide the swarm reconfiguration while eliminating the coupling effects between swarm parameters. The swarm is enabled to accurately track predefined trajectories while performing continuous reconfiguration with desired states during the entire process. By integrating the process control of swarm pattern and locomotion, the swarm can dynamically adapt to constrained unstructured spaces and achieve robust collision avoidance.

title: Hybrid GRU-Attention Network for Multi-Dimensional Mental Health Risk Prediction: A Comparative Deep Learning Analysis
authors: A.Akilandeswari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Mental health risk assessment is the most critical issue with healthcare analytics because of the multifaceted interaction between psychological, behavioral, and time aspects. Last generation machine learning methods have difficulty in long-range dependencies and cannot dynamically rank features in terms of their predictive value. We present Hybrid GRU-Attention Network, which is a combination of Gated Recurrent Units and multi-head attention models to predict mental health risks holistically. Extensive testing on real-world clinical data proves that our model could achieve a high level of performance with 98.70 percent accuracy and 0.0307 loss which is better than Standard GRU (97.70 percent accuracy), LSTM (96.75 percent accuracy) and BidirectionalGRU (96.90 percent accuracy). The model has good convergent characteristics, a small amount of overfitting and a great deal of confidence calibration and therefore may be used to implement the model in clinical decision support systems. Our work contributes to computational psychiatry since it offers a robust, interpretable and correct mental health risk assessment framework.

title: Airspeed Sensor Reliability Assessment for Robust UAV Control
authors: Atharva Phand, Javed Sayyad, Manoj Kumar Shukla, Manoj Singh Adhikari, Swapnil Narkhede
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) stability, control, and navigation depend on precise airspeed measurement. Airspeed sensors, which are usually based on dynamic pressure, can introduce significant measurement errors due to their sensitivity to environmental and flight-related factors, such as temperature changes, wind disturbances, altitude variations, and UAV orientation. A high-fidelity MATLAB-based simulation framework is presented in this study to assess sensor performance during takeoff, cruise, and landing. It also includes significant parameters such as pitch, roll, yaw, altitude, and weather. Controlled flight tests, aside from the simulation, also enable evaluation of calibration methods and verification of sensor accuracy. For enhanced measurement reliability, the study also examines adaptive solutions, including real-time error compensation and sensor location. The findings give insights into sensor responses under dynamic flight conditions, informing the design of reliable airspeed sensing and smart flight control systems. The outcomes have relevance to the application of UAVs in search and rescue missions, environmental monitoring, precision delivery, and autonomous aerial missions.

title: Evaluation of Deep Learning Architectures for Network Intrusion Detection
authors: Priya Arora, Soham Biren Katlariwala, Mitali Raj, Mahdisa Moradi, Sunita Sahu
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet-connected systems has significantly expanded the surface of potential cyber threats, necessitating efficient Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) capable of handling complex and high-volume network traffic. In this paper, we provide a detailed comparative evaluation of deep learning-based IDS models tested on two popular benchmark datasets: KDD Cup 99 and UNSW-NB15. Both datasets represent distinct eras and characteristics of network traffic, with KDD focusing on classical attack types and UNSW-NB15 incorporating modern, real-world traffic diversity. Our proposed framework integrates preprocessing, feature selection, and multiple neural architectures including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Deep Neural Networks (DNN), and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) to evaluate their performance under unified experimental conditions. The study systematically examines accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics, revealing that CNN-based models outperform other architectures on both datasets due to their hierarchical feature extraction capabilities. Furthermore, feature correlation analysis highlights dataset-specific dependencies that influence model generalization. This work provides a unified empirical benchmark and insights into how deep learning models adapt across legacy and modern intrusion datasets, contributing to the ongoing advancement of data-driven network security research.

title: An Efficient Underwater Acoustic Sensor Network with Delay-Aware Error Analysis and CNN-based Energy Management
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: An Underwater Acoustic Sensor Network (UASN) comprises acoustic communication-based sensors to collect data underwater. We developed the EUDECE model of underwater acoustic communication and data classification, integrating physical-layer modelling and machine learning. The signal propagation by Urick, including path loss and absorption, nodes estimate ambient noise to determine the Signal-To-Noise Ratio (SNR) for transmissions. The application of an RTS/CTS-based MAC scheme ensures optimal energy for maintaining SINR. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) performs signal processing for proper feature extraction, assisted by the use of dropout layers for the prevention of overfitting. A Support Vector Machine (SVM) data classifier for accurate data classification, the two models are utilised to make better-informed decisions by the network. The performance analysis of throughput, average delay, average traffic time, channel utilisation, Q-value, packet delivery ratio, and per packet is compared by the existing model CADDR, ATODI, APERU, with the EUDECE model.

title: Real-Time Image Processing Technology for Robotic Vision Systems in Precision Agriculture
authors: Lingdong Mei, Shuo Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: To address the challenges of poor robustness and real-time performance of robotic vision systems in precision agriculture field operations due to drastic changes in lighting, visual and weather diversity, and other factors, this paper constructs a lightweight and improved YOLOv8n model. The original backbone is replaced with HGNetV2 to significantly reduce the convolutional computational burden and floating-point operations. LSKA attention is embedded in SPPF to achieve selective aggregation of multi-scale context and channel recalibration. BiFPN is used for top-down and bottom-up bidirectional weighted feature fusion to enhance multi-scale information interaction. Finally, DSC is used to reconstruct the prediction head to compress parameters and reduce inference computation. An end-to-end evaluation was conducted using a public field weed dataset and an embedded platform. The improved YOLOv8n model achieved 92.13%/95.88%/93.2% accuracy in the morning, midday, and evening on the test set. The model achieved an FPS of 68.50 and an average inference latency of 14.60 ms, improving weed recognition accuracy in complex environments and real-time processing capabilities for edge deployment. This model provides an engineered, lightweight path for real-time image processing and inference latency constraints in robotic vision systems.

title: LoRaWAN and Arduino based Smart Campus Energy Monitoring
authors: Leo Raju, Bhuvanesh N P, Dhanushram K, Prasanth V A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This study presents a smart campus energy monitoring system leveraging the LoRaWAN and Arduino technologies to reduce excessive energy use and advance sustainability. As compared to existing short-range communication technologies such as Wi-Fi and ZigBee, LoRaWAN offers long-range low-power data transmission capabilities, which are useful for campus-wide monitoring. The smart campus energy monitoring platform design utilizes PZEM-004T sensors and Arduino Mega 2560 at the transmitting nodes to collect electrical parameters such as voltage, current, power, and energy consumption and transmits this data to a receiver node via a LoRa module. The receiver node transmits data to a cloud platform via LoRa Gateway and ESP8266 and provides real-time data accessible on a web-based interface. The framework for a smart campus energy monitoring platform provided is a way to promote energy awareness, enables remote monitoring, and supports data-driven decision making for increased energy efficiency.

title: Predictive Maintenance of Power MOSFETs with a Baseline RUL Prototype
authors: Nayan Kathait, Shivani Sharma, Rishibrind Kumar Upadhyay
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Power MOSFETs are integral to a wide range of critical systems, including consumer electronics, automotive, and aerospace applications, where their failure can result in significant operational risks. Accurate estimation of Remaining Useful Life (RUL) is essential for enabling Predictive Maintenance (PdM) strategies to enhance system reliability and safety. In this study, we present a data-driven approach utilizing time-series analysis of the NASA Prognostics Centre of Excellence (PCoE) MOSFET Ageing Dataset. Key features such as temperature-normalized ON-state resistance degradation (ΔRDS(ON)) are extracted to characterize the device's ageing behavior. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network is employed to model temporal degradation patterns and predict RUL. Our results indicate that ΔRDS(ON) is a strong indicator of MOSFET health, and the proposed LSTM-based model achieves competitive accuracy when benchmarked against existing methods on a subset of the dataset. This work contributes a comparative survey of machine learning techniques for MOSFET prognostics and a baseline LSTM prototype implementation.

title: Machine Learning–Driven Failure Prediction in Large-Scale Cloud Systems
authors: Rahul Amte
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The dependability of large-scale distributed systems has become a vital economic and operational concern as the world increasingly relies on cloud computing infrastructure. By 2025, hyperscale data centers are adopting proactive predictive maintenance (PdM) driven by Machine Learning (ML). This research paper provides a thorough overview of the state-of-the-art methodologies for predicting failures in cloud environments, concentrating on advances established up to the beginning of 2025. The effectiveness of hybrid Deep Learning frameworks, specifically the 1DCNN-Transformer architecture, is investigated in detail, as it has proven highly capable of capturing both local and global trends in massive log streams. Furthermore, the application of dynamic feature selection algorithms, such as Wear-out-updating Ensemble Feature Ranking (WEFR), is explored within the scope of Solid-State Drive (SSD) failure prediction based on heterogeneous datasets. The report also examines the Federated Learning (FL) paradigm as a mechanism for cross-organizational cooperation while maintaining data privacy, highlighting adaptive structures that reduce communication overheads by up to 97%. Finally, it explores how these predictive models are incorporated into autonomous self-healing ecosystems, noting the financial implications of downtime and the ethical consequences of algorithmic bias in the age of automation.

title: IoT-Driven Innovations in Food Processing for Sustainable Industry 4.0 Development
authors: Ashish Avasthi, Ranu Burad, Prerna Srivastava, Vijay Singh, Suresh Kadwa, Udit Mamodiya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is fundamentally transforming the food processing industry by enabling real-time monitoring, enhanced traceability, and automation across complex supply chains. As food safety and quality demand continuous improvement amid rising consumer awareness and regulatory pressures, IoT facilitates data-driven decision-making from farm to fork. This paper explores the integration of IoT technologies within food processing systems and their synergy with Industry 4.0 principles, encompassing cyber-physical systems, connectivity, and automation. Key applications include environmental monitoring, predictive maintenance, smart packaging, and supply chain transparency. We discuss how IoT drives efficiency gains, reduces waste, and supports sustainability goals. Furthermore, this study addresses challenges including data security, privacy concerns, and implementation barriers. Experimental examples of sensor deployment and analytical models for process optimization are presented. By advancing IoT adoption within Industry 4.0 frameworks, the food processing sector can enhance safety standards, operational resilience, and consumer trust, ensuring sustainable and innovative food production for future demands.

title: Chemical Potential Driven Transition from Single Band to Dual band Resonance in Graphene Hybrid Reconfigurable THz Antenna for 6G Applications
authors: Arpita Raj, Kajal Pandey, Ravi Gupta, Ravinder Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: A technique has been designed to transition into dual band graphene metallic hybrid THz antenna to cover the entire 2.4–6 THz frequency range. The tunability of surface plasmonic resonance through the variation of chemical potential plays a significant role in modern nano electronic and terahertz devices. In this it demonstrate the conversion of a single band to dual band response by increasing the chemical potential upto to 0.9 eV. The variation of chemical potential of graphene shift the fermi energy level, concentrating carrier density and enhancing the plasmon coupling. This modifies the resonance condition, leading to the splitting the fundamental mode, where the single band response evolves into dual band response. The Dual band of THz antenna is suitable for the applications of multifunctional terahertz antenna, for sensors and the modulators.

title: Weed Recognition Using Deep Learning Techniques: A Review
authors: Nisha Choudhary, Om Prakash Sangwan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Weed infestation continues to be a thorn in agriculture, reducing crop yield and increasing production costs. Deep learning has proved to be a robust alternative to traditional and chemical-based weed management, with automatic, precise, and scalable outputs. This review compares three prominent strategies: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Vision Transformers (ViTs), and Hybrid (CNN-Transformer) models on various datasets and studies. Experiments demonstrate that CNNs are effective but perform poorly under actual field conditions, ViTs excel through global context capture but require extensive datasets and a great deal of computational resources, while hybrids share both the strengths to provide the best balance among accuracy, energy efficiency, and scalability. In spite of these developments, challenges like class imbalance, limited interpretability, and generalization are still present, calling for future research on lightweight, interpretable, and field-deployable solutions to sustainable weed control. These advancements can improve food security and reduce chemical usage in farming.

title: KisanConnect: A Unified Smart Service Platform for Farmers and Rythu Seva Kedram
authors: Mamidi Tuhi, K. Sree Vijayalakshmi, Inala Jaswanth Naidu, T.V.N.G. Swetha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Agriculture remains the backbone of rural India, yet farmers continue to face challenges in accessing timely information, essential agricultural inputs, and government welfare benefits. The Government of Andhra Pradesh has launched Rythu Seva Kendrams (RSKs) for farmer welfare. Rythu Seva Kendram is a place where farmers can get the necessary seeds, fertilizers, and other assistance. Here, all the requirements of farmers can be fulfilled. Farmers visit Rythu Seva Kendram located in their respective area. Until farmers physically visit the RSKs office, there is no means of knowing whether the required fertilizers are available or whether the office is open. There is no platform to connect RSK staff and farmers. So, this paper presents progressive web app (app) to reduce the gap between RSK staff and farmers. This system reduces the need for frequent visits to RSKs, as all communication takes place through the application. The app simplifies the work of both RSK staff and farmers. Farmers can access stock details, scheme information, request required fertilizers, pesticides. we included a caching technique for providing offline accessibility. So, the data is available to users in offline mode. The RSK staff can make use of this app to monitor stock and sales data digitally, replacing the need for maintaining hard copies of records. The objective of proposed paper is reduce the communication gap between farmers and RSK officials through mobile app. Our aim is to make all necessary services available to farmers in a few clicks. Additionally, our innovative approach reduces the workload of the officials also.

title: Smart Coastal Erosion Monitoring using IoT
authors: Rishant Kumar Singh, Bishal Swain, Bharti
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Coastal erosion is a significant menace to ecological balance, infrastructure and lives of the people on the coast in the world. Though the conventional systems of tracking and monitoring are of help, they tend not to be adequately placed to provide real time, constant information which can be acted on to act on time. To resolve this dilemma, this paper would propose a Smart Coastal Erosion Monitoring System wherein Internet of Things (IoT) technology is used as the driving force. The system relies on a network of IoT-based sensors that are connected to collect valuable data on linear changes, waves, soil humidity, as well as weather conditions. These data flows are transported to a cloud cloud computing system to be intelligently processed to anticipate early signs of erosion, conduct predictive estimates using data-driven models. The proposed framework is concerned with scalability, low costs of implementation and also capabilities of integrating with geographic information systems (GIS) to enhance visualization. The results that it is expected to achieve include high degree of accuracy in detecting erosion, proactive assessment of risks, and policy implications to the policy makers and the environmental agencies.
