Lastly, the solution would be beneficial in securing sensitive coastlines, increasing the disaster preparedness, and assisting in sustainable management of coastal lands using IoT.

title: Optimizing Supervised Learning via Entropy Regularization
authors: Ruchi Garg, Sumit Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Entropy, a measure of uncertainty in information theory, plays a critical role in understanding the behavior of probabilistic models. In supervised machine learning, optimization techniques often neglect entropy dynamics, leading to overconfident predictions, poor calibration, and reduced robustness. This paper introduces a principled framework for entropy-aware optimization, integrating entropy metrics into loss functions, regularization, and training dynamics. We propose entropy-adaptive mechanisms that dynamically respond to prediction uncertainty, benchmark them across diverse datasets, and demonstrate improvements in accuracy, calibration, and resilience to noisy labels. Our theoretical analysis connects entropy-aware learning to information bottleneck principles and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, offering a robust foundation for future research.

title: Vulnerability Discovery Modeling in multi-version software using Weibull- Logistic distribution
authors: Anchal Dhillon, Vijay Kumar, Yoshinobu Tamura, Sunil K. Khatri
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Unresolved security weaknesses raised serious concerns since they have the potential to allow serious breaches and threaten software system. A vulnerability is a loophole or flaw in a security system that an attacker could take advantage of and cause harm or loss. Since significant portions of code are carried over into subsequent releases, the vulnerabilities may remain and resurface in later versions. These vulnerabilities are often introduced during development as a result of ongoing upgrades and the addition of new features. It is crucial to identify not only when vulnerabilities are introduced but how they are discovered over time because they remain across software generations. A systematic framework for investigating vulnerability discovery trends and forecasting the accumulation of defects has been made available by Vulnerability Discovery Modeling (VDM). However, a large amount of the work that has been done is limited to single-release software, ignoring the impact of shared code and inherited vulnerabilities. In this work, we prefer a multi-version VDM derived from generalized Weibull–logistic distribution. The proposed work intends to examine the impact of vulnerabilities in previous releases and their recurrence in upgraded systems via code inheritance. The model assesses the potential for unfixed vulnerabilities from previous iterations to reappear during new code testing and impact the current release. Real datasets from three distinct Windows Server releases are used to test and validate the model. The prediction performance shows that the newly proposed generalized Weibull-Logistic VDM performs better than existing multi-release VDM. The proposed model offers a more practical framework for evaluating persistent security threats in extensive software ecosystems by incorporating vulnerability inheritance into the discovery process.

title: Swarm Intelligence for Real-Time Traffic Flow Prediction in Urban Mobility Networks
authors: Shivendra Kumar Singh, Dharmendra Sharma, Saleh Naji Sreseh, Maki Mahdi Abdulhasan, Hassan Mohammed Abed, P. Gomathi
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Owing to physical capacity limitations, unstable traffic flows, and increasing vehicle densities, metropolitan mobility networks are now subject to a great deal of stress. This study explores the potential for the development of an adaptive and dynamic model applied to real-time traffic flow prediction using swarm intelligence and specifically Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Ant Colony Optimization (ACO). The idea is to use the characteristics of swarm intelligence, such as collective behavior and decentralized decision making, to build the proposed Swarm Intelligence-Based Traffic Predictor (SITP) that adjusts in real time to developing road conditions and traffic sensor data. Performance models were trained and validated using real traffic datasets obtained from urban traffic monitoring systems. The results of the study suggest improved prediction performance, improved convergence time, and improved adaptability over traditional machine learning techniques. The paper describes the system architecture, algorithm, simulation results, and comparison to demonstrate the potential of SITP for intelligent urban mobility management.

title: Facial Expression Recognition Using Fine-Tuned ResNet-50 Model
authors: Priyal Bhusate, Pranav Chinthala, Aditya Ulangwar, Shivam Aher, Kanak Bansal, Urvee Bhagwat, Ajay Chhajed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) explores facial expressions recognition for various applications such as mental health assessment and human-computer interaction, by fine-tuning a ResNet-50 model using Low-Rank Adaptation methods on the FER20113 dataset. The system processes facial images, detects faces, and predicts emotional status using deep learning techniques and optimized training strategies. While the approach achieves notable accuracy for several expression’s categories, there are challenges such as class imbalance, resource needed for training, and issues with misclassification and noisy data. The work aims to provide clinicians and researchers with improved tools for emotional assessment but recognizes current limitations and areas for future scope enhancement.

title: An Adaptive Edge-Cloud Task Offloading Framework using Reinforcement Learning for Latency-Sensitive AI Applications
authors: Anshu Vashisth, Gagandeep Kaur, Bhupinder Kaur, Gaurav Sethi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Applications of artificial intelligence (AI) and real-time video analytics, autonomous perception, augmented reality, and intelligent healthcare are latency-constrained and cannot be supported by cloud-centric architectures alone. Although edge computing can reduce the latency by executing tasks nearer to the data sources, the scarcity of edge resources and dynamic networks render static or heuristic-based offloading schemes of tasks not optimal. Current methods are usually dependent on fixed thresholds, decision by rules, or offline optimization, and thus provide no flexibility to the changes in workload, network traffic, and service level demands at runtime. The paper presents a dynamically task placement-based adaptive edge-cloud task offloading framework, constructed based on reinforcement learning or the use of reinforcing learning (RL), to address latency-sensitive AI task workloads dynamically. The model of the offloading decision process is the Markov Decision Process (MDP), in which the agent will learn an optimal policy by taking into account the characteristics of different tasks, availability of edge resources, network latency, and cloud processing delay. A reward mechanism is constructed to ensure a reduction in end-to-end latency and energy efficiency, as well as reliability in task completion. Larger-scale simulations also show that the proposed framework lowers the average latency by 18-25% relative to both the static and heuristic baselines, converges faster on dynamically changing workloads, and remains stable to network changes. The findings show that adaptive offloading that is RL-based offers a high-quality and scalable solution to next-generation edge-cloud AI systems.

title: AI-Powered FinTech Analytics for Transactional Transparency and Fraud Mitigation in Industrial IoT Ecosystems
authors: Swetha Sistla, Mohan Sankaran, Nagaraju Jooluri, Kiran Veernapu, Priyameet Kaur Keer, Sonia Vaz
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The intensive introduction of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) technologies has already increased the quantity and complexity of machine-to-machine financial transactions, which has become a significant challenge in terms of the clarity of the story and exclusion of threats of fraud. The idea proposed in this paper is AI-based FinTech analytics that feature both supervised and non-supervised machine learning systems when detecting fraud in industrial transactional environments. Advanced feature engineering and imbalance-sensitive model evaluation metrics are used to improve detection accuracy and reliability. Explainable artificial intelligence methods have been used to foster regulatory trust. The experimental findings show that there is a high rate of fraud detection, a low rate of false alarms, and applicability in real time, which validates the efficiency of the proposed approach in light of safe and transparent IIoT-based financial systems. The superior performance of XGBoost (97.6%) is attributed to the optimization of the gradient for fraud detection.

title: Optimized YOLOv8 with Spatial Path Guidance for Reliable Outdoor Navigation Assistance for Visually Impaired Individuals
authors: V. Murali Bhaskaran, Nanditha N, Neela A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Vision-based outdoor navigation systems for visually impaired individuals often struggle with detection reliability, limited spatial guidance, and a lack of real-world testing. This paper presents a better assistive navigation framework that improves YOLOv8 object detection. It uses optimized training methods with a specially selected outdoor dataset that includes 35 important object classes. The system has a spatial path guidance module that looks at obstacle distribution and distance to offer real-time direction advice for safer navigation. A calibrated monocular distance estimation method is used to provide reliable awareness of nearby objects. An intelligent SOS mechanism supports emergency alerts and live location sharing. The framework is tested by comparing it with standard object detection models and through real-world outdoor experiments in various scenarios. The results show improved detection accuracy, fewer false alerts, and better navigation reliability, demonstrating the effectiveness of this approach for outdoor mobility assistance.

title: Policy-Aware Adaptive Data Processing Framework for Multi-Tenant Cloud Analytics Platforms
authors: Jitendra Gopaluni, Voolla Sandeep Kumar, Satvik Bhasin, Pushpanjali Chauhan
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Multi-tenant cloud analytics systems have to be able to handle heterogeneous workloads effectively and observe tenant-specific policies, including service-level agreements, data locality, security and cost limits. Old methods of managing resources are not suitable to address the dynamic load and the policy requirements. This paper presents a Policy-Aware Adaptive Data Processing Framework (PA-ADPF), which combines machine learning algorithms to collaboratively achieve performance and policy fidelity in multi-tenant cloud systems. The framework applies supervised learning to predict the risk of policy violation, regression to approximate the number of resources needed, and reinforcement learning to allow adaptive decisions regarding scheduling. Empirical analysis of simulated multi-tenant workloads indicates that the proposed framework is highly effective to reduce the execution latency and policy violations and to enhance the throughput and resource utilization in comparison with the baseline approaches. The findings support the idea that integrating policy awareness into machine learning (ML)based decision-making allows scalable and efficient with average latency as 295 ms, throughput is 690 jobs per hour and resource utilization is 83%.

title: DeepRiskCast-Predicting Financial Fraud Using Behavioral Sequence Learning
authors: Moghal Yaseen Pasha, Ravi Bajulal Bhandari, Md Ankushavali, Rahul Pradhan, Anandakumar Haldorai, Rayappan Lotus
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Financial institutions are faced with mounting difficulties in detecting sophisticated fraud as their opponents exploit growing irregularities in behavior and multi-channel transactional flows. Traditional rule-based and static machine learning systems have difficulties in capturing temporal pattern, high dimensional signals and evolving fraud tactics and results in high false positives and missed detections. This work addresses this problem by introducing DeepRiskCast, a behavioral sequence learning framework grounded on the fusion of micro temporal encoding, relational context and adaptive attention for effective fraud prediction. The goal is to develop a model that will be able to detect subtle and temporally diffused fraud signals and keep the operational noise low. The methodology incorporates the combination of temporal convolution modules, Transformer-based long-range attention and ephemeral graph augmentation, which is enhanced using contrastive pretraining and cost-sensitive optimization. Experiments done on BankSim-Extended dataset shows that DeepRiskCast is a better predictor than state-of-the-art baselines with a AUC of 0.937, AUPR of 0.68, F1-Score of 0.678 and a significant decrease in false positives to only 23 per 10k transactions. These results validate the efficacy of modeling user activity as enriched behavioral sequences and suggest that DeepRiskCast is a plausible, scalable, explainable and operationally feasible solution in the modern day fraud detection ecosystems.

title: Winding-Factor-Based Analysis of Pole-Slot Combinations for a Reduction-Gear Magsplit
authors: Tae-Yun Ha, Yong-Jae Kim, Eui-Jong Park
abstract: Magnetic power-split devices (Magsplit) based on magnetic gearing can integrate power-splitting and motor functions. A Magsplit comprises a PM rotor, a pole-piece rotor, and a stator; accordingly, the air-gap magnetic field contains both the PM rotor pole pair component and pole-piece induced asynchronous space harmonics. Therefore, this study investigates a reduction-gear-based Magsplit, quantifies the coupling of stator windings to each component using a winding factor-based approach, and screens pole-slot and coil-pitch combinations. As a result, an optimal winding configuration is derived that enhances coupling to the target asynchronous harmonic while suppressing coupling to the PM component. The selected configurations are then assessed under locked-rotor (stall-torque) conditions using FEA, and the final prototype is validated through no-load back-EMF and stall-torque measurements.

title: MIMO Extension of ATSC 3.0: System Design
authors: Sungjun Ahn, Bo-Mi Lim, Sunhyoung Kwon, Sung-Ik Park
abstract: This paper presents and proposes the system design and features pertaining to ATSC 3.0 MIMO Extension. The scope is the physical layer and closely related aspects, covering technology newly specified in A/322 (physical layer protocol), A/324 (related signaling and instructions processed in broadcast gateway), and A/327 (implementation and deployment guidelines). We detail the definition and baseline clarifications, processing and procedure descriptions, signaling parameters, and configuration constraints, together with the design rationale.

title: Consensus-Driven Event-Based Graph Information Bottleneck for Integrated Communication and Control
authors: Ziqiong Wang, Xiaoxue Yu, Rongpeng Li, Zhifeng Zhao
abstract: The integration of communication and control presents a critical challenge for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms operating in the formation control scenario. This co-design problem is typically formulated within the Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework. However, partial observability limitations will impair collaboration effectiveness, and a potential solution is to establish consensus through well-calibrated latent variables obtained from neighboring agents. Nevertheless, the rigid transmission of less informative content can still result in redundant information exchanges. Therefore, we propose a Consensus-Driven Event-Based Graph Information Bottleneck (CDE-GIB) method, which integrates the communication graph and information flow through a GIB regularizer to extract more concise message representations while avoiding the high computational complexity of inner-loop operations. Meanwhile, we investigate the potential impact of consensus errors induced by noise arising from multi-agent interactions. To further minimize the communication volume required for establishing consensus during interactions, we also develop a variable-threshold event-triggering mechanism. By simultaneously considering historical data and current observations, this mechanism capably evaluates the importance of information to determine whether an event should be triggered. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in terms of efficiency, adaptability, and noise robustness.

title: Design of Notching and Mechanical Stress Relieving Grooves for High-Speed PM Machine in EVs
authors: Wentao Zhang, Zhongze Wu, Wei Hua
abstract: In this paper, the rotor surface notching and mechanical stress relieving grooves are analysed and designed for the high-speed permanent magnet (PM) machine used in EVs. The rotor surface notching could efficiently suppress the torque ripple and noise of the PM machine, whilst the high-speed mechanical stress issue is tackled by the stress relieving grooves. Firstly, the spatial-temporal characteristic of the airgap flux density is analysed for the high-speed PM machine. The contribution of the rotor surface spatial positions on both electromagnetic torque and key order radial force density is revealed. This paper utilizes the q-axis notching for suppressing the torque ripple and the noise related key order radial force density, which features significant suppression effect across wide working conditions of the machine. However, the q-axis notching will raise the local mechanical stress concentrations in the rotor lamination. Therefore, the stress relieving grooves are designed and optimized based on the analogy between stress distribution and the fluid flow. The maximum mechanical stress is reduced from 387 MPa to 369 MPa, ensuring the safe operation of the PM rotor. Finally, the high-speed flat wire winding PM machine is built and tested, including the line voltage, torque-current characteristic, output power characteristic, noise spectrogram and the 22500-rpm rotor mechanical overspeed tests. The measured electromagnetic performance of the prototype agrees well with the simulation prediction, and the mechanical overspeed test validates the high-speed operation capability.

title: Eight-Switch Nine-Level Common-ground Inverter with Four-fold Voltage Gain for PV Systems
authors: Felipe B. Grigoletto, Sound Cedieu, Kelwin Silveira, Sze Sing Lee, Yam P. Siwakoti
abstract: Transformerless inverters based on the common-ground (CG) concept have attracted increasing attention in grid-connected photovoltaic (PV) systems due to their ability to suppress leakage current by enforcing a constant common-mode voltage. This paper proposes a nine-level common-ground transformerless inverter employing only eight power switches, three diodes, and four capacitors. The proposed topology achieves a quadruple voltage gain within a single-stage conversion structure, enabling the direct interface of low-voltage PV sources without additional dc-dc stages. In addition to leakage current mitigation, the multilevel configuration reduces output voltage dv/dt and improves waveform quality, while a small input LC filter effectively smooths the dc-side current without introducing significant complexity. The inverter is designed for operation under both conventional low-frequency grids (60 Hz) and high-frequency ac microgrids (400 Hz), allowing a reduction in passive component size and enhanced power density. The operating principles, modulation strategy, design guidelines, and closed-loop control are presented in detail. The effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed inverter are validated through comprehensive simulation and experimental results.

title: Anonymization, Not Elimination: Utility-Preserved Speech Anonymization
authors: Yunchong Xiao, Yuxiang Zhao, Ziyang Ma, Shuai Wang, Kai Yu, Jiachun Liao, Xie Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: The growing reliance on large-scale speech data has made privacy protection a critical concern. However, existing anonymization approaches often degrade data utility, for example by disrupting acoustic continuity or reducing vocal diversity, which compromises the value of speech data for downstream tasks such as Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), Text-to- Speech (TTS), and Speech Emotion Recognition (SER). Current evaluation practices are also limited, as they mainly rely on direct testing of anonymized speech with pretrained models, providing only a partial view of utility. To address these issues, we propose a novel two-stage framework that protects both linguistic content and acoustic identity while maintaining usability. For content privacy, we employ a generative speech editing model to seamlessly replace personally identifiable information (PII), and for voice privacy, we introduce F3-VA, a flow-matchingbased anonymization framework with a three-stage design that produces diverse and distinct anonymized speakers. To enable a more comprehensive assessment, we evaluate privacy using both acoustic- and content-based speaker verification metrics, and assess utility by training ASR, TTS, and SER models from scratch. Experimental results show that our framework achieves stronger privacy protection with minimal utility degradation compared to baselines from the VoicePrivacy Challenge, while the proposed evaluation protocol provides a more realistic reflection of the utility of anonymized speech under privacy protection.

title: Transforming Project Scheduling and Monitoring with MS Planner: Lessons from Real-World Case
authors: Praneel Kumar Mukherjee, Neha Surendranath, Priyam Ganguly
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: This article explains the steps required to manage a project and the events that occur as it evolves from a preliminary concept to operational implementation [1]. The project estimation method is analyzed as a problem-solving technique in which, a complex problem is decomposed into smaller problems. The features of Microsoft Planner (MS Planner) as an effective project management tool are explored through a case study approach, and the roles of PERT and CPM in project management are discussed. This paper also explains how to manage client expectations through a written agreement and the significance of risk and configuration management in project management.

title: The Low-Latency UCIe-Compliant Spiral Layouts of TSV Arrays Against the Clustered Faults
authors: Bowen Tan, Xiaole Cui, Zhaohong Lin
abstract: The chiplet based integrated system contains lots of inter-die interconnections. The through silicon vias (TSVs) and micro bumps are the common components of the vertical electrical interconnections in the $2.5 \mathrm{D} / 3 \mathrm{D}$ multi-die integration circuits. They are usually laid out as arrays to regulate the routing rules. Faults may occur in the TSVs, and the redundant resources based self-repair mechanisms are introduced to improve the yield of the entire integrated chip. In the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) standard, each advanced package module contains 32 data lanes and 2 redundant lanes, and the shift chain based intra-module self-repair mechanism is defined. However, the faults are usually clustered in certain region of the TSV array. The intra-module self-repair mechanism of UCIe standard is not able to cope with the cases that more than 2 clustered faults occur simultaneously in one module. An effective idea to improve the repair rate is to distribute the faults into different modules by regrouping the layout of TSV array. This work reports some new spiral layouts of rectangular and hexagonal TSV arrays. The self-repair schemes based on the proposed layouts are UCIe-compliant. Simulation results present that the proposed self-repair schemes are able to repair the clustered faults in TSV array with the low repair latencies.

title: An AI-Driven CNN–LSTM Framework for Multi-Stage Driver Drowsiness Detection with Real-Time Mobile Alerts
authors: Rajiv Kumar Yadav, Prabhat Kumar Chaudhary, Gargi Jha, Rupesh Dahi Shrestha, Ramesh Kumar Pudasaini
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Driver fatigue is a prominent source of traffic accidents and requires the need for accurate real-time detection algorithms. In this paper, the authors propose the design of "DrowsyDriveNet," a hybrid deep learning architecture that is capable of classifying the driver alertness level into four stages: "Alert," "Slight," "Moderate," and "Severe." In this proposed architecture, authors combine a spatial network that uses a frozen MobileNetV2 feature extractor with a temporal network that uses CNN and Bidirectional LSTM networks. In this architecture, the critical facial parameters such as Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR), Mouth Aspect Ratio (MAR), and circularity of eyes are computed over a window of 60 frames and are capable of determining the instantaneous and long-term behavior of the driver. A rule-based late fusion of the spatial and temporal networks has been carried out, and the proposed architecture is capable of achieving a high level of classification accuracy of 99.89%.

title: A GPU-enabled multi-frame reconstruction for 3D electrical tomography using Tensor cores
authors: Varun Kumar Tiwari, Mahmoud Meribout
abstract: Electrical tomography (ET) has emerged as a sustainable and non-invasive imaging technique, offering nonionizing and non-radioactive solutions for various applications. Advances in hardware systems have enabled high-speed data acquisition and image reconstruction, but the computational demands of 3D ET systems with large electrode arrays and complex finite element models present significant challenges for real-time deployment, particularly in resource-constrained edge environments. This paper proposes a GPU-enabled Tensor Core-based reconstruction strategy for designing high-speed ET systems suitable for Internet-of-Things and embedded platforms. By leveraging Tensor Core units and employing a multi-frame reconstruction technique, the proposed approach demonstrates substantial performance improvements over traditional methods. Five different algorithms, encompassing both non-iterative and iterative approaches, were implemented to validate the benefits of this strategy. Experimental results on an embedded GPU achieved a speed gain of over 13 times compared to a general-purpose computer, and over four times when using Tensor Cores instead of CUDA cores alone. The system achieved frame rates of up to 3048 frames per second for a 32-electrode setup with 2 million mesh elements, highlighting its potential to meet the computational demands of sustainable, efficient, real-time, and portable ET systems.

title: Antenna Coding Design for Multi-User Transmissions Using Pixel Antennas
authors: Hongyu Li, Shanpu Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: This work investigates exploiting the potential of pixel antennas, which are a reconfigurable antenna technology that can flexibly adjust the antenna characteristics through antenna coding, in multi-user transmissions. To that end, we propose a multi-user multi-input single-output (MISO) pixel antenna system, which deploys the pixel antenna at users, and develop the system model including pixel antenna with antenna coding and multi-user beamspace channels. Aiming at maximizing the sum rate performance, we first propose an algorithm to alternatively design the precoding at the transmitter and the antenna coding at users, which explores the performance boundary for the proposed multi-user MISO pixel antenna system. To reduce the computational complexity, we propose a codebook-based antenna coding design algorithm, where the antenna coder is online optimized from an offline codebook. To further enhance the computation efficiency, we propose a hierarchical codebook-based antenna coding design that uses a multi-layer hierarchical search to achieve a better performance-complexity trade-off. Simulation results show that, adopting the proposed algorithms, the multi-user MISO pixel antenna system can always outperform conventional multi-user MISO systems with fixed antennas. More importantly, results validate that the proposed (hierarchical) codebook-based algorithms can significantly reduce the computational complexity while maintaining a satisfactory sum rate performance.

title: Intelligent Bionic Polarization Orientation Method Using Improved U-Net Full-sky Imaging Cloud Recognition model for Cloudy Conditions
authors: Qi Wu, Jiangtao Yang
abstract: Accurate and reliable navigation is critical for the Internet of Things (IoT). Polarization-based navigation has attracted extensive attention for autonomous heading estimation in small and unmanned platforms, particularly when satellite navigation is unavailable. However, conventional polarization navigation systems suffer from degraded heading accuracy under cloudy conditions, mainly due to unstable cloud identification and insufficient feature extraction in full-sky observations. To address these issues, this paper proposes a cloud layer identification method for full-sky images based on an improved U-Net. By integrating attention and residual learning, a novel Channel Weighting-Feature Fusion (CWFF) module is embedded into the U-Net structure to strengthen deep feature representation and cloud feature memorization. Furthermore, a feature-matrix-based cloud detection scheme is developed to skip explicit imaging and improve recognition efficiency. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves over 95% overall accuracy in cloud detection, with an average absolute error below 5%. Outdoor tests on an atmospheric polarization measurement system demonstrate that the root-mean-square error (RMSE) is reduced to 0.181°, effectively improving polarization-based heading accuracy under cloudy environments.

title: Egocentric Online Action Segmentation via Evidential Temporal Contextualization
authors: Xun Jiang, Xing Xu, Zhangye Han, Zheng Wang, Jingkuan Song, Heng Tao Shen
abstract: The Egocentric Online Action Segmentation (EOAS) task aims to temporally segment a streaming egocentric video into a set of actions. Due to the first-person perspective views and online constraints, the EOAS task raised two intrinsic difficulties: (1) The shot movements could impact the egocentric vision and make it difficult to observe overall activities. (2) The visual signals are processed in real-time, requiring models to understand the observed visual frame without any post-contextual cues after the observed frame. To address these two problems, we proposed a novel EOAS approach named Evidential Temporal Contextualization (ETC), which consists of two key modules: (1) Context-Aggregated TRansformer that predicts and aggregates potential post context to enhance representation of frame-level features, effectively addressing the insufficient temporal contextualization in streaming videos. (2) Evidential Action Contextualization that leverages evidential deep learning to quantify the uncertainty in frame-level classification and dynamically adjust the model training and inference process accordingly. Extensive experiments on three public EOAS benchmark datasets, i.e., GTEA, EgoProceL, and EgoPER, demonstrate our proposed ETC approach outperforms recent state-of-the-art methods. Additionally, we also conduct more ablation studies and generalize our proposed ETC method to exocentric videos, furtherly prove its superiority. Our code is available at https://github.com/XunCHN/ETC.

title: Language-Guided Adaptive Spatial-Frequency-Aware Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Chunpeng Wu, Shuli Cheng, Liejun Wang, Yankang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing change detection (RSCD) plays a crucial role in urban planning, disaster assessment, and ecological monitoring. However, the inherent complexity of remotely sensed images and the interference of non-targeted changes, such as illumination changes and ground renovation, often lead to the problem of misdetection of change areas by models. Moreover, many real-world changes occur at the semantic level rather than through explicit geometric or structural differences, making them difficult to distinguish based solely on visual appearance. Although deep learning-based methods have achieved significant progress in this field, most existing approaches struggle to effectively balance and address the complexities mentioned above due to insufficient semantic guidance. To this end, we propose a Language-Guided Adaptive Spatial-Frequency-Aware Network (LGSFNet) to enhance the model’s semantic guidance and discrimination ability for changing regions by introducing textual high-level semantic information at low cost. In this approach, textual prompts guide the model to focus more on change-related regions, thereby learning more discriminative features. Through disentangled fusion, this method provides a new perspective for traditional unimodal RSCD to utilize the high-level semantic information of text. Furthermore, we design a lightweight Dual-Domain Collaborative Sparse Attention mechanism in both spatial and frequency domains, which mimics human vision by adaptively emphasizing salient information. Combined with wavelet transforms, this mechanism further enhances the model’s sensitivity to complex change patterns, improving both discriminative power and robustness. Extensive experiments conducted on three widely used benchmarks—LEVIR, BCDD, and SYSU—demonstrate that LGSFNet achieves competitive performance compared with state-of-the-art methods.

title: High-performance near-infrared photodetector based on Ag NPs/MoS QDs&sol;SWCNTs&sol;AlO&sol;InP heterostructure for optical communication_supp2-3686231.mp4
authors: Jun Chen
abstract: Near-infrared (NIR) photodetectors have emerged as critical components in optoelectronic systems. However, conventional bulk semiconductor photodetectors are limited by lattice mismatch and inappropriate band alignment. This work proposes a near-infrared (NIR) photodetector based on Ag NPs/MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> QDs/SWCNTs/Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/InP structure. The light absorption is enhanced by the localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) effect from Ag NPs and the quantum confinement effect from MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> QDs. An Al₂O₃ thin film serves as a passivation layer to suppress dark current. The device achieves a responsivity as high as 414.5 mA/W at 1 V and a detectivity of 4.07 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</sup> Jones at 0.05 V. The device also responds rapidly, exhibiting a rise time of 2.4 μs and a fall time of 36 μs. Furthermore, successful optical transmission experiments have been conducted using this photodetector.

title: An Adaptive Inverse-Free Robust DZNN for Solving Future Linear Equation and Inequality Systems With Manipulator Application
authors: Binbin Qiu, Zijian Huang, Chao Zhong, Jinjin Guo, Yu Han
abstract: Future linear equation and inequality systems (FLEISs) are ubiquitous in dynamic engineering applications, yet solving them efficiently remains a challenge. Most existing models suffer from a fundamental tradeoff: they either grapple with the heavy computational complexity of matrix inversion or lack sufficient robustness against various noises while pursuing fast convergence. To overcome these limitations, this article proposes a novel adaptive inverse-free robust discretized zeroing neural network (AIFR-DZNN) model. The core innovation lies in the synergistic fusion of an inverse-free architecture with adaptive and robust mechanisms. Specifically, an inverse-free substitution strategy is employed to circumvent the matrix inversion bottleneck, thereby significantly reducing the computational complexity. Simultaneously, an adaptive convergence parameter and an error-summation term are seamlessly embedded to accelerate the convergence rate and enhance the robustness under noisy conditions. Rigorous theoretical analyses guarantee the stability and convergence of the proposed AIFR-DZNN model. Furthermore, comparative numerical experiments demonstrate its superior performance. Ultimately, the proposed AIFR-DZNN model is successfully applied to the path-tracking control of robotic manipulators, validating its efficacy and practicality in real-world manipulator applications.

title: A Compact Hybrid-Ring Circuit With Signal-Routing for Simultaneous 0° and 180° Phase-Difference Outputs
authors: Chieh Lee, Ching-Wen Tang
abstract: A compact hybrid-ring circuit with signal routing capability and simultaneous 0°/180° phase-difference outputs is proposed. The proposed topology integrates a power divider and a miniaturized 180° hybrid into a unified ring-based structure, enabling dual-phase signal generation within a compact layout. To achieve wideband phase inversion while maintaining size reduction, a slotted transmission line is incorporated into the hybrid section. Compared with conventional hybrid circuits that typically support only a single-phase operation, the proposed design provides enhanced functionality with controllable phase states and flexible signal routing. A prototype is designed, fabricated, and experimentally characterized. The measured results show good agreement with full-wave simulations, confirming the validity and effectiveness of the proposed design.

title: A Synchronization Solution for Bi-static ISAC under NLOS with Rich Multipaths
authors: Shengli Ding, Baolong Chen, Yannan Yuan, Junjie Tan, Dajie Jiang, I Chih-Lin, Daqing Zhang
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is expected to play a prominent role in 6G. Avoiding full duplex transceivers, bi-static sensing is free from self-interference and able to leverage ubiquitous network devices, thus considered an indispensable scenario of ISAC. However, bi-static sensing must resolve the non-ideal synchronization of the transceiver nodes. Such timing offset (TO) remains a challenging issue, especially in the non-line-of-sight (NLOS) condition with rich multipaths. This article makes the best use of all multipaths, deriving the cyclic shift relation between the delay spectrums measured at the two sides of transceivers to estimate the TO. Based on this theoretical analysis, an algorithm called enhanced round-trip measurement (eRTM) is developed to mitigate the TO. Specifically, the cyclic cross-correlation between the amplitudes of the two normalized delay spectrums measured at the two sides of the transceivers respectively is conducted to estimate the TO, which is then used for TO mitigation. Extensive simulations have verified the advantages of the eRTM algorithm, especially in NLOS conditions and with low signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs). In addition, field tests conducted with a bi-static ISAC prototype system and eRTM algorithm, have achieved centimeter-level positioning accuracy even in NLOS conditions with rich multipaths, confirming the validities of our theoretical analysis as well as the proposed algorithm.

title: Vision-Based End-to-End Learning for UAV Traversal of Irregular Gaps Via Differentiable Simulation_supp1-3685922.mp4
authors: Danping Zou
abstract: Navigation through narrow and irregular gaps is an essential skill in autonomous drones for applications such as inspection, search-and-rescue, and disaster response. However, traditional planning and control methods rely on explicit gap extraction and measurement, while recent end-to-end approaches often assume regularly shaped gaps, leading to poor generalization and limited practicality. In this work, we present a fully vision-based, end-to-end framework that maps depth images directly to control commands, enabling drones to traverse complex gaps within unseen environments. Operating in the Special Euclidean group $SE(3)$, where position and orientation are tightly coupled, the framework leverages differentiable simulation, a Stop-Gradient operator, and a Bimodal Initialization Distribution to achieve stable traversal through consecutive gaps. Two auxiliary prediction modules—a gap-crossing success classifier and a traversability predictor—further enhance continuous navigation and safety. Extensive simulation and real-world experiments demonstrate the approach's effectiveness, generalization capability, and practical robustness.

title: Multi-source Fusion Self-Calibrated U-shaped Network for Hydraulic System Fault Diagnosis
authors: Xiansong He, Yibo Song, Jinyuan Wang, Xiaoli Zhao, Wenxiang Deng, Weilin Zhu, Jian Hu, Gongning Li, Jianyong Yao
abstract: Reliable condition awareness is vital for modern aircraft and industrial equipment, yet fault diagnosis of hydraulic systems remains challenging due to hidden failure modes, nonlinear dynamics, and multi-source heterogeneity. To address this, an end-to-end intelligent diagnosis framework that integrates multi-source fusion self-calibrated U-shaped network (MFSCU-Net)is proposed. Displacement, pressure, and flow signals are fused across data and feature levels, while a U-shaped encoder-decoder with Self-Calibrated convolutions enables multi-scale representation, adaptive recalibration, and deep feature interaction. This design enhances weak fault signatures and improves sensitivity to incipient faults. Experiments on electrohydrostatic actuator and electrohydraulic proportional servo valve datasets demonstrate that MFSCU-Net achieves superior accuracy and robustness over benchmark methods.

title: Adaptive Residual Mamba Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Junyi Zhang, Renwen Chen, Fei Liu, Aocheng He, Boyu Zheng, Xintong Hou
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Although convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Transformer-based models have achieved considerable success in the remote sensing change detection (RSCD) field, they often struggle to balance local detail preservation and global dependency modeling. Recently, state-space models such as Mamba have shown promising potential for efficient long-range representation learning. However, most existing Mamba-based RSCD methods are constrained by fixed residual connection mechanisms and selective scanning strategies, resulting in limited feature adaptability in spatial dependency modeling. To address these challenges, we propose Adaptive Residual Mamba (AR-Mamba), a novel Mamba-based framework that introduces adaptive residual and cross-gated scanning mechanisms. Specifically, the adaptive residual state (ARS) block employs adaptive kernel convolver and learnable scaling to dynamically calibrate residual signals. The cross-gated bi-scanning (CGBS) strategy performs bidirectional spatial scanning and cross-directional gating. Experimental results on the LEVIR-CD, SYSU-CD and WHU-CD datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed AR-Mamba, which achieves F1-score improvements of 0.82%, 1.95% and 1.62% over ChangeMamba, respectively.

title: Deep Learning-Based Process Control of Microrobot Swarms Guided by Phase Diagrams_supp3-3686187.mp4
authors: Jiangfan Yu
abstract: Microrobot swarms with locomotion dexterity and shape reconfigurability show immense potential in biomedical applications. Automatic control strategies are critical for the navigation of swarms in unstructured environments. Existing control methods mainly focus on initial and final states of swarms, and swarm process control designed to avoid undesired states throughout the control process is yet investigated. In this work, we develop a deep learning-based process control strategy for swarms guided by phase diagrams. Two deep neural networks are respectively built to model the swarm shape and kinematics. Control approaches based on precise swarm models are designed to automatically tune multiple swarm parameters. A phase diagram-based controller is proposed to guide the swarm reconfiguration while eliminating the coupling effects between swarm parameters. The swarm is enabled to accurately track predefined trajectories while performing continuous reconfiguration with desired states during the entire process. By integrating the process control of swarm pattern and locomotion, the swarm can dynamically adapt to constrained unstructured spaces and achieve robust collision avoidance.

title: Layer 0 and Layer 1 Integrated Smart Contract Kill Switch for Cross-Chain Security in Decentralized Systems
authors: Soonhong Kwon, Wooyoung Son, Jong-Hyouk Lee
abstract: The revised EU Data Act requires the incorporation of smart contract kill switch mechanisms. Nevertheless, most existing designs are either confined to single chain architectures or dependent on centralized control, thereby conflicting with the decentralization principles of blockchain systems and limiting their applicability in cross-chain environments. This limitation becomes particularly critical in IoT-driven industrial contexts, where security incidents demand synchronized execution control across multiple chains. Under such circumstances, single chain oriented kill switch mechanisms inherently suffer from structural constraints. To address these challenges, we propose an integrated Layer 0 and Layer 1 smart contract kill switch framework. The proposed framework enables distributed decision-making across heterogeneous blockchain systems by supporting diverse consensus algorithms, including PoW, PoS, PBFT, Paxos, and Raft. Cross-chain propagation of kill switch activation signals is securely facilitated through interoperability solutions such as CCMP and LayerZero. Importantly, activation decisions made on one chain are not mandatorily enforced on others; instead, each chain independently determines whether to execute the kill switch via its own consensus process, thereby preserving both autonomy and decentralization. For performance evaluation, repeated experiments were conducted across 50 use cases derived from exhaustive combinations of two interoperability mechanisms and five consensus algorithms. The analysis considered average activation time, gas consumption, and message delay, alongside Time to Activation (TTA) and tail-latency metrics to assess real-time responsiveness. Network experiments using Linux tc-netem demonstrate that even under severely degraded network conditions, the cross-chain p99 TTA is bounded at 12.2596 s, establishing a predictable worst-case upper limit. These results highlight the structural robustness of the proposed framework by guaranteeing a bounded reaction time even in environments characterized by increased latency and consensus overhead, rather than merely improving average-case performance.

title: Magnetically Actuated Composite Vocal Fold Phantom for CT/TA Muscle Mimicry
authors: Onur Tavli, Kuter Erdil, Alp Kayra Dagistanli, Daghan Gokdel, Onur Ferhanoglu
abstract: We present a magnetically actuated vocal fold–phantom phonation system capable of reproducing longitudinal tension modulation to mimic cricothyroid (CT) and thyroarytenoid (TA) muscle activation. We first formulate the electromagnetic force model and describe howthe resulting artificial muscle activation alters longitudinal stiffness, transverse stiffness, and vibratory behavior. Vocal fold phantoms are fabricated using RTV silicone mixed with Fe powder and cast in 3D-printed molds. Leveraging gravity during curing, Fe powder are concentrated near the transverse surface closest to the actuation coil, enhancing magnetic responsiveness while maintaining tissue-like mechanical properties. Finite-element simulations are used to investigate how longitudinal loading modifies vibratory modes and dynamics. Experimentally, laser Doppler vibrometry (LDV) and knife-edge optical sensing validate the analytical and simulation results. Magnetic actuation enabled longitudinal elongations up to 2.0 mm, providing a tunable phonation frequency range of 127–191 Hz, consistent with simulation. Repeatability testing over 10 hours revealed only ±95 μm standard deviation in elongation, demonstrating the reliability and stability of both the actuator and the phantom. These results establish a tunable, muscle-mimicking platform that can facilitate controlled investigations of tension-dependent phonatory mechanics and support future laryngological research.

title: Terahertz Chip Interconnects Based on Beam-Lead Structures
authors: Binbin Ma, Shixiong Liang, Yi Wu, Yang Yu, Sheng Li, Wenwei Wang, Meng Zhang, Yang Zhao, Ming Yin, Kaixue Ma
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work suggests using low-loss beam-lead technology instead of wire bonding to prevent performance degradation in THz chip interconnects. Full 3-D finite-element method (FEM) simulations and lumped-element analysis indicate the parasitic reduction process, proving this structure’s superiority. Comparative measurements were taken in four frequency bands: 170–220 GHz, 240–320 GHz, 420–470 GHz, and 500–700 GHz. Experimental results show that the beam-lead interconnection substantially reduces insertion loss by approximately 0.5 dB in the 170–320 GHz range. More crucially, the performance advantage increases as the frequency rises to higher THz frequencies. In the 500–700 GHz range, beam-lead technology outperforms wire bonding by about 3 dB due to reduced inductive degradation. This study shows that beam-lead technology can connect future terahertz (THz) integrated systems with high-performance and wideband connections.

title: Lottery Preferences, Skewness, and Stock Returns in The Emerging Market
authors: Tran Trong Huynh, Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: This study investigates whether skewness is a priced risk factor in the Malaysian equity market, an emerging economy characterized by volatile returns, limited market depth, and strong retail investor participation. While traditional asset-pricing models such as the Carhart four-factor (FF4) framework explain variation in returns through market, size, value, and momentum effects, they do not explicitly account for higher-order moments such as skewness, which capture asymmetry in return distributions and exposure to tail risk. Using daily stock returns from January 2004 to June 2025, this study constructs monthly measures of return skewness for all listed firms. Stocks are sorted each month into portfolios based on lagged skewness, and the performance of a long–short strategy (PLS = low-skew minus high-skew) is evaluated. The results reveal a strong and statistically significant negative relation between skewness and subsequent returns: low-skew portfolios outperform high-skew portfolios by over 1% per month. Importantly, the PLS portfolio maintains a significantly positive alpha after controlling for the Malaysian four-factor model, demonstrating that skewness is not subsumed by conventional risks. These findings provide novel evidence that skewness is a priced anomaly in Malaysia, highlighting the limitations of classical factor models and offering practical implications for investors and policymakers in emerging markets.

title: A Modular Simulation Framework for Adaptive Routing in Fog-Cloud Architecture
authors: Neeraj Gupta
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The Internet of Things can be modeled as a three-layer user-fog-cloud architecture. The main requirements in such systems are low latency on fog subsystems and balanced load distribution on cloud subsystems. This article presents a modular simulation framework that evaluates various fog routing strategies and cloud backends that are modelled as Erlang C, Erlang A, and G/G/c formulations to capture various assumptions under dynamic traffic variability. Nine performance metrics, including fog and cloud waiting time, offloading percentage, abandonment rate, and delta comparisons, were tracked to evaluate the performance of the system. Contrary to theoretical beliefs, it was found that priority-aware and energy-aware routing strategies lead to higher abandonment of jobs due to aggressive offloading. Cloud wait times were almost the same across the strategy, pointing to the backend saturation. The work highlights the importance of simulation-based validation. The results suggest developing intelligent routing strategies and AI-based load balancing algorithms to balance latency, energy efficiency, and service continuity in next-generation distributed computing.

title: Detecting Common Injection-Based Vulnerabilities in Web-Applications
authors: Mohd Danish, Abhinav Sushil Varshney, Vivek Jangra, Sunil Kumar Chowdhary, Nidhi Sindhwani Anand, Ramnaresh Yadav
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The research paper showcases a general method of detecting injection based vulnerabilities in the web applications. These type of vulnerabilities allows the attackers to manipulate various parameters leading to theft of data, identity fraud as well as system compromise. The key types of injections include SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting XSS and Command Injection. The currently available tools often fail to detect all three of these or are too costly. The technique mentioned herein the paper detects all these injection flaws by comparing both the application’s response as well as the behaviour before and after sending a crafted payload. By conducting experiments on multiple vulnerable web apps it was seen that our method outperforms both open-source tools as well as rivals that of commercial ones and thus achieving broad detection coverage with very few false positives.

title: Flood Dynamics in the Ganga River Basin: A Multi-Temporal SAR-Based Change Detection Approach
authors: G Uma Prasana, M Ramesh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) is crucial for monitoring natural disasters and supporting effective disaster management, offering distinct advantages over multispectral optical imagery due to its long-wavelength interaction with the Earth's surface. This study utilizes Sentinel-1A C-band SAR data to identify flood-affected regions near the confluence of the Ganga and Ghaghara Rivers in Chhapra city, Saran district, Bihar. Two Ground Range Detected (GRD) SAR images are pre-processed using the Sentinel Application Platform (SNAP) to extract relevant data. Multi-temporal change detection is performed by stacking images captured before and during the flood event. To enhance flood visualization, a resampled, atmospheric corrected Sentinel-2A multispectral image is used to generate various False Colour Composites (FCC). The resulting flood extent from Sentinel-1A, Sentinel-2A, and meteorological observations are compared. SAR imagery clearly reveals inundated areas along the riverbanks and at the river confluence, whereas optical imagery, while showing water presence struggles to distinguish between floodwaters and the main river channel. Overall, this study contributes to a deeper understanding of flood dynamics, supports flood risk assessment, and aids in developing effective mitigation strategies for vulnerable regions.

title: Metamaterial-based Antenna Design for Compact IoT Devices
authors: D.Lingaraja, B. Yemanth Kumar, V.Sasidhar Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The ruthless proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) is the ecosystem, including smart cities and bracelet health watches, puts the hardware, and most notably the antennas, which facilitate wireless connectivity, under high expectations. Traditional antenna designs are gradually becoming insufficient, as they have a devastating trade-off between physical size, bandwidth of operation and radiation efficiency. This trade- off is particularly acute in the context of ultra- compact and low-cost form factors which modern IoT devices are commonly constrained by, frequently compelling engineers to tolerate the compromised performance which can become an obstacle to the successful transmission of data, inter-operability between devices utilizing different communication standards such as LoRaWAN, Zigbee, and 5G-NB-IoT. In order to overcome these intrinsic drawbacks, a ground breaking engineering solution has emerged in the form of metamaterial- based antennas (MAs), which exploits the artificially created materials properties, which would not have existed naturally, to radically reformulate the idea of an antenna design concept. These antennas are being used to realize unparalleled miniaturization through the use of a negative refractive index, composite right/left-handed (CRLH) transmission lines, and electromagnetic band-gap (EBG) antennas, which generate effectively at intra and subwavelength scales as well as increasing gain and directivity. In this paper, the recent advances in the MA design procedures are fully reviewed such as multi-band and frequency-agile reconfigurable design that best fits the crowded and heterogeneous RF spectrum of IoT. It also includes an in-depth account of performance metrics that are achieved and openly states the outstanding challenges to achieve a widespread commercialization, including the complexity of fabrics production, the bandwidth of some of these designs, and how they can be integrated with other electronic devices.

title: Privacy-Preserving Medical Chatbot: A Local Deployment Framework using Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning and Quantized Inference
authors: Pranav Pratap Singh Bahri, Pawan Joshi, Rashmi Bhel
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into the healthcare sector is currently caught in a difficult balancing act: achieving high-performance conversational AI while meeting the rigorous privacy standards of HIPAA and GDPR. While cloud-based LLM APIs offer significant power, they necessitate the transfer of sensitive patient data to external servers, which introduces substantial security risks. This paper introduces a robust framework for building medical chatbots that operate entirely on local, consumer-grade hardware to eliminate these vulnerabilities. By utilizing the open-source Gemma 2B model, we applied Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA) for memory-efficient fine-tuning on medical datasets and deployed the system via the GGUF format with 4-bit (Q4_K_M) quantization. Our architecture ensures complete data sovereignty, meaning patient queries never leave the host device. To ensure the model remains professional and helpful, we implemented strict behavioral controls—including a 1.2 repetition penalty and specialized stopping markers—that result in a consistent, 5-point structured output. The final result is a compact 1.5 GB model that achieves a 2.4x size reduction without compromising its medical accuracy. By detailing our solutions for toolchain compilation and runtime stability, we demonstrate that specialized, private medical AI is a feasible reality for edge devices today.

title: Design of Energy-Efficient Ternary Logic Circuits Utilizing GNR Field-Effect Transistors
authors: Bogyam Saikumar, P. Surendra Babu, Shaik Javid Basha, Y. Mallikarjuna Rao, Erfan Abbasian, Shams Ul Haq
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Legacy silicon binary circuits are limited by two primary bottlenecks such as high static power leakage and the excessive physical footprint of interconnecting wires. To mitigate these limitations, the multiple-valued logic (MVL) and the adoption of emerging nano-electronic devices represent two promising paradigms for modern circuit design. A novel approach to designing high-performance digital ternary gates with graphene nanoribbon field effect transistors (GNRFETs) is introduced in this study. The GNRFET has unique properties such as modifying the width of graphene nanoribbons (GNRs) to obtain desired threshold voltage values. The novel circuit designs such as Buffer/NOT, NAND/AND and NOR/OR circuits presented in this work. The proposed design logic functions and its complement depends on the control signal (S). Additionally, in these gates, the higher impedance state is presented that helps to save the power while gates are not in use. The presented gates are implemented in HSPICE software to acquire the transient response and performances including delay, power and power delay product (PDP), respectively. Furthermore, the presented gates are compared to the conventional CNTFET based gates in terms of performance. It is noted that the GNRFET gates show an average performance improvement up to 75.40% than the conventional CNTFET gates.

title: Machine Learning based Predictive Analytics for Student Dropout and Academic Success
authors: Kanmani K, Hari Kumar Palani, Loganayagi S, C. Sivasankar, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, G. Vasanthi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Student attrition in higher education is acknowledged as a major concern that influences both institutional sustainability and the long-term academic and professional paths of learners. In many institutions, rising dropout rates place pressure on academic planning, financial stability, and student support systems, which makes the early identification of vulnerable students increasingly important. In this context, a comprehensive machine learning framework is applied in the present study to predict student dropout and academic success using the well-established dataset from the UCI Machine Learning Repository.A wide range of classification algorithms is systematically examined, including Decision Trees, Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, Logistic Regression, and several ensemble approaches. Each model is evaluated to understand its predictive capability, generalization potential, and suitability for real-world academic monitoring systems. The ensemble model that demonstrated the highest effectiveness achieved an accuracy of 89.7 percent, a precision of 87.3 percent, a recall of 91.2 percent, and an F1-score of 89.1 percent, indicating a strong balance between identifying true dropouts and minimizing misclassification. The feature correlation analysis further indicates that curricular units credited in the first semester at 0.68, admission grade at 0.54, and tuition fee payment status at 0.47 show the most influential predictive associations. These factors reflect academic preparedness, financial consistency, and early academic engagement, all of which contribute meaningfully to student persistence. The overall findings provide practical and data-driven insights that can support the development of early intervention systems. By applying these predictive indicators, institutions can design proactive measures, strengthen academic advising, and offer targeted assistance that helps reduce dropout rates and fosters sustained student success.

title: Real-Time State Management and Data Visualization in Cloud-Based Financial Applications
authors: Naveen Kumar Vayyasi
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The latest digital world has created large amounts of information for detailed knowledge to be stored by financial institutions and businesses as a result of their customers’ online activities. The present study provides an overview of how data collected and analyzed through cloud technologies will help develop more effective methods of analyzing and personalizing data so that businesses can meet their customers’ needs. This is done by identifying customer preferences, patterns, and other behaviours by extracting useful information from this information using various cloud-based systems, which further enhances the business’s ability to promote loyalty among its customers. The exploration of how organizations utilize these resources for analysis will also improve customer engagement. Personalization has become one of the primary means by which organizations engage with customers on a more personal level. By utilizing all types of data from various sources through the use of cloud-based data management, organizations can create and provide their customers with customized products and services. These organizations utilize various ways to personalize their products and services through the use of machine learning and collaborative filtering algorithms. The proposed financial prediction framework shows that the Transformer produced the most accurate prediction at 96% followed closely by the LSTM with 94% and the GRU with 92%. The GRU has the lowest latency at 40 ms, the LSTM model with 47 ms and the Transformer with 62 ms.

title: Enhancing the Fama–French Five-Factor Model with Tail Risk and Geopolitical Uncertainty
authors: Tran Trong Huynh, Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The Fama–French five-factor (FF5) model provides a widely used framework for explaining cross-sectional stock returns but omits important risk dimensions, notably downside tail risk and geopolitical uncertainty. Tail risk captures the probability of extreme losses beyond volatility- based measures, while the Global Political Risk (GPR) index reflects uncertainty from wars, trade disputes, and political instability. These risks are particularly relevant in emerging markets, where return distributions are skewed and markets are highly sensitive to shocks. This study extends the FF5 model by incorporating firm-level Value-at-Risk (VaR) and changes in GPR to forecast monthly excess returns of all Ho Chi Minh Stock Exchange (HOSE) stocks from January 2010 to December 2024. Three approaches are compared: Ordinary Least Squares (OLS), Support Vector Regression (SVR), and a Random Walk (RW) benchmark. Out-of-sample tests show that both OLS and SVR reduce forecast errors by approximately 35% relative to RW, with VaR providing significant explanatory power, while GPR remains insignificant at the monthly horizon. The results highlight the value of incorporating tail-risk measures into asset-pricing models, the competitiveness of simple interpretable methods, and practical implications for investment strategies in emerging markets.

title: A Secure Cloud–AI Collaborative Framework for Financially Intelligent and Autonomous Manufacturing Enterprises
authors: Bhanuprakash Madupati, Rama Krishna Cherukuri, Priyameet Kaur Keer, Nithesh Naik, Niranjan N Prabhu, Shahneela Jamal Kidwai
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The paper is a proposal of secure cloud-AI cooperative model to empower financially intelligent and autonomous manufacturing businesses. The architecture combines Internet of Things data of industries, financial data of enterprises, and machine learning models on the cloud system. It involves single security platform that can be able to support scalable and reliable decision-making. Deterministic ML strategies are used to forecast financial risk, cost, and profitability. It can be further used by an independent decision engine to maximize manufacturing process. The experimental analysis by classification and regression indicators illustrates the presence of a better forecasting level, lower operational price, and effective mitigation of financial risks. The security analysis provides better data protection at minimal overheads of the system, which renders the framework to apply to the real-life intelligent manufacturing system. The current research proposes a Cloud–AI secure cooperating model as the basis for integrating security-aware cost optimization with a unified framework of adaptive resource orchestration and real-time governance using AI. The new solution is different from other current solutions (like FinOps) which treat costs or automation separately, but instead combine security, operation intelligence, and cost efficiency in one cloud-enabled manufacturing system.

title: Design and Analysis of Cylindrical GAAFET using HfO2 High-k Dielectricfor Low Leakage with Reduced Sub-Threshold Swing
authors: Ayushi Rajput, Ravi Rastogi, Anurag Govind Rao, Arjesh Jha, Devendra Kumar Tripathi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation-based analysis of a Cylindrical Gate-All-Around Field-Effect Transistor (GAAFET) utilizing Hafnium Dioxide (HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) as a high-k gate dielectric, modelled using Schrödinger–Poisson quantum confinement and mobility models in Silvaco ATLAS TCAD. The proposed device features a gate length of 20 nm and leverages the superior electrostatic control of the gate-all-around architecture compared to planar and FinFET structures. Prior studies on nanowire and nanosheet GAAFETs with high-k dielectrics have demonstrated improved short-channel control and leakage reduction; however, cylindrical geometries remain relatively underexplored. This gap motivates the present work, which combines cylindrical symmetry with HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> dielectric to achieve enhanced leakage suppression and scalability. Simulation results confirm significant improvements in sub-threshold swing (SS), drain-induced barrier lowering (DIBL), drive current (Ion), and off-state leakage current (Ioff), yielding a high Ion/Ioff ratio and excellent switching efficiency. The novelty lies in demonstrating that cylindrical HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> GAAFETs at the 20 nm node offer promising leakage control and energy savings for future CMOS technologies beyond 5 nm.

title: Solar-Biomass Waste Energy-Based Standalone System with IoT-ML Algorithms: A Comprehensive Study in a South Indian Remote Village
authors: Renugadevi P, Sudha Anbalagan, Srimathi R
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The low electrification of a remote village is a result of frequent setbacks through intermittent supply of the grid, high technical losses, and poor control of the system. This holistic research becomes acquainted with a solar-biomass standalone system (OFF-Grid) of a south Indian village (100 households) with the aid of IoT (Internet of Things) empowered real-time monitoring and machine learning (ML) algorithms for predictive load forecasting, optimal energy controls, and fault detection. Compared to the traditional systems that experience difficulties in the supply interruption and a higher loss of energy, this system increases the solar irradiation and agricultural biomass. A techno-economic analysis conducted by HOMER Pro and MATLAB reveals a competitive levelized cost of energy of Rs. 10.88/kWh, a system uptime of 99.7%, a renewable content of 97%, and a payback period of 4.4 years, which is superior in performance amongst past performances of solar-only and biomass-only models. Consequences outline 1 year of avoiding 82 tons of CO2 emissions and more than 16% improvement in efficiency as a result of ML-IoT integration. The architecture, adaptive control policies, and real-time analytics offer resilient operation, and the challenges associated with supply irregularity, fault management, and economic viability are reduced. This paper will deal with the shortcomings of the earlier standalone renewable system, including a lack of real-time control, a lack of synergy between resources, and a lack of means to detect failures. The work provides a dependable solar biomass system to electrify the rural areas, bridging between high-tech energy technology and IoT-ML.

title: Machine Learning Algorithms for Predictive Maintenance in Electronic Systems
authors: Anuja Singh, Ashwani Maurya, Pradeep Kumar Verma, Lalit Kumar, Pankaj Vishwakarma, Sarika Shrivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Predictive maintenance (PdM) has emerged as a transformative approach to maintenance of electronic systems by enabling the early detection of operational faults before they escalate into costly failures. Leveraging machine learning (ML) techniques, PdM models analyze sensor data capturing device operational states to identify subtle failure patterns. This study applies three prominent supervised ML models—Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, and Neural Networks—to a dataset of sensor signals collected from electronic devices under normal and faulty conditions. Using comprehensive evaluation metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, with a sturdy 5-fold cross-validation framework, the performance of these models is rigorously compared. The experimental results demonstrate that ML-based PdM substantially improves fault detection reliability compared to traditional approaches and provides practical advantages that enable smarter maintenance scheduling, reducing downtime and operational costs significantly.

title: Smart Monitoring System for Rapid Forest Fire and Smoke Identification
authors: Konda Hari Krishna, Muddisetti Pallavi, Molaka Prashamsha, Nallathimmagari Sharanya Reddy, Nandi Thrinath, Sm Rafi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Wildfires currently pose an existential threat to global ecosystems, demanding scalable and immediate detection capabilities which legacy satellite and manual observation systems fail to provide. This research presents a high-speed, dual-stage computer vision framework designed to overcome the latency and accuracy limitations of existing fire monitoring solutions. By integrating a YOLOv9-based object detector with a specialized ResNet50 classification filter, our system achieves a critical balance between real-time responsiveness and false-positive suppression. We evaluate this architecture against a diverse dataset of flame and smoke imagery, demonstrating how deep neural networks can be optimized for deployment in resource-constrained surveillance networks. The study further identifies key bottlenecks in adversarial weather robustness and proposes a roadmap for next-generation edge-AI fire perception.

title: A Self-Biased Hybrid Voltage Reference with Ultra-Low Power, Low Noise and High Temperature Stability Using DTMOS
authors: Sweety, Sanjana Verma, Komal Duggal, Kanchan Sharma, Vandana Niranjan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This paper presents a self-biased hybrid (CMOS+BJT) voltage reference with ultra-low power, low noise and high temperature stability using dynamic threshold MOSFET(DTMOS). Stacked diode-connected MOS transistors and a DTMOS architecture are used in the design to minimize power consumption, and a bipolar junction transistor is used to reduce the voltage variation. A self-biased architecture is used to reduce the start-up time without the need for external biasing sources. To reduce temperature-dependent changes in the reference voltage, a temperature compensation network is integrated. The circuit is simulated in 180-nm CMOS technology, requires a 1.14V supply and generates a reference voltage of 741.44mV. The suggested design delivers a temperature coefficient of 6.8 ppm/°C from −50°C to 85°C, a line sensitivity of 0.19%/V, power consumption of 8.8nW and low output noise of 17.92 µV/√Hz at 1 Hz.

title: IoT Sensor Network and Ethereum-based Blockchain for Enhanced Pest Identification and Water Management in Vineyards
authors: Kiran Bharadwaj Vedula, Rajesh Arunachalam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The emergent high-speed growth of precision agriculture requires solid frameworks that will improve sustainability, efficiency, and transparency in managing vineyards. The proposed research suggests a system that uses IoT sensor networks, machine learning models, and Ethereum-based blockchain to solve two important problems: pest identification and the optimization of water resources. The IoT layer was used to implement soil moisture, climate, and imaging sensors to gather real-time data about the vineyard. At the edge, preprocessing and deep learning algorithms were used with a blockchain-enabled Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to provide correct pest detection. At the same time, the timing of irrigation was automated by IoT-enabled soil moisture monitoring, which greatly decreased the amount of wasted water. This ensured integrity of data and trust in the farmers as the Ethereum blockchain layer offered immutable storage, smart contracts to make decisions, and secure events logging. The results of the experiments revealed that the proposed system had a 95.8% pest detection accuracy, which was higher than the traditional and baseline machine learning methods. The efficiency of water management increased too by reducing water consumption by 55.2 % and doubling the crop productivity by 26.8 %t. Moreover, the blockchain application has scored high security of 0.93, confirming that it is a reliable solution even with moderate latency overhead. In this study, the authors emphasize the opportunities of converging the IoT with blockchain and transforming viticulture through sustainable practices, data safety, and efficiency.

title: Trustworthy AI in Cloud Security: Explainability, Robustness, and Regulatory Compliance Frameworks
authors: Wong Nelson Zee Yang, Lau Jim Yung Cheng, Missie Phreoza Dayzency, Jahnavi Anilkumar Kachhia, Vishal Verma, Hussana Johar R B
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Cloud computing has established itself as the backbone of the contemporary digital infrastructure but poses complex cybersecurity problems because of its distributed and multi-tenant characteristics as well as dynamism.
