Intrusion detection, anomaly detection, as well as automated threat mitigation have been greatly enhanced by Artificial Intelligence (AI). Nonetheless, black-box AI systems have problems with transparency, strength, and regulation. In this paper, the author suggests a single Trustworthy AI model that combines Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), adversarial robustness methods, and governance with compliance in a cloud-native security architecture. The models include hierarchy in data processing, threat identification on the basis of AI, explainability on the basis of SHAP, adversarial defense techniques, and modules of compliance monitoring. The experimental analysis shows that model resilience is improved against FGSM, PGD and data poisoning attacks significantly and that it can be interpreted. The offered architecture will ensure the gap between transparency, robustness, and regulatory compliance and will provide a holistic approach to cloud cybersecurity.

title: Robust Ensemble Decision Tree Model for Precise Fault Analysis in Transmission Lines Under Dynamic Conditions
authors: Sunihta Dharnsai, Sushil Kumar Chaturvedi, Nookala Venu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: In power system network, transmission line plays a vital role in providing the link between generating station and load centre. The power from generating station is transferred to the load centre through the transmission line spread over long distances. Depending on the geographical location and related climatic conditions transmission line undergoes several environmental abnormalities like high-speed wind, rain and snowfall. Thus, the probability of fault occurrence in transmission line is higher than the other components of power system network. The reliable operation of any power system network is dependent on the associated protection scheme. The protection schemes should provide necessary information regarding the state of the transmission line (healthy or faulty), so as to help the power system engineers for taking corrective action for ensuring the stable and secured operation of the power system. In addition to the fault detection, a reliable protection scheme should also provide the information regarding the type and location of fault, which further helps in the early restoration of supply post fault. The major challenges associated with distance relay involve lesser relay reach, slow response and higher chances of maloperation during load variation and power swings. The intrusion of harmonics in the current-voltage profile during nonlinear loading further complicates the protection task. In order to address above mentioned issue, the present work is focussed on the development of reliable protection scheme for transmission line protection with immunity against stressed conditions involving variation in loading conditions (linear and nonlinear loading). In this regard, bagging tree algorithm is exploited for the development of the proposed protection schemes for performing the detection and classification of faults in transmission line.

title: FFT and AI-Integrated Signal Analysis for Arrhythmia Detection from ECG Data
authors: K. M. Manikandan, D. Pradhaban, L. Haldurai, L Mirudhuba Jayne, S Vinusha
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The temporal and frequency characteristics of the electrical activity of the heart need to be captured by employing cutting-edge signal processing to properly detect arrhythmias in electrocardiograms (ECGs). The proposed framework leverages the Convolutional-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Conv-BiLSTM) and Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) hybrid model to achieve frequency-conscientious temporal learning. The adaptive frequency attention mechanism of FFT makes it easier to break down electrocardiogram (ECG) signals into discriminative spectral coefficients. The next step is the combination of these coefficients with time-domain embeddings. The large MIT-BIH Arrhythmia dataset is used for validation purposes to ensure accuracy. During the experiments, the developed FFT-Conv-BiLSTM model performs better compared to current designs based on CNN, LSTM having an accuracy (99.6%). An integrated spectral-temporal framework could be a better strategy for handling morphological distortions, noise, and inter-patient variability.

title: Pull-Out Bearing Performance of Transmission Tower Pile Foundation Based on SVM Algorithm and Meter-Wave Radar
authors: Yanzhong Ju, Zheng Wei, Dehong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The conventional techniques find it challenging to quantitatively determine the bearing capacity of the transmission tower piles foundations and the way that they interact with soil around them. According to the support vector machines (SVMs), this paper applies meter-wave radar to nondestructively detect the soil around the piles. Through processing and analysis of the radar echo signals, vital characteristic parameters, the distribution of dielectric constant of the soil, its stratification attributes and localities of possible defects are obtained. The nonlinear problem is reduced to a high-dimensional linear problem with the help of kernel function mapping, and complex mapping relationship between the radar properties of the soil around the piles and the pullout bearing capacity is established. The predictive performance of this method is excellent, and the correlation between predicted and measured values is high: the dielectric constant provides an additional 38.5 percent of prediction; the dielectric constant increases bearing capacity by 45.6 percent when it grows by 12.3 to 24.2. The findings show that the meter-wave radar features and SVMs model is able to provide the pile-soil interaction mechanism, which offers a nondestructive testing technology alternative to monitoring health and safety evaluation of transmission tower foundations.

title: A Comprehensive Framework for Deepfake Detection: Fusing Visual and Audio Modalities with Advanced Deep Learning Models
authors: Surag Sunil, Raseek C, Jayakrushna Sahoo
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The emergence of deepfake technology presents considerable challenges to multimedia authentication. This study introduces an innovative detection framework that analyses both visual and audio data. The methodology combines Convolutional Neural Networks in conjunction with Generative Adversarial Networks. Performance remains robust against sophisticated manipulation techniques. Strategic frame extraction reduces computational demands without sacrificing accuracy. Validation in the Deepfake Detection Challenge Dataset achieved 93.9% accuracy. The dual-modality approach addresses falsified content that undermines digital trust.

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Compressive Strength in High-Performance Concrete
authors: Rupesh Kumar Tipu, Owais Ahmad Shah
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This study explores the application of machine learning models to predict the 28 days compressive strength of high-performance concrete (HPC). A dataset comprising 610 samples with 16 input features and 1 output label was utilized, collected through an extensive literature review. The input features represent crucial components of concrete mixtures, including water content, cement content, slag activity index, and more. The dataset underwent scaling between 0 and 1 to ensure uniformity. Five popular machine learning models including Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest Regression (RFR), Gradient Boosting Regression (GBR), XGBoost, and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) were employed with 10-fold cross-validation. Performance metrics, including R-squared (R2), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), were used to assess the models. The results indicated that the Gradient Boosting Regressor and XGBoost outperformed other models, demonstrating high accuracy and reliability. These findings provide valuable insights for optimizing concrete mix designs and contribute to the advancement of data-driven approaches in concrete technology, fostering efficiency and sustainability in construction practices.

title: Physics-Aware Diffusion Models for Cyclone Damage Assessment Using Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Pathanjali Chowdaiah, Sharmila Chidravalli, Vimuktha E. Salis, Vineetha Edwina Jathanna
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Assessment for cyclone damage with remote sensing images is difficult due to the scarcity of post-event images and corresponding ground-truth data. In this paper, we present a physics-informed diffusion framework for cyclone damage assessment based on pre-cyclone Sentinel-2 images and physics-informed environmental priors. In our method, a lightweight diffusion network is coupled with a physics-informed loss term based on wind physics and geographical properties to spatially predict cyclone damage likelihood with zero post-event ground truth. Unlike photorealistic image restoration tasks, our method predicts interpretable cyclone damage segments overlayed onto pre-event images to spatially highlight likely affected areas. Furthermore, we use SSIM measure–based cyclone damage severity mapping to provide continuous and spatially detailed characterization of structural damage. Validation experiments on real Sentinel-2 images show that our method can spatially coherently and physically consistently localize cyclone damage with high internal consistency between our overlays and SSIM measure–based heatmaps. These outcomes indicate that physics-informed diffusion models have shown promise for very early cyclone damage assessment and risk-informed decision support on cyclone-related disaster management tasks based on remote sensing images.

title: Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting Platform for Low-Power Wearable Vital Signal Monitoring
authors: Kavyashri K, Priscilla A, Santhiya Sri H, R. Parameshwari
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Wearable health monitoring systems enable continuous observation of physiological parameters but are often constrained by battery dependency, frequent recharging, and limited operational lifetime. To overcome these challenges, this paper presents a piezoelectric energy harvesting system for a selfpowered vital signal monitoring application. The proposed system utilizes piezoelectric materials to harvest electrical energy from human motion, enabling sustainable power generation during daily activities. The harvested energy is conditioned through rectification, voltage boosting, and energy storage circuits to deliver a regulated power supply suitable for low-power biomedical sensors. The system demonstrates reliable operation under motion-based energy input, reducing dependence on conventional batteries. Experimental and simulation results validate the feasibility of the proposed piezoelectric energy harvesting approach for sustainable and long-term wearable healthcare applications.

title: RSA-Enhanced Adaptive Noise Cancellation with Multi-Verse Optimizer for Robust ECG Signal Denoising and Classification
authors: Aravinth J, Sidhiksha T S
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signals are widely used in medical diagnosis to study electrical activity of the heart. However, ECG data are sensitive to a variety of noise sources during acquisition, including baseline wander, white Gaussian noise (WGN), artifacts of muscle activity, and power line interference (PLI). These noises distorts the original signal and decrease the effectiveness of later analysis. Noise cancellation methods will improve the quality of the signal by removing unnecessary parts and increasing signal to noise ratio. This facilitates more precise analysis and enhances the performance of algorithms for anomaly detection and classification. This study provides a hybrid approach of combining Multi-Verse Optimizer (MVO)-based feature selection with an RSA-enhanced Adaptive Noise Cancellation (RSA-ANC) method. Multi-Objective Optimization (MVO) is applied to select features retaining key ECG characteristics while simultaneously suppressing noise levels, whereas the RSA-ANC system is applied for efficient denoising functions. Experimental results shows that the proposed system outperforms existing state-of-the-art ECG noise reduction methods as well as standalone MVO. After the denoising step, the system efficiently classifies arrhythmias (ARR) and Normal Sinus Rhythm (NSR) signals, and it shows potential for reliable clinical decision-making assistance.

title: Design of a Sensor-Assisted Five-Bar Finger Rehabilitation Device
authors: J. Sofia Bobby, Madhu Sri K, Lilly P
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Finger rehabilitation plays an important role in restoring hand function following neurological and musculoskeletal impairments, yet conventional therapy methods often lack repeatability and objective performance feedback. This paper presents the design and experimental evaluation of a compact finger rehabilitation device that supports adaptive active-assist therapy. The system is based on a five-bar linkage mechanism fabricated using thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) to achieve physiologically aligned finger flexion—extension while maintaining comfort and mechanical simplicity. The device integrates multiple sensing modalities, including inertial measurement, surface electromyography, force sensing, and galvanic skin response monitoring, to enable closed-loop control and safe operation. Experimental validation was conducted on healthy volunteers using a structured rehabilitation protocol. The results demonstrate stable motion repeatability, controlled finger movement within a physiological range, and reliable sensor-driven adjustment of assistance based on user effort and comfort. Compared to manual finger exercises, the proposed device provides improved motion consistency and quantitative control while maintaining comparable comfort levels. The use of widely available components and standard fabrication methods enables easy replication and integration by other researchers, making the system a practical platform for future rehabilitation studies and clinical evaluation.

title: Design and Implementation of a Low-Noise Signal Acquisition System for Small Animal Physiological Monitoring
authors: L Suganthi, D Gayathri, S Arun Karthick
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Accurate acquisition of physiological signals such as ECG, blood pressure, and temperature is critical in small animal biomedical studies. Analog monitor circuits are normally prone to problems such as signal degradation, motion artifacts, and environmental noise, which affect the quality of the acquired data. The objective of this research work is to develop a wearable signal acquisition circuit that ensures accurate and trustworthy physiological acquisition of small animals. The proposed circuit includes an instrumentation amplifier designed using INA333 with high common-mode rejection and low noise. The amplifier output is followed by high-pass and low-pass filters for efficient signal processing. The processed signals are then converted to digital form using an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) for analysis. The circuit is simulated and developed using Multisim software with a focus on gain maximization, power optimization, and noise reduction. This design will help improve the clarity of signals with integrity and address significant problems in biomedical signal acquisition and therefore will be suitable for continuous monitoring.

title: Hybrid Posture Monitoring System Using Computer Vision and Sensor-Based Analysis
authors: R. Prem Kumar, Abinaya Ramesh, Dharshini Padmanaban, Divya Dharshini Veeraraghavan
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Prolonged improper sitting posture is one of the leading ergonomic issues associated with musculoskeletal disorders and improper spinal alignment. Early identification and constant correction are critical towards ensuring proper spinal alignment and alleviating fatigue. For this purpose, this paper proposes a hybrid posture analysis system that utilizes both force-based sensing and computer vision. The proposed work will make use of force-sensitive resistor sensors connected to an ESP32 microcontroller for identifying pressure variations in seating positions, in addition to a vision module utilizing a webcam and OpenCV with MediaPipe Pose for identifying improper upper limb sitting posture positions such as slouching and neck bending. The solutions derived from these approaches will be processed using a Python programming language setup. The future scope of this work will incorporate an intelligent posture predictor with cloud analysis support.

title: An Efficient Low Power Design of Multiplierless Discrete Cosine Transform
authors: Kavitha S, Swetha M, Swetha M, Thanishka K
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: An effective low-power multiplierless Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) architecture by using merely addition and subtraction instead of power-hungry multiplication processes is presented in this paper. The suggested architecture drastically lowers computing complexity. The DCT design uses optimized arithmetic units to achieve low power consumption and area efficiency. Because it uses fewer adders and subtractors, the multiplierless 8-point DCT structure is appropriate for errortolerant image processing applications. Power consumption, delay, and power-delay product (PDP) are used to assess the architecture. Using an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> block-based method, the suggested DCT is applied for image compression, and simulation results demonstrate significant reduction in power and area while maintaining acceptable performance. The suggested design is ideal for low-power multimedia and medical image processing applications because of these properties.

title: The Role of AES and DES in Securing Data Transmission
authors: Kusuma G S, P Nithya Sree, J R Mahesh, N Yuva Shree, Ankitha A
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Information Technology, Electronics and Intelligent Communication Systems (ICITEICS)
abstract: A comprehensive comparative analysis among various symmetric encryption algorithms, mainly the Advanced Encryption Standard and Data Encryption Standard. The study meticulously dissects the operational mechanics of AES algorithm, shedding light on their key expansion, substitution, permutation, and mixing stages. By juxtaposing the performance, security, and computational overhead of these encryption standards across diverse platforms and scenarios, aiming to discern the relative strengths and potential areas for enhancement among the AES, DES, ASH and similar encryption algorithms. AES and DES serve as key milestones in the evolution of symmetric encryption, each with unique operational mechanisms and levels of resilience to cryptanalysis.

title: Human-Centric and Socio-Technical Design Support for Cyber-Physical Systems: A Systematic Investigation

title: Photocurable Polysiloxane-Based Microfluidic Chip for Convenient Sample Partitioning and Digital Nucleic Acid Assay_supp3-3682335.mp4
authors: Tao Zhang
abstract: Digital nucleic acid assay, capable of absolute quantification with high accuracy, shows promising potentials in various fields. Among others, chamber-based digital assay (cDA) is one of the most important techniques. However, challenges in sample partitioning often cause a cDA chip to suffer from complicated design/fabrication or tedious surface modification. While the polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS)-based chip can partially solve this problem, its slow thermal curing process forms a serious obstacle in chip manufacturing. To address these issues, we herein propose a novel photocurable polysiloxane-based cDA chip (pcDA), which enables not only simple, rapid fabrication, but also convenient sample partitioning under either negative or positive pressure. The chip molding process can be completed in less than 1 minute, and sample partitioning in several minutes. Moreover, both the isothermal method (e.g., digital recombinase polymerase amplification, dRPA) and digital polymerase chain reaction (dPCR) have been achieved, with the proof-of-concept demonstration of E.coli. and S.aureus. detection showing satisfactory results, which features a relatively wide dynamic range of 0-5000 copies/μL and high sensitivity at 1 copy/μL level. It is therefore reasonably believed that the proposed pcDA may well address the current issues of cDA technique, providing a new and viable platform for precise digital nucleic acid assay.

title: Consistency-Aware Local Path Planning for Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships Under Perception Uncertainty: A Field-Validated Framework_supp2-3677261.mp4
authors: Chenguang Liu
abstract: Ensuring consistent and reliable collision avoidance under perception uncertainty remains a major challenge for maritime autonomous surface ships (MASS). This paper proposes a lightweight and consistency-aware local path planning framework, called consistent Kalman virtual potential field (CKVPF), to improve decision robustness in dynamic and uncertain maritime environments. The framework combines virtual potential field-based planning with Kalman-filtered state estimation and introduces a historical consistency constraint to reduce decision fluctuations over time. CKVPF is implemented on a 45-meter-long MASS platform and tested through full-scale sea trials, with onboard real-time execution achieved using a Raspberry Pi 5. The experiments cover representative encounter scenarios, including head-on, overtaking, crossing and mixed-traffic, where the target ships exhibit unstable motion patterns. Results show that CKVPF maintains consistent decision-making and safely avoids collisions, achieving a 48.9% improvement in navigation path stability compared to baseline methods, while maintaining an average planning time of 177.8 ms. These findings demonstrate the method’s practical applicability and real-time performance for autonomous navigation under real-world uncertainty.

title: Consistency-Aware Local Path Planning for Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships Under Perception Uncertainty: A Field-Validated Framework_supp1-3677261.mp4
authors: Chenguang Liu
abstract: Ensuring consistent and reliable collision avoidance under perception uncertainty remains a major challenge for maritime autonomous surface ships (MASS). This paper proposes a lightweight and consistency-aware local path planning framework, called consistent Kalman virtual potential field (CKVPF), to improve decision robustness in dynamic and uncertain maritime environments. The framework combines virtual potential field-based planning with Kalman-filtered state estimation and introduces a historical consistency constraint to reduce decision fluctuations over time. CKVPF is implemented on a 45-meter-long MASS platform and tested through full-scale sea trials, with onboard real-time execution achieved using a Raspberry Pi 5. The experiments cover representative encounter scenarios, including head-on, overtaking, crossing and mixed-traffic, where the target ships exhibit unstable motion patterns. Results show that CKVPF maintains consistent decision-making and safely avoids collisions, achieving a 48.9% improvement in navigation path stability compared to baseline methods, while maintaining an average planning time of 177.8 ms. These findings demonstrate the method’s practical applicability and real-time performance for autonomous navigation under real-world uncertainty.

title: Application of Quantum Heuristic Optimization Algorithm in Image Recognition Feature Selection
authors: Jin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: In image recognition, the high-dimensional image feature leads to the phenomenon of "curse of dimensionality", which greatly limits the training speed and generalization performance of classification model. Therefore, this paper proposes to introduce parallelism and superposition property of quantum computing into feature selection. First, based on the concept of superposition states of quantum bits, the probability distribution of feature subsets is constructed. Second, the quantum rotating gate model is used to simulate the coherent evolution process of quantum states. Finally, the quantum tunneling effect is introduced to design the mutation strategy. Experimental results show that, compared with the traditional feature selection algorithm, the proposed method can achieve 95.2% accuracy on the test set, while only selecting 18.5% of features, and the accuracy will only drop 7.2% in high-dimensional situation. The experimental results also verify that, through the parallel search and quantum tunneling property, the quantum inspired mechanism provides an effective solution to overcome the performance limitation of traditional feature selection algorithm.

title: RECON-RANK - Unified Reconnaissance Automation Tool
authors: Jeyadev U S, Samuel K, Deepica M, Mohamed Adnaan, John Aravindhar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Reconnaissance is the foundational phase of cybersecurity assessments, yet existing tools overwhelm beginners with complex outputs and require experts to correlate multi-source data to identify potential attack paths manually. RECON-RANK is a web-based framework that automates and simplifies this process by combining simulated reconnaissance with AI-driven analysis. The system collects DNS information, open ports, discovered directories, and technology stack details, and processes them using an intelligent agent built with Google’s Genkit and the Gemini model. The AI synthesises findings into a multi-step attack path simulation and provides corresponding Blue Team remediation guidance. Developed using a serverless architecture with Next.js, Firebase Authentication, and Firestore, RECON-RANK offers secure user access, persistent scan history, and interactive attack-graph visualisation. By unifying data collection and automated vulnerability interpretation, the platform addresses key gaps in traditional reconnaissance workflows, enhancing analyst efficiency while making cybersecurity more accessible to learners.

title: An Optimized Gradient Boosting Approach for Efficient Ransomware Detection
authors: Divya Kapil, Atika Gupta, Varsha Mittal, Nidhi Mehra, Vinay Tamta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Ransomware poses a major cybersecurity threat by encrypting critical data and disrupting system operations. Conventional detection techniques based on static signatures or limited behavioral analysis often struggle against advanced variants employing obfuscation and polymorphism. This study proposes a Light Gradient Boosting Machine–based framework that utilizes a hybrid feature representation integrating both static and dynamic behavioral indicators of executable files. Static features are extracted from Portable Executable metadata and section attributes, while dynamic features capture runtime behaviors such as file system, registry, and network activities. The framework incorporates automated feature selection, hyperparameter optimization, and cross-validation to improve learning efficiency and generalization. Comparative evaluation with other ensemble methods highlights its superior adaptability and resilience against evolving ransomware behaviors. The results demonstrate that hybrid feature engineering combined with optimized gradient boosting provides a scalable and effective approach for intelligent ransomware detection.

title: A Blockchain-based Self-Sovereign Identity Framework for Secure and Privacy-Preserving Digital Identity Management
authors: G. Maheswari, G. Siva Koushik Reddy, J. Nikhil Naik, G. Kavya Sree, Cuddapah Anitha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The rapid expansion of digital services has heightened the demand for privacy-oriented, user-centred and security fenced identity management solutions. Traditional centralized identity systems pose a leakage of privacy information, data breaches and single points of failure for users. In order to overcome these challenges in this paper, a blockchain-based self-sovereign identity framework is proposed to achieve these requirements in a way where digital identity management is achieved in a secure environment with decentralization and preserving privacy. The proposed framework combines decentralized identifiers (DIDs) and the verifiable credentials ones (VCs), the smart contracts and the decentralized storages to establish a process of the discrete delivery, tamperproof, and authorization for use. The guarantees of privacy are achieved using cryptographic signatures, integrity validation using hashes and permission control of credential sharings. That framework also tests the effect of the blockchain consensus mechanism on the latency of identity verification and discusses blockchain resilience against Sybil attacks and key compromise scenarios. A performance analysis between the two establishes the enhancements in security, transparency, and scalability compared to traditional centralized systems. In order to support its large-scale deployment, the proposed architecture isolates the on-chain metadata from the off-chain credential storage. The results confirm that the framework is a scalable and privacy-preserving digital identity solution that is suitable for cloud and distributed environments.

title: Design and Analysis of Graphene based Metasurface Unit cells using CST Microwave Studio
authors: Swathi Dasi, G ManmadhaRao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and electromagnetic analysis of graphene-based metasurface unit cells using CST Microwave Studio. Its ultra-thin structure and helpful electromagnetic properties, graphene is used as the metasurface material. Unit-cell geometries in the shapes of circles and rhombuses are designed to study their frequency response and resonance properties. To accurately represent an infinite metasurface, each unit cell's electromagnetic performance is measured under periodic boundary conditions. To understand the related field limitations and resonance mechanisms, electric and magnetic field distributions are analyzed at resonance frequencies in addition to frequency-domain analysis. The simulation results highlight the applicability of graphene metasurface unit cells in advanced electromagnetic applications by showing that unit-cell geometry significantly influences the resonance frequency and field behaviour.

title: Human-Computer Collaborative Music Creation Algorithm Based On Modal Emotion Fusion
authors: Yonghui Ji
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Current artificial intelligence (AI) music creation technology is rapidly developing, but most systems still focus on generating formal elements such as melody and harmony, limiting their ability to capture and convey emotion, a core dimension of musical expression. Problem: Existing human-machine collaborative music solutions often rely on unimodal emotional information (such as audio or text), which can lead to one-sided emotional expression. Furthermore, human-machine interaction is often a one-way process of "machine generation - human modification," lacking dynamic understanding and collaborative adaptation of human creative intent. This paper's structure and content: First, a multimodal emotional dataset (covering audio, lyrics, and user interaction feedback) is constructed. Second, a "modal emotional feature fusion module" is designed to achieve deep cross-modal emotional information fusion through an improved Transformer attention mechanism. Finally, a "dynamic human-machine collaborative creation mechanism" is proposed, which establishes a multi-node manual intervention interface to achieve real-time matching of machine and human creative intent. Experimental results show that the proposed algorithm consumes only 28% of the time in the "detail optimization" phase, significantly lower than unimodal models (45%) and non-collaborative models (38%). However, its real-time performance in complex creative scenarios (such as multi-instrument arrangements) needs to be further improved.

title: A Novel Pseudo-Voltage Enabled Fault Identification and Categorization for DC Microgrids
authors: Debasish Sur, Tapas Kumar Saha, Dola Sinha
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: DC microgrids emerged as a key solution for the integration of unconventional energy resources and to enhance power system reliability. However, providing protection for negative sequence currents is challenging, as there are no natural zero-crossings and fault location has to be quickly accomplished. This paper introduces a new MATLAB-based simulation study of DC microgrid system from fault detection viewpoint-discussion is made based on local measurements and through the use of pseudo voltage as an indicator. Fault and the type of fault can be detected by analysing the changing pattern of pseudo voltage between "positive" and "negative" ends. The exemplary results of this research demonstrate an excellent accuracy and performance of the pseudo voltage-based method for fault detection in a DC microgrid.

title: TERGATE-Warn: Temporal Graph Attention for Equitable Educational Early Warning
authors: C. Sivasankar, Hari Kumar Palani, A. Fathima, K. Gunasekaran, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, G. Vasanthi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Educational dropout continues to be recognized as a critical challenge that affects quality education and exacerbates social inequalities on a global scale. The phenomenon of student attrition represents not only individual academic failure but also systemic inadequacy in educational support mechanisms, particularly within online and distance learning environments where engagement monitoring becomes more complex. In this research, TERGATE-Warn (Temporal-Relational Graph Attentive Network for Early Warning) is introduced as an advanced deep learning architecture specifically developed for the prediction of student academic risk through comprehensive analysis of behavioral and demographic patterns. Within the proposed model, temporal engagement patterns extracted from virtual learning environment interactions are integrated with static demographic features through a sophisticated multi-head attention mechanism, enabling early identification of at-risk students before critical intervention windows close. The architecture employs bidirectional LSTM networks to capture temporal dependencies in both forward and backward directions across extended time periods, while dedicated feature fusion modules combine these temporal representations with encoded static characteristics. The Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD) was utilized in this study, encompassing 2000 student records with 30-week temporal sequences and 12 static features including demographic information and academic performance indicators, through which remarkable performance metrics were obtained. Data preprocessing involved encoding categorical variables, aggregating weekly engagement metrics from daily click counts, and constructing balanced training sets through stratified sampling to ensure representative class distributions. By the TERGATE-Warn architecture, an AUC score of 0.8976, F1-score of 0.8156, and overall accuracy of 82.25% were demonstrated, results that substantially outperform existing baseline models across multiple evaluation metrics. Through comparative analysis conducted against six established methods including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, LSTM, Static GNN, and Multi-modal BERT, superiority was established with improvements ranging from 8% to 18% in AUC scores across different baseline methods. The confusion matrix analysis revealed high true positive rates with acceptably low false positive rates, confirming the practical utility of the system for institutional deployment. Furthermore, the model generates interpretable temporal importance scores and feature-level attributions, addressing critical transparency requirements for educational applications where explainability is essential for stakeholder trust and actionable intervention planning.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Traffic Management in Smart Cities
authors: Abizer Safdari, Omar Hasan Ahmed, Duaa Ghazi Halob, Hussein Basim Furaijl, Mohanad Kareem, Sabeeh Thamir Fadhil
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion continues to be a prominent issue in smart cities that has the potential to affect productivity, safety, and environmental quality in our cities. Fixed-time or rule-based traffic control systems fail to dynamically adapt to surrounding traffic and cannot dynamically optimize flow. This paper proposes an adaptive traffic control framework based on reinforcement learning (RL), which incorporates real-time data from sensors, GPS, and the IoT ecosystem. In this framework, the RL agent is continuously learning optimal signal policies through interaction with the traffic environment, which will avoid delays and increase throughput. The traffic intersections can be modeled as agents and thus be optimized, which means that RL is used to respond to varying densities while optimizing long-term cumulative rewards. Experimental results from our simulated smart city traffic networks found that there was a significant decrease in average waiting times and reduced congestion. Our framework demonstrates a new benchmark for scalable, data-driven, self-learning urban traffic management systems in modern cities.

title: AI-Based Optimization in Energy Storage and Battery Management Systems: Recent Advances and Future Trends
authors: Sajjan Singh, Pallavi Kumari, Sweta Payala, Nagendar Yamsani, Kapil Kumar Sharma
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Energy storage and battery management are key to grid reliability and electrical transportation. AI technology is increasingly utilised for optimising charge-discharge cycles, predicting RUL, and improving energy efficiency. Recent AI methodologies for BMS, such as reinforcement learning, neural networks, genetic algorithms, and fuzzy logic, are surveyed in this review. The paper also highlights studies on SOH/RUL prediction, thermal management, and fault diagnosis of battery systems with different chemistries. At the algorithm level, the BMS AI-based method showed better adaptability and predictive capabilities than the traditional rule-based method, according to comparative studies. Challenges involve data imbalance, real-time integration, and explainability. The paper ends with some trends such as federated BMS, digital twin, and integration of edge AI. The open challenges include the limitations of real-world deployment, cybersecurity, and standardisation.

title: Secure Energy Transactions Using Blockchain and Soft Computing in Smart Grids
authors: Sarvesh Kumar, Abdul Jaleel Abdul Wahhab Abdul Razzaq, Taha Raad Al-Shaikhli, Wasan M. Dawie, Zainab Alassedi, Ali Hatam
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The increasing tendency of the introduction of distributed energy resources and smart metering technologies into the modern power grids has introduced the opportunities and challenges into the effective delivery of the safe, transparent and efficient transactions of the energy. The traditional energy systems are incapable of supporting increasing demands of decentralization, cybersecurity, and real-time processing. It is a paradigm shift of combining the safe and non-alterable energy transfer with the help of Blockchain technologies and the Soft Computing techniques such as Fuzzy Logic, Neural Networks, and Evolutionary Algorithms to turn Smart Grids into smart, smart demand forecast and smart energy delivery. The blockchain encourages permanence of transactions and peer-to-peer exchange of energy compared with soft computing which encourages resilience in decision making in the face of uncertainty. The simulated smart grid data is used to develop and deploy a hybrid model that demonstrates the expediting transactions, security, and load balancing. The results indicate that such a converged solution has the potential to work in the creation of future-proofed, reliable, and smart energy infrastructure.

title: Comparative analysis of different diaphragm shapes for a MEMS resonant pressure sensor
authors: Anshu Sharma, Bhanu Sharma, Rahil Sharma
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Micro-resonant pressure sensors fabricated from MEMS technology are vividly useful in the arenas of biomedicine, military, automotive etc. Resonant pressure sensors account for change in frequency of oscillation in a resonator of the pressure sensor. This variation in the frequency is the measure of the applied pressure. Achievement of optimum values for device parameters is affected by the choice of diaphragm shape. The analysis presented in this paper aims to accentuate the impact of diaphragm shape on stress, vibrations and sensitivity of a vibrating diaphragm. Details pertaining to maximum stress, modal parameters such as resonant frequency, optimum mode and modal difference are obtained by simulation using a finite element analysis tool COMSOL. This paper presents a descriptive parameter analysis for different vibrating diaphragm shapes that can be used in a vibrating diaphragm micro-resonant pressure sensor.

title: An Autonomous Self-Recovery Framework for Network Systems Using Reinforced Causal Decision Intelligence During Cyber Attacks
authors: Het Mehta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The increasing sophistication of cyber-attacks creates serious challenges to the resilience of contemporary network structures, especially in Software-Defined Networking (SDN) and distributed systems. Conventional intrusion detection and response strategies tend to be inadequate in ensuring real-time adaptability and self-recovery. In response to these challenges, this research work introduces AutoShield-Net, a self-recovery system based on artificial intelligence that automatically counteracts cyber-attacks with Reinforced Causal Decision Intelligence (RCDI). RCDI integrates causal inference for identifying root causes, reinforcement learning for adaptive policy tuning, and graph neural networks (GNN) for representing attack propagation over changing network structures. The system itself isolates attacked nodes, reroutes traffic, and adapts recovery tactics through network feedback. Experiments performed on the CICIDS2017 dataset and an emulated SDN environment indicate that AutoShield-Net realizes a recovery success rate of 94.6%, mitigates average mitigation latency by 37%, and enhances network service continuity by 42% over current AI-based approaches. Moreover, the proposed solution keeps the false positive rate less than 2.1% while providing an overall F1-score of 96.3% in detecting and recovering from multi-stage attacks.

title: Cognitive Vulnerability Management: A Self-Learning Framework for Enterprise Cyber Resilience
authors: Durga Bramarambika Sailaja Varri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Enterprise cybersecurity faces unprecedented challenges from sophisticated threats that evolve faster than traditional defense mechanisms can adapt. Cognitive vulnerability management represents a paradigm shift by incorporating self-learning capabilities that enable systems to autonomously identify and respond to emerging threats. This paper proposes a comprehensive framework that integrates cognitive computing principles with vulnerability management to enhance enterprise cyber resilience. The framework leverages machine learning algorithms and self-awareness mechanisms to create adaptive security systems that continuously improve their defensive capabilities. Through analysis of cognitive security components and self-learning architectures, we demonstrate how enterprises can implement autonomous threat detection and response systems. The study reveals that cognitive vulnerability management achieves superior performance compared to traditional approaches, with significant improvements in detection speed and adaptive response capabilities. Our findings provide a foundation for organizations seeking to implement next-generation security frameworks that can evolve alongside emerging cyber threats. The proposed framework addresses critical gaps in current vulnerability management practices by enabling proactive rather than reactive security postures.

title: Next-Generation Cloud-Native Cyber Defense Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Lee Desmond Cherng En, Adabert Alleessa Angelly, Sharun Shasya Qistina, Naveen Kumar Puppala, Nagadevi Bala Nagaram, Nikita Chawla
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The modern digital infrastructures have a considerable high attack surface due to the rapid growth of cloud-native architectures, micro services, and AI-powered applications. The conventional signature-based infrastructures of intrusion detection are inadequate to counter the zero-day attacks, advanced persistent threats (APTs) and dynamic cloud vulnerabilities. In this paper, it is suggested to utilize a next-generation framework of cyber defence based on deep learning intrusion detection and a cloud-native security orchestration and automatic response mechanisms. The suggested system takes advantage of hybrid CNN-LSTM models that are deployed as Kubernetes-based micro services to provide scalable, adaptive, and intelligent threats detection. The experimental findings show better accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score than a traditional machine learning and signature-based methodology. The architecture is consistent with the current DevSecOps and AI-enabled cloud engineering paradigms, which will guarantee scalable and resilient cyber protection.

title: Exploring AI-Enabled Work Environments and Organizational Politics as Predictors of Faculty Turnover Intentions in Asian Higher Education
authors: K R Sowmya, K. Jagannathan, M. Vijayakumar, R. Jessi, P. Prasanna, N. Sugundan
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Workplace plays a key role in every person’s life. Peace in the workplace is mandatory to produce effectively and in order to stay affiliated with the organisation. However, the peace at workplace is disturbed through several ways of which organisational politics is one important aspect. Its prevalence is evident across industries across countries for more than five decades. Across various studies that exists it’s clearly evident that there is no one solution for it and it has to be overcome. The critical focus is to understand the faculty members’ perception of organisational politics working in higher education institutions in an Asian perspective covering five countries. By using one-way ANOVA, Multiple regression analysis and Structural Equation Modelling (SEM), the study investigated how individual attributes and structural dynamics within institutions shape faculty members’ political perceptions and how these perceptions in turn influence their intention to leave their jobs. The research negates the null hypotheses and establishes that organizational politics and turnover intentions are determined by a complicated interaction of institutional and personal factors. The study highlights that unmanaged political environments undermine human capital sustainability and threaten the long-term quality and continuity of higher education institutions. By framing governance transparency, participative leadership, and fair institutional practices as forms of SDG-driven organizational innovation, the study contributes to sustainability discourse by demonstrating how ethical and human-centric management practices can strengthen institutional resilience and support sustainable development goals in higher education.

title: Enhance Data Analysis with Bidirectional Recurrent Neural Networks (BRNNs) for Multi-Scale Financial Market Prediction: A Foundation Model with Adaptive Temporal Attention — A Technical Deep Dive
authors: A.Akilandeswari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Financial market prediction is a serious issue as it is volatile by nature, lacks linear relations, and multi-scaled temporal variations that range between intraday oscillations to long-term trends. Conventional forecasting models do not normally model the two-way temporal relationships and they have problems with adaptive feature extraction at different time horizons. We propose an Enhanced Bidirectional Recurrent Neural Network Foundation Model (Enhanced BRNN-Foundation) to overcome these limitations to enable the implementation of adaptive temporal attention mechanisms to facilitate multi-scale financial prediction. The suggested model is based on both bidirectional LSTM and GRU networks with attention based temporal fusion to identify both forward and backward dependencies in sequential financial data. Substantial experiments carried on real-life financial data show that the Enhanced BRNN-Foundation model also achieves high performance compared to classical deep learning models such as Standard BRNN, LSTM, BiLSTM, GRU, CNN-LSTM, and Transformer models. The proposed model gives the lowest Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.353, Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.352, and Root Mean Squared Error of 0.594 with the highest R 2 score of -23,247.3, which implies its better predictive power and generalization. The model is shown to converge steadily in training and validation and its prediction error is close to zero meaning that the model is accurate in real-time financial prediction. It is a scalable, adaptive and robust financial institutions, algorithm trading systems, risk management frameworks and investment decision making process foundation model.

title: AI in Early Detection of Colorectal Cancer
authors: Nayab Fatima, Pratibha Maurya, Rajiv Pandey
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: AI-powered early detection of colorectal cancer (CRC) has emerged as a transformative solution to a significant public health challenge, particularly in regions experiencing a rise in cases due to lifestyle shifts. This paper explores the potential of AI, including machine learning, deep learning, and image analysis, to enhance CRC screening. We conducted a comprehensive review of existing literature and analyzed the application of advanced algorithms, such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), for interpreting colonoscopy images, histopathology slides, and clinical data. Our findings indicate that these AI tools can identify polyps, abnormal tissue, and pre-cancerous changes with high accuracy, addressing the limitations of traditional, invasive diagnostic methods which often lead to delays and missed early-stage signs. The research also addresses key challenges, including data availability, clinical integration, and ethical considerations. The results underscore the growing role of AI in making CRC screening more accessible and efficient, offering a promising future for cancer diagnostics.

title: Agentic AI and Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Dental Imaging
authors: Rajesh Lingam
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The AI in dental imaging is limited by the centralized data aggregation concept, privacy laws, and disparate clinical data. There are no current studies that demonstrate how agentic AI and federated learning (FL) are complementary, but current literature is still divided into separate studies. This work presents a systematic literature review on peer-reviewed journal and conference articles on agentic AI and federated learning in dental imaging. Research was found within leading scientific databases and evaluated in terms of architectural design, learning and coordination plans, and clinical implementation factors. Architectural patterns that the review recognizes encompass client-based, server-based and hybrid federated architectural designs, which are supported by privacy-sensitive mechanisms, heterogeneity-sensitive optimization and clinician-in-the-loop subunits. Deep learning models built on Federated (especially YOLO) as well as high-level aggregation strategies are common, but largely validation is done via simulation. The most common problems involve non-IID data, integration of workflow, explainability, trust in clinicians and regulatory compliance. The solution to these is system level design beyond algorithmic performance. The review summarizes the current literature in a single taxonomy and conceptual framework and provides a research agenda on scalable, trusted, and implemented agentic federated dental imaging systems.

title: Solar and IoT based Smart Parking Platform for EV Wireless Charging
authors: M. Selvi, Shanmugapriya S, Selestinasarvini R, G Kalaiarasi, Yogitha R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: As the automotive industry rapidly transitions from internal combustion engine vehicles to electric vehicles (EVs), the demand for efficient, sustainable, and intelligent charging infrastructure has become increasingly critical. This project presents a Solar and IoT-Enabled Smart Parking Platform integrated with Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technology to provide a seamless and eco-friendly Wireless EV charging solution. The system employs inductive coupling to wirelessly charge EVs in designated parking slots, eliminating the need for conventional cables. An IoT-based RFID authentication system ensures secure access, enabling automated slot reservation, charging initiation, and fee deduction. A web-based dashboard allows real-time monitoring, reservation management, and access to historical session data, which is stored on a cloud server for analytics and remote accessibility. Solar photovoltaic panels serve as the primary power source, reducing grid dependency and promoting renewable energy utilization. An ESP32 microcontroller manages real-time communication between hardware components and the cloud, ensuring efficient operation. This integrated solution enhances user convenience, operational efficiency, and sustainability, representing a forward-looking model for intelligent and smart Wireless EV charging infrastructure.

title: Enhancement of Underwater Images Using a Tkinter-Based Python Application: A Multi-Level Filter Approach
authors: E Sahaya Chithra, A.Yasmin Jenifer, T.Bhanu Shree, P. Nandhini, Immanuel Prabaharan S, G. Sindhu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This project presents a desktop application developed in Python using the Tkinter library to enhance underwater images degraded by murky water, low contrast, and other distortions. The goal is to improve visual clarity and assist in identifying underwater objects through the integration of several advanced image processing techniques. The application incorporates filters such as Anisotropic Diffusion for smoothing while preserving edges, Color CLAHE for improving contrast, polarization-based correction to reduce surface reflection, Watershed Segmentation for detecting object boundaries, GAT-based denoising to eliminate noise, and dehazing methods to restore visual depth and detail. To assess performance, two image enhancement pipelines—IACPWGED and DIACPWGE—were tested. Among these, the IACPWGED sequence produced better visual results, particularly on highly turbid images. Processed images are also used to create high-quality datasets that support training and evaluation of machine learning models for underwater object detection. The application features a user-friendly graphical interface with options to upload images, select the desired enhancement pipeline, and view the results in real time. The interface uses a responsive grid layout, with buttons, labels, and radio options for interaction. This tool provides an efficient solution for improving underwater images and serves as a base for future research in marine analysis and automated detection systems.

title: Development and Validation of a Multichannel fNIRS System for Cognitive Load Assessment
authors: Dharshini A, Pooja Lakshmi G, Srisanthosh M, Janani Arivudaiyanambi
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The assessment of cognitive load is crucial in understanding how individuals interact with cognitively demanding tasks such as learning, problem-solving, or gaming. Functional Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (fNIRS) provides hemodynamic signals qualitatively from the prefrontal cortex, offering a window into the neural correlates of cognitive workload. In this study, the design, development, and validation of a four-channel fNIRS system are presented to obtain objective, real-time insights into brain activity. The objective of cognitive load assessment has to be met with rigorous validation of the developed system. Hence, the fNIRS system is subject to validation using different strategies. The system features a multi-distance channel configuration, wireless data acquisition, and signal processing. Validation of the developed system has been done in a bright light environment, a dark environment and through a physiological test such as the arterial occlusion test. The dark and ambient light tests related results reveal consistent and expected trends. The same follows for the arterial occlusion test as well. The outcomes of this research are a testament to the usage of the developed system for the assessment of cognitive workload and user-centered design strategies in domains such as education, gaming, and neuroergonomics.

title: Making encodings easier to adopt
authors: Jialin Ding
abstract: None

title: A UCIe Compatible Repair Scheme for the Clustered Faults in the Hexagonal Array of Interconnect Lanes
authors: Zhaohong Lin, Xiaole Cui, Juncheng Pu, Huan Yang
abstract: The chiplet-based 2.5D/3D multi-die integrated chips have numerous inter-die interconnect lanes, which may suffer from the faults in Through Silicon Vias (TSVs) or micro bumps. The faults are usually clustered in some regions. The redundant lanes are introduced for repairing, to maintain the product yield. The hexagonal array of lanes offers higher interconnect density than the rectangular array, but it requires more redundant lanes if faults are located within the same module. In the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) standard, each Advanced Package module contains 32 data lanes and 2 redundant lanes, which limit the repair rate for the clustered faults. This work proposes a UCIe-compatible repair scheme which increases the probability of distributing faulty lanes in different modules. It achieves a higher repair rate for the clustered faults with a low proportion of redundant lanes. Simulation results show that for 8 faults in various cluster windows, the proposed scheme achieves a repair rate above 98% while consuming less area than previous approaches.

title: On the Insecurity of Internally Sampled Honeyword Schemes
authors: Ding Wang, Tingwei Fan, Fei Duan, Zhenduo Hou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Honeywords are plausible-looking decoy passwords associated with each user’s real password to timely detect password leakage. The more indistinguishable the honeywords are, the more secure a honeyword scheme is. However, honeyword schemes that externally generate honeywords can only approximate, but not equate, the distribution of user-chosen passwords, so they are unlikely to achieve the ideal indistinguishability. To address this issue, internally sampled honeyword schemes that sample honeywords from other users’ passwords have been proposed. In this work, we first reveal two critical security and two critical usability flaws in existing internally sampled honeyword schemes, i.e., Honeyindex (TDSC’16) and Superword (COSE’21). We then formalize a generic framework for sound internally sampled honeyword schemes, and propose variants for both Honeyindex and Superword. To principally evaluate the security of our framework, we propose Bayesian and intersection attack theories leading to attackers’ optimal distinguishing strategies, and evaluate them under three major attacker models each with varied capabilities (e.g., using leaked datasets and users’ personal information). Evaluation results show that, when 40 sweetwords are associated with each user (as recommended at IEEE S&P’22), with only one guess per account, the basic attacker’s success rate can reach 3.82%∼4.12%, and she can identify 4.31%∼5.04% of all real passwords with 104 honeyword login attempts, breaking the ideal 2.50%(=1/40) security. Two more advanced attackers can identify 18.6%∼44.8% and 20.6%∼43.6% of all real passwords in 104 honeyword login attempts, respectively. When multiple password files are available, the intersection attack alone identifies 18.3%∼18.6% of real passwords. We also explore the impacts of denial-of-service attacks. In all, this work reveals the inherent insecurity of internally sampled honeyword schemes.

title: Entropy Based DDoS Detection and Mitigation Methods in SDN: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Safayat Jamil Siam, Mohd Rashidi Che Beson, Ku Nurul Fazira Ku Azir, Md Maruf Hassan
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Software Defined Networking (SDN) presents a novel method of network administration that could improve security and streamline operations. However, it has certain drawbacks like any new technology which could affect its usability and availability. Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks are common threat for SDN controllers. Although they cannot be completely avoided, but their impact can be reduced. For this reason, it is necessary to develop a practical remedy for recognizing and reducing the impact of DDoS attack in SDN environment. In that case, entropy can play a vital role. Entropy is the level of unpredictability in a dataset. In networking and SDN, entropy is used to analyze traffic patterns and spot network abnormalities from average performance. In recent years, many researches have been emphasized on the application of entropy for developing mechanisms to spot and lowering the impact of DDoS attack in SDN environment. This study's purpose is to evaluate the articles which discuss the application of entropy for developing DDoS identification and prevention strategy and find out the best solution.

title: Performance Analysis of 3-D FCC-MDW Codes for Cardinality Enrichment in OCDMA Systems
authors: A.J. Rupok, M.R.C. Beson, K.N.F. Azir, B. Nakarmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: We propose the three-dimensional Flexible Cross-Correlation – Modified Double Weight (3-D FCC-MDW) OCDMA scheme that integrates one-dimensional codes over the wavelength, time, and spatial dimensions to increase number of users and reduce interference. OCDMA systems often suffer from MAI, PIIN, and limited user capacity, which severely degrade BER performance; thus, an efficient multidomain coding approach is essential to address these challenges. The impacts of noises are assessed when evaluating system performance at STM-16. At a BER of 10⁻⁹, the system supports up to 200 users, demonstrating cardinality increases of 122%, 614%, and 769% compared to 3-D MD, 3-D DCS/MD, and 2-D FCC-MDW, respectively. The proposed scheme also shows the best receiver sensitivity, achieving reliable operation at –22 dBm. Analysis of weight configurations confirms that optimal multidomain weight allocation, especially (3,2,2), enhances interference suppression and user enrichment for OCDMA system.

title: Application-Aware Early-Exit Fault Classification for Video Decoder Using Miter-Based Analysis
authors: Jun-Tsung Wu, Tong-Yu Hsieh
abstract: Traditional structural testing often overlooks the actual impact of hardware faults on system-level applications, especially in error-tolerant components like video decoders. This work presents an application-aware fault classification framework that integrates a miter-based architecture with an early-exit strategy, using object detection accuracy as the evaluation target. By monitoring the accumulation of output errors during video decoding, the framework identifies application-critical faults early-typically within the first six frames of a 300-frame sequence-based on a predefined error threshold. Experimental results show that this approach achieves 100% classification precision while reducing simulation time by over 98%.

title: EMBER: A Cycle-based Framework for Early-Stage Reliability Assessment in Parametric RTL Designs
authors: Alessandro Veronesi, Leticia Maria Bolzani Pöhls, Michele Favalli, Milos Krstic, Davide Bertozzi
abstract: Modern trends towards higher architectural complexity and smaller technology nodes do not align with the requirement for reliability that many application domains expose. While system robustness remains one of the most critical aspects for missioncritical application domains, the currently available tools allow designers to accurately investigate the reliability only at the late design stages, limiting the effectiveness of their intervention in addressing architectural vulnerabilities.In this context, this paper presents a novel framework for early-stage reliability assessment that enables fast evaluations to guide the RTL design process, without compromising analysis quality or relying on imprecise high-level fault injection models. In the presented analysis, the paper shows how the proposed framework leads to a significant time saving when targeting highly parametric hardware designs, achieving up to 79 x compile time reduction, and up to $37.6 x$ simulation time reduction when compared to other state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Surrogate-Assisted Modal Model Validation of a Flexible Flying Wing Aircraft
authors: Adarsha Adhikari, Li Wei
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: This study presents a surrogate-assisted framework for modal model validation of a flexible flying wing aircraft using experimentally derived frequencies. A detailed geometric model was created in SolidWorks and analyzed in ANSYS to obtain baseline modal characteristics, which showed discrepancies when compared with Curve-fit Frequency Domain Decomposition (CFDD) data. Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (MRMR) sensitivity analysis was used to reduce nine material parameters of the composite wing structure to five dominant inputs. Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) was employed to generate training data for Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) surrogate models. Surrogate modeling was adopted to enable fast evaluations during optimization and avoid repeated finite element analyses. The surrogates were integrated with MATLAB’s fmincon algorithm to minimize the mean squared error between predicted and experimental first five CFDD extracted natural frequencies of the flexible wing. The optimized parameters reduced the MSE from 15.47 Hz<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> to 13.60 Hz<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, demonstrating improved agreement for the dominant lower-order modes relevant to structural dynamic validation of flexible flying wings.

title: Keynote--What’s in a Name? Why the Meaning of Privacy in PETs Matters
authors: Helen Nissenbaum
abstract: The broad intentions behind privacy enhancing techniques (PETs) are admirable. However, their success requires more than a sound technical implementation of high level design principles; it requires a sound conception of privacy. Based on work with Kirsten Martin and Vitaly Shmatikov, I argue that principles popularized by industry actors are misaligned not only with the philosophical definition of privacy as contextual integrity, but also with actual privacy expectations, revealed through factorial vignette user-studies. Our findings underscore why successful privacy-enhancing systems must begin with the explicit adoption of a meaningful conception of privacy.

title: IEEE Standard for Local and Metropolitan Area Networks--Frame Replication and Elimination for Reliability Corrigendum 1: Technical and Editorial Corrections
authors: N/A
abstract: This corrigendum to IEEE Std 802.1CB™-2017 provides technical and editorial corrections.

title: Transformative Learning in the Gen-AI Landscape: Challenges and Capacities for Organizational Evolution
authors: Umair Tanveer, Thinh Gia Hoang, Shamaila Ishaq, Jing Dai
abstract: Generative artificial intelligence (Gen-AI) marks a transformative phase for organizations, creating both major opportunities and challenges. Anchored in organisational learning theory, this study examines how organizations adopt Gen-AI by unpacking the related challenges, learning processes, and required capabilities. Using a multiple-case study design with semistructured interviews across four pioneering organizations, the research highlights the importance of adaptability, rapid decision-making, risk tolerance, and a strong learning culture in shaping organizational learning during Gen-AI adoption. The findings show that Gen-AI creates distinctive learning demands compared with earlier technologies. Its cognitive reasoning and adaptive functionalities require organizations to engage in iterative, continuous learning to integrate these tools into both strategic and operational systems. The study offers practical guidance for organizations seeking to overcome adoption challenges, strengthen learning cultures, and realize Gen-AI's transformative potential. It also contributes to the broader Gen-AI discourse by providing a theoretically grounded and empirically supported roadmap for managing AI integration, including strategies such as iterative feedback loops, risk-tolerant decision-making, and embedding ethical considerations in AI governance.

title: DuetGS: Two-Stage Controllable 3D Human Reconstruction from Dual Images_supp2-3685443.mp4
authors: Wan Bo
abstract: Creating realistic and fully detailed 3D human models using a minimal number of views has long been a challenging goal in 3D human reconstruction. Reconstructing a realistic human model from only two images (front and back) is particularly difficult due to the limited 3D information available, leading to two major challenges: (1) it is difficult to establish spatial consistency for reconstruction due to the lack of sufficient images for reliable matching, and (2) incomplete field of view results in missing color information. To address these challenges, we propose DuetGS, a novel pipeline that divides the reconstruction process into two stages: geometry reconstruction and color reconstruction. For geometry reconstruction, we employ a data-driven neural network to recover a full-body mesh from the front and back images, providing the spatial positioning for Gaussians. For color reconstruction, we adapt Gaussian Splatting and integrate our proposed unsupervised color propagation method to establish the color details of the Gaussians. Furthermore, our Gaussians are directly mapped to the mesh, allowing us to control their rotation and translation through mesh manipulation. This mapping ensures compatibility with various animation techniques. Extensive experiments on the THUman, CustomHumans, and PeopleSnapshot datasets demonstrate that our approach outperforms existing methods in terms of reconstruction accuracy and visual quality.

title: Adaptive Fuzzy Logic Controllers for Smart Grid Load Balancing in Urban Energy Systems
authors: Koushik Chakraborty, Paras Nath Singh, Hameed H. Khalaf, Saleh Naji Sreseh, Maki Mahdi Abdulhasan, Dhiraj Kapila
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Smart grids play a crucial role in next-generation urban energy systems, which incorporate renewable energy resources, real-time monitoring, and energy management interventions. A critical challenge for these power systems is load distribution under variable energy demand and the inherently dynamic nature of renewable energy sources. In this paper, we propose the application of an active feedback load controller (AFLC) to enable load balancing in urban power systems. The system electively adjusts control logic using feedback, in real-time, to increase optimal load balance and grid reliability. Through simulation experiments, we demonstrate that the proposed controller provides superior system stability and response times over alternative controllers in the literature.

title: Automated Intracranial Hemorrhage Detection in Head CT Images Using Hybrid Texture Features and Ensemble Classification
authors: Chinmayee Pati, Soumya Ranjan Nayak, Alok Kumar Jagadev
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Intracranial Haemorrhage is a critical medical issue that must be detected and treated as soon as possible to avoid serious brain damage. Head computed tomography (CT) is a popular imaging technique for detecting hemorrhagic samples to allow early diagnosis and planning the treatment. However, interpreting CT scans can be difficult and varied amongst radiologists. Therefore, in recent years, the use of machine learning (ML) techniques in medical imaging has shown promise for improving diagnostic accuracy and efficiency. This research focusses on advanced feature engineering by extracting gray-level co-occurrence matrix (GLCM) and fractal features, particularly the Hausdorff dimension, from head CT scans. These features capture important textural properties and complicated structures of the images, providing useful features for model training. A dataset comprising these engineered features was created to train a series of classifiers. An ensemble technique using a voting classifier that combines the strengths of K-Nearest Neighbours, Decision Tree, and Random Forest algorithms was constructed, along with thorough hyperparameter tuning and cross-validation. The resulting model achieved an impressive accuracy of 92.% and an area under the curve (AUC) score of 90%, with the feature importance plot illustrating the significant contributions of the extracted GLCM and Hausdorff dimension features. This demonstrates the potential of advanced feature engineering-based ML implementation to improve haemorrhage identification in head CT imaging, as well as the relevance of feature engineering in the development of robust automated diagnostic tools.

title: Multimodal EEG and Deep Learning based Autism Spectrum Disorder Detection
authors: R K Yadav, Premananda Sahu, Prakash Srivastava
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder that is characterized by impaired communication and social interaction. Conventional diagnostic methods generally depend on behaviour observation, and clinical practices, which do not always receive priority due to their subjective assessment, are time-consuming and do not allow early diagnosis. Recent developments in neuroimaging and artificial intelligence research have encouraged the composition of EEG with deep learning techniques for the objective, data-driven diagnosis. In this work, multimodal EEG-Deep learning model for the classification of ASD and non-ASD that overcomes the limitations like small dataset, demographical bias and non-medical utility observed in previous research. For this reason, the authors have collected all the data from Autism Brain Imaging Data Exchange (ABIDE). The authors proposed a novel multimodal relevance-constrained transfer learning model that combines different types of brain features like time–frequency decomposition, entropy measures, and connectivity maps and multimodal fusion methods to preserve both spectral and spatial properties in the brain. Deep learning models, particularly CNNs with and without attention mechanism were used for automatic feature extraction and classification. In order to prevent overfitting, dropout, L2-regularization and EEG-specific data augmentation were used. Cross-validation by subject enabled generalization and stability across subjects. Performance metrics such as accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F1-score and area under curve (AUC) were computed by stratified evaluation. The proposed model reached overall classification accuracy of 99.45%, and with sensitivity and specificity higher than 98%, which performs better over traditional machine learning baseline like SVM, KNN. Theverdictsspecifies that multimodal EEG DL is a robust and interpretable method for early ASD diagnosis, that represents the potential to be combined into clinical settings.

title: Machine Learning Validation of a Physical Prime Random Number Generator
authors: Mauricio J. Ferreira, Nuno A. Silva, Armando N. Pinto, Nelson J. Muga
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Random prime number generation is crucial for the implementation of several encryption and signature protocols in cryptographic applications. Recently, Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs) have emerged as a solution to obtain information-theoretically secure randomness, setting them apart from algorithmic generators. Nonetheless, their output is still prone to deterioration under a flawed implementation and thus requires continuous statistical validation. This process is uniquely challenging for prime number generators since they cannot be submitted to the traditional testing suites, which are primarily designed to test uniform pseudorandom sources.
