title: An Efficient Motion Parameters Estimation Method for Ground Maneuvering Targets in Wide-Swath MIMO-SAR
authors: Ling Qiao, Mingwei Shen, Guodong Jin, Di Wu, Daiyin Zhu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurately estimating the motion parameters of ground maneuvering targets in airborne wide-swath multiple-input multiple-output synthetic aperture radar (MIMO-SAR) is challenging under low pulse repetition frequency and strong clutter. Rather than resampling the periodic nonuniform sampling (PNS) sequence for parameter estimation, the proposed method confines spectrum reconstruction to clutter preprocessing and exploits the restored PNS structure to efficiently estimate radial acceleration and radial velocity. First, the azimuth degrees of freedom of the MIMO-SAR are leveraged to construct equivalent dual-channel data, and conjugate-symmetric difference operators suppress clutter, improving the signal-to-clutter-plus-noise ratio while preserving the PNS structure. Then, a parallel differential-phase model with a common slope and group-dependent intercepts is formulated, and a group-coherent generalized likelihood ratio test (GC-GLRT) estimator incorporating coherence-adaptive window selection is designed for robust estimation of radial acceleration. By treating radial velocity as a nuisance parameter through intra-group coherent integration and inter-group noncoherent combination, the original two-dimensional velocity–acceleration search is reduced to a one-dimensional acceleration search. Finally, after acceleration compensation, multi-delay differential-phase observations derived from the PNS structure are combined to form closure-phase observations, yielding an ambiguous radial velocity estimate. The velocity ambiguity is resolved using reference-delay observations together with a coherence-weighted decision rule, and the final estimate is refined through a weighted least-squares (WLS) fit to the residual phase. Simulation results show accuracy comparable to the generalized Radon-Fourier transform (GRFT) with substantially reduced computational complexity, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Towards Reliable Disparity Estimation: Uncertainty-Aware Stereo Matching Framework for Satellite Images
authors: Jiahao Rao, Rui Liu, Jun Chen, Xin Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: As a critical task in the field of Earth observation, satellite images stereo matching (SISM) aims to estimate disparity from a pair of epipolar rectified images and reconstruct the 3D structure of the Earth’s surface. However, the local features in repetitive patterns and texture-less regions of satellite images (collectively referred to as ill-posed regions) are highly similar, causing existing SISM methods to produce unreliable disparity estimations. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel uncertainty-aware framework for SISM (UA-SISM). First, a disparity estimation network is employed to construct a disparity space containing multiple coarse disparity samples. To quantitatively analyze the reliability of the model’s predictions, we model the disparity as Gaussian distribution and design a distribution-level uncertainty estimator (DLUE). The DLUE computes distribution-level uncertainty by comparing differences among multiple disparity samples. Based on this, we derive a specific loss function for estimating distribution-level uncertainty. Next, we adopt an uncertainty-error alignment (UEA) strategy to promote the co-convergence of disparity error and distribution-level uncertainty, thereby improving the reliability of the model’s predictions. Finally, we compute sample-level uncertainty to refine the disparity in the disparity space. This further enhances model robustness in ill-posed regions. Extensive experiments on two publicly available SISM datasets show that UA-SISM achieves state-of-the-art performance and obtains optimal results in ill-posed regions of satellite images. Our code is available at: https://github.com/Henryrjh/UA-SISM.

title: A Multi-Axis Convolution Network for Small and High-Aspect-Ratio Object Detection in Remote Sensing
authors: Feng Zhang, Jiaofeng Wang, Jie Mao, Chunru Dong, Qiang Hua, Jie Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing object detection (RSOD) is vital for tasks ranging from environmental monitoring to infrastructure analysis. However, due to the unique perspective and extensive spatial coverage of remote sensing images, feature attenuation and localization errors occur, posing significant challenges for reliable detection of small objects and High-Aspect-Ratio (HAR) objects in object detection tasks. To address these limitations, we propose the Multi-Axis Convolution Network (MACNet), a novel architecture designed to enhance feature representation for small objects and improve shape discrimination for HAR objects. MACNet introduces two innovative modules: the Large-Kernel Dual-Branch (LKDB) module, which utilizes large-kernel convolutions and parallel spatial and channel attention to enhance contextual understanding and mitigate feature attenuation of small objects, and the Direction-aware Convolution (DC) module, which employs dual orthogonal strip convolutions to capture distinctive shape patterns that is crucial for HAR objects. Additionally, we propose a Dynamically weighted KFIoU (DKFIoU) loss function to balance the contribution of center point loss and IoU loss. DKFIoU adaptively weights the center point loss and a Gaussian-approximated IoU based on object size and aspect ratio, thereby improving the detection of HAR objects and prioritizing small objects by reducing localization errors. Comprehensive evaluations on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that MACNet significantly outperforms current state-of-the-art methods, particularly for small objects and HAR objects. Ablation studies provide valuable insights into the efficacy of our proposed components. https://github.com/ww66671/mac net.

title: A Dynamic Frequency Selection Module for Geometry-Aware Building Extraction in VHR Remote Sensing Image
authors: Yi Huang, Siyu Hong, Shaohua Lei, Hui Chen, Peijun Du, Jinlong Sun, Haitao Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Reliable measurement of building footprints, encompassing precise boundary localization and planar area estimation, is fundamental to urban applications using very high-resolution (VHR) remote sensing imagery. Conventional semantic segmentation pipelines optimize pixel-wise accuracy but often suffer from blurred edges and shape distortions, leading to biased geometric measurements. Although recent frequency-domain methods attempt to recover structural details, they typically rely on static filters that fail to adapt across diverse building topologies and complex background clutter. To overcome this limitation, we propose the Dynamic Frequency Selection Module (DFSM), a plug-and-play adaptive frequency selection module for geometry-aware building extraction. DFSM parameterizes the frequency cutoff into dynamically learnable, continuous variables, functioning as a content-aware filter. It adaptively modulates the spectral passband—widening it to suppress high-frequency noise for small, cluttered buildings, and narrowing it to effectively sharpen edge contours for large-scale structures. Subsequently, an attention-based fusion mechanism integrates these frequency representations with spatial context, optimizing the inherent trade-off between regional area integrity and boundary precision. Comprehensive evaluations on the ISPRS Vaihingen and WHU datasets demonstrate that DFSM consistently enhances both CNN- and Transformer-based segmenters. Beyond improving conventional regional metrics, DFSM elevates Boundary-Intersection over Union (Boundary-IoU) by 3%–4% and significantly reduces the 95% Hausdorff distance. Notably, DFSM outperforms explicit boundary-supervised baselines without requiring auxiliary edge annotations or multi-task loss tuning. By systematically tightening the 95% coverage intervals for geometric biases, DFSM provides a robust, architecture-agnostic solution for geometry-aware building extraction in remote sensing.

title: Gradient-Based Topology Optimization of Permanent Magnet Electrodynamic Suspensions Using a 3-D Hybrid Analytical Model
authors: Louis Beauloye, Bruno Dehez
abstract: This paper presents a novel gradient-based topology optimization method for the design of permanent magnet electrodynamic suspensions, using a 3-D hybrid analytical model. It enables the exploration of new magnet configurations, with the aim of improving both the lift-to-weight and lift-to-drag ratios. The results reveal that modifying the magnet arrangement can change the trade-off between these competing objectives. In particular, a configuration with alternating poles in both the longitudinal and the transverse directions offers significantly better performance than the classical single-direction arrangement. The method is further validated on a small-scale experimental setup. Six Pareto optimal topologies are identified, manufactured, and tested. The resulting front is reconstructed experimentally and aligns with simulation predictions.

title: A Novel DFT-Based Flexible RSMA Scheme for 6G Networks
authors: Kenza Abela, Ayoub Ammar Boudjelal, Shaima Abidrabbu, Hüseyin Arslan
abstract: Sixth-generation (6G) networks require multiple-access schemes that jointly provide high spectral efficiency (SE), low receiver complexity, and robustness in frequency-selective channels. Although rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) offers flexible interference management, its practical deployment is often limited by reliance on successive interference cancellation (SIC). This paper proposes a flexible RSMA framework in which the common stream is spread over configurable discrete Fourier transform (DFT) blocks, while private streams are transmitted using conventional orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM). By jointly tuning the DFT block structure and adopting a peak-aware power allocation strategy, the proposed design mitigates interference and reduces reliance on SIC. Simulation results demonstrate improved bit error rate (BER) performance while maintaining SE comparable to RSMA and NOMA under perfect channel state information (CSI), and significantly outperforming these baselines under imperfect CSI conditions.

title: Reciprocity-Based Sensing for Bistatic ISAC
authors: Jinho Choi
abstract: We propose a reciprocity-based bistatic sensing framework for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems, exploiting the asymmetry in angular resolvability between spatially separated nodes. By leveraging reciprocal transmissions, the approach enables nodes with limited angular resolution to resolve closely spaced targets, while matched-filter-based delay refinement facilitates reliable target pairing at the central unit (CU). Simulation results show that the reciprocity-based framework effectively suppresses ghost targets, enhances multi-target association, and achieves high-accuracy bistatic localization, even in scenarios with asymmetric node capabilities.

title: Movable-Antenna Enabled Robust Vehicular Consumer Networks Under Imperfect CSI
authors: Xuhui Zhang, Chunjie Wang, Wenchao Liu, Huijun Xing, Jinke Ren, Zheng Xing, Yanyan Shen
abstract: The accelerating advancement of intelligent transportation systems has established consumer-oriented vehicular networks (CVNs) as a critical infrastructure for next-generation connected mobility. However, the high mobility of vehicular users (VUs) introduces significant channel state information (CSI) uncertainty, which severely undermines the performance of conventional fixed-position antenna systems. To address this, this paper explores the deployment of movable-antennas (MAs) to enhance communication robustness in CVNs under imperfect CSI conditions. We develop a joint optimization framework that dynamically coordinates the spatial positioning of MAs and transmit beamforming at the base station, with the objective of maximizing the worst-case sum rate across all VUs. The problem is formulated as a non-convex max–min optimization problem, subject to bounded CSI estimation errors, transmit power limits, and physical constraints on antenna displacement. By adopting an alternating optimization strategy, the original problem is decomposed into tractable subproblems, solved via techniques including the S-Procedure, Schur complement, and successive convex approximation. Numerical evaluations confirm that the proposed approach achieves substantial gains over existing benchmarks in terms of worst-case throughput.

title: Digital Twin Framework for Interpretable Aero-Engine RUL Prediction via Multi-Branch LSTM and Unity Visualization
authors: Anping Wan, Zengzhen Zhu, Khalil Al-Bukhaiti, Rui Yin, Jiantao Yuan, Xiaomin Cheng, Xiaosheng Ji
abstract: Aero-engines, as pivotal components in aviation, demand robust prognostics and health management (PHM) to mitigate degradation risks and optimize maintenance amid complex operational stresses. Conventional scheduled strategies often yield inefficiencies, prompting the integration of digital twins (DTs) for enhanced predictive capabilities. This study introduces a comprehensive DT-based aero-engine health management framework, stratified into physical, data, model, and application layers, augmented by a Unity-driven 3D visualization module for immersive virtual-real interactions. The physical layer captures real-time sensor data from turbofan components, feeding into the data layer for preprocessing, fusion, and lifecycle storage. The model layer employs a novel physically decoupled multi- branch long short-term memory (LSTM) network with attention fusion, segmenting sensor features (e.g., temperature, pressure) into specialized branches to discern degradation channels while dynamically weighting salient contributions. Outputs manifest in the application layer via interactive dashboards for RUL fore- casting, anomaly alerts, and maintenance simulations. Validated on the N-CMAPSS DS01 dataset, the model attains an R2 of 0.9376 and RMSE of 5.76 cycles, outperforming single-branch LSTM (R2 = 0.7306) and Transformer (R2 = 0.7810) baselines by leveraging physical priors for superior trend capture and interpretability. Unity integration enables real-time 3D mapping of predictions, fostering user-centric diagnostics. This framework advances PHM by harmonizing deep learning with DTs, boosting accuracy, usability, and decision-making in civil aviation. Limitations in multi-condition scenarios underscore avenues for future uncertainty modeling and lightweight adaptations. Note to Practitioners This digital twin (DT) framework advances aero-engine prognostics and health management (PHM) by delivering interpretable remaining useful life (RUL) predictions to combat inefficiencies in traditional scheduled maintenance, which costs aviation billions in downtime and overhauls. Structured across physical, data, model, and application layers, it processes real- time turbofan sensor data (e.g., temperature, pressure) to support proactive decision-making. The core multi-branch LSTM with attention fusion decouples features by physics (e.g., temperature branches), dynamically weighting degradation signals, such as turbine exhaust, for superior accuracy and transparency. On the N-C MAPSS DS01 dataset, it reaches R2 = 0.9376 and RMSE = 5.76 cycles, surpassing the single-branch LSTM (R2 = 0.7306) and Transformer (R2 = 0.7810) baselines by 20–28%. Benefits include 10-15% longer on-the-wing times and reduced inspections. Unity 3D visualization maps RUL onto interactive models, enabling engineers to identify anomalies, simulate maintenance, and receive alerts, bridging AI to actionable insights without requiring expertise. Deployable via edge cloud for avionics, it scales to variable flight sizes; future work will add uncertainty modeling for noisy data. This empowers predictive stewardship, boosting safety and economics.

title: Advanced Surface EMG Analysis of Biceps and Triceps Activation Across Performance Levels During a Prolonged Forearm Plank
authors: Nithya Rajagopalan, Harini V, Ramya K
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Surface electromyography (sEMG) is a common tool for the evaluation of neuromuscular activation and endurance during sustained tasks, which apply to upper limb rehabilitation. In our current study, the muscles of activation that were studied were the biceps brachii and triceps brachii, when the participants were engaged in a sustained forearm plank exercise of different degrees of proficiency. There were thirteen healthy participants who were considered novice, intermediate or advanced depending on their plank-hold durations. The surface electromyography (sEMG) data collection and analysis were done with a BIOPAC MP45 system and then processed in MATLAB, where root-mean-square (RMS) amplitude was chosen as the most significant measure. Unpaired ANOVA and Kruskal-Wallis tests showed statistically no significant relationship between the mean RMS value, yet the effect-size analysis showed a big range of variation among the performance levels. Correlation coefficients between trials were moderate, while trial-by-trial RMS amplitude trends suggested fatigue-related compensatory activation in novice participants and stable activation in advanced participants. These results show that metrics of variability and fatigue-related changes are useful for gaining practical insight into sustained isometric neuromuscular performance, independent of statistical significance. All signal processing and feature extraction were performed using MATLAB R2024b (MathWorks, USA Variability, trial to trial consistency and fatigue related activation are focused on the study as opposed to relying on single-trial amplitude alone.

title: Quantitative Electrochemical Detection of Paracetamol Using 3D Printed Electrodes
authors: Akanksha Patel, Prakash Deodhar, Sanjeev Kumar, Amit Kumar Singh, M. Marieswaran
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The study presents the design, fabrication and electrochemical detection of paracetamol using 3D-printed conductive electrodes. Thermoplastic composite Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene (ABS) reinforced with a Carbon Black (CB) filament was used to fabricate the working, reference and counter electrodes using Fused Deposition Modelling (FDM) technique. Paracetamol drug is commonly used as analgesic and antipyretic in pharmaceutical tablet formulations. The electrochemical technique - Differential Pulse Voltammetry (DPV) is used for the detection of analytes (drugs). Well-defined and sharp oxidation peaks were observed at lower concentrations (1.5 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.5 \mu ~\mathrm{g} / \text{ml}$</tex>), while at higher concentrations (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0} \boldsymbol{\mu} \mathbf{g} / \mathbf{m l}$</tex>), the peak current stabilized. A 3D printed sensor displayed two linear calibration ranges for its response. A low-concentration range showed a high value of sensitivity, which is linked to the high rate of electron transfer at the active sites. Additionally, the high concentration region showed low sensitivity, resulting from saturation of the active sites of electrodes by the oxidized forms of paracetamol. This work highlights the possibilities offered by 3D-printed conductive polymer electrodes. Such electrodes are low-cost, customizable and eco-friendly alternatives to traditional electrochemical sensing electrodes. The lower concentration sensing capability reported in this work aims towards the improvement of pharmaceutical quality control and point-of-care diagnostics.

title: A Lightweight Deep Learning Model for Non-Invasive Bilirubin Grading by Multi-Colour Space Images and Metadata Fusion
authors: P Premalatha, Thiyam Beeta Deepa, S. Madhumithaa
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Neonatal bilirubin assessment typically relies on an invasive blood test, which is restricted by continuous monitoring and early detection in limited-resource environments. This work proposed an automated, non-invasive bilirubin grading using a deep learning model, which combined multi-colour space neonate image features and metadata fusion. The non-bilirubin and bilirubin neonate images were pre-processed to extract RGB, HSV, YCbCr, Lab, and grayscale features. These extracted features were then utilized to generate pseudo-labels for three bilirubin severity grades through K-means clustering. The proposed framework, combined with a lightweight MobileNet CNN model and trained independently with Adam and AdamW optimizers to evaluate their performance. The MobileNet model with Adam and AdamW optimizers achieved 97% and 98% accuracy, respectively. However, AdamW performed smoother decision boundaries and reduced misclassification at grades 0 and 1. These results indicate that the proposed model is feasible as cost-effective, and clinically beneficial way to monitor the neonate bilirubin level in real-time, especially in limited resource areas.

title: A Novel Wideband, Compact Flexible Microstrip Grid Array Antenna for Wearable Application
authors: Uthayakumar G S, Padma Sri S, Mohan C
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: A compact and flexible <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 4$</tex> rectangular gridarray antenna is presented for next-generation millimeterwave <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 G$</tex> communication systems. The antenna is implemented on a flexible PDMS substrate with an overall size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$55 \times 30 \times 1$</tex> mmt, providing excellent mechanical compliance for wearable and conformal applications. The dielectric constant of PDMS, calculated from its material properties, supports stable wideband performance with low electromagnetic loss. The proposed grid-loaded rectangular radiating structure enhances the effective current path and achieves multiresonant behaviour across a wide frequency region. Simulation results demonstrate a peak gain of 12.6 dBi and a broad impedance bandwidth extending from 4 GHz to 8.4 GHz, making the antenna suitable for midband and sub-6 GHz 5G platforms. With its lightweight construction, high gain, wideband capability, and structural flexibility, the antenna is well suited for wearable communication devices and emerging IoT-based flexible electronic systems.

title: Spatiotemporal Analysis of Drought Patterns in Tamil Nadu (2000-2024): Trends, Hotspots, and Climate Drivers
authors: Kamatchi K. S., Reshma M.
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Drought, a recurring and complex hydroclimatic hazard, poses severe challenges to water security, agriculture, and livelihoods in semi-arid regions like Tamil Nadu. This study presents a comprehensive spatiotemporal assessment of drought patterns in Tamil Nadu for the period 2000–2024, integrating satellite-derived indices and rainfall-based analyses to identify evolving drought trends, hotspots, and climatic drivers. Using multi-temporal Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI-3, SPI-6, and SPI-12) and Precipitation Intensity Index (PII), the research evaluates both the magnitude and temporal variability of droughts at district and seasonal scales. Results reveal a rising trend in drought frequency and severity, with major events observed in 2002, 2009, 2012, 2016, and 2019. The SPI analysis highlights a declining rainfall trend across most districts, while increasing PII values indicate greater rainfall concentration and temporal irregularity. The drought trend map underscores persistent hotspots in Ramanathapuram, Sivaganga, Virudhunagar, Thoothukudi, and Pudukottai, regions characterized by severe rainfall deficits and groundwater stress. A notable shift in peak drought periods from December–January to February–March reflects altered hydroclimatic dynamics, affecting cropping patterns and water resource availability. The study demonstrates the utility of remote sensing and GIS techniques in quantifying spatial drought variability and supports the integration of these tools into early-warning systems and policy frameworks for adaptive drought management.

title: Adaptive Fuzzy Models for Urban Air Quality Index Prediction Using Remote Sensing Data
authors: Nilesh Kumar Dubey, Saleh Naji Sreseh, Ahmed Read Al-Tameemi, Mouhamd Hashim, Dharmendra Sharma, J.Prabakaran
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Predicting urban air quality is critical for public health, environmental planning, and sustainable development. Forecasting models often struggle to capture the nonlinear, uncertain, and spatially variable aspects of atmospheric pollution in populous urban regions. This paper outlines an adaptive fuzzy inference system for air quality predictions, using remote sensing data, specifically satellite-derived measurements of particulate matter (PM2.5, PM10), nitrogen dioxide (NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), sulfur dioxide (SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), and ozone (O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) and including meteorological parameters. The model integrates the spatially distributed environmental data as inputs with fuzzy logic rules that can adapt over time to local atmospheric trends. Remote sensing data are pre-processed by employing feature normalization and filtering to address cloud cover, ensuring modeled measurements obtain telling information of local atmospheric conditions and continuity. This adaptive fuzzy inference system has a feedback loop for updating the fuzzy membership functions and fuzzy rule weights within the model, using a feedback loop based on historical air quality assessment records and meteorological trends, implementing real time environmental data to fully account for local air quality variability. Experimental results covering three urban zones using MODIS and Sentinel-5P data indicated that the adaptive fuzzy inference system has higher accuracy and robustness in predicting air quality than conventional approaches. Thus, this adaptive fuzzy inference system can provide a scalable and explainable framework for real time air quality forecasting that can be applied to inform decisions and direct environmental interventions.

title: Gradient Descent Optimized Neural Networks for Real-Time Tumor Segmentation in MRI Images
authors: S. Rekha, G. Anita Hebsiba, R. Rajesh, S Keerthi, C Sandhiya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Proper diagnosis and planning of treatment in medical imaging requires correct tumor segmentation based on MRI images. The proposed Gradient Descent Optimized Neural Network (GDONN) involves the use of an adaptive gradient-based optimization strategy to address instability and slow convergence of the traditional segmentation models. The technique uses a composite loss of Dice-Cross Entropy to provide better precision of boundaries and vanishing gradients during training. An MRI dataset of 5,000 tumor and non-tumor images that were preprocessed was used to evaluate the models. The experimental outcomes are better performance with an average segmentation accuracy of 98.62%, Dice Similarity Coefficient of 97.84%, precision of 98.11%, recall of 98.27%, and F1-score of 97.95%. The model is capable of real-time segmentation with an inference rate of 0.41 seconds per image and has a high degree of robustness to changes in intensity and noise. In conclusion, the GDONN framework will make sure that tumors can be detected with great efficiency, reliability, and speed, which enables MRI-based medical imaging systems to test their diagnostic abilities.

title: Skin Cancer Segmentation and Classification Using Multi-Task Deep Learning with EfficientNet and Feature Pyramid Network
authors: Seethalakshmi K, K Jayaveera Kumar, B V Pravallika, A Bhagyalakshmi, R Panneerselvi
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Accurately examining dermoscopic images is essential for spotting melanoma and other skin conditions at an early stage, yet most deep-learning systems still handle segmentation, feature detection, and disease classification as if they were unrelated tasks. Treating them separately often leads to repeated computation and weaker generalization. In this work, a single multi-task framework is introduced to manage all three tasks at once. It relies on an EfficientNet backbone to extract shared representations, a U-Net-style decoder that delineates the boundaries of lesions, a lightweight multi-label feature detection branch that identifies key dermoscopic structures, and a classification head that differentiates melanoma from nevus and seborrheic keratosis. Since the ISIC 2017 dataset is severely imbalanced, especially for melanoma, a dynamic oversampling strategy with continuous augmentation was included to give the minority classes more variation without touching the actual dataset. Over several experiments, the system maintained stable and well-rounded performance. Achieved 84 % accuracy during the validation phase and a macro F1-score of 0.8058, while on the final test set, the accuracy settled to 75.8 % with a macro F1-score of 0.6990. The segmentation branch turned out to be quite reliable, maintaining Dice scores above 0.83, while the feature detection head hovered around a mean F1-score of 0.60. These confirm that the system indeed processes both the overall lesion shape and the finer internal details. From these results, it appears that sharing feature representations across tasks leads to better stability and generalization of performance. In this respect, this framework sets a very good basis for further improvements toward clinical decision support and automated skin analysis.

title: A Method for Blockchain Financial Transaction Fraud Detection Based on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Yuemei Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: To enhance the security of the blockchain financial transaction environment, this paper constructs a blockchain financial transaction fraud detection model based on Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). In the GNN layer, the walk-based model classifies nodes based on their walking directions. The message-passing model performs hierarchical feature aggregation for the classified financial transaction nodes, normalizes the feature matrix, and learns the weights of neighboring nodes, ultimately achieving node embedding. The knowledge graph model displays the transaction relationship graph structure of the embedded nodes and infers the financial transaction process. Finally, the result output layer outputs the local neighborhood structure of the nodes, the inference results of financial transaction risks, and the addresses with potential fraud risks. Experiments show that the detection model proposed in this paper achieves an accuracy rate of over 98% for eight types of financial transaction fraud behaviors, with both false negative and false positive rates below 0.05. The accuracy, recall, precision, and F1 score are all around 98%, indicating that the detection model can effectively safeguard financial transactions in the blockchain.

title: Digital Twin-Enabled Diagnostics: A Paradigm Shift in Personalized Healthcare
authors: Smita Shakya, Umang Varshney, Palvi Gupta
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Digital twin (DT) technology is quickly developing into a transformational tool in healthcare, offering dynamic, patient-centric virtual representations that allow for ongoing monitoring, predictive diagnostics, and personalized treatment selection. Conventional diagnostic methods typically rely on static or episodic input data; however, DT enabled diagnostics allow for real-time input from medical imaging, electronic health records, and wearable IoT data collection to produce increasingly complex digital models of patients or organs. DTs, thus defined, may utilize artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) to simulate the duration of disease and can predict patient way in therapies while offering clinicians data-based solutions for clinical judgment. This paper proposes a holistic conceptual model for DT enabled diagnostics that will create a new paradigm toward supporting precision medicine by integrating multimodal data acquisition, analytics, and real-time simulation. Case examples employed or explored in cardiology, oncology, and neurology highlight how DTs are less likely to miss abnormalities, better support planning surgical procedures, and better monitor chronic disease progression than traditional diagnostic approaches. Given our comparison, the highlights of a DT conceptual model as a diagnostic technology include accuracy, predictive reliability, and personalization to individual growth rates in theory will enhance diagnostic capabilities, which was traditionally limited. DTs in healthcare are confronted with challenges, such as data privacy and sharing, interoperability of systems, computational costs, and needing to create and establish regulatory standards. Tackling these limitations will involve converging secure data-sharing mechanisms (e.g., blockchain), scalable AI models, and ethical governance frameworks. In the end, DT-enabled diagnostics will be more than incremental progress; it will be a shift toward preventive, precise, patient-centered healthcare with the potential to reshape clinical practice and influence the future of global health systems.

title: A Preliminary Study on Monitoring Psychological Stress Using Physiological Signals
authors: Reham Alnaser Alsukhni, Amit Krishna Dwivedi
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: A framework based on machine learning for stress detection using PPG and ECG signals, derived from the DREAMER and CLAS databases, is presented. Signal preprocessing included filtering and time and frequency domain features extraction. Different validation strategies, such as Leave-One-Subject-Out (LOSO) and K-fold, were used to explore several classification algorithms, including Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), K-nearest Neighbors (KNN), Logistic Regression (LR) and Decision Tree (DT). The RF algorithm achieved the best performance on the CLAS dataset, achieving an average accuracy of 94.78%± 0.48 and a F1-score of 91% under LOSO validation for ECG signals. Furthermore, the RF outperformed other classifiers, achieving an average accuracy of 81.16±0.66 and a F1-score of 77% using the DREAMER dataset. For PPG-based analysis on the CLAS dataset, both RF and SVM outperformed other classifiers, achieving promising results with an average accuracy of 90.81± 0.17 and a F1-score of 72%, and 91.61±0.11 and a F1-score of 77%, respectively, using the LOSO validation. The RF consistently delivered the most reliable and robust results across both datasets. The findings highlight the potential of using physiological signals to support real-time mental health monitoring in stress recognition. Over, the study presented contributes to the development of multimodal stress detection techniques and provides a robust and reliable framework for AI-driven human–computer interaction systems.

title: A Computational Techniques using LSTM and GRU for English to Hindi Translation
authors: Susheela Vishnoi, Surbhi Sharma, Ankur Mishra
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Neural machine translation has emerged as an effective approach for translation between languages. In this paper, we investigate the task of translation for relatively low-resource languages and, more specifically, the challenges associated with domain-specific translation for low-resource languages. We look at how NMT-based translation has evolved from basic architectures, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM), and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and we compare their results with an advanced transformer-based pre-trained model, namely Helsinki-opus-mt-en-hi. For this purpose, we are utilizing a modest dataset of ∼40k English-Hindi sentence pairs as the computational setup is limited. We present various translation-related metrics, including BLEU, METEOR, and TER scores for the base models and the fine-tuned model for which the METEOR and TER scores weren’t previously reported. This research aims to contribute to understanding NMT for low-resource languages and aid further sustainable research and industry innovation in the field of machine translation.

title: Smart Emergency Landing System For Aircraft
authors: S. Kausalya, T. Baranidharan, M. Kirubasanjay, J. Monik
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The security of the planes when they are faced with some unforeseen inflight incidents is a very serious issue in the current aviation industry. This paper will be discussing a Smart Emergency Landing System (SELS) as a system that is intended to guide the decisions of pilots in making safe and real-time emergency landings. SELS combines various sensors, such as GPS, vibration, temperature and ultrasonic sensors with an STM32 microcontroller to constantly check the position, altitude and integrity of aircraft. The sensor data is sent to ground station through the LoRa communication module facilitating long distance and low latency information exchange. The ground station has a 16x2 LLD-based networked interface powered by NodeMCU and the onboard display has a compact OLED interface that shows critical flight parameters and recommended landing options. SELS will increase pilot decision-making and decrease forced landings through real-time observation and warning signs, as well as high-quality airborne-ground communication, which will improve overall aviation safety. It is a scalable system, and in the future work, it can be expanded with AI-based predictive maintenance and automated landing support.

title: Smart Power Conditioning of PEM Fuel Cells for Sustainable Hybrid Energy Systems: Modeling, Simulation, and Performance Optimization
authors: Devi Parvathi Seelam, Aditya Dubey, Deepak Neeboriya, Sushil Kumar Chaturvedi, Aryan Prajapati, Nookala Venu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Proton Exchange Membrane (PEM) fuel cells are widely regarded as one of the cleanest and most efficient energy sources, thus are becoming a preferred choice in hybrid renewable energy systems. What makes PEM fuel cells remarkable is their capability of utilizing only hydrogen and oxygen to generate energy, thereby releasing no carbon emissions. Hence, these are the most suitable devices to be used as energy storage for future power grids or as micro-generation sources in isolated areas. This paper talks about the modeling and performance benchmarking of a power conditioning unit (PCU) which is powered by a PEM fuel cell through MATLAB/Simulink. The article discusses the different facets of the electrochemical conversion process, the thermal and water management mechanisms, the DC-DC converters' integration for voltage control. Simulation results display the operation of the system under varying loads with a lithium-ion battery storage unit charging process with good energy flow showing stable voltage and current traces. These results indicate system stability, improved power quality and battery state-of-charge performance at a reasonable level, therefore, it is an argument in favor of PEM technology use in renewable energy systems either grid-connected or off-grid ones. Material, control, and hybrid energy are the sectors where the development of next-generation technologies will accelerate the large-scale deployment of PEM fuel cell systems.

title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning for Medical Device Maintenance
authors: Sonali Patil, Deepali Patil, Shradha Vinayak Shilwant, Mily Lal, Manisha Bhende, Vijay Patil
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The increasing complexity and criticality of medical equipment in modern healthcare systems demand reliable and intelligent maintenance strategies to minimize downtime and ensure patient safety. In traditional predictive maintenance, it typically relies on data aggregation in a centralized manner, which poses data privacy and security concerns. In view of those risks associated with traditional data aggregation, a privacy-preserving FL approach for predictive maintenance in the healthcare setting is proposed in this work. This allows different healthcare institutions to collaborate for a common goal and learn together through model aggregation in a manner that no institution shares its operational or sensor data. In fact, each healthcare institution can develop its own DL-based model using its own MD’s TS data. However, the updated model data can pose SII, which can be protected using DP. At the central server, the aggregation strategy FL’s natural requirement of dealing with different data distributions can be handled with FedProx aggregation. The experimental performance indicates that the proposed framework provides performance comparable to that of the C-H model while performing much better than the L-H model and preserving data confidentiality. This further supports that data confidentiality can be maintained through FL. Finally, in this paper, it has been shown that the feasibility and potential of privacy-preserving FL can be applied in MD’s preventive maintenance.

title: Revolutionizing Plant Leaf Disease Classification Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Monali Sanyal, Sharmistha Ghosh, Anunay Ghosh, Soumyabrata Saha, Suparna Dasgupta, Soumya Bhattacharyya, Paramita Sarkar, Kankana Sengupta, Sankhadip Karmakar, Subham Chatterjee, Sunirmal Kumar
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Improving the productivity in agriculture by conserving The integrity of the environment is one of the chief goals. for Indian agriculture. Foliage diseases of crops are known to Seriously affect plant health and further yield. Proper Diagnosis, including precise categorization, would go a long way in reducing the impact that these diseases bring upon and sequent determine food security. This paper will present a An inexpensive method to identify potato leaf automatically diseases using Convolutional Neural Networks. We show their higher accuracy in disease classification compared with traditional machine learning models. This study integrates a large, annotated dataset, advanced pre-processing techniques, and fine-tuned net models, which altogether contribute to robust Performance: this work provides a scalable solution for solving Agricultural challenges such as supporting farmers with timely and reliable diagnostic tools.

title: Machine Learning Optimization of Dual-Band Terahertz CP MIMO DRA for Medical Imaging Applications
authors: Sandeep Kumar, Gagandeep Bharti
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to the design and optimization of dual-band terahertz (THz) circularly polarized (CP) multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) dielectric resonator antennas (DRAs) for medical imaging applications. THz imaging offers exceptional promise for non-invasive medical diagnostics due to its non-ionizing nature and superior resolution compared to conventional techniques. However, designing effective THz antennas for biomedical applications remains challenging due to complex electromagnetic interactions with biological tissues. This paper introduces a machine learning-optimized design methodology that enhances antenna performance metrics critical for medical imaging applications. Our dual-band CP MIMO DRA operates at 0.75 THz and 1.25 THz, demonstrating optimized axial ratio (<3 dB), improved gain (>8 dBi), enhanced bandwidth (>15%), and reduced mutual coupling (<-20 dB) between MIMO elements. The proposed antenna's performance was validated using both simulation and experimental approaches, including tissue-loading studies with multilayer skin phantoms. The implementation of machine learning algorithms, specifically genetic algorithms and artificial neural networks, facilitated optimization across multiple parameters simultaneously, resulting in an antenna design surpassing conventional methodologies in terms of both performance and design efficiency.

title: Development of Dynamic Line Rating Through UPQC Control for Enhanced Grid Stability
authors: N. Sridhar, Naresh Kumar Reddy N, Sreenivasulu B, Vamsi C, Reddy Basha G
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: As society grapples with greater energy consumption and sustainability, sustainable energy systems must be designed and constructed efficiently to support this expectation. Since Power Quality and integration of Renewable Energy Sources (RES) are becoming major concerns in modern power grids, this research proposes a Unified Power Quality Conditioner (UPQC) as a solution for "Integration of Renewable Energy Sources" for enhancing Power Quality in a Three-Phase (3ϕ) Power Grid System. The UPQC integrates several critical components to address issues related to Power Quality by supporting the fundamentals of and providing sustainable energy utilization. However, due to the nature of renewable energy, the UPQC will experience voltage fluctuations and other challenges in terms of Power Quality, such as voltage sags and swells. At the center of the UPQC's ability to enhance Power Quality is the series and shunt converters used within the system. The series converter will help mitigate voltage sag and swell conditions for a smooth and continuous power supply, while the shunt converter mitigate harmonic disturbances and provide a reactive power compensation function. The UPQC is further enhanced through the use of the PI Controller, which optimizes the efficiency of the energy generated. The Series Converter maintains a stable DC link voltage and regulates the flow of energy from the energy source into the grid in an effective manner. By facilitating the management of voltage sags and swells and improving the reliability and consistency of the power supply, the UPQC ultimately allows for a more reliable and stable Power Supply at the end-user level. Finally, this paper is implemented by using MATLAB Simulink 2024a, and it performs better than a power factor of 1 and overall system performance based on the comparative analysis.

title: A Deep Learning Architectures for Liver Disease Detection: From CNNs to Transformers and Beyond
authors: Pranjal Kaushik, Himanshu Vashishtha, Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Manoj Diwakar, Ajay Krishan Gairola, Prabhishek Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease (MAFLD), previously referred to as Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD), is currently established as the world’s most prevalent chronic liver disease. MAFLD impacts almost 25 to 30% of the world’s population. The condition has the potential to develop into extreme conditions such as nonalcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH), fibrosis, cirrhosis, and hepatocellular carcinoma. MAFLD is among the primary causes of liver transplantation globally, accounting for approximately 20 to 25% of liver transplants in the United States. Its mortality rate also keeps growing, as liver-related complications and cardiovascular disease are the most common causes of death. Transformers and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), among other trending model architectures are applied in deep learning for image classification and recognition activities. This article is a comparative review of CNNs, Vision Transformers (ViTs), and other mentioned detection models for automated histological classification and localization of such conditions. In the course of this review, we seek to bridge deep learning technology with applied clinical uses. We emphasize how models such as CNNs, Transformers, have the potential to greatly enhance liver disease detection and classification at the cellular level.

title: Fusion of Spatial and Relational Learning: An Extended Brain-GCN-Net Hybrid Model for Brain Tumor Detection & Classification
authors: Sonam Dung, Rajneesh Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The brain is one of the most complex organs in the human body, that comprises of billons of interconnected cells working in harmony. Abnormal growth of brain cells or tissues is referred to as a brain tumor. When the size of tumor within the cranial cavity increases, it exerts pressure on all nearby tissues and that lead to structural and functional damage of the brain. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is one of the vital diagnostic tool, that enable radiologists to visualize internal structures noninvasively. An image-based medical diagnostic system, plays an essential role in assisting clinicians in the detection & classification of brain tumors.Brain tumor diagnosis remains a complex task due to the heterogeneous nature of tumor structures in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). Various models & techniques have been implemented for detection & Classification task with many pros &cons. An Extended Brain-GCN- Net couples robust local representation learning (CNN) with relational reasoning across patches/slices/modalities (GNN) to enable patient-level inference. This combination typically improves the performance by providing more transparent, clinically relevant attributions via node and edge importance. GNNs are employed to propagate information across the graph, that help in relational reasoning and robust representation learning. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that our approach outperforms CNN and other traditional learning baselines in terms of accuracy, F1-score, and AUC thereby improving computer-aided diagnosis in neuro-oncology. Our proposed model Extended Brain-GCN-Net gives accuracy of 99.3% where as the base model gives out an accuracy of 93.68% thereby leading to early & accurate detection of brain tumor.

title: Adaptive Supervisory Energy Management for Hybrid Electric Vehicles Considering Road Terrain
authors: D. Sharmitha, Manoj T, Premaadithya P, Dhanasekaran R, Nagarjun P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Hybrid electric vehicles need smart energy management to balance fuel usage, battery health, and vehicle performance in different driving conditions. This work proposes a terrain-based energy management approach that uses road slope information along with driver behavior to make better powertrain control decisions. A rule-based supervisory controller is used to keep the battery within safe limits and to reduce unnecessary engine operation. Regenerative braking is applied during braking and downhill driving to recover energy and improve overall efficiency. The proposed strategy is evaluated using MATLAB/Simulink under both urban and highway driving conditions. The simulation results show stable battery state-of-charge, improved energy usage, and noticeable fuel savings, indicating that the approach is effective for intelligent and adaptive energy management in hybrid electric vehicles.

title: Adaptive Machine Learning–Driven Operational Risk Assessment for Autonomous Digital Transformation Systems
authors: Parvathy Raman, Loveleen Gaur
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Systems for autonomous digital transformation are based on machine learning (ML)-based control loops and self-managing infrastructure where operational failures often propagate through complex interactions. Static rules-based traditional risk assessment methodologies are not sufficient to address such an environment. This paper discusses an adaptive, ML model for operational risk assessment of autonomous digital systems under uncertainty. Operational risk is modeled as a forward-looking probabilistic signal derived from temporal task-event telemetry. We assess the approach with task event traces from Google cluster tasks. Risk events are generated by extreme temporal variations in task lifecycle transitions, indicating the scheduling instability that comes ahead of operational degradation. Performance analysis results show the performance to be excellent under extreme class imbalance (positive rate ≈ 0.54%), obtaining an ROC-AUC of 1.00 and precision-recall AUC of 0.959. Results from risk timeline analysis confirm higher risk scores are in fact typically observed before events of instability, substantiating the fit of this framework for the early warning of autonomous digital transformation systems.

title: A Blockchain-Enabled Secure Federated Learning Framework for Decentralized AI Systems
authors: K. Dinesh Kumar, Yaramati Surya Venkata Chaitanya, Lakkoju Hemanth Sai, I. Govindharaj, B. Prabhu Shankar, S. Edwin Raja
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) allows cooperative model training without the exchange of raw information, which makes it suitable in the privacy-intensive distributed setting. Nonetheless, traditional FL systems are based on centralized aggregation servers, and they are also assumed to participate honestly, which makes them vulnerable to model poisoning and free-rider attacks as well as transparency. This paper suggests TrustChain-FL, which is a federated learning framework based on blockchain with a secure federated learning system, which combines a permissioned blockchain with FL to provide decentralized trust, immutable model update records, and automatic validation via smart contracts. The results in experiments conducted in a simulated federated learning setting show that TrustChain-FL is effective in the scenario of mitigating malicious updates without losing model accuracy and convergence efficiency. The given framework can be applied to decentralized AI applications, including healthcare, smart cities, and industrial IoT. Experimental outcome reaches the best accuracy of up to 92.6% and huge resistance to poisoning attacks when compared to traditional FL Unlike current blockchain-assisted FL systems, TrustChain-FL is decoupled between update validation and aggregation with sparse permissioned consensus.

title: Design of a VLSI Enabled Quantum-Resistant Blockchain Architecture with AI (Artificial Intelligence)–Driven Intrusion Detection System and Computer Vision for Secure Embedded, Internet of Things Edge Systems
authors: Adinath M, Parvesh S M, Vinay Bhopana, Abinandhan R, Pranavesh Aravind, Aadhitya S, Seshua Sai M, Shamili E
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The paper describes a new architecture, which closely incorporates VLSI-optimized post-quantum cryptography with blockchain, deep-learning-based intrusion detection, and computer vision to secure IoT-edge and autonomous vehicle networks. A hardware based implementation of a lattice-based crypto-engine (LUT-CLA-QTL design) with quantum-resistant authentication and ledger operation is proposed to be synthesized with 7-3 nm ASIC flows [1] [2]. These lattice signatures are implemented with a permissioned blockchain protocol to authenticate IoT devices and offer data integrity in resource-constrained edge networks. At the same time, an AI-based intrusion detection system (IDS) is implemented in the edge, using hybrid CNN-LSTM models, Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and transformer-based models, which are quantized through the use of TensorFlow Lite or PyTorch Mobile to make inferences on-the-edge [3] [4]. Computer vision modules (e.g. object detectors based on YOLOv8) offer situational threat awareness (e.g. identifying illegal individuals or vehicles through smart cameras) and inject corresponding alerts into the blockchain. We used Cadence and Synopsys flows ( Cadence Genus/ Inno vus, Synopsys Design Compiler ) to verify ASIC/FPGA with the architecture. Multi-nanometer gains (e.g. 0.45 mm2 at 3 nm, 1.00 ns, 35 mW) and FPGA verification on Xilinx UltraScale+ (3500 LUTs, 3.2 ns, 90 mW) [7] [8] are demonstrated by the ASIC results (Table 1). Deep models give over 95% accuracy of IDS with standard IoT data, and YOLOv8 gives real-time detection accuracy (~80-90) on surveillance applications. The built-in system has strong authentication, low-latency encryption, and high detection, which is appropriate to secure autonomous vehicles and intelligent city IoT.

title: 70 Years of the IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management: Trends and Patterns in Scholarly Publishing_supp1-3685972.pdf
authors: Jose M. Merigo
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive bibliometric and science mapping analysis of the IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management (IEEE TEM), one of the longest-standing journals in the field of technology, innovation and engineering management. Renamed in 1963, IEEE TEM has evolved from its earlier forms as the Transactions of the IRE Professional Group on Engineering Management (1954) and the IRE Transactions on Engineering Management (1955-1962). Drawing on data from Web of Science and Scopus (1954-2024), the study examines publication and citation trends, leading authors, institutions, and countries, as well as the journal's knowledge structure and thematic evolution. Standard bibliometric techniques-including co-citation, bibliographic coupling, and keyword co-occurrence (VOSviewer; Bibliometrix)-were employed to uncover intellectual foundations, collaborative networks, and emerging research frontiers. Findings highlight the journal's strong international orientation, increasing interdisciplinarity across business management, industrial engineering, computer science, and information systems, and a marked surge in activity since 2020. Analyses of influential documents, institutions, and topic clusters reveal IEEE TEM's central role in advancing research on innovation, project management, digital transformation, sustainability, and supply chain systems. The paper summarizes IEEE TEM's historical development and current standing and distills evidence-based implications from the reported indicators and maps.

title: Hardware Architecture for High Speed QPSK Communication Links using TDM and Multi Data Rate signals
authors: Meena D, Sarath K T, Vidyamol S, Preenu Acha Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Microwave Photonic technologies show rapid growth in current era of high-speed data communication, due to its support with broad bandwidth, immunity to electromagnetic interference, excellent isolation and high frequency operation capabilities. In this work, an optical QPSK communication system is proposed that operates with different input bit rate data streams. The architecture is further extended for the transmission of a Time Division Multiplexed (TDM) data stream with improved data handling capability and extracting different bit streams of data at the receiver end. Modelling of hardware architectures are carried out in a standard simulation environment. The paper describes about the architecture models in detail along with the results obtained during the evaluation process. In these architectures, a highly stable Mode Lock Laser (MLL) is used to provide the optical carrier signal. The time multiplexed signal over a single hardware increases the data handling capability suitable for high-speed data links.

title: Deep Learning Framework for Indian Sign Language Communication
authors: P.N.S.V.Tejaswini, T Srinivasarao, P.Neha Reddy, S.Harshith, V. Bala Venkata Ramana
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This research presents a deep learning-based framework for the real-time recognition and translation of Indian Sign Language (ISL) gestures into both text and spoken words. The goal is to improve communication between individuals who are deaf or hard-of-hearing and those who can hear. The framework uses a combined computer vision pipeline, integrating image preprocessing, keypoint extraction, and data augmentation to improve model performance. We collected a curated ISL dataset, which includes both isolated signs and continuous gesture sequences. Data collection followed standardized guidelines on lighting, background uniformity, signer variability, and class balance to ensure effectiveness in real-world conditions. For recognition, we created a hybrid neural network that uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to extract important spatial features from hand shapes and body postures, along with temporal models like Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNNs) to capture motion dynamics. The training strategy includes transfer learning, adaptive learning rates, early stopping, and dropout methods to reduce overfitting and enhance model performance. We used accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix analysis as evaluation metrics for isolated and continuous sign recognition tasks. Experimental results on our dataset show high accuracy (over 98%) for isolated sign recognition and competitive performance for continuous sign sequences. This highlights the model's ability to handle both static and dynamic gestures. We implemented a prototype application for both desktop and mobile platforms, achieving real-time speeds and smooth translation from ISL to text and speech. These results suggest the system can serve as a practical communication tool with potential use in education, accessibility, and public services.

title: Deep Learning and Predictive Analytics-based AI Agriculture Assistant Integrating CNN for Enhanced Farm Productivity
authors: Janani. V, Pavithra. T, Subikshaa. M, Prathiba P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The traditional approach to farming is gradually being replaced by modern technologies in the agricultural sector, which still use the age-old method and have to deal with the problem of disease control. This naturally leads to the question of how to increase the yield of crops without the automatic removal of the affected areas, which means the use of accurate and reliable methods in the first place. The crop-growing or plant-raising areas are divided into three main categories: those that require less human intervention, those that are good for crop rotation, and those that are going to be abandoned. The trained model applied to the data considers all the parameters of soil and weather in the specific area, predicts the best crop, and at the same time gives an estimate of the amount of crop that can be harvested. Thus, the farmer is helped to make the right decision, cut the costs, and increase productivity. Moreover, the model uses CNN to process disease images and revealed diseased areas through non-invasive techniques without delaying harvest time. The system, or model, prescribes fertilizers or pesticides respectively to the diseases and the plants can be easily saved and the soil can be easily balanced. All the forecasting and recommending happens through real-time SMS alerts, thus giving the farmers the chance to act quickly and lessen their losses. The new AI-based system combines cutting-edge techniques to enhance the agricultural experience and help the farmers improve their crops by employing fewer wrongs and being more efficient. The integrated approach is data-driven, which is a substantial advance over conventional methods, thus giving a dependable, precise, and tech-enabled resolution for the practice of modern farming.

title: Comparing Hybrid and Traditional Agile Practices on Software Quality and Delivery Speed in Large-Scale Enterprise Teams
authors: Velmurugan S, Sundaram K Meenakshi, Anusya S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This study presents an empirical comparison of hybrid Agile and traditional Agile practices in large-scale enterprise software development environments. Using project-level data collected from distributed enterprise teams over an 18-month observation period, the research evaluates software quality and delivery performance through defect-related metrics, delivery speed, and schedule predictability. The findings indicate that while traditional Agile approaches achieve higher short-term delivery speed, hybrid Agile practices demonstrate superior quality stability and delivery reliability as project scale and complexity increase. The results highlight the importance of governance-supported Agile models for sustaining software quality and predictable delivery in long-term enterprise systems.
